      exact hspan (eq_top_iff'.2 (by push Not at hcon; exact hcon))
    -- append `y` and recurse
    obtain ⟨w, hw, hwc⟩ :=
      ih (k + 1) (Fin.snoc c y) (linearIndependent_finSnoc.2 ⟨hc, hy⟩) (by omega)
    refine ⟨fun j => w (Fin.cast (by omega) j),
      hw.comp _ (finCongr (show k + (d + 1) = k + 1 + d by omega)).injective, fun i => ?_⟩
    have hcast : (Fin.cast (show k + (d + 1) = k + 1 + d by omega) (Fin.castAdd (d + 1) i))
        = Fin.castAdd d (Fin.castSucc i) := by ext; simp
    simp only [hcast, hwc, Fin.snoc_castSucc]

end Extend

/-! ### Linear independence of smooth sections is an open condition -/

section Fibre

/- The fibrewise dimension facts below hold for *any* real vector bundle: they read a
trivialization as a linear equivalence onto the model fibre and use nothing else.  In particular
they must not be stated in the inner-product context of `section Openness`, since the indefinite
development (`LeeLib.Ch02.PseudoAdaptedFrame`) needs them for bundles whose fibres carry no
inner product at all. -/
variable
  {B : Type*} [TopologicalSpace B]
  {F : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup F] [NormedSpace ℝ F]
  {V : B → Type*} [TopologicalSpace (TotalSpace F V)]
  [∀ x, TopologicalSpace (V x)] [∀ x, AddCommGroup (V x)] [∀ x, Module ℝ (V x)]
  [FiberBundle F V] [VectorBundle ℝ F V]

blocks.tex:
Induct on $d$. If $d = 0$, take $w = c$.

Suppose the claim holds for $d$, and let $k + d + 1 \leq m$. Then $\dim \mathrm{span}(c_1, \ldots, c_k) = k < m$, so $\mathrm{span}(c_1, \ldots, c_k) \neq V$ and there is $y \in V \setminus \mathrm{span}(c_1, \ldots, c_k)$. The family $(c_1, \ldots, c_k, y)$ is linearly independent: in a vanishing linear combination $\sum_i a_i c_i + b y = 0$, a nonzero $b$ would give $y = -b^{-1}\sum_i a_i c_i \in \mathrm{span}(c_1,\ldots,c_k)$, contrary to the choice of $y$; so $b = 0$, and then all $a_i$ vanish by independence of the $c_i$. Applying the inductive hypothesis to this family of $k+1$ vectors and to $d$ (note $(k+1) + d \le m$) yields a linearly independent family of $k + 1 + d = k + (d+1)$ vectors whose first $k+1$ members are $c_1, \ldots, c_k, y$; in particular its first $k$ members are $c_1, \ldots, c_k$.

blocks.label: lem:open-linear-independence
blocks.title: Linear independence of smooth sections is an open condition
blocks.body: Let $V \to B$ be a smooth vector bundle with model fibre $F$, let $u \subseteq B$ be open, and let $s_1, \ldots, s_k$ be smooth sections of $V$ over $u$. If $(s_1|_{x_0}, \ldots, s_k|_{x_0})$ is linearly independent in $V_{x_0}$ for some $x_0 \in u$, then there is an open set $v$ with $x_0 \in v \subseteq u$ such that $(s_1|_x, \ldots, s_k|_x)$ is linearly independent in $V_x$ for every $x \in v$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.exists_isLocalIndepOn_nhds
lean.code:
theorem exists_isLocalIndepOn_nhds
    (hsm : ∀ i, ContMDiffOn IB (IB.prod 𝓘(ℝ, F)) n (T% (X i)) u) (hu : IsOpen u) (hx₀ : x₀ ∈ u)
    (hli : LinearIndependent ℝ (X · x₀)) :
    ∃ v, IsOpen v ∧ x₀ ∈ v ∧ v ⊆ u ∧ IsLocalIndepOn IB F n X v := by
  set e := trivializationAt F V x₀ with he
  have hx₀e : x₀ ∈ e.baseSet := mem_baseSet_trivializationAt F V x₀
  -- the family read in the trivialization at `x₀`
  set c : B → (ι → F) := fun x i => (e ⟨x, X i x⟩).2 with hc
  have hcont : ContinuousAt c x₀ := by
    refine continuousAt_pi.2 fun i => ?_
    have h := (hsm i).contMDiffAt (hu.mem_nhds hx₀)
    rw [contMDiffAt_section] at h
    exact h.continuousAt
  have hcli : LinearIndependent ℝ (c x₀) := by
    have hcomp : c x₀ = ⇑(e.continuousLinearEquivAt ℝ x₀ hx₀e).toLinearEquiv.toLinearMap
        ∘ (X · x₀) := funext fun i => trivializationAt_snd_eq e hx₀e (X i x₀)
    rw [hcomp]
    exact hli.map' _ (LinearMap.ker_eq_bot_of_injective
      (e.continuousLinearEquivAt ℝ x₀ hx₀e).injective)
  -- linear independence persists near `x₀`, and so do membership in `u` and in the base set
  have hev : ∀ᶠ x in 𝓝 x₀, LinearIndependent ℝ (c x) ∧ x ∈ u ∧ x ∈ e.baseSet := by
    refine (hcont.eventually hcli.eventually).and (Filter.Eventually.and ?_ ?_)
    · exact hu.mem_nhds hx₀
    · exact e.open_baseSet.mem_nhds hx₀e
  obtain ⟨v, hvsub, hvopen, hx₀v⟩ := mem_nhds_iff.1 hev
  refine ⟨v, hvopen, hx₀v, fun x hx => (hvsub hx).2.1, ?_⟩
  refine ⟨fun {x} hx => ?_, fun i => (hsm i).mono fun x hx => (hvsub hx).2.1⟩
  obtain ⟨hcx, -, hxe⟩ := hvsub hx
  -- transport independence back through the fibrewise equivalence
  have hcomp : c x = ⇑(e.continuousLinearEquivAt ℝ x hxe).toLinearEquiv.toLinearMap ∘ (X · x) :=
    funext fun i => trivializationAt_snd_eq e hxe (X i x)
  rw [hcomp] at hcx
  exact hcx.of_comp _

blocks.tex:
First, the set of linearly independent $k$-tuples is open in $F^k$. Let $(v_1, \ldots, v_k)$ be linearly independent and let $L : \mathbb{R}^k \to F$ be the injective linear map $a \mapsto \sum_i a_i v_i$. As $\mathbb{R}^k$ is finite dimensional, injectivity of $L$ gives a $\kappa > 0$ with $\|La\| \geq \kappa \|a\|$ for all $a$. Let $(v_1', \ldots, v_k')$ satisfy $\delta := \sum_i \|v_i' - v_i\| < \kappa$, and let $L'$ be the corresponding map. Then
\[
\|L'a - La\| = \Big\| \sum_i a_i (v_i' - v_i) \Big\| \leq \max_i |a_i| \sum_i \|v_i' - v_i\| \leq \delta \|a\|,
\]
so $\|L'a\| \geq \|La\| - \delta\|a\| \geq (\kappa - \delta)\|a\| > 0$ for $a \neq 0$. Hence $L'$ is injective, i.e. $(v_i')$ is linearly independent.

Now choose a local trivialization $\Phi$ of $V$ over an open set containing $x_0$, and write $\Phi_x : V_x \to F$ for its fibrewise linear isomorphism. Put $c(x) = (\Phi_x(s_1|_x), \ldots, \Phi_x(s_k|_x)) \in F^k$. Each component is continuous at $x_0$ because $s_i$ is a smooth section, so $c$ is continuous at $x_0$; and $c(x_0)$ is linearly independent, being the image of a linearly independent tuple under the isomorphism $\Phi_{x_0}$. By the previous paragraph linear independence holds on a neighbourhood of $c(x_0)$, so by continuity there is an open $v$ with $x_0 \in v$, contained in $u$ and in the domain of $\Phi$, such that $c(x)$ is linearly independent for every $x \in v$. For such $x$, $(s_i|_x)_i$ is the image of $c(x)$ under the isomorphism $\Phi_x^{-1}$ and is therefore linearly independent.

blocks.label: lem:partial-frame-extension
blocks.title: Extension of a partial frame
blocks.body: Let $V \to B$ be a smooth vector bundle of rank $m$, let $u \subseteq B$ be open, and let $(s_1, \ldots, s_k)$ be a local $k$-frame for $V$ over $u$. Then $k \leq m$, and for each $x_0 \in u$ there are an open set $v$ with $x_0 \in v \subseteq u$ and a smooth local frame $(E_1, \ldots, E_m)$ for $V$ over $v$ with $E_i = s_i$ on $v$ for $1 \leq i \leq k$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.exists_isLocalFrameOn_extend
lean.code:
theorem exists_isLocalFrameOn_extend {k : ℕ} {X : Fin k → (x : B) → V x} {u : Set B} {x₀ : B}
    (hX : IsLocalIndepOn IB F n X u) (hu : IsOpen u) (hx₀ : x₀ ∈ u) :
    ∃ (v : Set B) (Y : Fin (finrank ℝ F) → (x : B) → V x) (hk : k ≤ finrank ℝ F),
      IsOpen v ∧ x₀ ∈ v ∧ v ⊆ u ∧ IsLocalFrameOn IB F n Y v ∧
        ∀ (i : Fin k) (x : B), x ∈ v → Y (Fin.castLE hk i) x = X i x := by
  classical
  haveI := finiteDimensional_fibre (F := F) (V := V) x₀
  set m := finrank ℝ F with hm
  set e := trivializationAt F V x₀ with he
  have hx₀e : x₀ ∈ e.baseSet := mem_baseSet_trivializationAt F V x₀
  set Φ := e.continuousLinearEquivAt ℝ x₀ hx₀e with hΦ
  -- the family, read in the trivialization at `x₀`
  set c : Fin k → F := fun i => Φ (X i x₀) with hcdef
  have hc : LinearIndependent ℝ c :=
    (hX.linearIndependent hx₀).map' Φ.toLinearEquiv.toLinearMap
      (LinearMap.ker_eq_bot_of_injective Φ.injective)
  have hk : k ≤ m := by
    simpa using hc.fintype_card_le_finrank
  -- extend it to a basis of `F`, keeping `c` in the first `k` slots
  obtain ⟨w, hw, hwc⟩ :=
    exists_linearIndependent_castAdd (F := F) (m - k) k c hc (by omega)
  set w' : Fin m → F := fun j => w (Fin.cast (by omega) j) with hw'def
  have hw' : LinearIndependent ℝ w' :=
    hw.comp _ (finCongr (show m = k + (m - k) by omega)).injective
  have hw'c : ∀ i : Fin k, w' (Fin.castLE hk i) = c i := fun i => by
    have : (Fin.cast (show m = k + (m - k) by omega) (Fin.castLE hk i))
        = Fin.castAdd (m - k) i := by ext; simp
    simp only [hw'def, this, hwc]
  set b : Basis (Fin m) ℝ F :=
    Basis.mk hw' (hw'.span_eq_top_of_card_eq_finrank' (by simp [hm])).ge with hbdef
  have hb : ∀ j, b j = w' j := fun j => Basis.mk_apply _ _ j
  -- the enlarged family: `X` where it exists, constant-in-`e` sections beyond
  set Y : Fin m → (x : B) → V x :=
    fun j x => if h : (j : ℕ) < k then X ⟨j, h⟩ x else e.localFrame b j x with hYdef
  -- `Y` restricted to the first `k` slots is `X`
  have hYX : ∀ (i : Fin k) (x : B), Y (Fin.castLE hk i) x = X i x := fun i x => by
    simp only [hYdef, Fin.coe_castLE, dif_pos i.isLt, Fin.eta]
  -- at `x₀` every `Y j` is the `e`-preimage of `w' j`, so `Y · x₀` is independent
  have hYx₀ : ∀ j, Y j x₀ = Φ.symm (w' j) := fun j => by
    by_cases h : (j : ℕ) < k
    · have hj : Fin.castLE hk ⟨(j : ℕ), h⟩ = j := by ext; simp
      rw [← hj, hYX ⟨(j : ℕ), h⟩ x₀, hw'c ⟨(j : ℕ), h⟩, hcdef]
      simp
    · simp only [hYdef, dif_neg h, e.localFrame_apply_of_mem_baseSet b hx₀e,
        Trivialization.basisAt, Basis.map_apply, hb j]
      rfl
  have hYli : LinearIndependent ℝ (Y · x₀) := by
    have : (Y · x₀) = ⇑Φ.symm.toLinearEquiv.toLinearMap ∘ w' := funext hYx₀
    rw [this]
    exact hw'.map' _ (LinearMap.ker_eq_bot_of_injective Φ.symm.injective)
  -- `Y` is smooth on `u ∩ e.baseSet`
  have hYsm : ∀ j, ContMDiffOn IB (IB.prod 𝓘(ℝ, F)) n (T% (Y j)) (u ∩ e.baseSet) := fun j => by
    by_cases h : (j : ℕ) < k
    · exact ((hX.contMDiffOn ⟨(j : ℕ), h⟩).mono Set.inter_subset_left).congr
        fun x _ => by simp only [hYdef, dif_pos h]
    · exact ((e.contMDiffOn_localFrame_baseSet n b j).mono Set.inter_subset_right).congr
        fun x _ => by simp only [hYdef, dif_neg h]
  obtain ⟨v, hvopen, hx₀v, hvsub, hvindep⟩ :=
    exists_isLocalIndepOn_nhds hYsm (hu.inter e.open_baseSet) ⟨hx₀, hx₀e⟩ hYli
  exact ⟨v, Y, hk, hvopen, hx₀v, fun x hx => (hvsub hx).1,
  -- … (truncated)

deps.title: Ordered extension of a linearly independent family
deps.label: lem:ordered-linear-extension

deps.title: Linear independence of smooth sections is an open condition
deps.label: lem:open-linear-independence

blocks.tex:
Fix a local trivialization $\Phi$ of $V$ over an open set containing $x_0$, with fibrewise isomorphisms $\Phi_x : V_x \to F$. The vectors $c_i := \Phi_{x_0}(s_i|_{x_0})$, $1 \leq i \leq k$, are linearly independent in $F$, whence $k \leq \dim F = m$. By Lemma \ref{lem:ordered-linear-extension} with $d = m - k$ they extend to a linearly independent family $(w_1, \ldots, w_m)$ in $F$ with $w_i = c_i$ for $i \leq k$; being $m$ independent vectors in the $m$-dimensional space $F$, it is a basis.

Over $u \cap \mathrm{dom}\,\Phi$ define
\[
E_i = s_i \quad (1 \leq i \leq k), \qquad E_j|_x = \Phi_x^{-1}(w_j) \quad (k < j \leq m).
\]
For $i \leq k$ the section $E_i$ is smooth by hypothesis; for $j > k$ it is smooth because it is constant in the trivialization $\Phi$.

At $x_0$ we have $E_j|_{x_0} = \Phi_{x_0}^{-1}(w_j)$ for \emph{every} $j$: for $j > k$ by definition, and for $j \leq k$ because $w_j = c_j = \Phi_{x_0}(s_j|_{x_0})$. So $(E_1|_{x_0}, \ldots, E_m|_{x_0})$ is the image of the basis $(w_j)_j$ under the isomorphism $\Phi_{x_0}^{-1}$, hence a basis of $V_{x_0}$; in particular it is linearly independent. By Lemma \ref{lem:open-linear-independence} there is an open $v$ with $x_0 \in v \subseteq u \cap \mathrm{dom}\,\Phi$ on which $(E_1|_x, \ldots, E_m|_x)$ is linearly independent at every point. Since $\dim V_x = m$, a linearly independent family of $m$ vectors in $V_x$ spans $V_x$; so $(E_1, \ldots, E_m)$ is a smooth local frame for $V$ over $v$, and it agrees with $s$ in its first $k$ members by construction.

blocks.label: lem:pushforward-smooth
blocks.title: Smoothness of the pushforward of a vector field
blocks.body: Let $F : M \to \tilde{M}$ be a smooth map and let $X$ be a smooth vector field on an open set $u \subseteq M$. Then $x \mapsto dF_x(X_x)$ is a smooth section of the ambient tangent bundle $F^* T\tilde{M}$ over $u$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.contMDiffOn_pushforward
lean.code:
theorem contMDiffOn_pushforward {f : C^∞⟮I, M; I', M'⟯} {X : (x : M) → TangentSpace I x}
    {u : Set M} (hu : IsOpen u) (hX : ContMDiffOn I (I.prod 𝓘(ℝ, E)) ∞ (T% X) u) :
    ContMDiffOn I (I.prod 𝓘(ℝ, E')) ∞
      (fun x => TotalSpace.mk' E' x (pushforward f X x)) u := fun x hx =>
  (contMDiffAt_pushforward (hX.contMDiffAt (hu.mem_nhds hx))).contMDiffWithinAt

deps.title: The Ambient Tangent Bundle Along a Submanifold
deps.label: lem:ambient-tangent-bundle-riemannian

blocks.tex:
Fix $x_0 \in u$, a trivialization $\Sigma$ of $TM$ around $x_0$ and a trivialization $\Theta$ of $T\tilde{M}$ around $F(x_0)$, with fibrewise isomorphisms $\Sigma_x$ and $\Theta_y$. By construction a trivialization of $F^* T\tilde M$ at $x_0$ is $\Theta$ read along $F$: its fibre map at $x$ is $\Theta_{F(x)}$. Hence, for $x$ in the neighbourhood of $x_0$ on which $x \in \mathrm{dom}\,\Sigma$ and $F(x) \in \mathrm{dom}\,\Theta$, the section $x \mapsto dF_x(X_x)$ read in that trivialization is
\[
x \longmapsto \Theta_{F(x)}\big(dF_x(X_x)\big) = D(x)\,\xi(x), \qquad
D(x) := \Theta_{F(x)} \circ dF_x \circ \Sigma_x^{-1}, \quad \xi(x) := \Sigma_x(X_x),
\]
the equality holding because $\Sigma_x^{-1}(\xi(x)) = X_x$.

The map $\xi$ is smooth because $X$ is a smooth section read in $\Sigma$, and $D$ — the differential of $F$ in tangent coordinates — is smooth because $F$ is smooth. Evaluating a smooth family of continuous linear maps on a smooth family of vectors is smooth, so the coordinate representation is smooth at $x_0$. As $x_0 \in u$ was arbitrary, the section is smooth on $u$.

Suppose $(\tilde{M}, \tilde{g})$ is a Riemannian manifold and $M \subseteq \tilde{M}$ is a smooth submanifold with or without boundary in $\tilde{M}$. Given $p \in M$, a vector $v \in T_p \tilde M$ is said to be \textit{normal to} $M$ if $\langle v,w\rangle = 0$ for every $w \in T_p M$. The space of all vectors normal to $M$ at $p$ is a subspace of $T_p \tilde{M}$, called the \textit{normal space at} $p$ and denoted by $N_p M = (T_p M)^\perp$. At each $p \in M$, the ambient tangent space $T_p \tilde{M}$ splits as an orthogonal direct sum $T_p \tilde{M} = T_p M \oplus N_p M$. A section $N$ of the ambient tangent bundle $T\tilde{M}|_M$ is called a \textit{normal vector field along} $M$ if $N_p \in N_p M$ for each $p \in M$. The set
\[
NM = \bigsqcup_{p \in M} N_p M
\]
is called the \textit{normal bundle of} $M$.

blocks.label: lem:ambient-tangent-bundle-riemannian
blocks.title: The Ambient Tangent Bundle Along a Submanifold
blocks.body:
Let $(\tilde M, \tilde g)$ be a Riemannian manifold and let $F : M \to \tilde M$ be a smooth map. Then the pullback bundle $F^* T\tilde M$ over $M$, whose fibre at $p$ is $T_{F(p)}\tilde M$, carries the ambient inner product $\tilde g_{F(p)}$ on each fibre, and this inner product varies smoothly with $p$; that is, $F^* T\tilde M$ is a Riemannian vector bundle over $M$.

When $F$ is the inclusion of a submanifold $M \subseteq \tilde M$, this bundle is the \emph{ambient tangent bundle along $M$}, written $T\tilde M|_M$: the bundle in which the splitting $T_p\tilde M = T_p M \oplus N_p M$ and the projections $\pi^\top, \pi^\perp$ of Proposition \ref{prop:normal-bundle} live.

deps.title: The metric induced by an immersion
deps.label: def:induced-metric

blocks.tex:
The fibre of $F^* T\tilde M$ at $p$ \emph{is} $T_{F(p)}\tilde M$, so it carries $\tilde g_{F(p)}$ with no choice involved; symmetry and positive definiteness are those of $\tilde g$ at the point $F(p)$. Only the smooth dependence on $p$ requires an argument.

Fix $p_0 \in M$. Smoothness of a metric on a vector bundle means that, read through a local trivialization of the bundle near $p_0$, the metric is a smooth map into the space of bilinear forms on the model fibre. By construction, a trivialization of $F^* T\tilde M$ over $p_0$ is the pullback along $F$ of a trivialization $\Phi$ of $T\tilde M$ over $F(p_0)$: its fibre map at $p$ is the fibre map of $\Phi$ at $F(p)$. Hence, for $p$ in the neighbourhood of $p_0$ on which $F(p)$ stays in the domain of $\Phi$, the metric of $F^* T\tilde M$ read through the pulled-back trivialization at $p$ equals the metric $\tilde g$ read through $\Phi$ at $F(p)$.

The map $p \mapsto (\tilde g \text{ read through } \Phi)(F(p))$ is a composition of $F$, which is smooth, with the coordinate representation of $\tilde g$, which is smooth because $\tilde g$ is a smooth metric. So the coordinate representation of the pulled-back metric is smooth at $p_0$, and $p_0$ was arbitrary.

blocks.label: prop:adapted-orthonormal-frames-immersed
blocks.title: Existence of Adapted Orthonormal Frames, immersed form
blocks.body:
Let $(\tilde M, \tilde g)$ be a Riemannian $m$-manifold, let $M$ be an $n$-manifold and let
$F : M \to \tilde M$ be a smooth immersion. Then $n \le m$, and every $p \in M$ has a
neighborhood $v$ carrying a smooth orthonormal frame $(E_1, \ldots, E_m)$ for the ambient
tangent bundle $F^* T\tilde M$ of Lemma \ref{lem:ambient-tangent-bundle-riemannian} such that,
at every $x \in v$,
\[
  \operatorname{span}(E_1|_x, \ldots, E_n|_x) \;=\; \operatorname{im} dF_x .
\]
Since $\operatorname{im} dF_x$ is Lee's $T_x M$ viewed inside $T_{F(x)}\tilde M$, this says
exactly that the frame is adapted to $M$. It is Proposition \ref{prop:adapted-orthonormal-frames}
with the submanifold presented by an immersion rather than by an embedding, so it applies to
immersed submanifolds as well; the price is that the frame is a frame of $F^* T\tilde M$ over
$M$ rather than of $T\tilde M$ over an open subset of $\tilde M$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.exists_adapted_orthonormalFrame
lean.code:
theorem exists_adapted_orthonormalFrame (g' : RiemannianMetric I' M') (f : C^∞⟮I, M; I', M'⟯)
    (himm : ∀ x : M, Function.Injective (mfderiv I I' f x)) (p : M) :
    ∃ (v : Set M) (Y : Fin (finrank ℝ E') → (x : M) →
        ((f : M → M') *ᵖ (TangentSpace I' : M' → Type _)) x) (hn : finrank ℝ E ≤ finrank ℝ E'),
      IsOpen v ∧ p ∈ v ∧ IsLocalFrameOn I E' ∞ Y v ∧
      (∀ x ∈ v, ∀ i j, g'.inner (f x) (Y i x) (Y j x) = if i = j then 1 else 0) ∧
      (∀ x ∈ v, ∀ i : Fin (finrank ℝ E), Y (Fin.castLE hn i) x ∈ tangentRange f x) ∧
      (∀ x ∈ v, span ℝ (Set.range fun i : Fin (finrank ℝ E) => Y (Fin.castLE hn i) x)
        = tangentRange f x) := by
  classical
  -- the ambient metric makes `T M̃|_M` a Riemannian bundle, so Gram-Schmidt runs in its fibres
  letI : Bundle.RiemannianBundle ((f : M → M') *ᵖ (TangentSpace I' : M' → Type _)) :=
    ⟨(g'.pullback f).toRiemannianMetric⟩
  set V := ((f : M → M') *ᵖ (TangentSpace I' : M' → Type _)) with hV
  -- a chart frame for `TM` around `p`
  set e := trivializationAt E (TangentSpace I) p with he
  have hpe : p ∈ e.baseSet := mem_baseSet_trivializationAt E (TangentSpace I) p
  set X := e.localFrame (Module.finBasis ℝ E) with hXdef
  have hX : IsLocalFrameOn I E ∞ X e.baseSet :=
    e.isLocalFrameOn_localFrame_baseSet I ∞ (Module.finBasis ℝ E)
  -- push it forward into the ambient bundle: `n` independent smooth sections of `V`
  set Z : Fin (finrank ℝ E) → (x : M) → V x := fun i x => pushforward f (X i) x with hZdef
  have hZ : IsLocalIndepOn I E' ∞ Z e.baseSet :=
    { linearIndependent := fun {x} hx =>
        linearIndependent_pushforward (himm x) (hX.linearIndependent hx)
      contMDiffOn := fun i => contMDiffOn_pushforward e.open_baseSet (hX.contMDiffOn i) }
  -- extend them to a full frame of `V` near `p`
  obtain ⟨v, Y₀, hn, hvopen, hpv, hvsub, hY₀, hY₀Z⟩ :=
    exists_isLocalFrameOn_extend (IB := I) (F := E') (V := V) hZ e.open_baseSet hpe
  -- every member of `Y₀` below the cut is a pushforward, hence tangent to `M`
  have hY₀tan : ∀ x ∈ v, ∀ i : Fin (finrank ℝ E), Y₀ (Fin.castLE hn i) x ∈ tangentRange f x :=
    fun x hx i => by
      rw [hY₀Z i x hx]; exact pushforward_mem_tangentRange f (X i) x
  -- Gram-Schmidt preserves the flag, so `GS_k` stays inside the span of `Y₀_1, …, Y₀_k`;
  -- for `k` below the cut those are all tangent, and the span of a set of tangent vectors is
  -- tangent.  This is the whole reason the *ordered* extension mattered.
  have hGStan : ∀ x ∈ v, ∀ i : Fin (finrank ℝ E),
      gramSchmidtFrame (V := V) Y₀ (Fin.castLE hn i) x ∈ tangentRange f x := by
    intro x hx i
    have hmem : gramSchmidtFrame (V := V) Y₀ (Fin.castLE hn i) x
        ∈ span ℝ ((fun j => gramSchmidtFrame (V := V) Y₀ j x) '' Set.Iic (Fin.castLE hn i)) :=
      subset_span (Set.mem_image_of_mem _ (Set.mem_Iic.2 le_rfl))
    refine span_le.2 ?_
      ((span_gramSchmidtFrame_Iic (V := V) Y₀ x (Fin.castLE hn i)).le hmem)
    rintro _ ⟨j, hj, rfl⟩
    have hjlt : (j : ℕ) < finrank ℝ E := lt_of_le_of_lt hj i.isLt
    have hjeq : j = Fin.castLE hn ⟨(j : ℕ), hjlt⟩ := by ext; simp
    rw [hjeq]
    exact hY₀tan x hx ⟨(j : ℕ), hjlt⟩
  refine ⟨v, gramSchmidtFrame (V := V) Y₀, hn, hvopen, hpv,
    isLocalFrameOn_gramSchmidtFrame (IB := I) (F := E') (V := V) hY₀, ?_, hGStan, ?_⟩
  · -- orthonormality, read back through `g̃`
    intro x hx i j
    have hon := gramSchmidtFrame_orthonormal (IB := I) (F := E') (V := V) hY₀.isLocalIndepOn hx
    show ⟪gramSchmidtFrame (V := V) Y₀ i x, gramSchmidtFrame (V := V) Y₀ j x⟫_ℝ = _
    rcases eq_or_ne i j with rfl | hij
    · rw [if_pos rfl, real_inner_self_eq_norm_sq, hon.1 i, one_pow]
    · rw [if_neg hij]; exact hon.2 hij
  · -- `n` independent tangent vectors inside the `n`-dimensional `tangentRange` span it
    intro x hx
  -- … (truncated)

deps.title: Smoothness of the pushforward of a vector field
deps.label: lem:pushforward-smooth

deps.title: The Ambient Tangent Bundle Along a Submanifold
deps.label: lem:ambient-tangent-bundle-riemannian

blocks.tex:
Fix $p \in M$ and let $(X_1, \ldots, X_n)$ be any smooth local frame for $TM$ on an open
$u \ni p$ — for instance the frame induced on the base set of a trivialization of $TM$ at $p$ by
a basis of the model space. Put
\[
  Z_i|_x := dF_x(X_i|_x), \qquad i = 1, \ldots, n, \quad x \in u .
\]
By Lemma \ref{lem:pushforward-smooth} each $Z_i$ is a smooth section of $F^* T\tilde M$ over $u$.
At each $x \in u$ the family $(Z_i|_x)_i$ is linearly independent: $(X_i|_x)_i$ is, and $dF_x$ is
injective because $F$ is an immersion. So $(Z_1, \ldots, Z_n)$ is a local $n$-frame for
$F^* T\tilde M$ over $u$.

By Lemma \ref{lem:partial-frame-extension} applied to this $n$-frame — the rank of
$F^* T\tilde M$ is $m$ — we get $n \le m$, an open $v$ with $p \in v \subseteq u$, and a smooth
local frame $(Y_1, \ldots, Y_m)$ for $F^* T\tilde M$ over $v$ with $Y_i = Z_i$ on $v$ for
$i \le n$.

By Lemma \ref{lem:ambient-tangent-bundle-riemannian} the ambient metric $\tilde g$ makes
$F^* T\tilde M$ a Riemannian bundle, so Proposition \ref{prop:existence-orthonormal-frames}
applies to it: the fibrewise Gram--Schmidt orthonormalization $(E_1, \ldots, E_m)$ of
$(Y_1, \ldots, Y_m)$ is again a smooth local frame over $v$, is orthonormal with respect to
$\tilde g$ at every point of $v$, and satisfies the flag condition
$\operatorname{span}(E_1|_x, \ldots, E_k|_x) = \operatorname{span}(Y_1|_x, \ldots, Y_k|_x)$ for
every $k$ and every $x \in v$.

It remains to identify the span of the first $n$ members. Fix $x \in v$ and $i \le n$. The flag
condition at $k = i$ gives
$E_i|_x \in \operatorname{span}(Y_1|_x, \ldots, Y_i|_x) = \operatorname{span}(Z_1|_x, \ldots, Z_i|_x)$,
and each $Z_j|_x = dF_x(X_j|_x)$ lies in $\operatorname{im} dF_x$, which is a subspace; hence
$E_i|_x \in \operatorname{im} dF_x$. Therefore
$\operatorname{span}(E_1|_x, \ldots, E_n|_x) \subseteq \operatorname{im} dF_x$. Both sides have
dimension $n$: the left because $(E_1|_x, \ldots, E_n|_x)$ is orthonormal, hence linearly
independent, and the right because $dF_x$ is injective on the $n$-dimensional $T_x M$. A subspace
of a space of the same finite dimension is the whole space, so the inclusion is an equality.

blocks.label: def:normal-space
blocks.title: The Normal Space
blocks.body:
Let $(\tilde M, \tilde g)$ be a Riemannian $m$-manifold and let $F : M \to \tilde M$ present an
$n$-manifold $M$ as a smooth immersion. For $x \in M$, the \emph{normal space to $M$ at $x$} is
the subspace of the ambient tangent space $T_{F(x)}\tilde M$ given by
\[
  N_x M = \bigl\{\, v \in T_{F(x)}\tilde M \;:\; \tilde g_{F(x)}(v, w) = 0
    \ \text{ for every } w \in T_x M \,\bigr\},
\]
where, as elsewhere in this chapter, $T_x M$ denotes the image $dF_x(T_x M) \subseteq
T_{F(x)}\tilde M$. It is a linear subspace, being the intersection of the kernels of the linear
functionals $\tilde g_{F(x)}(\cdot, w)$ for $w \in T_x M$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.normalSpace
lean.code:
noncomputable def normalSpace :
    Submodule ℝ (((f : M → M') *ᵖ (TangentSpace I' : M' → Type _)) x) where
  carrier := {v | ∀ w ∈ tangentRange f x, g'.inner (f x) v w = 0}
  add_mem' := by
    intro a b ha hb w hw
    have key : (g'.inner (f x)) (a + b) = (g'.inner (f x)) a + (g'.inner (f x)) b :=
      (g'.inner (f x)).map_add a b
    rw [key, ContinuousLinearMap.add_apply, ha w hw, hb w hw, add_zero]
  zero_mem' := by
    intro w _
    have key : (g'.inner (f x))
        (0 : ((f : M → M') *ᵖ (TangentSpace I' : M' → Type _)) x) = 0 := map_zero _
    rw [key, ContinuousLinearMap.zero_apply]
  smul_mem' := by
    intro c a ha w hw
    have key : (g'.inner (f x)) (c • a) = c • (g'.inner (f x)) a :=
      (g'.inner (f x)).map_smul c a
    rw [key, ContinuousLinearMap.smul_apply, ha w hw, smul_zero]

@[simp]

deps.title: The Ambient Tangent Bundle Along a Submanifold
deps.label: lem:ambient-tangent-bundle-riemannian

blocks.label: lem:normal-space-orthonormal-frame
blocks.title: Local Orthonormal Frames for the Normal Space
blocks.body:
Let $(\tilde M, \tilde g)$ be a Riemannian $m$-manifold and let $F : M \to \tilde M$ present an
$n$-manifold $M$ as a smooth immersion. Then $n \le m$, and every $p \in M$ has a neighbourhood
$v$ carrying smooth sections $N_1, \ldots, N_{m-n}$ of the ambient tangent bundle $F^*T\tilde M$
over $v$ whose values at each $x \in v$ form an orthonormal basis of $N_x M$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.exists_orthonormalFrame_normalSpace
lean.code:
theorem exists_orthonormalFrame_normalSpace (g' : RiemannianMetric I' M') (f : C^∞⟮I, M; I', M'⟯)
    (himm : ∀ x : M, Function.Injective (mfderiv I I' f x)) (p : M) :
    ∃ (v : Set M) (hn : finrank ℝ E ≤ finrank ℝ E')
      (N : Fin (finrank ℝ E' - finrank ℝ E) → (x : M) →
        ((f : M → M') *ᵖ (TangentSpace I' : M' → Type _)) x),
      IsOpen v ∧ p ∈ v ∧
      (∀ j, ContMDiffOn I (I.prod 𝓘(ℝ, E')) ∞ (fun x => TotalSpace.mk' E' x (N j x)) v) ∧
      (∀ x ∈ v, ∀ i j, g'.inner (f x) (N i x) (N j x) = if i = j then 1 else 0) ∧
      (∀ x ∈ v, ∀ j, N j x ∈ normalSpace g' f x) ∧
      (∀ x ∈ v, span ℝ (Set.range fun j => N j x) = normalSpace g' f x) := by
  classical
  obtain ⟨v, Y, hn, hvopen, hpv, hY, hYon, hYtan, hYspan⟩ :=
    exists_adapted_orthonormalFrame g' f himm p
  -- the last `m - n` members of the adapted frame
  set N : Fin (finrank ℝ E' - finrank ℝ E) → (x : M) →
      ((f : M → M') *ᵖ (TangentSpace I' : M' → Type _)) x :=
    fun j x => Y (finUpper hn j) x with hN
  -- each of them is normal: it is orthogonal to every member below the cut, hence to their span
  have hnormal : ∀ x ∈ v, ∀ j, N j x ∈ normalSpace g' f x := by
    intro x hx j w hw
    rw [← hYspan x hx] at hw
    refine innerAt_span_eq_zero (N j x) _ ?_ w hw
    rintro _ ⟨i, rfl⟩
    rw [hN, hYon x hx (finUpper hn j) (Fin.castLE hn i),
      if_neg (castLE_ne_finUpper hn i j).symm]
  refine ⟨v, hn, N, hvopen, hpv, fun j => hY.contMDiffOn _, ?_, hnormal, ?_⟩
  · -- orthonormality is inherited from the adapted frame
    intro x hx i j
    rw [hN, hYon x hx (finUpper hn i) (finUpper hn j)]
    by_cases hij : i = j
    · rw [if_pos hij, if_pos (by rw [hij])]
    · rw [if_neg hij, if_neg (fun h => hij (finUpper_injective hn h))]
  · -- they span the normal space
    intro x hx
    refine le_antisymm ?_ ?_
    · rw [span_le]
      rintro _ ⟨j, rfl⟩
      exact hnormal x hx j
    · -- expand a normal vector in the orthonormal basis `(Y a x)`
      intro w hw
      have hbasis : ∀ a, (hY.toBasisAt hx) a = Y a x := fun a => hY.toBasisAt_coe hx a
      have hexp : ∑ a, (hY.toBasisAt hx).repr w a • Y a x = w := by
        conv_rhs => rw [← (hY.toBasisAt hx).sum_repr w]
        exact Finset.sum_congr rfl fun a _ => by rw [hbasis a]
      -- the coefficient of `Y a x` is `⟪Y a x, w⟫`, by orthonormality
      have hrepr : ∀ a, g'.inner (f x) (Y a x) w = (hY.toBasisAt hx).repr w a := by
        intro a
        have h1 : g'.inner (f x) (Y a x) w
            = ∑ b, (hY.toBasisAt hx).repr w b * g'.inner (f x) (Y a x) (Y b x) := by
          conv_lhs => rw [← hexp]
          exact innerAt_sum_right _ _ _
        rw [h1, Finset.sum_eq_single a]
        · rw [hYon x hx a a, if_pos rfl, mul_one]
        · intro b _ hb
          rw [hYon x hx a b, if_neg (Ne.symm hb), mul_zero]
        · intro h
          exact absurd (Finset.mem_univ a) h
      -- the coefficients below the cut vanish, because `w` is normal
      have hlow : ∀ i : Fin (finrank ℝ E), (hY.toBasisAt hx).repr w (Fin.castLE hn i) = 0 := by
        intro i
  -- … (truncated)

deps.title: Existence of Adapted Orthonormal Frames, immersed form
deps.label: prop:adapted-orthonormal-frames-immersed

blocks.tex:
By Proposition \ref{prop:adapted-orthonormal-frames-immersed} there are $n \le m$, a
neighbourhood $v$ of $p$, and a smooth local frame $(E_1, \ldots, E_m)$ for $F^*T\tilde M$ over
$v$ that is orthonormal with respect to $\tilde g$ at every $x \in v$ and whose first $n$ members
span $T_x M$ at every $x \in v$. Put $N_j = E_{n+j}$ for $1 \le j \le m-n$; these are smooth
sections over $v$, and they are orthonormal at each $x \in v$ because $(E_a)_a$ is.

Fix $x \in v$. Each $N_j|_x$ is normal: for $i \le n$ we have $n + j \ne i$, so
$\tilde g(N_j|_x, E_i|_x) = 0$ by orthonormality; since $E_1|_x, \ldots, E_n|_x$ span $T_x M$ and
$\tilde g$ is linear in its second argument, $\tilde g(N_j|_x, w) = 0$ for every $w \in T_x M$.
Hence $\operatorname{span}(N_1|_x, \ldots, N_{m-n}|_x) \subseteq N_x M$, as $N_x M$ is a subspace.

Conversely let $w \in N_x M$. Since $(E_a|_x)_{a=1}^m$ is a basis of $T_{F(x)}\tilde M$, write
$w = \sum_{a=1}^m c_a E_a|_x$. Pairing with $E_b|_x$ and using orthonormality gives
\[
  \tilde g(E_b|_x, w) = \sum_{a=1}^m c_a \, \tilde g(E_b|_x, E_a|_x) = c_b .
\]
For $b \le n$ we have $E_b|_x \in T_x M$, and $w$ is normal, so $c_b = \tilde g(E_b|_x, w) = 0$.
Therefore $w = \sum_{j=1}^{m-n} c_{n+j} N_j|_x$ lies in
$\operatorname{span}(N_1|_x, \ldots, N_{m-n}|_x)$.

The two inclusions give $\operatorname{span}(N_1|_x, \ldots, N_{m-n}|_x) = N_x M$; together with
orthonormality, $(N_j|_x)_j$ is an orthonormal basis of $N_x M$.

blocks.label: prop:normal-bundle
blocks.title: The Normal Bundle
blocks.body: If $\tilde{M}$ is a Riemannian $m$-manifold and $M \subseteq \tilde{M}$ is an immersed or embedded $n$-dimensional submanifold with or without boundary, then $NM$ is a smooth rank-$(m-n)$ vector subbundle of the ambient tangent bundle $T\tilde{M}|_M$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.contMDiffVectorBundle_normalSpace
lean.code:
theorem contMDiffVectorBundle_normalSpace (g' : RiemannianMetric I' M') (f : C^∞⟮I, M; I', M'⟯)
    (himm : ∀ x : M, Function.Injective (mfderiv I I' f x)) :
    letI : Bundle.RiemannianBundle ((f : M → M') *ᵖ (TangentSpace I' : M' → Type _)) :=
      ⟨(g'.pullback f).toRiemannianMetric⟩
    letI h := hasLocalSubframes_normalSpace g' f himm
    letI := subTotalSpaceTopology h
    letI := subFiberBundle h
    letI := subVectorBundle h
    ContMDiffVectorBundle ∞ (EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin (finrank ℝ E' - finrank ℝ E)))
      (fun x => ↥(normalSpace g' f x)) I := by
  letI : Bundle.RiemannianBundle ((f : M → M') *ᵖ (TangentSpace I' : M' → Type _)) :=
    ⟨(g'.pullback f).toRiemannianMetric⟩
  exact subContMDiffVectorBundle (F := E') (hasLocalSubframes_normalSpace g' f himm)

deps.title: Local Orthonormal Frames for the Normal Space
deps.label: lem:normal-space-orthonormal-frame

deps.title: The Ambient Tangent Bundle Along a Submanifold
deps.label: lem:ambient-tangent-bundle-riemannian

blocks.tex:
By Lemma \ref{lem:ambient-tangent-bundle-riemannian} the ambient tangent bundle $T\tilde M|_M = F^*T\tilde M$ is a smooth vector bundle over $M$ carrying the fibre metric $\tilde g$, so Lemma \ref{lem:local-frame-criterion-for-subbundles} applies to it. Take the family of subspaces $D_p = N_p M \subseteq T_p\tilde M$, each of dimension $m - n$.

Its hypothesis is Lemma \ref{lem:normal-space-orthonormal-frame}: every $p \in M$ has a neighbourhood $v$ carrying smooth sections $N_1, \ldots, N_{m-n}$ of $T\tilde M|_M$ whose values at each $x \in v$ form an orthonormal basis --- in particular a basis --- of $N_x M$. Lemma \ref{lem:local-frame-criterion-for-subbundles} therefore makes $NM = \bigcup_{p \in M} N_p M$ a smooth rank-$(m-n)$ subbundle of $T\tilde M|_M$.

blocks.label: rem:normal-bundle-projections
blocks.title: rem:normal-bundle-projections
blocks.body:
Lee's statement of the preceding proposition also asserts the existence of smooth bundle homomorphisms
\[
\pi^\top : T\tilde{M}|_M \to TM, \quad \pi^\perp : T\tilde{M}|_M \to NM,
\]
the \emph{tangential and normal projections}, restricting at each $p \in M$ to the orthogonal projections of $T_p\tilde M$ onto $T_p M$ and $N_p M$. These are the fibrewise orthogonal projections of the splitting $T_p\tilde M = T_p M \oplus N_p M$; their smoothness follows from the same adapted orthonormal frame, in which $\pi^\top$ and $\pi^\perp$ are the truncations of the coordinate expansion to the first $n$ and the last $m-n$ members. They are not part of the node above.

deps.title: Existence of Adapted Orthonormal Frames, immersed form
deps.label: prop:adapted-orthonormal-frames-immersed

deps.title: Local Orthonormal Frames for the Normal Space
deps.label: lem:normal-space-orthonormal-frame

blocks.tex:
Given any point $p \in M$, Theorem A.16 shows that there is a neighborhood $U$ of $p$ in $\widetilde{M}$ that is embedded in $\widetilde{M}$, and then Proposition 2.14 shows that there is a smooth orthonormal frame $(E_1, \ldots, E_m)$ that is adapted to $U$ on some neighborhood $\widetilde{U}$ of $p$ in $\widetilde{M}$. This means that the restrictions of $(E_1, \ldots, E_n)$ to $U \cap \widetilde{U}$ form a local orthonormal frame for $M$. Given such an adapted frame, the restrictions of the last $m - n$ vector fields $(E_{n+1}, \ldots, E_m)$ to $M$ form a smooth local frame for $NM$, so it follows from Lemma A.34 that $NM$ is a smooth subbundle.

The bundle homomorphisms $\pi^\top$ and $\pi^\perp$ are defined pointwise as orthogonal projections onto the tangent and normal spaces, respectively, which shows that they are uniquely defined. In terms of an adapted orthonormal frame, they can be written
\[
\pi^\top(X^1 E_1 + \cdots + X^m E_m) = X^1 E_1 + \cdots + X^n E_n,
\]
\[
\pi^\perp(X^1 E_1 + \cdots + X^m E_m) = X^{n+1} E_{n+1} + \cdots + X^m E_m,
\]
which shows that they are smooth.

In case $\widetilde{M}$ is a manifold with boundary, the preceding constructions do not always work, because there is not a fully general construction of slice coordinates in that case. However, there is a satisfactory result in case the submanifold is the boundary itself, using boundary coordinates in place of slice coordinates.

Suppose $(M, g)$ is a Riemannian manifold with boundary. We will always consider $\partial M$ to be a Riemannian submanifold with the induced metric.

blocks.label: prop:outward-pointing-normal
blocks.title: Existence of Outward-Pointing Normal
blocks.body: If $(M, g)$ is a smooth Riemannian manifold with boundary, the normal bundle to $\partial M$ is a smooth rank-1 vector bundle over $\partial M$, and there is a unique smooth outward-pointing unit normal vector field along all of $\partial M$.

blocks.tex:
\begin{exercise}

\dcref{ch2:2.18}
\uses{prop:induced-orientation-boundary}
Prove this proposition. [Hint: Use the paragraph preceding Prop. B.17 as a starting point.]
\end{exercise}

Computations on a submanifold $M \subset \widetilde{M}$ are usually carried out most conveniently in terms of a \emph{smooth local parametrization}: this is a smooth map $X : U \to \widetilde{M}$, where $U$ is an open subset of $\mathbb{R}^n$ (or $\mathbb{R}^n_+$ in case $M$ has a boundary), such that $X(U)$ is an open subset of $M$, and such that $X$, regarded as a map from $U$ into $M$, is a diffeomorphism onto its image. Note that we can think of $X$ either as a map into $M$ or as a map into $\widetilde{M}$; both maps are typically denoted by the same symbol $X$. If we put $V = X(U) \subseteq M$ and $\varphi = X^{-1} : V \to U$, then $(V, \varphi)$ is a smooth coordinate chart on $M$.

Suppose $(M, g)$ is a Riemannian submanifold of $(\widetilde{M}, \widetilde{g})$ and $X : U \to \widetilde{M}$ is a smooth local parametrization of $M$. The coordinate representation of $g$ in these coordinates is given by the following 2-tensor field on $U$:
\[
(\varphi^{-1})^* g = X^* g = X^* \iota^* \widetilde{g} = (\iota \circ X)^* \widetilde{g}.
\]
Since $\iota \circ X$ is just the map $X$ itself, regarded as a map into $\tilde{M}$, this is really just $X^*\tilde{g}$. The simplicity of the formula for the pullback of a tensor field makes this expression exceedingly easy to compute, once a coordinate expression for $\tilde{g}$ is known. For example, if $M$ is an immersed $n$-dimensional Riemannian submanifold of $\mathbb{R}^m$ and $X : U \to \mathbb{R}^m$ is a smooth local parametrization of $M$, the induced metric on $U$ is just
\[
g = X^*\tilde{g} = \sum_{i=1}^m (dX^i)^2 = \sum_{i=1}^m \left(\sum_{j=1}^n \frac{\partial X^i}{\partial u^j} du^j\right)^2 = \sum_{i=1}^m \sum_{j,k=1}^n \frac{\partial X^i}{\partial u^j}\frac{\partial X^i}{\partial u^k} du^j du^k.
\]

blocks.label: ex:metrics-in-graph-coordinates
blocks.title: Metrics in Graph Coordinates
blocks.body:
If $U \subseteq \mathbb{R}^n$ is an open set and $f : U \to \mathbb{R}$ is a smooth function, then the \textbf{graph of $f$} is the subset $\Gamma(f) = \{(x, f(x)) : x \in U\} \subseteq \mathbb{R}^{n+1}$, which is an embedded submanifold of dimension $n$. It has a global parametrization $X : U \to \mathbb{R}^{n+1}$ called a \textbf{graph parametrization}, given by $X(u) = (u, f(u))$; the corresponding coordinates $(u^1,\ldots,u^n)$ on $M$ are called \textbf{graph coordinates}. In graph coordinates, the induced metric of $\Gamma(f)$ is
\[
X^*\tilde{g} = X^*\left((dx^1)^2 + \cdots + (dx^{n+1})^2\right) = (du^1)^2 + \cdots + (du^n)^2 + df^2.
\]

Applying this to the upper hemisphere of $S^2$ with the parametrization $X : \mathbb{R}^2 \to \mathbb{R}^3$ given by
\[
X(u,v) = \left(u, v, \sqrt{1-u^2-v^2}\right),
\]
we see that the round metric on $S^2$ can be written locally as
\[
\hat{g} = X^*\bar{g} = du^2 + dv^2 + \left(\frac{u\,du + v\,dv}{\sqrt{1-u^2-v^2}}\right)^2
\]
\[
= \frac{(1-v^2) du^2 + (1-u^2) dv^2 + 2uv\,du\,dv}{1-u^2-v^2}.
\]

blocks.label: ex:surfaces-of-revolution
blocks.title: Surfaces of Revolution

blocks.label: ex:n-torus-riemannian-submanifold
blocks.title: The n-Torus as a Riemannian Submanifold
blocks.body: The smooth covering map $X: \mathbb{R}^n \to \mathbb{T}^n$ described in Example A.52 restricts to a smooth local parametrization on any sufficiently small open subset of $\mathbb{R}^n$, and the induced metric is equal to the Euclidean metric in $(u^i)$ coordinates, and therefore the induced metric on $\mathbb{T}^n$ is flat.

blocks.label: lem:smooth-tensor-field-add
blocks.title: Sums of smooth sections
blocks.body: Let $V$ be a smooth vector bundle over a smooth manifold $B$, and let $s, t$ be smooth sections of $V$. Then $s + t$ is a smooth section of $V$.

blocks.label: lem:smooth-tensor-field-smul
blocks.title: Smooth rescalings of smooth sections
blocks.body: Let $V$ be a smooth vector bundle over a smooth manifold $B$, let $s$ be a smooth section of $V$, and let $c : B \to \mathbb{R}$ be smooth. Then $b \mapsto c(b)\,s_b$ is a smooth section of $V$.

blocks.label: lem:product-form-positive-definite
blocks.title: Positive definiteness of the product form
blocks.body: Let $(M_1,g_1)$ and $(M_2,g_2)$ be Riemannian manifolds, and let $g_1 \oplus g_2$ be the symmetric $2$-tensor field on $M_1 \times M_2$ defined by (2.12). Then $g_1 \oplus g_2$ is positive definite at every point.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.prodForm_pos
lean.code:
theorem prodForm_pos (g : RiemannianMetric I M) (h : RiemannianMetric J N) (x : M × N)
    (v : TangentSpace (I.prod J) x) (hv : v ≠ 0) : 0 < prodForm g h x v v := by
  rw [prodForm_apply]
  rcases tangentVector_prod_ne_zero hv with h1 | h2
  · exact add_pos_of_pos_of_nonneg (g.pos x.1 v.1 h1) (h.innerAt_self_nonneg x.2 v.2)
  · exact add_pos_of_nonneg_of_pos (g.innerAt_self_nonneg x.1 v.1) (h.pos x.2 v.2 h2)

blocks.tex:
Fix $(p_1,p_2) \in M_1 \times M_2$ and let $v = (v_1,v_2) \in T_{p_1}M_1 \oplus T_{p_2}M_2$ be nonzero. Then $v_1 \neq 0$ or $v_2 \neq 0$. By (2.12),
\[
(g_1 \oplus g_2)_{(p_1,p_2)}(v,v) = g_1|_{p_1}(v_1,v_1) + g_2|_{p_2}(v_2,v_2),
\]
and both summands are nonnegative because $g_1$ and $g_2$ are positive definite. If $v_1 \neq 0$ the first summand is strictly positive and the second is nonnegative; if $v_2 \neq 0$ the second is strictly positive and the first is nonnegative. In either case the sum is strictly positive.

blocks.label: def:product-metric
blocks.title: The Product Metric
blocks.body: Let $(M_1,g_1)$ and $(M_2,g_2)$ be Riemannian manifolds. The \emph{product metric} $g_1 \oplus g_2$ on $M_1 \times M_2$ is the Riemannian metric defined by (2.12). It is symmetric because $g_1$ and $g_2$ are, it is positive definite by Lemma \ref{lem:product-form-positive-definite}, and it is a smooth $2$-tensor field by (2.12') together with Lemma \ref{lem:smooth-tensor-field-add}, each summand being a pullback of a smooth $2$-tensor field along a smooth map.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.prodMetric
lean.code:
noncomputable def prodMetric (g : RiemannianMetric I M) (h : RiemannianMetric J N) :
    RiemannianMetric (I.prod J) (M × N) where
  inner x := prodForm g h x
  symm x v w := prodForm_symm g h x v w
  pos x v hv := prodForm_pos g h x v hv
  isVonNBounded x :=
    isVonNBounded_of_posDef (F := E × E') (prodForm g h x) (fun v hv => prodForm_pos g h x v hv)
  contMDiff := prodForm_contMDiff g h

deps.title: Positive definiteness of the product form
deps.label: lem:product-form-positive-definite

blocks.label: lem:warped-product-form-positive-definite
blocks.title: Positive definiteness of the warped product form
blocks.body: Let $(M_1,g_1)$ and $(M_2,g_2)$ be Riemannian manifolds and let $f : M_1 \to \mathbb{R}$ be nowhere vanishing. Then $g_1 \oplus f^2 g_2$ is positive definite at every point.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.warpedProductForm_pos
lean.code:
theorem warpedProductForm_pos (g : RiemannianMetric I M) (h : RiemannianMetric J N) {f : M → ℝ}
    (hf : ∀ p, f p ≠ 0) (x : M × N) (v : TangentSpace (I.prod J) x) (hv : v ≠ 0) :
    0 < warpedProductForm g h f x v v := by
  have hfx : 0 < (f x.1) ^ 2 := sq_pos_of_ne_zero (hf x.1)
  rw [warpedProductForm_apply]
  rcases tangentVector_prod_ne_zero hv with h1 | h2
  · exact add_pos_of_pos_of_nonneg (g.pos x.1 v.1 h1)
      (mul_nonneg hfx.le (h.innerAt_self_nonneg x.2 v.2))
  · exact add_pos_of_nonneg_of_pos (g.innerAt_self_nonneg x.1 v.1)
      (mul_pos hfx (h.pos x.2 v.2 h2))

deps.title: Positive definiteness of the product form
deps.label: lem:product-form-positive-definite

blocks.tex:
Fix $(p_1,p_2)$ and a nonzero $v = (v_1,v_2) \in T_{p_1}M_1 \oplus T_{p_2}M_2$, so that $v_1 
eq 0$ or $v_2 
eq 0$. Since $f(p_1) 
eq 0$ we have $f(p_1)^2 > 0$, and
\[
(g_1 \oplus f^2 g_2)_{(p_1,p_2)}(v,v) = g_1|_{p_1}(v_1,v_1) + f(p_1)^2\, g_2|_{p_2}(v_2,v_2).
\]
Both summands are nonnegative, by positive definiteness of $g_1$ and $g_2$ and $f(p_1)^2 > 0$. If $v_1 
eq 0$ the first is strictly positive; if $v_2 
eq 0$ then $g_2|_{p_2}(v_2,v_2) > 0$ and hence the second is strictly positive, being a product of two strictly positive numbers. In either case the sum is strictly positive.

blocks.label: def:warped-product
blocks.title: Warped Products
blocks.body:
Let $(M_1,g_1)$ and $(M_2,g_2)$ be Riemannian manifolds and let $f : M_1 \to \mathbb{R}^+$ be a strictly positive smooth function. The \emph{warped product} $M_1 \times_f M_2$ is the product manifold $M_1 \times M_2$ endowed with the Riemannian metric $g_1 \oplus f^2 g_2$.

That $g_1 \oplus f^2 g_2$ is a Riemannian metric follows as for the product metric: it is symmetric because $g_1$ and $g_2$ are, positive definite by Lemma \ref{lem:warped-product-form-positive-definite}, and smooth because
\[
g_1 \oplus f^2 g_2 = \pi_1^* g_1 + (f \circ \pi_1)^2\, \pi_2^* g_2,
\]
whose first summand is smooth as in Definition \ref{def:product-metric} and whose second is the smooth function $(f \circ \pi_1)^2$ times a smooth $2$-tensor field, hence smooth by Lemma \ref{lem:smooth-tensor-field-smul}; the sum is then smooth by Lemma \ref{lem:smooth-tensor-field-add}.

Note that $f$ enters the metric only through $f^2$, so the definition makes sense for any nowhere-vanishing smooth $f$, and only $f(p_1) \neq 0$ is used.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.warpedProductMetric
lean.code:
noncomputable def warpedProductMetric (g : RiemannianMetric I M) (h : RiemannianMetric J N)
    {f : M → ℝ} (hf : ContMDiff I 𝓘(ℝ, ℝ) ∞ f) (hf0 : ∀ p, f p ≠ 0) :
    RiemannianMetric (I.prod J) (M × N) where
  inner x := warpedProductForm g h f x
  symm x v w := warpedProductForm_symm g h f x v w
  pos x v hv := warpedProductForm_pos g h hf0 x v hv
  isVonNBounded x :=
    isVonNBounded_of_posDef (F := E × E') (warpedProductForm g h f x)
      (fun v hv => warpedProductForm_pos g h hf0 x v hv)
  contMDiff := warpedProductForm_contMDiff g h hf

deps.title: Positive definiteness of the warped product form
deps.label: lem:warped-product-form-positive-definite

blocks.label: prop:warped-product-constant-one
blocks.title: Constant warping gives the product metric
blocks.body: With $f \equiv 1$, the warped product $M_1 \times_f M_2$ is the product manifold $M_1 \times M_2$ with the product metric $g_1 \oplus g_2$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.warpedProductMetric_one
lean.code:
theorem warpedProductMetric_one (g : RiemannianMetric I M) (h : RiemannianMetric J N)
    (hf : ContMDiff I 𝓘(ℝ, ℝ) ∞ (fun _ : M => (1 : ℝ)))
    (hf0 : ∀ p : M, (fun _ : M => (1 : ℝ)) p ≠ 0) :
    warpedProductMetric g h hf hf0 = prodMetric g h := by
  have key : (fun x : M × N => warpedProductForm g h (fun _ : M => (1 : ℝ)) x)
      = fun x : M × N => prodForm g h x := by
    funext x
    refine ContinuousLinearMap.ext fun v => ContinuousLinearMap.ext fun w => ?_
    simp
  -- every field but `inner` is a `Prop`, so proof irrelevance closes the rest
  simp only [warpedProductMetric, prodMetric]
  congr 1

blocks.tex:
Both metrics are defined on the same underlying manifold $M_1 \times M_2$, so it suffices to compare them at each point. For every $(p_1,p_2)$ and all $(v_1,v_2), (w_1,w_2) \in T_{p_1}M_1 \oplus T_{p_2}M_2$,
\[
(g_1 \oplus f^2 g_2)_{(p_1,p_2)}((v_1,v_2),(w_1,w_2)) = g_1|_{p_1}(v_1,w_1) + 1^2\, g_2|_{p_2}(v_2,w_2),
\]
which is exactly the right-hand side of (2.12). A Riemannian metric is determined by the bilinear form it assigns to each point, so the two metrics are equal.

blocks.label: ex:warped-products
blocks.title: Warped Products
blocks.body:
\noindent (a) With $f \equiv 1$, the warped product $M_1 \times_f M_2$ is just the space $M_1 \times M_2$ with the product metric; this is Proposition \ref{prop:warped-product-constant-one}.

\noindent (b) Every surface of revolution can be expressed as a warped product, as follows. Let $H$ be the half-plane $\{(r,z) : r > 0\}$, let $C \subseteq H$ be an embedded smooth 1-dimensional submanifold, and let $S_C \subseteq \mathbb{R}^3$ denote the corresponding surface of revolution as in Example 2.20. Endow $C$ with the Riemannian metric induced from the Euclidean metric on $H$, and let $S^1$ be endowed with its standard metric. Let $f : C \to \mathbb{R}$ be the distance to the $z$-axis: $f(r,z) = r$. Then Problem 2-3 shows that $S_C$ is isometric to the warped product $C \times_f S^1$.

(c) If we let $\rho$ denote the standard coordinate function on $\mathbb{R}^+ \subset \mathbb{R}$, then the map $\Phi(\rho,\omega) = \rho\omega$ gives an isometry from the warped product $\mathbb{R}^+ \times_\rho S^{n-1}$ to $\mathbb{R}^n \setminus \{0\}$ with its Euclidean metric (see Problem 2-4). \hfill //

deps.title: Constant warping gives the product metric
deps.label: prop:warped-product-constant-one

blocks.tex:
Unlike submanifolds and products of Riemannian manifolds, which automatically inherit Riemannian metrics of their own, quotients of Riemannian manifolds inherit Riemannian metrics only under very special circumstances. In this section, we see what those circumstances are.

Suppose $\tilde{M}$ and $M$ are smooth manifolds, $\pi : \tilde{M} \to M$ is a smooth submersion, and $\tilde{g}$ is a Riemannian metric on $\tilde{M}$. By the submersion level set theorem (Corollary A.25), each fiber $\tilde{M}_y = \pi^{-1}(y)$ is a properly embedded smooth submanifold of $\tilde{M}$. At each point $x \in \tilde{M}$, we define two subspaces of the tangent space $T_x \tilde{M}$ as follows: the \textbf{vertical tangent space at $x$} is
\[
V_x = \ker d\pi_x = T_x(\tilde{M}_{\pi(x)})
\]
(that is, the tangent space to the fiber containing $x$), and the \textbf{horizontal tangent space at $x$} is its orthogonal complement:
\[
H_x = (V_x)^\perp.
\]
Then the tangent space $T_x \tilde{M}$ decomposes as an orthogonal direct sum $T_x \tilde{M} = H_x \oplus V_x$. Note that the vertical space is well defined for every submersion, because it does not refer to the metric; but the horizontal space depends on the metric.

A vector field on $\tilde{M}$ is said to be a \textbf{horizontal vector field} if its value at each point lies in the horizontal space at that point; a \textbf{vertical vector field} is defined similarly. Given a vector field $X$ on $M$, a vector field $\tilde{X}$ on $\tilde{M}$ is called a \textbf{horizontal lift of $X$} if $\tilde{X}$ is horizontal and $\pi$-related to $X$. (The latter property means that $d\pi_x(\tilde{X}_x) = X_{\pi(x)}$ for each $x \in \tilde{M}$.)

The next proposition is the principal tool for doing computations on Riemannian submersions.

Proposition \ref{prop:horizontal-vector-field-properties} below is part (a) of
Lee's Proposition 2.25; part (b) is Proposition
\ref{prop:horizontal-lift-of-vector-field} and part (c) is Proposition
\ref{prop:horizontal-lift-realizes-horizontal-vector}, both stated once the
horizontal lift has been constructed.

Lee derives all three parts from the rank theorem: it puts $\pi$ into the normal
form $(x^1,\ldots,x^m) \mapsto (x^1,\ldots,x^n)$, so that $V_q$ is spanned by
$\partial_{n+1}|_q,\ldots,\partial_m|_q$ on a chart, and Gram--Schmidt applied to
the reordered coordinate frame $(\partial_{n+1},\ldots,\partial_m,\partial_1,\ldots,\partial_n)$
produces a smooth orthonormal frame $(E_1,\ldots,E_m)$ whose first $m-n$ entries
span $V_q$ and whose last $n$ span $H_q$; the components of $W$ in that frame are
smooth, which gives the smoothness of $W^H$ and $W^V$. The treatment below
replaces that frame by an explicit formula for the horizontal lift. This costs
nothing in generality, and it buys the smoothness assertions directly: the lift is
a rational expression in $\tilde{g}$ and $d\pi$, and inversion of invertible
operators is smooth.

blocks.label: prop:horizontal-vector-field-properties
blocks.title: The Smooth Horizontal--Vertical Splitting of a Vector Field
blocks.body: Let $\tilde{M}$ and $M$ be smooth manifolds, let $\pi : \tilde{M} \to M$ be a smooth submersion, and let $\tilde{g}$ be a Riemannian metric on $\tilde{M}$. Every smooth vector field $W$ on $\tilde{M}$ can be expressed uniquely in the form $W = W^H + W^V$, where $W^H$ is horizontal, $W^V$ is vertical, and both $W^H$ and $W^V$ are smooth.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.existsUnique_horizontal_add_vertical_field
lean.code:
theorem existsUnique_horizontal_add_vertical_field (hπ : IsSubmersion π)
    {W : ∀ x : M, TangentSpace I x}
    (hW : ContMDiff I (I.prod 𝓘(ℝ, E)) ∞ (fun x => Bundle.TotalSpace.mk' E x (W x))) :
    ∃ WH WV : ∀ x : M, TangentSpace I x,
      ContMDiff I (I.prod 𝓘(ℝ, E)) ∞ (fun x => Bundle.TotalSpace.mk' E x (WH x)) ∧
      ContMDiff I (I.prod 𝓘(ℝ, E)) ∞ (fun x => Bundle.TotalSpace.mk' E x (WV x)) ∧
      (∀ x, WH x ∈ horizontalSpace g π x) ∧
      (∀ x, WV x ∈ verticalSpace π x) ∧
      (∀ x, W x = WH x + WV x) ∧
      (∀ ZH ZV : ∀ x : M, TangentSpace I x,
        (∀ x, ZH x ∈ horizontalSpace g π x) → (∀ x, ZV x ∈ verticalSpace π x) →
        (∀ x, W x = ZH x + ZV x) → (∀ x, ZH x = WH x) ∧ (∀ x, ZV x = WV x)) := by
  refine ⟨fun x => horizontalProj g π x (W x), fun x => verticalProj g π x (W x),
    contMDiff_horizontalProjField g π hπ hW, contMDiff_verticalProjField g π hπ hW,
    fun x => horizontalProj_mem g π x _, fun x => verticalProj_mem g π hπ x _,
    fun x => (horizontalProj_add_verticalProj g π x (W x)).symm, ?_⟩
  intro ZH ZV hZH hZV hZ
  -- At each point, apply the pointwise uniqueness to the vector `W x`.
  have key : ∀ x, ZH x = horizontalProj g π x (W x) ∧ ZV x = verticalProj g π x (W x) := by
    intro x
    obtain ⟨p, -, huniq⟩ := existsUnique_horizontal_add_vertical g π hπ x (W x)
    -- Both pairs satisfy the defining property, so both equal the unique witness `p`.
    have h1 := huniq (ZH x, ZV x) ⟨hZH x, hZV x, hZ x⟩
    have h2 := huniq (horizontalProj g π x (W x), verticalProj g π x (W x))
      ⟨horizontalProj_mem g π x _, verticalProj_mem g π hπ x _,
        (horizontalProj_add_verticalProj g π x (W x)).symm⟩
    have h := h1.trans h2.symm
    exact ⟨congrArg Prod.fst h, congrArg Prod.snd h⟩
  exact ⟨fun x => (key x).1, fun x => (key x).2⟩

/-! ## Proposition 2.25(c): realizing a horizontal vector by a lift -/

deps.title: Smoothness of the Horizontal Projection of a Vector Field
deps.label: lem:horizontal-projection-field-smooth

blocks.tex:
At each point $x \in \tilde{M}$ the tangent vector $W_x$ splits uniquely as a horizontal plus a vertical vector, by Lemma \ref{lem:tangent-space-horizontal-vertical-splitting}; explicitly the two summands are $W^H_x = L_x(d\pi_x(W_x))$ and $W^V_x = W_x - W^H_x$, where $L_x$ is the horizontal lift of Definition \ref{def:horizontal-lift-tangent}. This already proves existence and uniqueness of the decomposition into rough vector fields, since uniqueness is a pointwise assertion and involves no smoothness.

It remains to show that the two rough fields so defined are smooth. The field $W^H$ is smooth by Lemma \ref{lem:horizontal-projection-field-smooth}, and $W^V = W - W^H$ is then smooth as a difference of smooth vector fields.

The smoothness of $W^H$ is the only analytic content of the proposition, and it is
isolated in the next two lemmas. The first observes that $x \mapsto d\pi_x(W_x)$ is
a smooth section along $\pi$ — a section of $\pi^* TM$ — and the second lifts such
a section back to $\tilde{M}$. The generality matters: $d\pi_x(W_x)$ depends on $x$
and not merely on $\pi(x)$, since two points of a fibre may carry unrelated values
of $W$, so $W^H$ is \emph{not} the lift of any vector field on $M$ and part (b)
does not apply to it.

blocks.label: lem:pushforward-along-map-smooth
blocks.title: The Pushforward of a Vector Field is a Smooth Section Along the Map
blocks.body: Let $F : \tilde{M} \to M$ be a smooth map and let $W$ be a smooth vector field on $\tilde{M}$. Then $x \mapsto dF_x(W_x)$ is a smooth section of $F^*TM$ along $F$, i.e.\ the map $x \mapsto (F(x), dF_x(W_x))$ is a smooth map $\tilde{M} \to TM$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.contMDiff_mfderivAlong
lean.code:
theorem contMDiff_mfderivAlong {W : ∀ x : M, TangentSpace I x}
    (hW : ContMDiff I (I.prod 𝓘(ℝ, E)) ∞ (fun x => Bundle.TotalSpace.mk' E x (W x))) :
    ContMDiff I (I'.prod 𝓘(ℝ, E')) ∞
      (fun x => Bundle.TotalSpace.mk' E' (π x) (mfderiv I I' π x (W x))) :=
  (π.contMDiff.contMDiff_tangentMap (m := ∞) (by simp)).comp hW

/-! ## Proposition 2.25(a): the smooth horizontal/vertical splitting -/

blocks.tex: The bundled differential $dF : T\tilde{M} \to TM$, $(x, v) \mapsto (F(x), dF_x(v))$, is a smooth map of tangent bundles because $F$ is smooth. The map in question is its composite with the smooth map $x \mapsto (x, W_x)$, which is smooth because $W$ is a smooth vector field.

blocks.label: lem:horizontal-lift-along-smooth
blocks.title: The Horizontal Lift of a Smooth Section Along $\pi$ is Smooth
blocks.body: Let $\pi : \tilde{M} \to M$ be a smooth submersion and $\tilde{g}$ a Riemannian metric on $\tilde{M}$. Let $X$ be a smooth section along $\pi$, that is, a family $X_x \in T_{\pi(x)}M$ depending smoothly on $x \in \tilde{M}$. Then $x \mapsto L_x(X_x)$ is a smooth vector field on $\tilde{M}$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.contMDiffAt_horizontalLiftAlong
lean.code:
theorem contMDiffAt_horizontalLiftAlong (hπ : IsSubmersion π)
    {X : ∀ x : M, TangentSpace I' (π x)} (x₀ : M)
    (hX : ContMDiffAt I (I'.prod 𝓘(ℝ, E')) ∞
      (fun x => Bundle.TotalSpace.mk' E' (π x) (X x)) x₀) :
    ContMDiffAt I (I.prod 𝓘(ℝ, E)) ∞
      (fun x => Bundle.TotalSpace.mk' E x (horizontalLiftAt g π x (X x))) x₀ := by
  rw [Bundle.contMDiffAt_section]
  have hx₀ : x₀ ∈ (trivializationAt E (TangentSpace I) x₀).baseSet := by simp
  have hy₀ : π x₀ ∈ (trivializationAt E' (TangentSpace I') (π x₀)).baseSet := by simp
  -- The section along `π`, read in the trivialization of `T M'` at `π x₀`.
  set u : M → E' := fun x => (trivializationAt E' (TangentSpace I') (π x₀) ⟨π x, X x⟩).2 with hu
  have hus : ContMDiffAt I 𝓘(ℝ, E') ∞ u x₀ := by
    rw [Bundle.contMDiffAt_totalSpace] at hX
    exact hX.2
  -- The trivialized metric is still positive definite: `θ` is an isomorphism.
  have hBpos : ∀ v : E, v ≠ 0 → 0 < metricInCoordinates g x₀ x₀ v v := by
    intro v hv
    rw [metricInCoordinates_apply g x₀ x₀ (mem_chart_source H x₀)]
    refine g.pos x₀ _ ?_
    exact fun h => hv (((trivializationAt E (TangentSpace I) x₀).continuousLinearEquivAt ℝ x₀
      hx₀).symm.map_eq_zero_iff.mp h)
  -- The trivialized differential is still surjective: it is `dπ` between two isomorphisms.
  have hAsurj : Function.Surjective (mfderivInCoordinates π x₀ x₀) := by
    intro w
    set ι₀ := (trivializationAt E' (TangentSpace I') (π x₀)).continuousLinearEquivAt ℝ (π x₀) hy₀
    set θ₀ := (trivializationAt E (TangentSpace I) x₀).continuousLinearEquivAt ℝ x₀ hx₀
    obtain ⟨s, hs⟩ := hπ x₀ (ι₀.symm w)
    refine ⟨θ₀ s, ?_⟩
    rw [mfderivInCoordinates_apply]
    have hθ : (trivializationAt E (TangentSpace I) x₀).symmL ℝ x₀ (θ₀ s) = s := θ₀.symm_apply_apply s
    rw [hθ, hs, ← Bundle.Trivialization.coe_continuousLinearEquivAt_eq (R := ℝ) _ hy₀]
    exact ι₀.apply_symm_apply w
  -- Near `x₀`, the trivialized lift is the lift of the trivialized data.
  have key : (fun x => (trivializationAt E (TangentSpace I) x₀
        ⟨x, horizontalLiftAt g π x (X x)⟩).2)
      =ᶠ[nhds x₀] (fun x => horizontalLift (metricInCoordinates g x₀ x)
        (mfderivInCoordinates π x₀ x) (u x)) := by
    filter_upwards [((chartAt H x₀).open_source).mem_nhds (mem_chart_source H x₀),
      π.contMDiff.continuous.continuousAt.preimage_mem_nhds
        (((chartAt H' (π x₀)).open_source).mem_nhds (mem_chart_source H' (π x₀)))]
      with x hx hy
    have hxb : x ∈ (trivializationAt E (TangentSpace I) x₀).baseSet := by simpa using hx
    have hyb : π x ∈ (trivializationAt E' (TangentSpace I') (π x₀)).baseSet := by simpa using hy
    set θ := (trivializationAt E (TangentSpace I) x₀).continuousLinearEquivAt ℝ x hxb
    set ι := (trivializationAt E' (TangentSpace I') (π x₀)).continuousLinearEquivAt ℝ (π x) hyb
    have hB' : ∀ a b : E, metricInCoordinates g x₀ x a b = g.inner x (θ.symm a) (θ.symm b) :=
      metricInCoordinates_apply g x₀ x hx
    have hA' : ∀ a : E, mfderivInCoordinates π x₀ x a = ι (mfderiv I I' π x (θ.symm a)) := by
      intro a
      rw [mfderivInCoordinates_apply,
        ← Bundle.Trivialization.coe_continuousLinearEquivAt_eq (R := ℝ) _ hyb]
      rfl
    rw [horizontalLift_congr (E := E) (E' := E') (F := E) (F' := E')
      (inner_pos g x) (hπ x) θ ι hB' hA' (u x)]
    have hiu : ι.symm (u x) = X x := ι.symm_apply_apply _
    show θ (horizontalLiftAt g π x (X x)) = _
    rw [← hiu]
    rfl
  rw [key.contMDiffAt_iff]
  exact (contMDiffAt_horizontalLift (contMDiffAt_metricInCoordinates g x₀)
  -- … (truncated)

deps.title: The horizontal lift depends smoothly on the data
deps.label: lem:horizontal-lift-smooth-in-data

deps.title: The horizontal lift of a tangent vector
deps.label: def:horizontal-lift-tangent

blocks.tex: Smoothness is local, so fix $x_0 \in \tilde{M}$ and read both tangent bundles in trivializations around $x_0$ and $\pi(x_0)$. In those trivializations $\tilde{g}$ and $d\pi$ become smooth families $B(x)$ of positive definite bilinear forms and $A(x)$ of surjective linear maps, and $X$ becomes a smooth $E'$-valued function $u(x)$; by Lemma \ref{lem:horizontal-lift-linear-spec} the trivialized lift of the trivialized data is the trivialized lift, so near $x_0$ the field in question reads as $x \mapsto \mathcal{L}(B(x), A(x))\, u(x)$, where $\mathcal{L}(B,A)$ is the linear-algebraic horizontal lift of Definition \ref{def:horizontal-lift-linear}. This is smooth by Lemma \ref{lem:horizontal-lift-smooth-in-data} together with the smoothness of $u$, since evaluation of a linear map on a vector is a continuous bilinear operation.

blocks.label: lem:horizontal-projection-field-smooth
blocks.title: Smoothness of the Horizontal Projection of a Vector Field
blocks.body: Let $\pi : \tilde{M} \to M$ be a smooth submersion, let $\tilde{g}$ be a Riemannian metric on $\tilde{M}$, and let $W$ be a smooth vector field on $\tilde{M}$. Then the horizontal part $W^H$, defined pointwise by $W^H_x = L_x(d\pi_x(W_x))$, is a smooth vector field on $\tilde{M}$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.contMDiff_horizontalProjField
lean.code:
theorem contMDiff_horizontalProjField (hπ : IsSubmersion π)
    {W : ∀ x : M, TangentSpace I x}
    (hW : ContMDiff I (I.prod 𝓘(ℝ, E)) ∞ (fun x => Bundle.TotalSpace.mk' E x (W x))) :
    ContMDiff I (I.prod 𝓘(ℝ, E)) ∞
      (fun x => Bundle.TotalSpace.mk' E x (horizontalProj g π x (W x))) :=
  fun x₀ => contMDiffAt_horizontalLiftAlong g π hπ x₀ (contMDiff_mfderivAlong π hW x₀)

deps.title: The Pushforward of a Vector Field is a Smooth Section Along the Map
deps.label: lem:pushforward-along-map-smooth

deps.title: The Horizontal Lift of a Smooth Section Along $\pi$ is Smooth
deps.label: lem:horizontal-lift-along-smooth

blocks.tex: By Lemma \ref{lem:pushforward-along-map-smooth} the family $x \mapsto d\pi_x(W_x)$ is a smooth section along $\pi$, and by Lemma \ref{lem:horizontal-lift-along-smooth} the horizontal lift of any such section is a smooth vector field. Composing the two gives the claim.

blocks.label: def:submersion
blocks.title: Smooth submersion
blocks.body: A smooth map $\pi : \tilde{M} \to M$ between smooth manifolds is a \emph{submersion} if its differential $d\pi_x : T_x\tilde{M} \to T_{\pi(x)}M$ is surjective at every $x \in \tilde{M}$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.IsSubmersion

blocks.label: def:horizontal-lift-linear
blocks.title: The horizontal lift of a surjection
blocks.body:
Let $B$ be a positive definite symmetric bilinear form on a finite-dimensional real vector space $E$, let $E'$ be a finite-dimensional real vector space, and let $A : E \to E'$ be surjective. Writing $A^t : (E')^* \to E^*$ for the transpose and identifying $B$ with the isomorphism $E \to E^*$, $v \mapsto B(v, \cdot)$, the \emph{horizontal lift} of $A$ relative to $B$ is
\[
L \;=\; B^{-1}A^t\bigl(AB^{-1}A^t\bigr)^{-1} \;:\; E' \to E ,
\]
the $B$-weighted Moore--Penrose right inverse of $A$. Lemma \ref{lem:horizontal-lift-linear-spec} shows that the two inverses exist and that $L$ is characterized by the properties appearing there.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.horizontalLift
lean.code:
noncomputable def horizontalLift (B : E →L[ℝ] E →L[ℝ] ℝ) (A : E →L[ℝ] E') :
    E' →L[ℝ] E :=
  (raisedTranspose B A).comp
    (ContinuousLinearMap.inverse (A.comp (raisedTranspose B A)))

blocks.label: lem:horizontal-lift-linear-spec
blocks.title: The horizontal lift is the unique $B$-orthogonal right inverse

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.horizontalLift_unique
lean.code:
theorem horizontalLift_unique {B : E →L[ℝ] E →L[ℝ] ℝ}
    (hB : ∀ x : E, x ≠ 0 → 0 < B x x) {A : E →L[ℝ] E'} (hA : Function.Surjective A)
    {L : E' →L[ℝ] E} (h1 : ∀ u : E', A (L u) = u)
    (h2 : ∀ (u : E') (w : E), A w = 0 → B (L u) w = 0) :
    L = horizontalLift B A := by
  ext u
  set d : E := L u - horizontalLift B A u with hd
  -- `d` is vertical …
  have hvert : A d = 0 := by
    rw [hd, map_sub, h1 u, horizontalLift_rightInverse hB hA u, sub_self]
  -- … and horizontal, hence `B d d = 0`
  have hdd : B d d = 0 := by
    have e1 : B (L u) d = 0 := h2 u d hvert
    have e2 : B (horizontalLift B A u) d = 0 := horizontalLift_horizontal hB A u hvert
    have : B d d = B (L u) d - B (horizontalLift B A u) d := by
      rw [hd]; simp
    rw [this, e1, e2, sub_zero]
  have : d = 0 := by
    by_contra hne
    exact (hB d hne).ne' hdd
  have := sub_eq_zero.mp this
  exact this

deps.title: The horizontal lift of a surjection
deps.label: def:horizontal-lift-linear

blocks.label: lem:horizontal-lift-smooth-in-data
blocks.title: The horizontal lift depends smoothly on the data
blocks.body: Let $E$, $E'$ be finite-dimensional real vector spaces, let $N$ be a smooth manifold, and let $x \mapsto B_x$ and $x \mapsto A_x$ be smooth maps from $N$ into the bilinear forms on $E$ and into $\operatorname{Hom}(E,E')$ respectively. If $B_{x_0}$ is positive definite and $A_{x_0}$ is surjective, then $x \mapsto L_x$, the horizontal lift of $A_x$ relative to $B_x$, is smooth at $x_0$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.contMDiffAt_horizontalLift
lean.code:
theorem contMDiffAt_horizontalLift
    {B : X → (E →L[ℝ] E →L[ℝ] ℝ)} {A : X → (E →L[ℝ] E')} {x₀ : X}
    (hBd : ContMDiffAt IX 𝓘(ℝ, E →L[ℝ] E →L[ℝ] ℝ) ∞ B x₀)
    (hAd : ContMDiffAt IX 𝓘(ℝ, E →L[ℝ] E') ∞ A x₀)
    (hB : ∀ v : E, v ≠ 0 → 0 < B x₀ v v) (hA : Function.Surjective (A x₀)) :
    ContMDiffAt IX 𝓘(ℝ, E' →L[ℝ] E) ∞ (fun x => horizontalLift (B x) (A x)) x₀ := by
  -- `x ↦ Aᵗ x` is smooth, being a continuous linear image of `A`.
  have htr : ContMDiffAt IX 𝓘(ℝ, (E' →L[ℝ] ℝ) →L[ℝ] (E →L[ℝ] ℝ)) ∞
      (fun x => transposeCLM (A x)) x₀ :=
    ContDiff.comp_contMDiffAt (ContinuousLinearMap.contDiff _) hAd
  -- `x ↦ (B x)⁻¹` is smooth at `x₀` because `B x₀` is invertible.
  have hBinv : ContMDiffAt IX 𝓘(ℝ, (E →L[ℝ] ℝ) →L[ℝ] E) ∞
      (fun x => ContinuousLinearMap.inverse (B x : E →L[ℝ] (E →L[ℝ] ℝ))) x₀ := by
    have h := ((isInvertible_of_posDef hB).contDiffAt_map_inverse (n := ∞)).comp_contMDiffAt hBd
    simpa [Function.comp_def] using h
  -- hence `x ↦ raisedTranspose (B x) (A x)` is smooth.
  have hS : ContMDiffAt IX 𝓘(ℝ, (E' →L[ℝ] ℝ) →L[ℝ] E) ∞
      (fun x => raisedTranspose (B x) (A x)) x₀ := hBinv.clm_comp htr
  -- `x ↦ A x ∘ raisedTranspose (B x) (A x)` is smooth and invertible at `x₀`.
  have hAS : ContMDiffAt IX 𝓘(ℝ, (E' →L[ℝ] ℝ) →L[ℝ] E') ∞
      (fun x => (A x).comp (raisedTranspose (B x) (A x))) x₀ := hAd.clm_comp hS
  have hASinv : ContMDiffAt IX 𝓘(ℝ, E' →L[ℝ] (E' →L[ℝ] ℝ)) ∞
      (fun x => ContinuousLinearMap.inverse ((A x).comp (raisedTranspose (B x) (A x)))) x₀ := by
    -- `f` must be pinned: unification would otherwise take it to be the constant `f x₀`.
    have h := ContDiffAt.comp_contMDiffAt (x := x₀) (g := ContinuousLinearMap.inverse)
      (f := fun x => (A x).comp (raisedTranspose (B x) (A x)))
      ((isInvertible_comp_raisedTranspose hB hA).contDiffAt_map_inverse (n := ∞)) hAS
    simpa [Function.comp_def] using h
  exact hS.clm_comp hASinv

end Smoothness

/-! ## Proposition 2.25(b): the horizontal lift of a smooth vector field is smooth

The pieces are now in place.  Fix `x₀ : M`.  Smoothness of a section is a
statement about a local trivialization (`Bundle.contMDiffAt_section`), so write
both the metric and the differential in the trivializations of `T M` at `x₀` and
of `T M'` at `π x₀`:

* `metricInCoordinates g x₀` is `g` so read; it is smooth because that is exactly
  what the `contMDiff` field of a `ContMDiffRiemannianMetric` says, and
  `metricInCoordinates_apply` identifies it as `g` conjugated by the fibre
  trivialization;
* `inTangentCoordinates I I' id π (mfderiv π) x₀` is `dπ` so read, and mathlib's
  `ContMDiffAt.mfderiv_const` says it is smooth — this is the one place where the
  smoothness of `x ↦ dπ_x` enters.

`horizontalLift_congr` then says the lift of this trivialized data *is* the
trivialized lift, and `contMDiffAt_horizontalLift` says the former is smooth.
-/

section VectorFieldLift

variable
  {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [NormedSpace ℝ E] [FiniteDimensional ℝ E]
  {H : Type*} [TopologicalSpace H] {I : ModelWithCorners ℝ E H}
  {M : Type*} [TopologicalSpace M] [ChartedSpace H M] [IsManifold I ∞ M]
  {E' : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E'] [NormedSpace ℝ E'] [FiniteDimensional ℝ E']
  {H' : Type*} [TopologicalSpace H'] {I' : ModelWithCorners ℝ E' H'}
  {M' : Type*} [TopologicalSpace M'] [ChartedSpace H' M'] [IsManifold I' ∞ M']
  -- … (truncated)

deps.title: The horizontal lift is the unique $B$-orthogonal right inverse
deps.label: lem:horizontal-lift-linear-spec

deps.title: The horizontal lift of a surjection
deps.label: def:horizontal-lift-linear

blocks.tex: By Definition \ref{def:horizontal-lift-linear}, $L_x = B_x^{-1}A_x^t(A_xB_x^{-1}A_x^t)^{-1}$. The map $A \mapsto A^t$ is linear, hence smooth. Inversion is smooth at every invertible operator, and composition of operators is bilinear, hence smooth. By Lemma \ref{lem:horizontal-lift-linear-spec} both $B_{x_0}$ and $A_{x_0}B_{x_0}^{-1}A_{x_0}^t$ are invertible, so each inversion in the formula is applied at an invertible operator at $x = x_0$, and the composite is smooth at $x_0$.

blocks.label: def:vertical-space
blocks.title: The vertical space
blocks.body: Let $\pi : \tilde{M} \to M$ be a smooth submersion. The \emph{vertical space} at $x \in \tilde{M}$ is $V_x = \ker d\pi_x \subseteq T_x\tilde{M}$. It refers to no metric.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.verticalSpace
lean.code:
noncomputable def verticalSpace (x : M) : Submodule ℝ (TangentSpace I x) :=
  LinearMap.ker (mfderiv I I' π x : TangentSpace I x →ₗ[ℝ] TangentSpace I' (π x))

omit [FiniteDimensional ℝ E] [IsManifold I ∞ M] [FiniteDimensional ℝ E']
  [IsManifold I' ∞ M'] in

blocks.label: def:horizontal-space
blocks.title: The horizontal space
blocks.body:
Let $\pi : \tilde{M} \to M$ be a smooth submersion and $\tilde{g}$ a Riemannian metric on $\tilde{M}$. The \emph{horizontal space} at $x \in \tilde{M}$ is
\[
H_x \;=\; \{\, v \in T_x\tilde{M} \;:\; \tilde{g}_x(v,w) = 0 \text{ for every } w \in V_x \,\} ,
\]
the $\tilde{g}_x$-orthogonal complement of $V_x$. Unlike $V_x$, it depends on $\tilde{g}$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.horizontalSpace
lean.code:
def horizontalSpace (x : M) : Submodule ℝ (TangentSpace I x) where
  carrier := {v | ∀ w ∈ verticalSpace π x, g.inner x v w = 0}
  add_mem' := fun ha hb w hw => by simp [ha w hw, hb w hw]
  zero_mem' := fun w _ => by simp
  smul_mem' := fun c _ ha w hw => by simp [ha w hw]

omit [FiniteDimensional ℝ E] [FiniteDimensional ℝ E'] [IsManifold I' ∞ M'] in

blocks.label: def:horizontal-lift-tangent
blocks.title: The horizontal lift of a tangent vector
blocks.body: Let $\pi : \tilde{M} \to M$ be a smooth submersion and $\tilde{g}$ a Riemannian metric on $\tilde{M}$. For $x \in \tilde{M}$, the \emph{horizontal lift at $x$} is the map $L_x : T_{\pi(x)}M \to T_x\tilde{M}$ given by Definition \ref{def:horizontal-lift-linear} applied to $B = \tilde{g}_x$ and $A = d\pi_x$. By Lemma \ref{lem:horizontal-lift-linear-spec} it is the unique linear map with $d\pi_x(L_x u) = u$ and $L_x u \in H_x$ for all $u$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.horizontalLiftAt
lean.code:
noncomputable def horizontalLiftAt (x : M) : TangentSpace I' (π x) →L[ℝ] TangentSpace I x :=
  horizontalLift (E := E) (E' := E') (show E →L[ℝ] E →L[ℝ] ℝ from g.inner x)
    (show E →L[ℝ] E' from mfderiv I I' π x)

omit [IsManifold I' ∞ M'] in

deps.title: The horizontal lift of a surjection
deps.label: def:horizontal-lift-linear

blocks.label: lem:tangent-space-horizontal-vertical-splitting
blocks.title: The tangent space splits
blocks.body:
Let $\pi : \tilde{M} \to M$ be a smooth submersion and $\tilde{g}$ a Riemannian metric on $\tilde{M}$. For every $x \in \tilde{M}$,
\[
T_x\tilde{M} = H_x \oplus V_x .
\]

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.isCompl_horizontalSpace_verticalSpace
lean.code:
theorem isCompl_horizontalSpace_verticalSpace (hπ : IsSubmersion π) (x : M) :
    IsCompl (horizontalSpace g π x) (verticalSpace π x) :=
  ⟨disjoint_iff.mpr (horizontalSpace_inf_verticalSpace g π x),
    codisjoint_iff.mpr (horizontalSpace_sup_verticalSpace g π hπ x)⟩

omit [IsManifold I' ∞ M'] in

deps.title: The horizontal lift is the unique $B$-orthogonal right inverse
deps.label: lem:horizontal-lift-linear-spec

deps.title: The horizontal lift of a tangent vector
deps.label: def:horizontal-lift-tangent

blocks.tex:
The two subspaces intersect trivially: if $v \in H_x \cap V_x$ then, taking $w = v$ in the definition of $H_x$, we get $\tilde{g}_x(v,v) = 0$, hence $v = 0$ because $\tilde{g}_x$ is positive definite.

They span: given $v \in T_x\tilde{M}$, set $v^H = L_x(d\pi_x v)$ and $v^V = v - v^H$, where $L_x$ is the horizontal lift of Definition \ref{def:horizontal-lift-tangent}. Then $v^H \in H_x$ by Lemma \ref{lem:horizontal-lift-linear-spec}(ii), and $d\pi_x(v^V) = d\pi_x v - d\pi_x(L_x(d\pi_x v)) = d\pi_x v - d\pi_x v = 0$ by Lemma \ref{lem:horizontal-lift-linear-spec}(i), so $v^V \in V_x$. Hence $v = v^H + v^V \in H_x + V_x$.

blocks.label: prop:horizontal-lift-of-vector-field
blocks.title: Existence and uniqueness of horizontal lifts
blocks.body: Let $\pi : \tilde{M} \to M$ be a smooth submersion and $\tilde{g}$ a Riemannian metric on $\tilde{M}$. Every $X \in \mathfrak{X}(M)$ has a horizontal lift $\tilde{X} \in \mathfrak{X}(\tilde{M})$: a smooth vector field with $\tilde{X}_x \in H_x$ and $d\pi_x(\tilde{X}_x) = X_{\pi(x)}$ for every $x \in \tilde{M}$. It is the only vector field with those two properties. This is part (b) of Proposition \ref{prop:horizontal-vector-field-properties}.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.exists_unique_horizontalLift
lean.code:
theorem exists_unique_horizontalLift (hπ : IsSubmersion π)
    {X : ∀ y : M', TangentSpace I' y}
    (hX : ContMDiff I' (I'.prod 𝓘(ℝ, E')) ∞ (fun y => Bundle.TotalSpace.mk' E' y (X y))) :
    ∃ Y : ∀ x : M, TangentSpace I x,
      ContMDiff I (I.prod 𝓘(ℝ, E)) ∞ (fun x => Bundle.TotalSpace.mk' E x (Y x)) ∧
      (∀ x, Y x ∈ horizontalSpace g π x) ∧
      (∀ x, mfderiv I I' π x (Y x) = X (π x)) ∧
      (∀ Z : ∀ x : M, TangentSpace I x, (∀ x, Z x ∈ horizontalSpace g π x) →
        (∀ x, mfderiv I I' π x (Z x) = X (π x)) → ∀ x, Z x = Y x) := by
  refine ⟨fun x => horizontalLiftAt g π x (X (π x)), contMDiff_horizontalLiftField g π hπ hX,
    fun x => horizontalLiftAt_mem g π x _, fun x => mfderiv_horizontalLiftAt g π hπ x _, ?_⟩
  intro Z hZh hZrel x
  -- Two horizontal vectors with the same image under `dπ_x` coincide.
  exact horizontalSpace_injOn_mfderiv g π hπ (hZh x) (horizontalLiftAt_mem g π x _)
    (by rw [hZrel x, mfderiv_horizontalLiftAt g π hπ x])

deps.title: The horizontal lift is the unique $B$-orthogonal right inverse
deps.label: lem:horizontal-lift-linear-spec

deps.title: The horizontal lift depends smoothly on the data
deps.label: lem:horizontal-lift-smooth-in-data

deps.title: The horizontal lift of a tangent vector
deps.label: def:horizontal-lift-tangent

blocks.tex:
Define $\tilde{X}_x = L_x(X_{\pi(x)})$, with $L_x$ as in Definition \ref{def:horizontal-lift-tangent}. Then $\tilde{X}_x \in H_x$ and $d\pi_x(\tilde{X}_x) = X_{\pi(x)}$ by Lemma \ref{lem:horizontal-lift-linear-spec}, so only smoothness and uniqueness remain.

Smoothness is local, so fix $x_0 \in \tilde{M}$ and work in local trivializations of $T\tilde{M}$ near $x_0$ and of $TM$ near $\pi(x_0)$; write $\theta_x : T_x\tilde{M} \to E$ and $\iota_y : T_yM \to E'$ for the resulting isomorphisms onto the model spaces. Set
\[
B_x(a,b) = \tilde{g}_x(\theta_x^{-1}a, \theta_x^{-1}b), \qquad A_x = \iota_{\pi(x)} \circ d\pi_x \circ \theta_x^{-1} .
\]
Then $x \mapsto B_x$ is smooth because $\tilde{g}$ is a smooth $2$-tensor field, and $x \mapsto A_x$ is smooth because $\pi$ is smooth and the differential of a smooth map is smooth in coordinates. Each $B_x$ is positive definite and each $A_x$ is surjective, both properties being carried across the isomorphisms $\theta_x$, $\iota_{\pi(x)}$ from $\tilde{g}_x$ and $d\pi_x$. Transporting the two defining properties of Lemma \ref{lem:horizontal-lift-linear-spec} through $\theta_x$ and $\iota_{\pi(x)}$ shows that the horizontal lift of $A_x$ relative to $B_x$ is $\theta_x \circ L_x \circ \iota_{\pi(x)}^{-1}$; uniqueness in that lemma is what makes this an identity rather than a definition. Hence
\[
\theta_x(\tilde{X}_x) \;=\; \bigl(\theta_x \circ L_x \circ \iota_{\pi(x)}^{-1}\bigr)\bigl(\iota_{\pi(x)}(X_{\pi(x)})\bigr),
\]
which is smooth at $x_0$ by Lemma \ref{lem:horizontal-lift-smooth-in-data} together with smoothness of $x \mapsto \iota_{\pi(x)}(X_{\pi(x)})$, the latter because $X$ is smooth and $\pi$ is smooth. As $\theta$ is the trivialization, $\tilde{X}$ is a smooth section near $x_0$.

For uniqueness, let $Z$ be any vector field with $Z_x \in H_x$ and $d\pi_x(Z_x) = X_{\pi(x)}$ for all $x$. Then $Z_x$ and $\tilde{X}_x$ are both horizontal with the same image under $d\pi_x$, so they agree by the uniqueness in Lemma \ref{lem:horizontal-lift-linear-spec}. Hence $Z = \tilde{X}$.

blocks.label: prop:horizontal-lift-realizes-horizontal-vector
blocks.title: Every Horizontal Vector is the Value of a Lift
blocks.body: Let $\pi : \tilde{M} \to M$ be a smooth submersion and $\tilde{g}$ a Riemannian metric on $\tilde{M}$. For every $x \in \tilde{M}$ and every $v \in H_x$, there is a vector field $X \in \mathfrak{X}(M)$ whose horizontal lift $\tilde{X}$ satisfies $\tilde{X}_x = v$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.exists_horizontalLift_eq
lean.code:
theorem exists_horizontalLift_eq [SigmaCompactSpace M'] [T2Space M'] (hπ : IsSubmersion π)
    {x : M} {v : TangentSpace I x} (hv : v ∈ horizontalSpace g π x) :
    ∃ X : ∀ y : M', TangentSpace I' y,
      ContMDiff I' (I'.prod 𝓘(ℝ, E')) ∞ (fun y => Bundle.TotalSpace.mk' E' y (X y)) ∧
      ContMDiff I (I.prod 𝓘(ℝ, E)) ∞
        (fun z => Bundle.TotalSpace.mk' E z (horizontalLiftAt g π z (X (π z)))) ∧
      horizontalLiftAt g π x (X (π x)) = v := by
  obtain ⟨X, hX, hXeq⟩ :=
    LeeLib.AppendixA.exists_contMDiffVectorField_eq (I := I') (π x) (mfderiv I I' π x v)
  refine ⟨X, hX, contMDiff_horizontalLiftField g π hπ hX, ?_⟩
  rw [hXeq]
  exact horizontalLiftAt_mfderiv_of_mem g π hπ hv

deps.title: Extension of a Tangent Vector to a Global Vector Field
deps.label: lem:vector-field-extension-point

deps.title: Existence and uniqueness of horizontal lifts
deps.label: prop:horizontal-lift-of-vector-field

deps.title: The horizontal lift is the unique $B$-orthogonal right inverse
deps.label: lem:horizontal-lift-linear-spec

deps.title: The horizontal lift of a tangent vector
deps.label: def:horizontal-lift-tangent

blocks.tex:
Push $v$ down to the tangent vector $d\pi_x(v) \in T_{\pi(x)}M$, and use Lemma \ref{lem:vector-field-extension-point} to choose a smooth vector field $X \in \mathfrak{X}(M)$ with $X_{\pi(x)} = d\pi_x(v)$.
