the components of $F^\#$ are obtained by multiplying the components of $F$ by $g^{kl}$ and
contracting one of the indices of $g^{kl}$ with the $i$th index of $F$. Similarly, if $i$ is a
contravariant index position, we can define a $(k - 1, l + 1)$-tensor $F^b$ by
\[
F^b(\alpha_1, \ldots, \alpha_{k+l}) = F(\alpha_1, \ldots, \alpha_{i-1}, \alpha_i^*, \alpha_{i+1}, \ldots, \alpha_{k+l}).
\]

In components, it is computed by multiplying by $g_{kl}$ and contracting.

For example, if $A$ is a mixed 3-tensor given in terms of a local frame by
\[
A = A_i^{jk} \varepsilon^i \otimes E_j \otimes \varepsilon^k
\]
(see (B.6)), we can lower its middle index to obtain a covariant 3-tensor $A^b$ with components
\[
A_{ijk} = g_{jl} A^l_{ik}.
\]

To avoid overly cumbersome notation, we use the symbols $F^\#$ and $F^b$ without explicitly specifying which index position the sharp or flat operator is to be applied to; when there is more than one choice, we will always stipulate in words what is meant.

Another important application of the flat and sharp operators is to extend the trace operator introduced in Appendix B to covariant tensors. If $h$ is any covariant $k$-tensor field on a Riemannian manifold with $k \ge 2$, we can raise one of its indices (say the last one for definiteness) and obtain a $(1, k-1)$-tensor $h^\#$. The trace of $h^\#$ is thus a well-defined covariant $(k-2)$-tensor field (see Exercise B.3). We define the \textbf{trace of $h$ with respect to $g$} as
\[
\text{tr}_g h = \text{tr}(h^\#).
\]

Sometimes we may wish to raise an index other than the last, or to take the trace on a pair of indices other than the last covariant and contravariant ones. In each such case, we will say in words what is meant.

The most important case is that of a covariant 2-tensor field. In this case, $h^\#$ is a $(1,1)$-tensor field, which can equivalently be regarded as an endomorphism field, and $\text{tr}_g h$ is just the ordinary trace of this endomorphism field. In terms of a basis, this is
\[
\text{tr}_g h = h_i^i = g^{ij} h_{ij}.
\]

In particular, in an orthonormal frame this is the ordinary trace of the matrix $(h_{ij})$ (the sum of its diagonal entries); but if the frame is not orthonormal, then this trace is different from the ordinary trace.

\begin{exercise}

\dcref{ch2:2.38}
If $g$ is a Riemannian metric on $M$ and $(E_i)$ is a local frame on $M$, there is a potential ambiguity about what the expression $(g^{ij})$ represents: we have defined it to mean the inverse matrix of $(g_{ij})$, but one could also interpret it as the components of the contravariant 2-tensor field $g^{\#\#}$ obtained by raising both of the indices of $g$. Show that these two interpretations lead to the same result.
\end{exercise}

blocks.label: prop:inner-products-of-tensors
blocks.title: Inner Products of Tensors

blocks.tex: \textit{Proof.} Problem 2-11. $\square$

Another important construction provided by a metric on an oriented manifold is a
canonical volume form.

The construction needs one notion that the development so far has not: an orientation of $M$
that varies smoothly from point to point. A choice of orientation of each tangent space
already singles out a form $dV_g|_x$ on each $T_x M$; what makes $x \mapsto dV_g|_x$ a
\emph{differential form}, rather than an unrelated family of forms, is exactly the smoothness
of that choice.

blocks.label: def:smooth-orientation
blocks.title: Smooth Orientation
blocks.body:
A \emph{pointwise orientation} of a smooth $n$-manifold $M$ is a choice of orientation $o_x$ of
the vector space $T_x M$ for each $x \in M$. It is \emph{smooth} if every $p \in M$ has an open
neighbourhood $u$ carrying a smooth local frame $(Y_1, \ldots, Y_n)$ for $TM$ that is
positively oriented throughout $u$: for every $x \in u$ the basis $(Y_1|_x, \ldots, Y_n|_x)$ of
$T_x M$ induces $o_x$. An \emph{oriented} Riemannian manifold is a Riemannian manifold together
with a smooth orientation.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.IsSmoothOrientation
lean.code:
def IsSmoothOrientation (o : PointwiseOrientation I M) : Prop :=
  ∀ p : M, ∃ (u : Set M) (Y : Fin (finrank ℝ E) → (x : M) → TangentSpace I x),
    IsOpen u ∧ p ∈ u ∧ ∃ hY : IsLocalFrameOn I E ∞ Y u,
      ∀ x, ∀ hx : x ∈ u, (hY.toBasisAt hx).orientation = o x

omit [FiniteDimensional ℝ E] in

blocks.label: lem:gram-schmidt-preserves-orientation
blocks.title: Gram--Schmidt Preserves Orientation
blocks.body:
Let $V$ be an $n$-dimensional real inner product space and let $b = (b_1, \ldots, b_n)$ be a
basis of $V$. Then the Gram--Schmidt orthonormalization of $b$ induces the same orientation of
$V$ as $b$ does.

blocks.tex:
Write $u_i$ for the unnormalized Gram--Schmidt vectors and $\hat u_i = u_i / |u_i|$ for their
normalizations, so that $(\hat u_i)$ is the orthonormalization of $b$. The change of basis from
$b$ to $(\hat u_i)$ is triangular, and its determinant equals
$\prod_{i=1}^{n} \langle \hat u_i, b_i \rangle$.

Each factor is positive. Indeed the recursion defining $u_i$ subtracts from $b_i$ a combination
of $u_1, \ldots, u_{i-1}$, each of which is orthogonal to $u_i$; hence
$\langle u_i, b_i \rangle = \langle u_i, u_i \rangle = |u_i|^2$, and therefore
$\langle \hat u_i, b_i \rangle = |u_i|$. Since $b$ is linearly independent, $u_i \ne 0$ and so
$|u_i| > 0$.

The determinant of the change of basis is thus positive, and a change of basis with positive
determinant preserves orientation.

blocks.label: prop:oriented-orthonormal-frames
blocks.title: Existence of Oriented Orthonormal Frames
blocks.body:
Let $(M,g)$ be a Riemannian $n$-manifold with a smooth orientation $o$. Then every $p \in M$ has
an open neighbourhood $u$ carrying a smooth local frame $(Z_1, \ldots, Z_n)$ for $TM$ that is
both orthonormal and positively oriented at every $x \in u$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.exists_orientedOrthonormalFrame_nhds
lean.code:
theorem exists_orientedOrthonormalFrame_nhds (g : RiemannianMetric I M)
    {o : PointwiseOrientation I M} (ho : IsSmoothOrientation o) (p : M) :
    ∃ (u : Set M) (Z : Fin (finrank ℝ E) → (x : M) → TangentSpace I x),
      IsOpen u ∧ p ∈ u ∧ ∃ hZ : IsLocalFrameOn I E ∞ Z u,
        (∀ x ∈ u, ∀ i j, g.inner x (Z i x) (Z j x) = if i = j then 1 else 0) ∧
        ∀ x, ∀ hx : x ∈ u, (hZ.toBasisAt hx).orientation = o x := by
  classical
  obtain ⟨u, Y, hu, hpu, hY, hor⟩ := ho p
  letI : Bundle.RiemannianBundle (TangentSpace I : M → Type _) := ⟨g.toRiemannianMetric⟩
  have hZframe : IsLocalFrameOn I E ∞
      (gramSchmidtFrame (V := (TangentSpace I : M → Type _)) Y) u :=
    isLocalFrameOn_gramSchmidtFrame (IB := I) (F := E) (V := (TangentSpace I : M → Type _)) hY
  refine ⟨u, gramSchmidtFrame (V := (TangentSpace I : M → Type _)) Y, hu, hpu, hZframe, ?_, ?_⟩
  · -- orthonormality, transported to `g.inner` exactly as `exists_orthonormalFrame` does
    intro x hx i j
    have hon := gramSchmidtFrame_orthonormal (IB := I) (F := E)
      (V := (TangentSpace I : M → Type _)) hY.isLocalIndepOn hx
    show ⟪gramSchmidtFrame (V := (TangentSpace I : M → Type _)) Y i x,
        gramSchmidtFrame (V := (TangentSpace I : M → Type _)) Y j x⟫_ℝ = _
    rcases eq_or_ne i j with rfl | hij
    · rw [if_pos rfl, real_inner_self_eq_norm_sq, hon.1 i, one_pow]
    · rw [if_neg hij]
      exact hon.2 hij
  · -- orientation: Gram-Schmidt did not flip it
    intro x hx
    haveI : FiniteDimensional ℝ (TangentSpace I x) := inferInstanceAs (FiniteDimensional ℝ E)
    have hcard : finrank ℝ (TangentSpace I x) = Fintype.card (Fin (finrank ℝ E)) := by
      rw [Fintype.card_fin]; rfl
    have hcoe : ⇑(hY.toBasisAt hx) = fun i => Y i x :=
      funext fun i => IsLocalFrameOn.toBasisAt_coe hY hx i
    -- the Gram-Schmidt'd frame's basis IS mathlib's `gramSchmidtOrthonormalBasis`
    have hbases : hZframe.toBasisAt hx
        = (gramSchmidtOrthonormalBasis hcard ⇑(hY.toBasisAt hx)).toBasis := by
      refine Basis.eq_of_apply_eq fun i => ?_
      rw [IsLocalFrameOn.toBasisAt_coe, OrthonormalBasis.coe_toBasis]
      rw [gramSchmidtOrthonormalBasis_apply hcard (f := ⇑(hY.toBasisAt hx)) (i := i) ?_]
      · rw [hcoe]; rfl
      · rw [hcoe]
        simpa [gramSchmidtNormed, norm_eq_zero] using
          gramSchmidt_ne_zero i (hY.linearIndependent hx)
    rw [hbases, InnerProductSpace.gramSchmidtOrthonormalBasis_orientation]
    exact hor x hx

end SmoothOrientation

/-! ## The Riemannian volume form -/

section VolumeForm

variable
  {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [NormedSpace ℝ E] [FiniteDimensional ℝ E]
  {H : Type*} [TopologicalSpace H] {I : ModelWithCorners ℝ E H}
  {M : Type*} [TopologicalSpace M] [ChartedSpace H M] [IsManifold I ∞ M]

blocks.tex:
By Definition \ref{def:smooth-orientation} there are an open $u \ni p$ and a smooth local frame
$(Y_1, \ldots, Y_n)$ on $u$ that is positively oriented at every $x \in u$. Orthonormalize it
fibrewise: by Proposition \ref{prop:existence-orthonormal-frames} the resulting family
$(Z_1, \ldots, Z_n)$ is again a smooth local frame on $u$, orthonormal at every $x \in u$. By
Lemma \ref{lem:gram-schmidt-preserves-orientation}, applied in the inner product space
$(T_x M, g_x)$, the basis $(Z_1|_x, \ldots, Z_n|_x)$ induces the same orientation as
$(Y_1|_x, \ldots, Y_n|_x)$, which is $o_x$.

The initial-span condition of Proposition \ref{prop:existence-orthonormal-frames} is not by
itself enough here: $\operatorname{span}(Z_1|_x, \ldots, Z_k|_x) =
\operatorname{span}(Y_1|_x, \ldots, Y_k|_x)$ for every $k$ does not determine the orientation of
$(Z_i|_x)$, as replacing $Z_1$ by $-Z_1$ shows. This is why the orientation must be tracked
through the orthonormalization, by Lemma \ref{lem:gram-schmidt-preserves-orientation}.

blocks.label: def:volume-form-pointwise
blocks.title: The Riemannian Volume Form
blocks.body:
Let $(M,g)$ be a Riemannian $n$-manifold and let $o$ be a pointwise orientation of $M$. For each
$x \in M$ let $dV_g|_x$ be the volume form of the oriented inner product space
$(T_x M, g_x, o_x)$, that is, the unique alternating $n$-form on $T_x M$ taking the value $1$ on
every orthonormal basis of $T_x M$ inducing $o_x$. The family $dV_g : x \mapsto dV_g|_x$ is the
\emph{Riemannian volume form} of $(M, g, o)$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.RiemannianMetric.volumeForm

blocks.label: prop:volume-form-determinant
blocks.title: The Determinant Formula
blocks.body:
Let $(M,g)$ be a Riemannian $n$-manifold with pointwise orientation $o$, let $(E_1,\ldots,E_n)$
be a local orthonormal frame on an open set $u$ that is positively oriented at $x \in u$, and let
$v_1, \ldots, v_n \in T_x M$. Then
\[
dV_g|_x(v_1, \ldots, v_n) = \det \bigl( \langle E_i|_x, v_j \rangle_g \bigr)_{i,j=1}^{n}.
\]

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.RiemannianMetric.volumeForm_apply_eq_det
lean.code:
theorem volumeForm_apply_eq_det (g : RiemannianMetric I M) (o : PointwiseOrientation I M)
    (hY : IsLocalFrameOn I E ∞ Y u)
    (hon : ∀ x ∈ u, ∀ i j, g.inner x (Y i x) (Y j x) = if i = j then 1 else 0)
    (hx : x ∈ u) (hor : (hY.toBasisAt hx).orientation = o x)
    (v : Fin (finrank ℝ E) → TangentSpace I x) :
    g.volumeForm o x v = (Matrix.of fun i j => g.inner x (Y i x) (v j)).det := by
  letI : Bundle.RiemannianBundle (TangentSpace I : M → Type _) := ⟨g.toRiemannianMetric⟩
  haveI : Fact (finrank ℝ (TangentSpace I x) = finrank ℝ E) := ⟨rfl⟩
  have hon' := orthonormal_toBasisAt g hY hon hx
  have := volumeFormL_apply_eq_det (o x) ((hY.toBasisAt hx).toOrthonormalBasis hon') (by
    rwa [Basis.toBasis_toOrthonormalBasis]) v
  simpa only [Basis.coe_toOrthonormalBasis, IsLocalFrameOn.toBasisAt_coe] using this

omit [FiniteDimensional ℝ E] in

blocks.tex:
The tuple $(E_1|_x, \ldots, E_n|_x)$ is an orthonormal basis of $(T_x M, g_x)$ inducing $o_x$, so
by Definition \ref{def:volume-form-pointwise} $dV_g|_x$ is the determinant form of that basis.
The determinant form of an orthonormal basis $E$ sends $(v_1,\ldots,v_n)$ to the determinant of
the matrix expressing the $v_j$ in the basis $E$, whose $(i,j)$ entry is the coefficient
$\langle E_i|_x, v_j \rangle_g$ of $v_j$ along $E_i|_x$.

blocks.label: prop:volume-form-value-one
blocks.title: The Characterizing Property of $dV_g$
blocks.body:
Let $(M,g)$ be a Riemannian $n$-manifold with pointwise orientation $o$ and let
$(E_1, \ldots, E_n)$ be a local orthonormal frame on an open set $u$ that is positively oriented
at $x \in u$. Then $dV_g|_x(E_1|_x, \ldots, E_n|_x) = 1$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.RiemannianMetric.volumeForm_apply_eq_one
lean.code:
theorem volumeForm_apply_eq_one (g : RiemannianMetric I M) (o : PointwiseOrientation I M)
    (hY : IsLocalFrameOn I E ∞ Y u)
    (hon : ∀ x ∈ u, ∀ i j, g.inner x (Y i x) (Y j x) = if i = j then 1 else 0)
    (hx : x ∈ u) (hor : (hY.toBasisAt hx).orientation = o x) :
    g.volumeForm o x (fun i => Y i x) = 1 := by
  letI : Bundle.RiemannianBundle (TangentSpace I : M → Type _) := ⟨g.toRiemannianMetric⟩
  haveI : Fact (finrank ℝ (TangentSpace I x) = finrank ℝ E) := ⟨rfl⟩
  have hon' := orthonormal_toBasisAt g hY hon hx
  have := volumeFormL_apply_eq_one (o x) ((hY.toBasisAt hx).toOrthonormalBasis hon') (by
    rwa [Basis.toBasis_toOrthonormalBasis])
  simpa only [Basis.coe_toOrthonormalBasis, IsLocalFrameOn.toBasisAt_coe] using this

omit [FiniteDimensional ℝ E] in

blocks.tex:
Immediate from Definition \ref{def:volume-form-pointwise}: $(E_1|_x, \ldots, E_n|_x)$ is an
orthonormal basis of $T_x M$ inducing $o_x$, and $dV_g|_x$ takes the value $1$ on every such
basis. Equivalently, the matrix of Proposition \ref{prop:volume-form-determinant} is the
identity, whose determinant is $1$.

blocks.label: prop:volume-form-uniqueness
blocks.title: Uniqueness of $dV_g$
blocks.body:
Let $(M,g)$ be a Riemannian $n$-manifold with pointwise orientation $o$, let $(E_1,\ldots,E_n)$
be a local orthonormal frame on an open set $u$ that is positively oriented at $x \in u$, and let
$\theta$ be an alternating $n$-form on $T_x M$ with $\theta(E_1|_x, \ldots, E_n|_x) = 1$. Then
$\theta = dV_g|_x$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.RiemannianMetric.volumeForm_unique
lean.code:
theorem volumeForm_unique (g : RiemannianMetric I M) (o : PointwiseOrientation I M)
    (hY : IsLocalFrameOn I E ∞ Y u)
    (hon : ∀ x ∈ u, ∀ i j, g.inner x (Y i x) (Y j x) = if i = j then 1 else 0)
    (hx : x ∈ u) (hor : (hY.toBasisAt hx).orientation = o x)
    (θ : (TangentSpace I x) [⋀^Fin (finrank ℝ E)]→L[ℝ] ℝ)
    (hθ : θ (fun i => Y i x) = 1) :
    θ = g.volumeForm o x := by
  have hcoe : ⇑(hY.toBasisAt hx) = fun i => Y i x :=
    funext fun i => IsLocalFrameOn.toBasisAt_coe hY hx i
  have hbasis : ∀ v : Fin (finrank ℝ E) → TangentSpace I x,
      θ v = (hY.toBasisAt hx).det v * θ (fun i => Y i x) := by
    intro v
    have := (θ.toAlternatingMap).eq_smul_basis_det (hY.toBasisAt hx)
    have hv := congrArg (fun f => f v) this
    simpa [Basis.det_apply, mul_comm, hcoe] using hv
  ext v
  rw [hbasis v, hθ, mul_one]
  letI : Bundle.RiemannianBundle (TangentSpace I : M → Type _) := ⟨g.toRiemannianMetric⟩
  haveI : Fact (finrank ℝ (TangentSpace I x) = finrank ℝ E) := ⟨rfl⟩
  have hon' := orthonormal_toBasisAt g hY hon hx
  have hrob := (o x).volumeForm_robust ((hY.toBasisAt hx).toOrthonormalBasis hon') (by
    rwa [Basis.toBasis_toOrthonormalBasis])
  show _ = (o x).volumeForm v
  rw [hrob, Basis.toBasis_toOrthonormalBasis]

/-! ### Lee 2.41(c): the coordinate formula `dV_g = √det(g_ij) ε¹ ∧ ⋯ ∧ εⁿ`

Lee states (c) for a coordinate frame `(∂/∂x^i)` with its dual coframe `(dx^i)`.  Neither exists
in the pinned mathlib — there is no coordinate frame of `TM` as a named object — so (c) is stated
here for an **arbitrary oriented smooth local frame** `(Y_i)` and a dual coframe `(ε^i)`.  That is
strictly more general and specializes to Lee's statement verbatim: a chart's coordinate frame is a
local frame (`Trivialization.localFrame`), `g_ij = g(∂_i, ∂_j)` is its `frameMatrix`, and `(dx^i)`
is its dual coframe. -/

blocks.tex:
An alternating $n$-form on an $n$-dimensional space is determined by its value on a single
basis: every such form $\phi$ satisfies $\phi = \phi(E_1|_x,\ldots,E_n|_x) \det\nolimits_E$,
where $\det_E$ is the determinant form of the basis $(E_i|_x)$. Both $\theta$ and $dV_g|_x$ take
the value $1$ there, the latter by Proposition \ref{prop:volume-form-value-one}, so both equal
$\det_E$.

blocks.label: lem:top-forms-proportional
blocks.title: Top-Degree Forms are Proportional
blocks.body:
Let $V$ be an $n$-dimensional real vector space, let $b = (b_1, \ldots, b_n)$ be a basis of $V$,
and let $\omega$ and $\theta$ be alternating $n$-forms on $V$ with
$\theta(b_1, \ldots, b_n) \ne 0$. Then
\[
\omega = \frac{\omega(b_1, \ldots, b_n)}{\theta(b_1, \ldots, b_n)} \, \theta .
\]

blocks.tex:
Every alternating $n$-form $\phi$ on $V$ satisfies $\phi = \phi(b_1,\ldots,b_n) \det\nolimits_b$,
where $\det_b$ is the determinant form of $b$. Applying this to $\theta$ and dividing by
$\theta(b_1,\ldots,b_n) \ne 0$ expresses $\det_b$ as a multiple of $\theta$; substituting that
into the same identity for $\omega$ gives the claim.

blocks.label: def:wedge-covectors
blocks.title: The Wedge Product of Covectors
blocks.body:
Let $V$ be a real topological vector space and let $f_1, \ldots, f_n$ be continuous linear
functionals on $V$. Their \emph{wedge product} is the alternating $n$-form
\[
(f_1 \wedge \cdots \wedge f_n)(v_1, \ldots, v_n) = \det \bigl( f_i(v_j) \bigr)_{i,j=1}^{n} .
\]
This is alternating and multilinear in $(v_1, \ldots, v_n)$ because the determinant is
alternating and multilinear in the columns of its matrix, and it is continuous because a
determinant is a polynomial in the entries $f_i(v_j)$, each of which depends continuously on
$v_j$. This is the determinant convention for the wedge product, as used throughout.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.wedgeCovectors
lean.code:
def wedgeCovectors (f : ι → (V →L[ℝ] ℝ)) : V [⋀^ι]→L[ℝ] ℝ where
  toMultilinearMap := (wedgeCovectorsₗ f).toMultilinearMap
  map_eq_zero_of_eq' := (wedgeCovectorsₗ f).map_eq_zero_of_eq'
  cont :=
    Continuous.congr
      (Continuous.matrix_det
        (continuous_pi fun i => continuous_pi fun j => (f i).continuous.comp (continuous_apply j)))
      fun v => (wedgeCovectorsₗ_apply f v).symm

@[simp]

blocks.label: prop:volume-form-coframe
blocks.title: The Coframe Characterization of $dV_g$
blocks.body:
Let $(M,g)$ be a Riemannian $n$-manifold with pointwise orientation $o$, and let
$(E_1, \ldots, E_n)$ be a local orthonormal frame on an open set $u$ that is positively oriented
at $x \in u$. Let $(e^1, \ldots, e^n)$ be the dual coframe, $e^i = \langle E_i, \cdot \rangle_g$.
Then
\[
dV_g|_x = e^1 \wedge \cdots \wedge e^n .
\]
This is characterization (a) of Lee's Proposition 2.41.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.RiemannianMetric.volumeForm_eq_wedgeCovectors
lean.code:
theorem volumeForm_eq_wedgeCovectors (g : RiemannianMetric I M) (o : PointwiseOrientation I M)
    (hY : IsLocalFrameOn I E ∞ Y u)
    (hon : ∀ x ∈ u, ∀ i j, g.inner x (Y i x) (Y j x) = if i = j then 1 else 0)
    (hx : x ∈ u) (hor : (hY.toBasisAt hx).orientation = o x) :
    g.volumeForm o x = wedgeCovectors (fun i => g.inner x (Y i x)) := by
  ext v
  rw [wedgeCovectors_apply]
  exact volumeForm_apply_eq_det g o hY hon hx hor v

omit [FiniteDimensional ℝ E] in

blocks.tex:
Both sides are alternating $n$-forms on $T_x M$, so it suffices to compare them on an arbitrary
tuple $(v_1, \ldots, v_n)$. By Definition \ref{def:wedge-covectors},
\[
(e^1 \wedge \cdots \wedge e^n)(v_1, \ldots, v_n)
  = \det \bigl( e^i(v_j) \bigr)_{i,j}
  = \det \bigl( \langle E_i|_x, v_j \rangle_g \bigr)_{i,j},
\]
which is $dV_g|_x(v_1, \ldots, v_n)$ by Proposition \ref{prop:volume-form-determinant}.

blocks.label: prop:volume-form-smooth
blocks.title: The Riemannian Volume Form is Smooth
blocks.body:
Let $(M,g)$ be a Riemannian $n$-manifold with a smooth orientation $o$. Then $dV_g$ is a smooth
$n$-form on $M$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.RiemannianMetric.contMDiff_volumeForm
lean.code:
theorem contMDiff_volumeForm (g : RiemannianMetric I M) {o : PointwiseOrientation I M}
    (ho : IsSmoothOrientation o) :
    ContMDiff I (I.prod 𝓘(ℝ, E [⋀^Fin (finrank ℝ E)]→L[ℝ] ℝ)) ∞
      (fun x => TotalSpace.mk' (E [⋀^Fin (finrank ℝ E)]→L[ℝ] ℝ) x (g.volumeForm o x)) :=
  fun x₀ => g.contMDiffAt_volumeForm ho x₀

/-! ### Lee's Proposition 2.41 itself

Everything above is either fibrewise or takes a local oriented orthonormal frame as an
argument.  Lee's proposition is a statement about the `n`-form *field* `dV_g` on all of `M`:
it exists, it is smooth, it is characterized by (b), and it is *unique* with that property.

Uniqueness globally does not follow from `volumeForm_unique` by itself — that lemma pins one
fibre, and only once a frame at that point is handed to it.  What upgrades it is
`exists_orientedOrthonormalFrame_nhds`: a smooth orientation supplies an oriented orthonormal
frame near *every* point, so property (b) can be invoked at each `x` in turn. -/

deps.title: Smoothness of the pairing of vector fields
deps.label: lem:inner-product-vector-fields-smooth

blocks.tex:
Smoothness is local, so fix $x_0 \in M$. Choose a local trivialization $e$ of $TM$ over an open
set containing $x_0$ and write $e^{-1}_x : \mathbb{R}^n \to T_x M$ for the induced isomorphism of
the fibre over $x$. The trivialization of $TM$ induces one of the bundle of $n$-forms, under
which the value of $dV_g$ at $x$ is read as the $n$-form
$\Phi(x) = dV_g|_x \circ e^{-1}_x$ on $\mathbb{R}^n$; thus $dV_g$ is smooth at $x_0$ if and only
if $x \mapsto \Phi(x)$ is.

Fix once and for all a basis $b = (b_1,\ldots,b_n)$ of $\mathbb{R}^n$. By Proposition
\ref{prop:oriented-orthonormal-frames} there are an open $u \ni x_0$ and a smooth local frame
$(Z_1, \ldots, Z_n)$ on $u$, orthonormal and positively oriented at every $x \in u$.

First, $\Phi(x_0)(b_1,\ldots,b_n) \ne 0$. Indeed $dV_g|_{x_0}(Z_1|_{x_0},\ldots,Z_n|_{x_0}) = 1$
by Proposition \ref{prop:volume-form-value-one}, so $dV_g|_{x_0} \ne 0$; and
$(e^{-1}_{x_0} b_1, \ldots, e^{-1}_{x_0} b_n)$ is a basis of $T_{x_0} M$, because $e^{-1}_{x_0}$
is an isomorphism; and a nonzero alternating $n$-form cannot vanish on a basis, since by Lemma
\ref{lem:top-forms-proportional} its value on a basis determines it.

Since the $n$-forms on $\mathbb{R}^n$ form a line, Lemma \ref{lem:top-forms-proportional} applied
with $\theta = \Phi(x_0)$ now gives, for every $x$,
\[
\Phi(x) = \frac{\Phi(x)(b_1,\ldots,b_n)}{\Phi(x_0)(b_1,\ldots,b_n)} \, \Phi(x_0) .
\]
The right-hand side is a scalar function of $x$ times the fixed form $\Phi(x_0)$, so it suffices
to prove that $x \mapsto \Phi(x)(b_1,\ldots,b_n)$ is smooth at $x_0$.

By Proposition \ref{prop:volume-form-determinant}, for every $x \in u$,
\[
\Phi(x)(b_1,\ldots,b_n) = dV_g|_x \bigl(e^{-1}_x b_1, \ldots, e^{-1}_x b_n\bigr)
  = \det \bigl( \langle Z_i|_x, e^{-1}_x b_j \rangle_g \bigr)_{i,j=1}^{n} .
\]
For each fixed $j$ the assignment $x \mapsto e^{-1}_x b_j$ is a smooth local section of $TM$: it
is the local frame induced on $TM$ by the trivialization $e$ and the basis $b$, which is constant
in that trivialization. Each $Z_i$ is smooth on $u$ by construction. Hence each entry
$x \mapsto \langle Z_i|_x, e^{-1}_x b_j \rangle_g$ is smooth on $u$ by Lemma
\ref{lem:inner-product-vector-fields-smooth}. A determinant is a polynomial in the entries of
its matrix, so $x \mapsto \Phi(x)(b_1,\ldots,b_n)$ is smooth on $u$, and in particular at $x_0$.

Characterization (c) of Proposition \ref{prop:riemannian-volume-form} computes $dV_g$ in oriented
local coordinates. It is proved for an arbitrary oriented local frame rather than only for a
coordinate frame: a coordinate frame $(\partial/\partial x^i)$ is a local frame, its matrix
$\bigl(g(\partial_i,\partial_j)\bigr)$ is Lee's $(g_{ij})$, and its dual coframe is $(dx^i)$, so
the general statement contains Lee's verbatim.

blocks.label: def:frame-matrix
blocks.title: The Matrix of $g$ in a Frame
blocks.body:
Let $(M,g)$ be a Riemannian manifold and $(Y_1,\ldots,Y_n)$ a local frame on an open set $u$. The
\emph{matrix of $g$ in the frame $(Y_i)$} at $x \in u$ is
\[
g_{ij}(x) = \langle Y_i|_x, Y_j|_x \rangle_g .
\]
For a coordinate frame $(\partial/\partial x^i)$ this is Lee's $(g_{ij})$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.RiemannianMetric.frameMatrix
lean.code:
def frameMatrix (g : RiemannianMetric I M)
    (Y : Fin (finrank ℝ E) → (x : M) → TangentSpace I x) (x : M) :
    Matrix (Fin (finrank ℝ E)) (Fin (finrank ℝ E)) ℝ :=
  Matrix.of fun i j => g.inner x (Y i x) (Y j x)

omit [FiniteDimensional ℝ E] in

blocks.label: lem:frame-matrix-nonsingular
blocks.title: The Gram Determinant of a Frame is Nonzero
blocks.body:
With the notation of Definition \ref{def:frame-matrix}, $\det\bigl(g_{ij}(x)\bigr) \ne 0$ for
every $x \in u$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.RiemannianMetric.frameMatrix_det_ne_zero
lean.code:
theorem frameMatrix_det_ne_zero (g : RiemannianMetric I M)
    (hY : IsLocalFrameOn I E ∞ Y u) (hx : x ∈ u) : (g.frameMatrix Y x).det ≠ 0 := by
  letI : Bundle.RiemannianBundle (TangentSpace I : M → Type _) := ⟨g.toRiemannianMetric⟩
  have hcoe : ⇑(hY.toBasisAt hx) = fun i => Y i x :=
    funext fun i => IsLocalFrameOn.toBasisAt_coe hY hx i
  have hli : LinearIndependent ℝ (fun i => Y i x) := hcoe ▸ hY.linearIndependent hx
  rw [frameMatrix_eq_gram]
  exact Matrix.det_gram_ne_zero_iff_linearIndependent.mpr hli

deps.title: The Matrix of $g$ in a Frame
deps.label: def:frame-matrix

blocks.tex:
The matrix $\bigl(g_{ij}(x)\bigr)$ is the Gram matrix of the family $(Y_1|_x,\ldots,Y_n|_x)$ with
respect to the inner product $\langle\cdot,\cdot\rangle_g$ on $T_x M$. A Gram matrix of a family
of vectors in an inner product space is nonsingular if and only if the family is linearly
independent. Since $(Y_i)$ is a frame, $(Y_i|_x)$ is a basis of $T_x M$, hence linearly
independent, and so $\det\bigl(g_{ij}(x)\bigr) \ne 0$.

blocks.label: def:dual-coframe
blocks.title: The Dual Coframe of a Frame
blocks.body:
With the notation of Definition \ref{def:frame-matrix}, write $\bigl(g^{ij}(x)\bigr)$ for the
inverse of the matrix $\bigl(g_{ij}(x)\bigr)$, which exists by Lemma
\ref{lem:frame-matrix-nonsingular}. The \emph{dual coframe} of $(Y_i)$ is the family of covectors
\[
\varepsilon^i|_x = \sum_{j} g^{ij}(x) \, \langle Y_j|_x, \cdot \rangle_g .
\]
For a coordinate frame this is the coordinate coframe $(dx^i)$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.RiemannianMetric.dualCoframe
lean.code:
def dualCoframe (g : RiemannianMetric I M)
    (Y : Fin (finrank ℝ E) → (x : M) → TangentSpace I x) (x : M) (i : Fin (finrank ℝ E)) :
    TangentSpace I x →L[ℝ] ℝ :=
  ∑ j, (g.frameMatrix Y x)⁻¹ i j • g.inner x (Y j x)

omit [FiniteDimensional ℝ E] in

deps.title: The Matrix of $g$ in a Frame
deps.label: def:frame-matrix

blocks.label: lem:dual-coframe-duality
blocks.title: The Dual Coframe is Dual
blocks.body:
With the notation of Definition \ref{def:dual-coframe}, $\varepsilon^i|_x(Y_k|_x) = \delta^i_k$
for all $i,k$ and all $x \in u$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.RiemannianMetric.dualCoframe_apply_frame
lean.code:
theorem dualCoframe_apply_frame (g : RiemannianMetric I M)
    (hY : IsLocalFrameOn I E ∞ Y u) (hx : x ∈ u) (i k : Fin (finrank ℝ E)) :
    g.dualCoframe Y x i (Y k x) = if i = k then 1 else 0 := by
  classical
  have hstep : g.dualCoframe Y x i (Y k x)
      = ((g.frameMatrix Y x)⁻¹ * g.frameMatrix Y x) i k := by
    simp [dualCoframe, frameMatrix, ContinuousLinearMap.sum_apply, Matrix.mul_apply]
  rw [hstep, Matrix.nonsing_inv_mul _ (Ne.isUnit (g.frameMatrix_det_ne_zero hY hx))]
  simp [Matrix.one_apply]

omit [FiniteDimensional ℝ E] in

deps.title: The Dual Coframe of a Frame
deps.label: def:dual-coframe

deps.title: The Matrix of $g$ in a Frame
deps.label: def:frame-matrix

deps.title: The Gram Determinant of a Frame is Nonzero
deps.label: lem:frame-matrix-nonsingular

blocks.tex:
By definition,
\[
\varepsilon^i|_x(Y_k|_x) = \sum_j g^{ij}(x) \, \langle Y_j|_x, Y_k|_x \rangle_g
  = \sum_j g^{ij}(x) \, g_{jk}(x),
\]
which is the $(i,k)$ entry of the product of $\bigl(g^{ij}(x)\bigr)$ with $\bigl(g_{ij}(x)\bigr)$.
Since the first matrix is by definition the inverse of the second, that product is the identity,
so the entry is $\delta^i_k$.

blocks.label: lem:volume-form-gram-determinant
blocks.title: The Gram Determinant Formula, Pointwise
blocks.body:
Let $V$ be an oriented $n$-dimensional inner product space with volume form $dV$, and let
$(b_1,\ldots,b_n)$ be a positively oriented basis of $V$, not assumed orthonormal. Then
\[
dV(b_1,\ldots,b_n) = \sqrt{\det\bigl(\langle b_i, b_j \rangle\bigr)} .
\]

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.volumeFormL_apply_eq_sqrt_det_gram
lean.code:
theorem volumeFormL_apply_eq_sqrt_det_gram (o : Orientation ℝ V (Fin n)) (b : Basis (Fin n) ℝ V)
    (hb : b.orientation = o) :
    o.volumeFormL ⇑b = Real.sqrt ((Matrix.gram ℝ ⇑b).det) := by
  classical
  haveI : FiniteDimensional ℝ V := Module.Basis.finiteDimensional_of_finite b
  have hcard : finrank ℝ V = Fintype.card (Fin n) := by
    rw [Fintype.card_fin]; exact Fact.out
  set e := gramSchmidtOrthonormalBasis hcard ⇑b with he
  have hor : e.toBasis.orientation = o := by
    rw [he, InnerProductSpace.gramSchmidtOrthonormalBasis_orientation, hb]
  set m : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ℝ := Matrix.of fun i j => ⟪e i, b j⟫_ℝ with hm
  have hdet : o.volumeFormL ⇑b = m.det := volumeFormL_apply_eq_det o e hor ⇑b
  have hgram : Matrix.gram ℝ ⇑b = mᴴ * m := by
    have h := Matrix.gram_eq_conjTranspose_mul (𝕜 := ℝ) e ⇑b
    rw [h]
    congr 1 <;> · ext i j; simp [hm, e.repr_apply_apply]
  have hpos : 0 < m.det := by
    have hd : e.toBasis.det ⇑b = m.det := by
      rw [Basis.det_apply]
      congr 1
      ext i j
      simp [hm, Basis.toMatrix_apply, e.coe_toBasis_repr_apply, e.repr_apply_apply]
    rw [← hd]
    exact (e.toBasis.orientation_eq_iff_det_pos b).mp (by rw [hor, hb])
  rw [hdet, hgram, Matrix.det_mul, Matrix.det_conjTranspose, RCLike.star_def,
    starRingEnd_apply, star_trivial, ← sq, Real.sqrt_sq hpos.le]

end Pointwise

/-! ## Smooth orientations of a manifold -/

section SmoothOrientation

variable
  {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [NormedSpace ℝ E] [FiniteDimensional ℝ E]
  {H : Type*} [TopologicalSpace H] {I : ModelWithCorners ℝ E H}
  {M : Type*} [TopologicalSpace M] [ChartedSpace H M] [IsManifold I ∞ M]

variable (I M) in

blocks.tex:
Let $(e_1,\ldots,e_n)$ be the Gram--Schmidt orthonormalization of $(b_i)$. By Lemma
\ref{lem:gram-schmidt-preserves-orientation} it is a positively oriented orthonormal basis. Let
$A$ be the matrix $A_{ij} = \langle e_i, b_j \rangle$, so that $b_j = \sum_i A_{ij} e_i$.

Expanding each $b_i$ in the orthonormal basis $(e_k)$ gives
\[
\langle b_i, b_j \rangle = \Bigl\langle \sum_k A_{ki} e_k, \sum_l A_{lj} e_l \Bigr\rangle
  = \sum_k A_{ki} A_{kj} = (A^{\mathsf T} A)_{ij},
\]
so $\det\bigl(\langle b_i, b_j\rangle\bigr) = \det(A^{\mathsf T}A) = (\det A)^2$.

By the determinant formula of Proposition \ref{prop:volume-form-determinant} applied to the
oriented orthonormal basis $(e_i)$, we have $dV(b_1,\ldots,b_n) = \det A$. Finally $\det A > 0$:
$\det A$ is the determinant of the change of basis from $(e_i)$ to $(b_j)$, and it is positive
exactly because the two bases induce the same orientation. Hence
$\sqrt{\det(\langle b_i,b_j\rangle)} = \sqrt{(\det A)^2} = |\det A| = \det A = dV(b_1,\ldots,b_n)$.

blocks.label: prop:volume-form-frame-value
blocks.title: The Value of $dV_g$ on an Oriented Frame
blocks.body:
Let $(M,g)$ be a Riemannian $n$-manifold with a pointwise orientation, and let $(Y_1,\ldots,Y_n)$
be a local frame on $u$ that is positively oriented at $x \in u$. Then
\[
dV_g|_x(Y_1|_x, \ldots, Y_n|_x) = \sqrt{\det\bigl(g_{ij}(x)\bigr)} .
\]

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.RiemannianMetric.volumeForm_apply_eq_sqrt_det_frameMatrix
lean.code:
theorem volumeForm_apply_eq_sqrt_det_frameMatrix (g : RiemannianMetric I M)
    (o : PointwiseOrientation I M) (hY : IsLocalFrameOn I E ∞ Y u) (hx : x ∈ u)
    (hor : (hY.toBasisAt hx).orientation = o x) :
    g.volumeForm o x (fun i => Y i x) = Real.sqrt (g.frameMatrix Y x).det := by
  letI : Bundle.RiemannianBundle (TangentSpace I : M → Type _) := ⟨g.toRiemannianMetric⟩
  haveI : Fact (finrank ℝ (TangentSpace I x) = finrank ℝ E) := ⟨rfl⟩
  have hcoe : ⇑(hY.toBasisAt hx) = fun i => Y i x :=
    funext fun i => IsLocalFrameOn.toBasisAt_coe hY hx i
  have h := volumeFormL_apply_eq_sqrt_det_gram (o x) (hY.toBasisAt hx) hor
  rw [hcoe] at h
  rw [show g.volumeForm o x (fun i => Y i x) = (o x).volumeFormL (fun i => Y i x) from rfl, h,
    frameMatrix_eq_gram]

omit [FiniteDimensional ℝ E] in

deps.title: The Matrix of $g$ in a Frame
deps.label: def:frame-matrix

blocks.tex:
Apply Lemma \ref{lem:volume-form-gram-determinant} to the oriented inner product space $T_x M$ and
its positively oriented basis $(Y_i|_x)$; the Gram matrix $\bigl(\langle Y_i|_x, Y_j|_x\rangle_g\bigr)$
is by Definition \ref{def:frame-matrix} the matrix $\bigl(g_{ij}(x)\bigr)$.

blocks.label: prop:volume-form-coordinate-formula
blocks.title: Lee 2.41(c): the Coordinate Formula for $dV_g$
blocks.body:
Let $(M,g)$ be a Riemannian $n$-manifold with a pointwise orientation, let $(Y_1,\ldots,Y_n)$ be a
local frame on $u$ that is positively oriented at $x \in u$, and let $(\varepsilon^1,\ldots,
\varepsilon^n)$ be any coframe dual to it at $x$, that is $\varepsilon^i(Y_k|_x) = \delta^i_k$.
Then
\[
dV_g|_x = \sqrt{\det\bigl(g_{ij}(x)\bigr)} \; \varepsilon^1 \wedge \cdots \wedge \varepsilon^n .
\]
In particular, for oriented local coordinates $(x^1,\ldots,x^n)$, taking $(Y_i)$ to be the
coordinate frame $(\partial/\partial x^i)$ and $\varepsilon^i = dx^i$ gives
$dV_g = \sqrt{\det(g_{ij})}\, dx^1 \wedge \cdots \wedge dx^n$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.RiemannianMetric.volumeForm_eq_sqrt_det_smul_wedgeCovectors
lean.code:
theorem volumeForm_eq_sqrt_det_smul_wedgeCovectors (g : RiemannianMetric I M)
    (o : PointwiseOrientation I M) (hY : IsLocalFrameOn I E ∞ Y u) (hx : x ∈ u)
    (hor : (hY.toBasisAt hx).orientation = o x)
    (ε : Fin (finrank ℝ E) → (TangentSpace I x →L[ℝ] ℝ))
    (hε : ∀ i k, ε i (Y k x) = if i = k then 1 else 0) :
    g.volumeForm o x = Real.sqrt (g.frameMatrix Y x).det • wedgeCovectors ε := by
  classical
  have hcoe : ⇑(hY.toBasisAt hx) = fun i => Y i x :=
    funext fun i => IsLocalFrameOn.toBasisAt_coe hY hx i
  have hwedge : wedgeCovectors ε (fun i => Y i x) = 1 := by
    rw [wedgeCovectors_apply,
      show (Matrix.of fun i j => ε i (Y j x)) = (1 : Matrix _ _ ℝ) by
        ext i j; rw [Matrix.of_apply, hε i j, Matrix.one_apply]]
    exact Matrix.det_one
  have hne : wedgeCovectors ε ⇑(hY.toBasisAt hx) ≠ 0 := by
    rw [hcoe, hwedge]; norm_num
  rw [ContinuousAlternatingMap.eq_smul_of_apply_basis_ne_zero (hY.toBasisAt hx)
    (g.volumeForm o x) (wedgeCovectors ε) hne, hcoe, hwedge,
    volumeForm_apply_eq_sqrt_det_frameMatrix g o hY hx hor, div_one]

/-! ### Smoothness: `dV_g` is an `n`-form, not merely a pointwise family

This is the existence half of Lee's Proposition 2.41, and the only part of the volume form that
is not fibrewise.  Everything above holds for an arbitrary pointwise orientation; smoothness is
exactly where `IsSmoothOrientation` is used, and it is what makes `dV_g` a *differential form*. -/

deps.title: The Matrix of $g$ in a Frame
deps.label: def:frame-matrix

deps.title: The Value of $dV_g$ on an Oriented Frame
deps.label: prop:volume-form-frame-value

blocks.tex:
Such a dual coframe exists, by Definition \ref{def:dual-coframe} and Lemma
\ref{lem:dual-coframe-duality}.

Both sides are $n$-forms on the $n$-dimensional space $T_x M$, and by Lemma
\ref{lem:top-forms-proportional} such a form is determined by its value on the single basis
$(Y_i|_x)$; so it suffices to check they agree there.

By Definition \ref{def:wedge-covectors},
\[
(\varepsilon^1 \wedge \cdots \wedge \varepsilon^n)(Y_1|_x,\ldots,Y_n|_x)
  = \det\bigl(\varepsilon^i(Y_j|_x)\bigr) = \det(\delta^i_j) = 1,
\]
so the right-hand side takes the value $\sqrt{\det(g_{ij}(x))}$ on $(Y_i|_x)$. The left-hand side
takes the same value by Proposition \ref{prop:volume-form-frame-value}.

Lee's Proposition 2.41 collects the preceding results: its characterizations (a), (b) and (c) are
Propositions \ref{prop:volume-form-coframe}, \ref{prop:volume-form-value-one} and
\ref{prop:volume-form-coordinate-formula} respectively.

blocks.label: prop:riemannian-volume-form
blocks.title: The Riemannian Volume Form
blocks.body:
Let $(M, g)$ be an oriented Riemannian $n$-manifold with or without boundary. There is a unique $n$-form $dV_g$ on
$M$, called the \emph{Riemannian volume form}, characterized by any one of the following
three equivalent properties:

\begin{enumerate}[label=(\alph*)]
\item If $(e^1, \ldots, e^n)$ is any local oriented orthonormal coframe for $T^* M$, then
$$dV_g = e^1 \wedge \cdots \wedge e^n.$$

\item If $(E_1, \ldots, E_n)$ is any local oriented orthonormal frame for $TM$, then
$$dV_g(E_1, \ldots, E_n) = 1.$$

\item If $(x^1, \ldots, x^n)$ are any oriented local coordinates, then
$$dV_g = \sqrt{\det(g_{ij})} \, dx^1 \wedge \cdots \wedge dx^n.$$
\end{enumerate}

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.RiemannianMetric.riemannian_volumeForm
lean.code:
theorem riemannian_volumeForm (g : RiemannianMetric I M) {o : PointwiseOrientation I M}
    (ho : IsSmoothOrientation o) :
    ∃! θ : ∀ x : M, (TangentSpace I x) [⋀^Fin (finrank ℝ E)]→L[ℝ] ℝ,
      ContMDiff I (I.prod 𝓘(ℝ, E [⋀^Fin (finrank ℝ E)]→L[ℝ] ℝ)) ∞
        (fun x => TotalSpace.mk' (E [⋀^Fin (finrank ℝ E)]→L[ℝ] ℝ) x (θ x)) ∧
      ∀ (u : Set M) (Y : Fin (finrank ℝ E) → (x : M) → TangentSpace I x)
        (hY : IsLocalFrameOn I E ∞ Y u),
        (∀ x ∈ u, ∀ i j, g.inner x (Y i x) (Y j x) = if i = j then 1 else 0) →
        ∀ (x : M) (hx : x ∈ u), (hY.toBasisAt hx).orientation = o x →
          θ x (fun i => Y i x) = 1 := by
  refine ⟨g.volumeForm o, ⟨contMDiff_volumeForm g ho, ?_⟩, ?_⟩
  · intro u Y hY hon x hx hor
    exact volumeForm_apply_eq_one g o hY hon hx hor
  · intro θ hθ
    exact volumeForm_eq_of_apply_orientedOrthonormalFrame_eq_one g ho θ
      (fun u Y hY hon x hx hor => hθ.2 u Y hY hon x hx hor)

deps.title: The Riemannian Volume Form is Smooth
deps.label: prop:volume-form-smooth

blocks.tex:
The form $dV_g$ is the one of Definition \ref{def:volume-form-pointwise}, which is defined at each
point as the volume form of the oriented inner product space $T_x M$; it is a smooth $n$-form by
Proposition \ref{prop:volume-form-smooth}, and it is the unique $n$-form with property (b) by
Proposition \ref{prop:volume-form-uniqueness}, since an $n$-form on an $n$-dimensional space is
determined by its value on one basis.

Properties (a), (b) and (c) hold for it by Propositions \ref{prop:volume-form-coframe},
\ref{prop:volume-form-value-one} and \ref{prop:volume-form-coordinate-formula} respectively.

The three are equivalent because each pins the form down on a single basis: a form satisfying (a)
or (c) takes the value $1$ on an oriented orthonormal frame, since by Definition
\ref{def:wedge-covectors} a wedge of a dual coframe evaluates on the dual frame to
$\det(\delta^i_j) = 1$, and $\det(g_{ij}) = 1$ for an orthonormal frame; so each of (a) and (c)
implies (b), and (b) determines the form uniquely by Proposition
\ref{prop:volume-form-uniqueness}.

The significance of the Riemannian volume form is that it allows us to integrate
functions on an oriented Riemannian manifold, not just differential forms. If $f$ is
a continuous, compactly supported real-valued function on an oriented Riemannian
$n$-manifold $(M, g)$ with or without boundary, then $f \, dV_g$ is a compactly supported
$n$-form. Therefore, the integral $\int_M f \, dV_g$ makes sense, and we define it to be the
\emph{integral of $f$ over $M$}. Similarly, if $M$ is compact, the \emph{volume of $M$} is defined to
be
$$\operatorname{Vol}(M) = \int_M dV_g = \int_M 1 \, dV_g.$$

In particular, if $D \subseteq M$ is a \emph{regular domain} (a closed, embedded codimension-$0$
submanifold with boundary), we can apply these definitions to $D$ with its induced
metric and thereby make sense of the integral of $f$ over $D$ and, in case $D$ is compact, the volume of $D$.

The notation $dV_g$ is chosen to emphasize the similarity of the integral $\int_M f \, dV_g$
with the standard integral of a function over an open subset of $\mathbb{R}^n$. It is \emph{not} meant
to imply that $dV_g$ is an exact form; in fact, if $M$ is a compact oriented manifold
without boundary, then $dV_g$ is never exact, because its integral over $M$ is positive, and exact forms integrate to zero by Stokes's theorem.

Because there are two conventions in common use for the wedge product (see p.~401), it should be noted that properties (a) and (c) of Proposition 2.41 are the same regardless of which convention is used; but property (b) holds only for the determinant convention that we use. If the Alt convention is used, the number 1 should be replaced by $1/n!$ in that formula.

Stating Exercise \ref{exer:isometry-preserves-volume-form} requires the pullback of a differential
form along a smooth map.

blocks.label: def:pullback-alternating
blocks.title: The Pullback of a Differential Form
blocks.body:
Let $\varphi : M \to \tilde{M}$ be a smooth map and $w$ a $k$-form on $\tilde{M}$. The
\emph{pullback} $\varphi^* w$ is the $k$-form on $M$ given at $x$ by
\[
(\varphi^* w)|_x(v_1,\ldots,v_k) = w|_{\varphi(x)}\bigl(d\varphi_x(v_1), \ldots, d\varphi_x(v_k)\bigr).
\]

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.pullbackAlternating
lean.code:
def pullbackAlternating {k : ℕ} (φ : M → M')
    (w : ∀ y : M', (TangentSpace I' y) [⋀^Fin k]→L[ℝ] ℝ) (x : M) :
    (TangentSpace I x) [⋀^Fin k]→L[ℝ] ℝ :=
  (w (φ x)).compContinuousLinearMap (mfderiv I I' φ x)

omit [FiniteDimensional ℝ E] [IsManifold I ∞ M] [IsManifold I' ∞ M'] in
@[simp]

blocks.label: lem:orthonormal-pushforward
blocks.title: A Metric-Preserving Map Pushes Orthonormal Frames Forward
blocks.body:
Let $\varphi : (M,g) \to (\tilde{M},\tilde{g})$ be metric-preserving and let $(E_i)$ be a
$g$-orthonormal frame on an open set $u$. Then for every $x \in u$ the family
$\bigl(d\varphi_x(E_i|_x)\bigr)$ is $\tilde{g}$-orthonormal.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.IsMetricPreserving.orthonormal_pushforward
lean.code:
theorem IsMetricPreserving.orthonormal_pushforward (h : IsMetricPreserving g g' φ)
    (hon : ∀ x ∈ u, ∀ i j, g.inner x (Y i x) (Y j x) = if i = j then 1 else 0) (hx : x ∈ u) :
    letI : Bundle.RiemannianBundle (TangentSpace I' : M' → Type _) := ⟨g'.toRiemannianMetric⟩
    Orthonormal ℝ (fun i => mfderiv I I' φ x (Y i x)) := by
  letI : Bundle.RiemannianBundle (TangentSpace I' : M' → Type _) := ⟨g'.toRiemannianMetric⟩
  rw [orthonormal_iff_ite]
  intro i j
  show ⟪mfderiv I I' φ x (Y i x), mfderiv I I' φ x (Y j x)⟫_ℝ = _
  rw [show ⟪mfderiv I I' φ x (Y i x), mfderiv I I' φ x (Y j x)⟫_ℝ
      = g'.inner (φ x) (mfderiv I I' φ x (Y i x)) (mfderiv I I' φ x (Y j x)) from rfl,
    h.inner_mfderiv x (Y i x) (Y j x)]
  exact hon x hx i j

omit [FiniteDimensional ℝ E] in

blocks.tex:
By the defining property of a metric-preserving map,
$\langle d\varphi_x v, d\varphi_x w \rangle_{\tilde{g}} = \langle v, w \rangle_g$ for all
$v,w \in T_x M$. Applying this to $v = E_i|_x$ and $w = E_j|_x$ gives
$\langle d\varphi_x E_i|_x, d\varphi_x E_j|_x \rangle_{\tilde{g}} = \langle E_i|_x, E_j|_x\rangle_g
= \delta_{ij}$.

blocks.label: lem:push-basis
blocks.title: The Pushforward Frame is a Basis
blocks.body:
In the situation of Lemma \ref{lem:orthonormal-pushforward}, the family
$\bigl(d\varphi_x(E_i|_x)\bigr)$ is a basis of $T_{\varphi(x)}\tilde{M}$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.IsMetricPreserving.pushBasis
lean.code:
def IsMetricPreserving.pushBasis (h : IsMetricPreserving g g' φ)
    (hon : ∀ x ∈ u, ∀ i j, g.inner x (Y i x) (Y j x) = if i = j then 1 else 0) (hx : x ∈ u) :
    Basis (Fin (finrank ℝ E)) ℝ (TangentSpace I' (φ x)) :=
  haveI : FiniteDimensional ℝ (TangentSpace I' (φ x)) := inferInstanceAs (FiniteDimensional ℝ E)
  Basis.mk (h.linearIndependent_pushforward hon hx)
    ((h.linearIndependent_pushforward hon hx).span_eq_top_of_card_eq_finrank'
      (by simp only [Fintype.card_fin]; rfl)).ge

@[simp]

deps.title: The Pushforward Frame is a Basis
deps.label: lem:push-basis

deps.title: The Pullback of a Differential Form
deps.label: def:pullback-alternating

deps.title: A Metric-Preserving Map Pushes Orthonormal Frames Forward
deps.label: lem:orthonormal-pushforward

blocks.tex:
An orthonormal family is linearly independent, so by Lemma \ref{lem:orthonormal-pushforward} the
family $\bigl(d\varphi_x(E_i|_x)\bigr)$ is linearly independent. It has $n$ members, and
$\dim T_{\varphi(x)}\tilde{M} = n$, so a linearly independent family of $n$ vectors in an
$n$-dimensional space spans, hence is a basis.

Fix $x \in M$ and let $(E_1,\ldots,E_n)$ be a local oriented orthonormal frame near $x$. By
Definition \ref{def:pullback-alternating},
\[
(\varphi^* dV_{\tilde{g}})|_x(E_1|_x,\ldots,E_n|_x)
  = dV_{\tilde{g}}|_{\varphi(x)}\bigl(d\varphi_x E_1|_x, \ldots, d\varphi_x E_n|_x\bigr).
\]
By Lemmas \ref{lem:orthonormal-pushforward} and \ref{lem:push-basis} the family
$\bigl(d\varphi_x E_i|_x\bigr)$ is an orthonormal basis of $T_{\varphi(x)}\tilde{M}$, and it is
positively oriented because $\varphi$ is orientation-preserving. So by Proposition
\ref{prop:volume-form-value-one} the right-hand side equals $1$.

Thus $(\varphi^* dV_{\tilde{g}})|_x$ is an $n$-form taking the value $1$ on the oriented
orthonormal frame $(E_i|_x)$. By Proposition \ref{prop:volume-form-uniqueness} the only such
$n$-form is $dV_g|_x$, so $(\varphi^* dV_{\tilde{g}})|_x = dV_g|_x$. As $x$ was arbitrary,
$\varphi^* dV_{\tilde{g}} = dV_g$.

For Riemannian hypersurfaces, we have the following important characterization of the volume form on the hypersurface in terms of that of the ambient manifold. If $X$ is a vector field and $\mu$ is a differential form, recall that $X \lrcorner \mu$ denotes \emph{interior multiplication} of $\mu$ by $X$ (see p.~401).

blocks.label: def:interior-multiplication
blocks.title: Interior Multiplication
blocks.body:
Let $V$ be a real topological vector space and let $\mu$ be an alternating $(k+1)$-form on $V$.
For $x \in V$, the \emph{interior multiplication} $x \lrcorner \mu$ is the alternating $k$-form
\[
  (x \lrcorner \mu)(v_1, \ldots, v_k) = \mu(x, v_1, \ldots, v_k).
\]
It is alternating because $\mu$ is: if $v_i = v_j$ with $i \ne j$, then the tuple
$(x, v_1, \ldots, v_k)$ has two equal entries. It is continuous because $\mu$ is and
$(v_1,\ldots,v_k) \mapsto (x, v_1, \ldots, v_k)$ is.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.camContract
lean.code:
noncomputable def camContract {n : ℕ} (w : V [⋀^Fin (n + 1)]→L[ℝ] ℝ) (x : V) :
    V [⋀^Fin n]→L[ℝ] ℝ where
  toAlternatingMap := AlternatingMap.curryLeft w.toAlternatingMap x
  cont := by
    refine w.cont.comp (continuous_pi fun i => ?_)
    refine Fin.cases ?_ (fun j => ?_) i
    · simpa using continuous_const
    · simpa using continuous_apply j

omit [IsTopologicalAddGroup V] [ContinuousSMul ℝ V] in
@[simp]

blocks.label: def:unit-normal
blocks.title: Unit Normal
blocks.body:
Let $(\tilde M, \tilde g)$ be a Riemannian manifold and let $F : M \to \tilde M$ present $M$ as a
smooth immersion. A family $N$ assigning to each $x \in M$ a vector $N_x \in T_{F(x)}\tilde M$ is
a \emph{unit normal} at $x$ if
\[
  \tilde g_{F(x)}(N_x, dF_x v) = 0 \quad\text{for every } v \in T_x M,
  \qquad \tilde g_{F(x)}(N_x, N_x) = 1,
\]
that is, if $N_x \in N_x M$ and $|N_x|_{\tilde g} = 1$.

blocks.label: lem:adapted-frame-cons-normal
blocks.title: The Adapted Frame Along a Hypersurface
blocks.body:
Let $(\tilde M, \tilde g)$ be a Riemannian $(n+1)$-manifold, let $F : M \to \tilde M$ present the
$n$-manifold $M$ as a smooth immersion, and let $g$ be a metric on $M$ that $F$ preserves. Let
$N$ be a unit normal at $x \in M$ and let $(E_1, \ldots, E_n)$ be a $g$-orthonormal basis of
$T_x M$. Then
\[
  \bigl(N_x, dF_x E_1, \ldots, dF_x E_n\bigr)
\]
is a $\tilde g$-orthonormal family in $T_{F(x)}\tilde M$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.inner_consNormal
lean.code:
theorem inner_consNormal (h : IsMetricPreserving g g' F) (hN : IsUnitNormalAt I g' F N x)
    (hon : ∀ i j, g.inner x (b i) (b j) = if i = j then 1 else 0)
    (a c : Fin (finrank ℝ E')) :
    g'.inner (F x) (consNormal I F hdim N x b a) (consNormal I F hdim N x b c) =
      if a = c then 1 else 0 := by
  simp only [consNormal]
  rw [inner_cons_normal h hN hon]
  simp

omit [FiniteDimensional ℝ E] [FiniteDimensional ℝ E'] in

blocks.tex:
There are four cases. First, $\tilde g(N_x, N_x) = 1$ because $N$ is a unit normal. Second,
$\tilde g(N_x, dF_x E_i) = 0$ for each $i$, because $N_x$ is normal and $dF_x E_i$ is tangent to
$M$; the same holds with the arguments exchanged, by symmetry of $\tilde g$. Third, for $i, j$,
\[
  \tilde g_{F(x)}(dF_x E_i, dF_x E_j) = g_x(E_i, E_j) = \delta_{ij},
\]
the first equality because $F$ preserves the metric and the second because $(E_i)$ is
orthonormal. So the family is orthonormal.

blocks.label: lem:adapted-frame-ambient-basis
blocks.title: The Adapted Frame is an Ambient Basis
blocks.body:
In the situation of Lemma \ref{lem:adapted-frame-cons-normal}, the family
$\bigl(N_x, dF_x E_1, \ldots, dF_x E_n\bigr)$ is a basis of $T_{F(x)}\tilde M$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.consNormalBasis
lean.code:
def consNormalBasis (h : IsMetricPreserving g g' F) (hN : IsUnitNormalAt I g' F N x)
    (hdim : finrank ℝ E' = finrank ℝ E + 1)
    (hon : ∀ i j, g.inner x (b i) (b j) = if i = j then 1 else 0) :
    Basis (Fin (finrank ℝ E')) ℝ (TangentSpace I' (F x)) :=
  haveI : FiniteDimensional ℝ (TangentSpace I' (F x)) := inferInstanceAs (FiniteDimensional ℝ E')
  Basis.mk (linearIndependent_consNormal (hdim := hdim) h hN hon)
    ((linearIndependent_consNormal (hdim := hdim) h hN hon).span_eq_top_of_card_eq_finrank'
      (by simp only [Fintype.card_fin]; rfl)).ge

omit [FiniteDimensional ℝ E] in
@[simp]

deps.title: The Adapted Frame Along a Hypersurface
deps.label: lem:adapted-frame-cons-normal

blocks.tex:
By Lemma \ref{lem:adapted-frame-cons-normal} the family is orthonormal, hence linearly
independent. It has $n+1$ members and $\dim T_{F(x)}\tilde M = n+1$, so a linearly independent
family of $n+1$ vectors in an $(n+1)$-dimensional space spans, hence is a basis.
For Riemannian hypersurfaces, we have the following important characterization of the volume form on the hypersurface in terms of that of the ambient manifold.

blocks.label: prop:hypersurface-volume-form-formula
blocks.title: Equation (2.17)

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.volumeForm_eq_normalRestrict
lean.code:
theorem volumeForm_eq_normalRestrict (h : IsMetricPreserving g g' F)
    (hN : IsUnitNormalAt I g' F N x) (hdim : finrank ℝ E' = finrank ℝ E + 1)
    (hY : IsLocalFrameOn I E ∞ Y u)
    (hon : ∀ y ∈ u, ∀ i j, g.inner y (Y i y) (Y j y) = if i = j then 1 else 0) (hx : x ∈ u)
    (hor : (hY.toBasisAt hx).orientation = o x)
    (hor' : (consNormalBasis (b := fun i => Y i x) h hN hdim (hon x hx)).orientation =
      o' (F x)) :
    normalRestrict I g' o' F hdim N x = g.volumeForm o x := by
  letI : Bundle.RiemannianBundle (TangentSpace I' : M' → Type _) := ⟨g'.toRiemannianMetric⟩
  haveI : Fact (finrank ℝ (TangentSpace I' (F x)) = finrank ℝ E') := ⟨rfl⟩
  refine RiemannianMetric.volumeForm_unique g o hY hon hx hor _ ?_
  rw [normalRestrict_apply]
  have hon' : Orthonormal ℝ ⇑(consNormalBasis (b := fun i => Y i x) h hN hdim (hon x hx)) := by
    rw [coe_consNormalBasis]
    exact orthonormal_consNormal (hdim := hdim) h hN (hon x hx)
  have hval := volumeFormL_apply_eq_one (o' (F x))
    ((consNormalBasis (b := fun i => Y i x) h hN hdim (hon x hx)).toOrthonormalBasis hon')
    (by rwa [Basis.toBasis_toOrthonormalBasis])
  simpa only [Basis.coe_toOrthonormalBasis, coe_consNormalBasis] using hval

/-! ## The orientation induced on a hypersurface by a unit normal

The orientation hypothesis `hor'` of `volumeForm_eq_normalRestrict` is not decorative: this
section constructs the orientation of `M` that satisfies it, so that (2.17) has content on every
hypersurface carrying a unit normal.  This is the "if" half of Lee's orientability criterion,
minus smoothness.

Mathlib has no orientation of a subspace, and no orientation induced on a hyperplane by a
transverse vector, so the construction is built from scratch.  It costs nothing, because
`Orientation ℝ V (Fin n)` *is* a ray of `n`-forms and `(N ⌟ dV_g̃)|_M` is already a nonvanishing
`n`-form on `T_x M`: the induced orientation is simply its ray. -/

deps.title: The Adapted Frame Along a Hypersurface
deps.label: lem:adapted-frame-cons-normal

deps.title: The Adapted Frame is an Ambient Basis
deps.label: lem:adapted-frame-ambient-basis

blocks.tex:
Evaluate the right-hand side on the frame $(E_1, \ldots, E_n)$: by definition of interior
multiplication and of the restriction, it equals
\[
  dV_{\tilde g}|_{F(x)}\bigl(N_x, dF_x E_1, \ldots, dF_x E_n\bigr).
\]
By Lemmas \ref{lem:adapted-frame-cons-normal} and \ref{lem:adapted-frame-ambient-basis} the
family $\bigl(N_x, dF_x E_1, \ldots, dF_x E_n\bigr)$ is an orthonormal basis of
$T_{F(x)}\tilde M$, and it is positively oriented by hypothesis. So by Proposition
\ref{prop:volume-form-value-one} the value is $1$.

Thus the right-hand side is an $n$-form on $T_x M$ taking the value $1$ on the oriented
orthonormal frame $(E_i|_x)$. By Proposition \ref{prop:volume-form-uniqueness} the only such
$n$-form is $dV_g|_x$, which is (2.17).

The orientation hypothesis of Proposition \ref{prop:hypersurface-volume-form-formula} is not an
extra assumption on $M$: a unit normal produces, with no choices, the orientation that satisfies
it. The construction rests on the observation that an orientation of an $n$-dimensional space
\emph{is} a ray of nonzero $n$-forms, so a nonvanishing $n$-form orients the space outright.

blocks.label: lem:nonvanishing-top-form-orientation
blocks.title: A Nonvanishing Top Form Orients the Space
blocks.body:
Let $V$ be an $n$-dimensional real vector space and let $\omega$ be a nonzero alternating
$n$-form on $V$. Then $\omega$ determines an orientation of $V$, namely the ray of $\omega$ in
the one-dimensional space of $n$-forms, and a basis $(b_1, \ldots, b_n)$ of $V$ is positively
oriented for it if and only if $\omega(b_1, \ldots, b_n) > 0$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.rayOfNeZero_eq_basis_orientation_iff
lean.code:
theorem rayOfNeZero_eq_basis_orientation_iff (b : Basis (Fin n) ℝ V)
    (w : V [⋀^Fin n]→ₗ[ℝ] ℝ) (hw : w ≠ 0) :
    rayOfNeZero ℝ w hw = b.orientation ↔ 0 < w b := by
  have hcoef : w b ≠ 0 := fun hz => hw (by rw [w.eq_smul_basis_det b, hz, zero_smul])
  rw [Module.Basis.orientation, ray_eq_iff]
  conv_lhs => rw [w.eq_smul_basis_det b]
  exact sameRay_smul_left_iff_of_ne b.det_ne_zero hcoef

end Ray

section VolumeFormNegOne

variable {V : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup V] [InnerProductSpace ℝ V]
  {n : ℕ} [Fact (finrank ℝ V = n)]

blocks.tex:
Every $n$-form on $V$ is a scalar multiple of the determinant form $\det{}_b$ of $b$; applying
the relation $\omega = c \cdot \det_b$ to $b$ itself and using $\det_b(b) = 1$ identifies the
scalar as $c = \omega(b)$, and $c \ne 0$ because $\omega \ne 0$. The orientation determined by
$b$ is the ray of $\det_b$, so the ray of $\omega = \omega(b) \cdot \det_b$ equals it exactly
when the scalar $\omega(b)$ is positive.

blocks.label: def:induced-orientation
blocks.title: The Orientation Induced by a Unit Normal
blocks.body:
In the situation of Proposition \ref{prop:hypersurface-volume-form-formula}, let $N$ be a unit
normal at every point of $M$. The \emph{induced orientation} of $M$ assigns to each $x \in M$ the
orientation of $T_x M$ determined, by Lemma \ref{lem:nonvanishing-top-form-orientation}, by the
$n$-form $\bigl((N \lrcorner dV_{\tilde g})|_M\bigr)_x$.

This is well defined: the form does not vanish, since its value on a $g$-orthonormal basis
$(E_i)$ of $T_x M$ is $dV_{\tilde g}(N_x, dF E_1, \ldots, dF E_n) = \pm 1$, the adapted family
being an orthonormal basis of $T_{F(x)}\tilde M$ by Lemma
\ref{lem:adapted-frame-ambient-basis}.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.inducedOrientation
lean.code:
def inducedOrientation (g : RiemannianMetric I M) (g' : RiemannianMetric I' M')
    (o' : PointwiseOrientation I' M') (F : M → M')
    (hdim : finrank ℝ E' = finrank ℝ E + 1) (N : (y : M) → TangentSpace I' (F y))
    (h : IsMetricPreserving g g' F) (hN : ∀ y, IsUnitNormalAt I g' F N y) :
    PointwiseOrientation I M := fun x =>
  rayOfNeZero ℝ (normalRestrict I g' o' F hdim N x).toAlternatingMap
    (normalRestrict_toAlternatingMap_ne_zero h (hN x) hdim)

deps.title: The Adapted Frame is an Ambient Basis
deps.label: lem:adapted-frame-ambient-basis

deps.title: A Nonvanishing Top Form Orients the Space
deps.label: lem:nonvanishing-top-form-orientation

blocks.label: lem:induced-orientation-characterization
blocks.title: The Induced Orientation is Lee's
blocks.body:
Let $(E_1, \ldots, E_n)$ be a $g$-orthonormal basis of $T_x M$. Then $(E_i)$ is positively
oriented for the induced orientation of $M$ if and only if the adapted basis
$\bigl(N_x, dF E_1, \ldots, dF E_n\bigr)$ is positively oriented in $\tilde M$.

That is, the induced orientation is exactly the one Lee describes.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.consNormalBasis_orientation_eq_iff
lean.code:
theorem consNormalBasis_orientation_eq_iff (g : RiemannianMetric I M)
    (h : IsMetricPreserving g g' F) (hN : ∀ y, IsUnitNormalAt I g' F N y)
    (hdim : finrank ℝ E' = finrank ℝ E + 1)
    (b : Basis (Fin (finrank ℝ E)) ℝ (TangentSpace I x))
    (hb : ∀ i j, g.inner x (b i) (b j) = if i = j then 1 else 0) :
    (consNormalBasis (b := ⇑b) h (hN x) hdim hb).orientation = o' (F x) ↔
      b.orientation = inducedOrientation g g' o' F hdim N h hN x := by
  letI : Bundle.RiemannianBundle (TangentSpace I' : M' → Type _) := ⟨g'.toRiemannianMetric⟩
  haveI : Fact (finrank ℝ (TangentSpace I' (F x)) = finrank ℝ E') := ⟨rfl⟩
  have hon' : Orthonormal ℝ ⇑(consNormalBasis (b := ⇑b) h (hN x) hdim hb) := by
    rw [coe_consNormalBasis]
    exact orthonormal_consNormal (hdim := hdim) h (hN x) hb
  have hcoe : ⇑((consNormalBasis (b := ⇑b) h (hN x) hdim hb).toOrthonormalBasis hon')
      = consNormal I F hdim N x ⇑b := by
    rw [Basis.coe_toOrthonormalBasis, coe_consNormalBasis]
  rw [inducedOrientation]
  conv_rhs => rw [eq_comm]
  rw [rayOfNeZero_eq_basis_orientation_iff]
  show _ ↔ 0 < (normalRestrict I g' o' F hdim N x) ⇑b
  rw [normalRestrict_apply]
  constructor
  · intro hpos
    have hval := volumeFormL_apply_eq_one (o' (F x))
      ((consNormalBasis (b := ⇑b) h (hN x) hdim hb).toOrthonormalBasis hon')
      (by rwa [Basis.toBasis_toOrthonormalBasis])
    rw [show ((consNormalBasis (b := ⇑b) h (hN x) hdim hb).toOrthonormalBasis hon' ·)
        = consNormal I F hdim N x ⇑b from hcoe] at hval
    rw [show g'.volumeForm o' (F x) (consNormal I F hdim N x ⇑b)
        = (o' (F x)).volumeFormL (consNormal I F hdim N x ⇑b) from rfl, hval]
    norm_num
  · intro hpos
    by_contra hne
    have hval := volumeFormL_apply_eq_neg_one (o' (F x))
      ((consNormalBasis (b := ⇑b) h (hN x) hdim hb).toOrthonormalBasis hon')
      (by rwa [Basis.toBasis_toOrthonormalBasis])
    rw [show ((consNormalBasis (b := ⇑b) h (hN x) hdim hb).toOrthonormalBasis hon' ·)
        = consNormal I F hdim N x ⇑b from hcoe] at hval
    rw [show g'.volumeForm o' (F x) (consNormal I F hdim N x ⇑b)
        = (o' (F x)).volumeFormL (consNormal I F hdim N x ⇑b) from rfl, hval] at hpos
    norm_num at hpos

/-! ### (2.17) with no orientation hypothesis -/

deps.title: The Orientation Induced by a Unit Normal
deps.label: def:induced-orientation

deps.title: The Adapted Frame is an Ambient Basis
deps.label: lem:adapted-frame-ambient-basis

deps.title: A Nonvanishing Top Form Orients the Space
deps.label: lem:nonvanishing-top-form-orientation

blocks.tex:
By Lemma \ref{lem:nonvanishing-top-form-orientation}, $(E_i)$ is positively oriented for the
induced orientation if and only if
\[
  \bigl((N \lrcorner dV_{\tilde g})|_M\bigr)_x(E_1, \ldots, E_n)
    = dV_{\tilde g}\bigl(N_x, dF E_1, \ldots, dF E_n\bigr) > 0.
\]
The adapted family is an orthonormal basis of $T_{F(x)}\tilde M$, so by Proposition
\ref{prop:volume-form-value-one} the right-hand side is $1$ if that basis is positively oriented
and $-1$ if it is not. So the value is positive exactly when the adapted basis is positively
oriented.

blocks.label: lem:top-form-eq-of-basis
blocks.title: Top Forms are Determined by One Basis
blocks.body:
Let $V$ be an $n$-dimensional real vector space and let $b$ be a basis of $V$. If two alternating
$n$-forms on $V$ agree on $b$, they are equal.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.eq_of_apply_basis
lean.code:
theorem eq_of_apply_basis {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℝ V] [TopologicalSpace V] {n : ℕ}
    (b : Basis (Fin n) ℝ V) (θ η : V [⋀^Fin n]→L[ℝ] ℝ) (hval : θ ⇑b = η ⇑b) : θ = η := by
  ext v
  have hθ := congrArg (fun f => f v) (θ.toAlternatingMap.eq_smul_basis_det b)
  have hη := congrArg (fun f => f v) (η.toAlternatingMap.eq_smul_basis_det b)
  simp only [AlternatingMap.smul_apply, smul_eq_mul] at hθ hη
  rw [show θ v = θ.toAlternatingMap v from rfl, hθ,
    show η v = η.toAlternatingMap v from rfl, hη]
  exact congrArg (fun c => c * b.det v) hval

blocks.tex:
Each $n$-form $\theta$ satisfies $\theta = \theta(b) \cdot \det_b$, so two forms agreeing on $b$
are the same multiple of $\det_b$.

blocks.label: prop:hypersurface-volume-form-induced
blocks.title: Equation (2.17) for the Induced Orientation
blocks.body:
Let $(\tilde M, \tilde g)$ be an oriented Riemannian $(n+1)$-manifold, let $F : M \to \tilde M$
present the $n$-manifold $M$ as a smooth immersion, let $g$ be a metric on $M$ that $F$
preserves, and let $N$ be a unit normal at every point of $M$. Then, for the induced orientation
of $M$,
\[
  dV_g = (N \lrcorner dV_{\tilde{g}})|_M
\]
at every point, with no compatibility hypothesis between the orientations of $M$ and $\tilde M$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.volumeForm_inducedOrientation_eq_normalRestrict
lean.code:
theorem volumeForm_inducedOrientation_eq_normalRestrict (g : RiemannianMetric I M)
    (h : IsMetricPreserving g g' F) (hN : ∀ y, IsUnitNormalAt I g' F N y)
    (hdim : finrank ℝ E' = finrank ℝ E + 1) (x : M) :
    g.volumeForm (inducedOrientation g g' o' F hdim N h hN) x =
      normalRestrict I g' o' F hdim N x := by
  classical
  letI : Bundle.RiemannianBundle (TangentSpace I' : M' → Type _) := ⟨g'.toRiemannianMetric⟩
  haveI : Fact (finrank ℝ (TangentSpace I' (F x)) = finrank ℝ E') := ⟨rfl⟩
  obtain ⟨b, hb⟩ := exists_orthonormalBasis_inner g x
  set o := inducedOrientation g g' o' F hdim N h hN with ho
  have hon' : Orthonormal ℝ ⇑(consNormalBasis (b := ⇑b) h (hN x) hdim hb) := by
    rw [coe_consNormalBasis]
    exact orthonormal_consNormal (hdim := hdim) h (hN x) hb
  have hcoe : ((consNormalBasis (b := ⇑b) h (hN x) hdim hb).toOrthonormalBasis hon' ·)
      = consNormal I F hdim N x ⇑b := by
    rw [Basis.coe_toOrthonormalBasis, coe_consNormalBasis]
  refine eq_of_apply_basis b _ _ ?_
  rw [normalRestrict_apply]
  rcases eq_or_ne (b.orientation) (o x) with hbo | hbo
  · -- `b` is positively oriented: both sides are `1`.
    rw [volumeForm_apply_basis_eq_one g o b hb hbo]
    have hval := volumeFormL_apply_eq_one (o' (F x))
      ((consNormalBasis (b := ⇑b) h (hN x) hdim hb).toOrthonormalBasis hon')
      (by rw [Basis.toBasis_toOrthonormalBasis]
          exact (consNormalBasis_orientation_eq_iff g h hN hdim b hb).mpr hbo)
    rw [hcoe] at hval
    exact hval.symm
  · -- `b` is negatively oriented: both sides are `-1`.
    rw [volumeForm_apply_basis_eq_neg_one g o b hb hbo]
    have hval := volumeFormL_apply_eq_neg_one (o' (F x))
      ((consNormalBasis (b := ⇑b) h (hN x) hdim hb).toOrthonormalBasis hon')
      (by rw [Basis.toBasis_toOrthonormalBasis]
          exact fun hc => hbo ((consNormalBasis_orientation_eq_iff g h hN hdim b hb).mp hc))
    rw [hcoe] at hval
    exact hval.symm

deps.title: Top Forms are Determined by One Basis
deps.label: lem:top-form-eq-of-basis

deps.title: The Orientation Induced by a Unit Normal
deps.label: def:induced-orientation

blocks.tex:
Fix $x \in M$ and choose a $g$-orthonormal basis $(E_i)$ of $T_x M$. By Lemma
\ref{lem:top-form-eq-of-basis} it suffices to see that both sides agree on $(E_i)$.

Suppose first that $(E_i)$ is positively oriented for the induced orientation. Then
$dV_g(E_1,\ldots,E_n) = 1$ by Proposition \ref{prop:volume-form-value-one}, and by Lemma
\ref{lem:induced-orientation-characterization} the adapted basis is positively oriented in
$\tilde M$, so $(N \lrcorner dV_{\tilde g})|_M$ also takes the value $1$ on $(E_i)$.

Otherwise $(E_i)$ is negatively oriented, and $dV_g(E_1,\ldots,E_n) = -1$; by Lemma
\ref{lem:induced-orientation-characterization} the adapted basis is then negatively oriented in
$\tilde M$, so $(N \lrcorner dV_{\tilde g})|_M$ takes the value $-1$ on $(E_i)$ as well. In both
cases the two forms agree on $(E_i)$, hence are equal.

blocks.label: lem:pullback-comp-section
blocks.title: Smoothness of a Pulled-Back Section
blocks.body:
Let $F : M \to \tilde M$ be a smooth map and let $Z$ be a smooth section of $T\tilde M$ defined
near $F(x_0)$. Then $x \mapsto Z_{F(x)}$ is a smooth section of the ambient tangent bundle
$F^* T\tilde M$ near $x_0$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.contMDiffAt_comp_section
lean.code:
theorem contMDiffAt_comp_section (f : C^n⟮IB', B'; IB, B⟯) {s : (y : B) → E y} {x₀ : B'}
    (hs : ContMDiffAt IB (IB.prod 𝓘(ℝ, F)) n (fun y => TotalSpace.mk' F y (s y)) (f x₀)) :
    ContMDiffAt IB' (IB'.prod 𝓘(ℝ, F)) n
      (fun x => TotalSpace.mk' F (E := (f : B' → B) *ᵖ E) x (s (f x))) x₀ := by
  rw [contMDiffAt_section]
  rw [contMDiffAt_section] at hs
  exact hs.comp x₀ f.contMDiff.contMDiffAt

end Comp

/-! ## Negating one member of a local frame -/

section NegFrame

variable
  {𝕜 : Type*} [NontriviallyNormedField 𝕜]
  {EB : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup EB] [NormedSpace 𝕜 EB]
  {HB : Type*} [TopologicalSpace HB] {IB : ModelWithCorners 𝕜 EB HB}
  {B : Type*} [TopologicalSpace B] [ChartedSpace HB B]
  {F : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup F] [NormedSpace 𝕜 F]
  {V : B → Type*} [TopologicalSpace (TotalSpace F V)]
  [∀ x, TopologicalSpace (V x)] [∀ x, AddCommGroup (V x)] [∀ x, Module 𝕜 (V x)]
  [FiberBundle F V] [VectorBundle 𝕜 F V]
  {n : ℕ∞ω} {ι : Type*} [DecidableEq ι] {Y : ι → (x : B) → V x} {u : Set B}

deps.title: The Ambient Tangent Bundle Along a Submanifold
deps.label: lem:ambient-tangent-bundle-riemannian

blocks.tex:
The fibre of $F^* T\tilde M$ at $x$ is $T_{F(x)}\tilde M$, and a local trivialization of
$F^* T\tilde M$ near $x_0$ is by definition a local trivialization of $T\tilde M$ near $F(x_0)$
precomposed with $F$. Read in these trivializations the section in question is $Z$, read in the
trivialization of $T\tilde M$, composed with $F$. Both are smooth, hence so is the composite.

blocks.label: def:smooth-normal-at
blocks.title: Smoothness of an Ambient Family at a Point
blocks.body:
Let $F : M \to \tilde M$ present $M$ as a smooth immersion in a Riemannian manifold
$(\tilde M, \tilde g)$, and let $N$ assign to each $x \in M$ a vector $N_x \in T_{F(x)}\tilde M$.
Then $N$ \emph{varies smoothly at} $x_0 \in M$ if it is a smooth section of the ambient tangent
bundle $F^* T\tilde M$ at $x_0$.

The notion is pointwise rather than global because the normal fields it is applied to are in
general only defined and smooth on a neighbourhood.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.IsSmoothNormalAt
lean.code:
def IsSmoothNormalAt (x₀ : M) : Prop :=
  ContMDiffAt I (I.prod 𝓘(ℝ, E')) ∞
    (fun x => TotalSpace.mk' E' (E := (f : M → M') *ᵖ (TangentSpace I' : M' → Type _)) x (N x)) x₀

variable (I f N) in

blocks.label: lem:frame-neg-member
blocks.title: Negating One Member of a Local Frame
blocks.body:
Let $(Y_i)_{i \in \iota}$ be a smooth local frame for a smooth vector bundle $V \to B$ over an
open set $u \subseteq B$, and fix an index $i_0 \in \iota$. Then the family obtained from $(Y_i)$
by replacing $Y_{i_0}$ with $-Y_{i_0}$ is again a smooth local frame for $V$ over $u$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.isLocalFrameOn_negMember
lean.code:
theorem isLocalFrameOn_negMember (hY : IsLocalFrameOn IB F n Y u) (i₀ : ι) :
    IsLocalFrameOn IB F n (negMember Y i₀) u where
  linearIndependent := fun {x} hx => by
    rw [negMember_apply]
    have hw : Function.update (fun i => Y i x) i₀ (-(Y i₀ x))
        = (Function.update (1 : ι → 𝕜ˣ) i₀ (-1)) • (fun i => Y i x) := by
      funext i
      rcases eq_or_ne i i₀ with rfl | h
      · simp
      · simp [h]
    rw [hw]
    exact (hY.linearIndependent hx).units_smul _
  generating := fun {x} hx => by
    refine le_trans (hY.generating hx) (Submodule.span_le.2 ?_)
    rintro _ ⟨i, rfl⟩
    rcases eq_or_ne i i₀ with rfl | h
    · have hmem : -(Y i x) ∈ Submodule.span 𝕜 (Set.range fun j => negMember Y i j x) :=
        Submodule.subset_span ⟨i, by simp [negMember]⟩
      simpa using Submodule.neg_mem _ hmem
    · exact Submodule.subset_span ⟨i, by simp [negMember, h]⟩
  contMDiffOn i := by
    rcases eq_or_ne i i₀ with rfl | h
    · have hi : negMember Y i i = -(Y i) := by simp [negMember]
      rw [hi]
      exact (hY.contMDiffOn i).neg_section
    · have hi : negMember Y i₀ i = Y i := by simp [negMember, h]
      rw [hi]
      exact hY.contMDiffOn i

end NegFrame

/-! ## The induced orientation is smooth -/

noncomputable section Hyper

variable
  {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [NormedSpace ℝ E] [FiniteDimensional ℝ E]
  {H : Type*} [TopologicalSpace H] {I : ModelWithCorners ℝ E H}
  {M : Type*} [TopologicalSpace M] [ChartedSpace H M] [IsManifold I ∞ M]
  {E' : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E'] [NormedSpace ℝ E'] [FiniteDimensional ℝ E']
  {H' : Type*} [TopologicalSpace H'] {I' : ModelWithCorners ℝ E' H'}
  {M' : Type*} [TopologicalSpace M'] [ChartedSpace H' M'] [IsManifold I' ∞ M']

variable {g : RiemannianMetric I M} {g' : RiemannianMetric I' M'} {f : C^∞⟮I, M; I', M'⟯}
  {o' : PointwiseOrientation I' M'} {hdim : finrank ℝ E' = finrank ℝ E + 1}
  {N : (y : M) → TangentSpace I' (f y)}

variable (I f N) in

blocks.tex:
The modified family is smooth because negation is a smooth bundle map. At each $x \in u$ it is
obtained from the basis $(Y_i|_x)$ of $V_x$ by scaling one member by the unit $-1$, so it is
linearly independent. Finally $-Y_{i_0}|_x$ and $Y_{i_0}|_x$ span the same line, so the modified
family spans the same subspace as $(Y_i|_x)$, namely all of $V_x$. Being independent and
spanning at every $x \in u$, it is a local frame.

blocks.label: lem:adapted-frame-smooth
blocks.title: The Adapted Frame Varies Smoothly
blocks.body:
Let $F : M \to \tilde M$ present the $n$-manifold $M$ as a smooth immersion in the
$(n+1)$-manifold $\tilde M$, let $N$ be a family of ambient vectors along $M$ that varies
smoothly, and let $(E_1, \ldots, E_n)$ be a smooth local frame for $TM$ over an open set $u$.
Then every member of the adapted family $\bigl(N, dF E_1, \ldots, dF E_n\bigr)$ is a smooth
section of $F^* T\tilde M$ over $u$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.contMDiffAt_consNormal
lean.code:
theorem contMDiffAt_consNormal {Y : Fin (finrank ℝ E) → (x : M) → TangentSpace I x} {u : Set M}
    (hY : IsLocalFrameOn I E ∞ Y u) (hu : IsOpen u) {x₀ : M} (hx₀ : x₀ ∈ u)
    (hN : IsSmoothNormalAt I f N x₀) (j : Fin (finrank ℝ E')) :
    ContMDiffAt I (I.prod 𝓘(ℝ, E')) ∞
      (fun x => TotalSpace.mk' E' (E := (f : M → M') *ᵖ (TangentSpace I' : M' → Type _)) x
        (consNormal I (f : M → M') hdim N x (Y · x) j)) x₀ := by
  simp only [consNormal]
  rcases Fin.eq_zero_or_eq_succ (finCongr hdim j) with h | ⟨i, h⟩
  · rw [h]
    simpa using hN
  · rw [h]
    simp only [Fin.cons_succ]
    exact contMDiffAt_pushforward (f := f) (X := Y i) (hY.contMDiffAt hu hx₀ i)

omit [FiniteDimensional ℝ E] in

blocks.tex:
The zeroth member is $N$, which is a smooth section by hypothesis. Each remaining member is a
pushforward $dF(E_i)$ of a smooth vector field, hence a smooth section of $F^* T\tilde M$ by
Lemma \ref{lem:pushforward-smooth}.

blocks.label: lem:normal-restrict-frame-smooth
blocks.title: Smoothness of the Value on a Frame
blocks.body:
Let $(\tilde M, \tilde g)$ be an oriented Riemannian $(n+1)$-manifold, let $F : M \to \tilde M$
present the $n$-manifold $M$ as a smooth immersion, let $N$ be a family of ambient vectors along
$M$ that varies smoothly, and let $(E_1, \ldots, E_n)$ be a smooth local frame for $TM$ over an
open set $u$. Then
\[
  x \longmapsto \bigl((N \lrcorner dV_{\tilde g})|_M\bigr)_x(E_1|_x, \ldots, E_n|_x)
    = dV_{\tilde g}|_{F(x)}\bigl(N_x, dF_x E_1|_x, \ldots, dF_x E_n|_x\bigr)
\]
is a smooth real-valued function on $u$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.contMDiffAt_normalRestrict_frame
lean.code:
theorem contMDiffAt_normalRestrict_frame (ho' : IsSmoothOrientation o')
    {Y : Fin (finrank ℝ E) → (x : M) → TangentSpace I x} {u : Set M}
    (hY : IsLocalFrameOn I E ∞ Y u) (hu : IsOpen u) {x₀ : M} (hx₀ : x₀ ∈ u)
    (hN : ∀ x ∈ u, IsSmoothNormalAt I f N x) :
    ContMDiffAt I 𝓘(ℝ, ℝ) ∞
      (fun x => normalRestrict I g' o' (f : M → M') hdim N x (Y · x)) x₀ := by
  classical
  letI : Bundle.RiemannianBundle ((f : M → M') *ᵖ (TangentSpace I' : M' → Type _)) :=
    ⟨(g'.pullback f).toRiemannianMetric⟩
  obtain ⟨w, Z, hwopen, hfx₀, hZ, hZon, hZor⟩ := exists_orientedOrthonormalFrame_nhds g' ho' (f x₀)
  set v : Set M := u ∩ (f : M → M') ⁻¹' w with hv
  have hvopen : IsOpen v := hu.inter (hwopen.preimage f.contMDiff.continuous)
  have hx₀v : x₀ ∈ v := ⟨hx₀, hfx₀⟩
  refine ContMDiffOn.contMDiffAt ?_ (hvopen.mem_nhds hx₀v)
  -- On `v`, Lee's determinant formula expresses the value against the ambient oriented frame.
  have hrepr : ∀ x ∈ v, normalRestrict I g' o' (f : M → M') hdim N x (Y · x)
      = (Matrix.of fun i j =>
          g'.inner (f x) (Z i (f x)) (consNormal I (f : M → M') hdim N x (Y · x) j)).det := by
    intro x hx
    rw [normalRestrict_apply]
    exact g'.volumeForm_apply_eq_det o' hZ hZon hx.2 (hZor _ hx.2) _
  refine ContMDiffOn.congr ?_ hrepr
  intro x hx
  have hentry : ∀ i j, ContMDiffWithinAt I 𝓘(ℝ, ℝ) ∞
      (fun y => g'.inner (f y) (Z i (f y)) (consNormal I (f : M → M') hdim N y (Y · y) j)) v x := by
    intro i j
    have h1 : ContMDiffAt I (I.prod 𝓘(ℝ, E')) ∞
        (fun y => TotalSpace.mk' E' (E := (f : M → M') *ᵖ (TangentSpace I' : M' → Type _)) y
          (Z i (f y))) x :=
      contMDiffAt_comp_section f (hZ.contMDiffAt hwopen hx.2 i)
    have h2 := contMDiffAt_consNormal (hdim := hdim) hY hu hx.1 (hN x hx.1) j
    exact ContMDiffWithinAt.inner_bundle (IB := I) (F := E')
      (E := ((f : M → M') *ᵖ (TangentSpace I' : M' → Type _))) (b := fun y => y) (IM := I)
      h1.contMDiffWithinAt h2.contMDiffWithinAt
  simp only [Matrix.det_apply']
  refine ContMDiffWithinAt.sum fun σ _ => ?_
  exact contMDiffWithinAt_const.mul (ContMDiffWithinAt.prod fun i _ => hentry (σ i) i)

omit [FiniteDimensional ℝ E] in

deps.title: Smoothness of an Ambient Family at a Point
deps.label: def:smooth-normal-at

deps.title: The Ambient Tangent Bundle Along a Submanifold
deps.label: lem:ambient-tangent-bundle-riemannian

blocks.tex:
Smoothness is local, so fix $x_0 \in u$. By Proposition \ref{prop:oriented-orthonormal-frames}
there is an open neighbourhood $w$ of $F(x_0)$ in $\tilde M$ carrying a smooth local frame
$(Z_0, \ldots, Z_n)$ for $T\tilde M$ that is orthonormal and positively oriented at every point
of $w$. Put $v = u \cap F^{-1}(w)$, an open neighbourhood of $x_0$.

For $x \in v$ the frame $(Z_a)$ is orthonormal and positively oriented at $F(x)$, so Proposition
\ref{prop:volume-form-determinant} gives, for any $v_0, \ldots, v_n \in T_{F(x)}\tilde M$,
\[
  dV_{\tilde g}|_{F(x)}(v_0, \ldots, v_n)
    = \det\bigl[\tilde g_{F(x)}(Z_a|_{F(x)}, v_b)\bigr]_{a,b}.
\]
Applying this to the adapted family $(W_0, \ldots, W_n) = \bigl(N, dF E_1, \ldots, dF E_n\bigr)$,
the function in question equals, on $v$,
\[
  x \longmapsto \det\bigl[\tilde g_{F(x)}\bigl(Z_a|_{F(x)}, W_b(x)\bigr)\bigr]_{a,b}.
\]
Each $x \mapsto Z_a|_{F(x)}$ is a smooth section of $F^* T\tilde M$ by Lemma
\ref{lem:pullback-comp-section}, and each $W_b$ is one by Lemma \ref{lem:adapted-frame-smooth}.
The metric of $F^* T\tilde M$ varies smoothly by Lemma
\ref{lem:ambient-tangent-bundle-riemannian}, so each matrix entry is a smooth function of
$x \in v$. A determinant is a polynomial in its entries, so the displayed function is smooth on
$v$, and in particular at $x_0$.

blocks.label: lem:normal-restrict-neg-member
blocks.title: Negating a Frame Member Negates the Value
blocks.body:
In the situation of Proposition \ref{prop:hypersurface-volume-form-formula}, let
$(E_1, \ldots, E_n)$ be a family of vectors in $T_x M$ and let $i_0$ be an index. Then
\[
  \bigl((N \lrcorner dV_{\tilde g})|_M\bigr)_x(E_1, \ldots, -E_{i_0}, \ldots, E_n)
    = - \bigl((N \lrcorner dV_{\tilde g})|_M\bigr)_x(E_1, \ldots, E_{i_0}, \ldots, E_n).
\]

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.normalRestrict_negMember
lean.code:
theorem normalRestrict_negMember
    {Y : Fin (finrank ℝ E) → (x : M) → TangentSpace I x} (i₀ : Fin (finrank ℝ E)) (x : M) :
    normalRestrict I g' o' (f : M → M') hdim N x (negMember Y i₀ · x)
      = - normalRestrict I g' o' (f : M → M') hdim N x (Y · x) := by
  classical
  rw [negMember_apply]
  show (normalRestrict I g' o' (f : M → M') hdim N x).toAlternatingMap
      (Function.update (fun i => Y i x) i₀ (-(Y i₀ x))) = _
  rw [AlternatingMap.map_update_neg, Function.update_eq_self]
  rfl

blocks.tex:
$\bigl((N \lrcorner dV_{\tilde g})|_M\bigr)_x$ is an alternating $n$-form on $T_x M$, hence
linear in each argument separately.

blocks.label: prop:hypersurface-orientable-of-normal
blocks.title: The Induced Orientation is Smooth
blocks.body:
Let $(\tilde M, \tilde g)$ be an oriented Riemannian $(n+1)$-manifold with $n \geq 1$, let
$F : M \to \tilde M$ present the $n$-manifold $M$ as a smooth immersion, let $g$ be a metric on
$M$ that $F$ preserves, and let $N$ be a smooth global unit normal field for $M$. Then the
induced orientation of $M$ is smooth. In particular, $M$ is orientable.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.isSmoothOrientation_inducedOrientation
lean.code:
theorem isSmoothOrientation_inducedOrientation (hpos : 0 < finrank ℝ E)
    (h : IsMetricPreserving g g' (f : M → M')) (hN : ∀ y, IsUnitNormalAt I g' (f : M → M') N y)
    (hNsmooth : IsSmoothNormalField I f N) (ho' : IsSmoothOrientation o') :
    IsSmoothOrientation (inducedOrientation g g' o' (f : M → M') hdim N h hN) := by
  classical
  -- a frame realizes the induced orientation exactly where the form is positive on it
  have hchar : ∀ {u' : Set M} {Z : Fin (finrank ℝ E) → (x : M) → TangentSpace I x}
      (hZ : IsLocalFrameOn I E ∞ Z u') {x : M} (hx : x ∈ u'),
      ((hZ.toBasisAt hx).orientation = inducedOrientation g g' o' (f : M → M') hdim N h hN x
        ↔ 0 < normalRestrict I g' o' (f : M → M') hdim N x (Z · x)) := by
    intro u' Z hZ x hx
    have hcoe : ⇑(hZ.toBasisAt hx) = fun i => Z i x := funext fun i => hZ.toBasisAt_coe hx i
    rw [inducedOrientation, eq_comm, rayOfNeZero_eq_basis_orientation_iff, hcoe]
    exact Iff.rfl
  intro p
  obtain ⟨u, Y, huopen, hpu, hY, hYon⟩ := exists_orthonormalFrame_nhds g p
  set s : M → ℝ := fun x => normalRestrict I g' o' (f : M → M') hdim N x (Y · x) with hsdef
  have hcont : ContinuousOn s u :=
    (contMDiffOn_normalRestrict_frame (hdim := hdim) ho' hY huopen
      (fun x _ => hNsmooth x)).continuousOn
  -- `(N ⌟ dV_g̃)|_M` is a nonzero top form, so it does not vanish on a basis
  have hne : ∀ x ∈ u, s x ≠ 0 := by
    intro x hx
    have h1 := normalRestrict_toAlternatingMap_ne_zero (o' := o') h (hN x) hdim
    have h2 := (AlternatingMap.map_basis_ne_zero_iff (hY.toBasisAt hx)
      (normalRestrict I g' o' (f : M → M') hdim N x).toAlternatingMap).mpr h1
    have hcoe : ⇑(hY.toBasisAt hx) = fun i => Y i x := funext fun i => hY.toBasisAt_coe hx i
    rw [hcoe] at h2
    exact h2
  rcases lt_or_gt_of_ne (hne p hpu) with hlt | hgt
  · -- the frame is negatively oriented near `p`: negate one member
    refine ⟨u ∩ s ⁻¹' (Set.Iio 0), negMember Y ⟨0, hpos⟩,
