By Lemma \ref{lem:pseudo-riemannian-submanifold-induced-tensor} this $g$ is induced by $\tilde{g}$ along $F$, so $F$ presents $M$ as a pseudo-Riemannian submanifold. Proposition \ref{prop:pseudo-riemannian-hypersurface-signature} applied to $g$ then gives signature $(r-1,s)$ in the positive case and $(r,s-1)$ in the negative one.

Every hypothesis of this corollary is one a reader has in hand: an ambient metric, a codimension-one immersion, and the sign of the normal vectors. Proposition \ref{prop:pseudo-riemannian-hypersurface-signature} takes the induced metric $g$ as given, and Definition \ref{def:pseudo-induced-metric} cannot produce it without nondegeneracy; for an indefinite $\tilde{g}$ that nondegeneracy is available from no source other than the non-nullity of the normals.

If $(\bar{M}, \bar{g})$ is a pseudo-Riemannian manifold and $f \in C^\infty(M)$, then the \emph{gradient} of $f$ is defined as the smooth vector field $\text{grad} f = (df)^\#$ just as in the Riemannian case.

The gradient of the preceding paragraph makes sense on any pseudo-Riemannian manifold, and its fibrewise construction is the following. On a vector space carrying a nondegenerate symmetric bilinear form there is no Riesz representation to appeal to, since the form need not be positive definite; nondegeneracy alone suffices.

blocks.label: def:bilinear-gradient
blocks.title: The gradient of a functional relative to a scalar product
blocks.body: Let $V$ be a finite-dimensional real vector space, let $B$ be a nondegenerate symmetric bilinear form on $V$, and let $a \in V^*$. The \emph{$B$-gradient} $a^\sharp$ of $a$ is the unique $v \in V$ with $B(v, w) = a(w)$ for all $w \in V$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.bilinGrad
lean.code:
noncomputable def bilinGrad (hnd : B.Nondegenerate) (a : Module.Dual ℝ V) : V :=
  (B.toDual hnd).symm a

blocks.tex: Consider the linear map $\Phi : V \to V^*$, $\Phi(v) = B(v,\cdot)$. Its kernel is $\{v : B(v,w) = 0 \text{ for all } w\}$, which is trivial exactly because $B$ is nondegenerate, so $\Phi$ is injective. Since $\dim V^* = \dim V < \infty$, $\Phi$ is therefore bijective, and $a^\sharp := \Phi^{-1}(a)$ is the unique vector with $B(a^\sharp, w) = a(w)$ for all $w$.

blocks.label: lem:bilinear-gradient-orthogonal-ker
blocks.title: The $B$-gradient is orthogonal to the kernel of the functional
blocks.body: With $V$, $B$ and $a$ as in Definition \ref{def:bilinear-gradient}, $a^\sharp \in (\ker a)^\perp$, where $\perp$ is taken with respect to $B$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.bilinGrad_mem_orthogonal_ker
lean.code:
theorem bilinGrad_mem_orthogonal_ker (hB : B.IsSymm) (hnd : B.Nondegenerate)
    (a : Module.Dual ℝ V) : bilinGrad B hnd a ∈ B.orthogonal (LinearMap.ker a) := by
  intro w hw
  show B w (bilinGrad B hnd a) = 0
  rw [hB.eq w (bilinGrad B hnd a), apply_bilinGrad B hnd a w]
  exact hw

end BilinGrad

section Criterion

variable (B : BilinForm ℝ V)

deps.title: The gradient of a functional relative to a scalar product
deps.label: def:bilinear-gradient

blocks.tex: Let $w \in \ker a$. By symmetry of $B$ and the defining property of $a^\sharp$, $B(w, a^\sharp) = B(a^\sharp, w) = a(w) = 0$. As $w$ was arbitrary, $a^\sharp \in (\ker a)^\perp$.

blocks.label: prop:embedding-criterion-pointwise
blocks.title: Signature drop on the kernel of a functional
blocks.body:
Let $V$ be a finite-dimensional real vector space, let $B$ be a nondegenerate symmetric bilinear form on $V$ of signature $(r,s)$, and let $a \in V^*$ be nonzero. If $B(a^\sharp, a^\sharp) > 0$ then $B|_{\ker a}$ has signature $(r-1, s)$; and if $B(a^\sharp, a^\sharp) < 0$ then $B|_{\ker a}$ has signature $(r, s-1)$.

This is the fibrewise content of Corollary \ref{cor:pseudo-riemannian-embedding-criterion}, with $V = T_p \bar{M}$, $B = \bar{g}_p$, $a = df_p$, $a^\sharp = \operatorname{grad} f|_p$ and $\ker a = T_p M$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.sigPos_sigNeg_restrict_ker_bilinGrad
lean.code:
theorem sigPos_sigNeg_restrict_ker_bilinGrad (hB : B.IsSymm) (hnd : B.Nondegenerate)
    {a : Module.Dual ℝ V} (ha : a ≠ 0) :
    (0 < B (bilinGrad B hnd a) (bilinGrad B hnd a) →
      sigPos (LinearMap.BilinMap.toQuadraticMap (B.restrict (LinearMap.ker a))) + 1
          = sigPos (LinearMap.BilinMap.toQuadraticMap B) ∧
        sigNeg (LinearMap.BilinMap.toQuadraticMap (B.restrict (LinearMap.ker a)))
          = sigNeg (LinearMap.BilinMap.toQuadraticMap B)) ∧
    (B (bilinGrad B hnd a) (bilinGrad B hnd a) < 0 →
      sigPos (LinearMap.BilinMap.toQuadraticMap (B.restrict (LinearMap.ker a)))
          = sigPos (LinearMap.BilinMap.toQuadraticMap B) ∧
        sigNeg (LinearMap.BilinMap.toQuadraticMap (B.restrict (LinearMap.ker a))) + 1
          = sigNeg (LinearMap.BilinMap.toQuadraticMap B)) :=
  sigPos_sigNeg_restrict_of_mem_orthogonal hB hnd
    (Module.Dual.finrank_ker_add_one_of_ne_zero ha)
    (bilinGrad_mem_orthogonal_ker B hB hnd a) (bilinGrad_ne_zero B hnd ha)

deps.title: The gradient of a functional relative to a scalar product
deps.label: def:bilinear-gradient

deps.title: The $B$-gradient is orthogonal to the kernel of the functional
deps.label: lem:bilinear-gradient-orthogonal-ker

deps.title: Signature of a Hypersurface's Tangent Space
deps.label: prop:hypersurface-signature-pointwise-hyperplane

blocks.tex:
Since $a 
eq 0$ it is surjective onto $\mathbb{R}$, so by rank--nullity $\dim \ker a + 1 = \dim V$; that is, $W = \ker a$ is a hyperplane. The vector $x = a^\sharp$ lies in $W^\perp$ by Lemma ef{lem:bilinear-gradient-orthogonal-ker}, and $x 
eq 0$: if $a^\sharp = 0$ then $a(w) = B(a^\sharp, w) = B(0,w) = 0$ for every $w$, contradicting $a 
eq 0$. Proposition ef{prop:hypersurface-signature-pointwise-hyperplane} applied to $W$ and $x$ now gives exactly the two claimed signature drops, according to the sign of $B(x,x) = B(a^\sharp, a^\sharp)$.

The signature computation above does not by itself say that $B|_{\ker a}$ is \emph{nondegenerate}, which is what "pseudo-Riemannian submanifold" asserts and what constructing the induced metric requires. It comes from the same source.

blocks.label: lem:embedding-criterion-nondegenerate-pointwise
blocks.title: Nondegeneracy on the kernel of a functional
blocks.body: Let $V$ be a finite-dimensional real vector space, let $B$ be a nondegenerate symmetric bilinear form on $V$, and let $a \in V^*$ be nonzero. If $B(a^\sharp, a^\sharp) \neq 0$ then $B|_{\ker a}$ is nondegenerate.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.nondegenerate_restrict_ker_bilinGrad
lean.code:
theorem nondegenerate_restrict_ker_bilinGrad (hB : B.IsSymm) (hnd : B.Nondegenerate)
    {a : Module.Dual ℝ V} (ha : a ≠ 0)
    (hnn : B (bilinGrad B hnd a) (bilinGrad B hnd a) ≠ 0) :
    (B.restrict (LinearMap.ker a)).Nondegenerate := by
  have heq : B.orthogonal (Submodule.span ℝ {bilinGrad B hnd a}) = LinearMap.ker a :=
    orthogonal_span_singleton_eq hB hnd
      (Module.Dual.finrank_ker_add_one_of_ne_zero ha)
      (bilinGrad_mem_orthogonal_ker B hB hnd a) (bilinGrad_ne_zero B hnd ha)
  rw [← heq]
  exact restrict_orthogonal_span_singleton_nondegenerate hB hnd hnn

deps.label: lem:bilinear-gradient-orthogonal-ker

deps.title: The Complement of a Non-Null Line is a Scalar Product Space
deps.label: lem:orthogonal-complement-non-null-line-nondegenerate

deps.title: A Hyperplane is the Complement of its Normal Line
deps.label: lem:hyperplane-eq-complement-normal-line

blocks.tex: Since $a \neq 0$, rank--nullity makes $\ker a$ a hyperplane, and $a^\sharp$ is a nonzero element of $(\ker a)^\perp$ by Lemma \ref{lem:bilinear-gradient-orthogonal-ker}. Hence $\ker a = \langle a^\sharp \rangle^{\perp}$ by Lemma \ref{lem:hyperplane-eq-complement-normal-line}. The hypothesis $B(a^\sharp, a^\sharp) \neq 0$ says the line $\langle a^\sharp\rangle$ is non-null, and the $B$-orthogonal complement of a non-null line is nondegenerate by Lemma \ref{lem:orthogonal-complement-non-null-line-nondegenerate}.

blocks.label: cor:pseudo-riemannian-embedding-criterion
blocks.title: cor:pseudo-riemannian-embedding-criterion
blocks.body: Suppose $(\bar{M}, \bar{g})$ is a pseudo-Riemannian manifold of signature $(r, s)$, $f \in C^\infty(\bar{M})$, and $M = f^{-1}(c)$ for some $c \in \mathbb{R}$. If $\bar{g}(\text{grad} f, \text{grad} f) > 0$ everywhere on $M$, then $M$ is an embedded pseudo-Riemannian submanifold of $\bar{M}$ of signature $(r-1, s)$; and if $\bar{g}(\text{grad} f, \text{grad} f) < 0$ everywhere on $M$, then $M$ is an embedded pseudo-Riemannian submanifold of $\bar{M}$ of signature $(r, s-1)$. In either case, $\text{grad} f$ is everywhere normal to $M$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.hasSignature_pseudoPullbackMetric_levelSet_of_pseudoGrad_neg
lean.code:
theorem hasSignature_pseudoPullbackMetric_levelSet_of_pseudoGrad_neg {r s : ℕ}
    (hsig : HasSignature g r s)
    (hneg : ∀ x : M, f x = c → g.form x (pseudoGrad g f x) (pseudoGrad g f x) < 0) :
    letI := levelSetChartedSpace hf n c (regular_of_pseudoGrad_neg c g hneg)
    haveI := isManifold_levelSet hf n c (regular_of_pseudoGrad_neg c g hneg)
    HasSignature (pseudoPullbackMetric g
      (levelSetInclMap hf n c (regular_of_pseudoGrad_neg c g hneg))
      (exists_pseudoPullbackForm_ne_zero g
        (levelSetInclMap hf n c (regular_of_pseudoGrad_neg c g hneg))
        (fun p => mfderiv_levelSet_val_injective hf n c (regular_of_pseudoGrad_neg c g hneg) p)
        (finrank_euclidean_add_one n)
        (fun p v hv hv0 => (forall_normal_neg_of_pseudoGrad_neg hf n c g
          (regular_of_pseudoGrad_neg c g hneg) hneg p v hv hv0).ne))) r (s - 1) := by
  letI := levelSetChartedSpace hf n c (regular_of_pseudoGrad_neg c g hneg)
  haveI := isManifold_levelSet hf n c (regular_of_pseudoGrad_neg c g hneg)
  exact hasSignature_pseudoPullbackMetric_of_forall_normal_neg g
    (levelSetInclMap hf n c (regular_of_pseudoGrad_neg c g hneg))
    (fun p => mfderiv_levelSet_val_injective hf n c (regular_of_pseudoGrad_neg c g hneg) p)
    (finrank_euclidean_add_one n) hsig
    (forall_normal_neg_of_pseudoGrad_neg hf n c g (regular_of_pseudoGrad_neg c g hneg) hneg)

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.hasSignature_pseudoPullbackMetric_levelSet_of_pseudoGrad_pos
lean.code:
theorem hasSignature_pseudoPullbackMetric_levelSet_of_pseudoGrad_pos {r s : ℕ}
    (hsig : HasSignature g r s)
    (hpos : ∀ x : M, f x = c → 0 < g.form x (pseudoGrad g f x) (pseudoGrad g f x)) :
    letI := levelSetChartedSpace hf n c (regular_of_pseudoGrad_pos c g hpos)
    haveI := isManifold_levelSet hf n c (regular_of_pseudoGrad_pos c g hpos)
    HasSignature (pseudoPullbackMetric g
      (levelSetInclMap hf n c (regular_of_pseudoGrad_pos c g hpos))
      (exists_pseudoPullbackForm_ne_zero g
        (levelSetInclMap hf n c (regular_of_pseudoGrad_pos c g hpos))
        (fun p => mfderiv_levelSet_val_injective hf n c (regular_of_pseudoGrad_pos c g hpos) p)
        (finrank_euclidean_add_one n)
        (fun p v hv hv0 => (forall_normal_pos_of_pseudoGrad_pos hf n c g
          (regular_of_pseudoGrad_pos c g hpos) hpos p v hv hv0).ne'))) (r - 1) s := by
  letI := levelSetChartedSpace hf n c (regular_of_pseudoGrad_pos c g hpos)
  haveI := isManifold_levelSet hf n c (regular_of_pseudoGrad_pos c g hpos)
  exact hasSignature_pseudoPullbackMetric_of_forall_normal_pos g
    (levelSetInclMap hf n c (regular_of_pseudoGrad_pos c g hpos))
    (fun p => mfderiv_levelSet_val_injective hf n c (regular_of_pseudoGrad_pos c g hpos) p)
    (finrank_euclidean_add_one n) hsig
    (forall_normal_pos_of_pseudoGrad_pos hf n c g (regular_of_pseudoGrad_pos c g hpos) hpos)

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.form_pseudoGrad_mfderiv_levelSet_val_eq_zero
lean.code:
theorem form_pseudoGrad_mfderiv_levelSet_val_eq_zero
    (hreg : ∀ x : M, f x = c → mfderiv I 𝓘(ℝ, ℝ) f x ≠ 0)
    (p : (f ⁻¹' {c} : Set M)) (w : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin n)) :
    letI := levelSetChartedSpace hf n c hreg
    g.form (↑p : M) (pseudoGrad g f ↑p)
        (mfderiv (𝓡 n) I ((↑) : (f ⁻¹' {c} : Set M) → M) p w) = 0 := by
  letI := levelSetChartedSpace hf n c hreg
  rw [form_pseudoGrad]
  exact mfderivReal_mfderiv_levelSet_val hf n c hreg p w

deps.title: The gradient of a functional relative to a scalar product
deps.label: def:bilinear-gradient

deps.title: The tangent space of a regular level set is the kernel of the differential
deps.label: prop:regular-level-set-tangent-kernel

deps.title: Signature drop on the kernel of a functional
deps.label: prop:embedding-criterion-pointwise

deps.title: A regular level set of a real-valued function is a smooth hypersurface
deps.label: prop:regular-level-set-hypersurface-codim-one

blocks.tex: The hypothesis $\bar{g}(\operatorname{grad} f, \operatorname{grad} f) \neq 0$ on $M$ forces $df_p \neq 0$ at each $p \in M$, so $c$ is a regular value of $f$; since $f$ is real-valued, the codimension-one case suffices, and Propositions \ref{prop:regular-level-set-hypersurface-codim-one} and \ref{prop:regular-level-set-tangent-kernel} show that $M = f^{-1}(c)$ is an embedded smooth hypersurface with $T_p M = \ker df_p$ for each $p \in M$. Fix such a $p$ and apply Proposition \ref{prop:embedding-criterion-pointwise} with $V = T_p \bar{M}$, $B = \bar{g}_p$ and $a = df_p$: since $\operatorname{grad} f|_p = (df_p)^\sharp$, the signature of $\bar{g}_p|_{T_p M}$ is $(r-1,s)$ when $\bar{g}_p(\operatorname{grad} f|_p, \operatorname{grad} f|_p) > 0$ and $(r,s-1)$ when it is negative. In particular $\bar{g}_p|_{T_p M}$ is nondegenerate at every $p$, so $M$ is a pseudo-Riemannian submanifold of the stated signature. That $\operatorname{grad} f$ is normal to $M$ is Lemma \ref{lem:bilinear-gradient-orthogonal-ker} applied at each $p$, since $T_p M = \ker df_p$.

As in the Riemannian case (Proposition \ref{prop:adapted-orthonormal-frames-immersed}), a submanifold is presented by a smooth immersion $F : M \to \bar{M}$, and the bundle in which an adapted frame lives is the ambient tangent bundle along $M$, that is, the pullback $F^* T\bar{M}$, whose fibre at $p$ is $T_{F(p)}\bar{M}$. The ambient metric restricts to each such fibre, making $F^* T\bar{M}$ a bundle of scalar product spaces.

blocks.label: lem:pullback-pseudo-metric-bundle
blocks.title: The Ambient Metric Along a Submanifold
blocks.body: Let $g$ be a smooth field of scalar products on a vector bundle $V \to B$ and let $F : B' \to B$ be smooth. Then $(F^* g)_x := g_{F(x)}$ is a smooth field of scalar products on the pullback bundle $F^* V \to B'$.

blocks.tex: Fibrewise there is nothing to prove: $(F^*V)_x$ \emph{is} $V_{F(x)}$, so $g_{F(x)}$ is already a bilinear form on it, and symmetry and nondegeneracy are those of $g$ at $F(x)$ verbatim. It remains to see that the form varies smoothly with $x \in B'$. Read in a trivialization of $F^*V$ at $x_0$, it is the field $g$ read in the trivialization of $V$ at $F(x_0)$ and precomposed with $F$; the first is smooth because $g$ is, the second because $F$ is, and a composite of smooth maps is smooth.

blocks.label: def:induced-tensor-along-map
blocks.title: The Tensor Induced Along a Map
blocks.body:
Let $F : M \to \bar{M}$ be smooth, let $\bar{g}$ be a pseudo-Riemannian metric on $\bar{M}$, and let $g$ be a pseudo-Riemannian metric on $M$. We say $g$ is \emph{induced by $\bar{g}$ along $F$}, and write $g = F^*\bar{g}$, if
\[
  g_p(v,w) \;=\; \bar{g}_{F(p)}\bigl(dF_p(v), dF_p(w)\bigr)
  \qquad\text{for all } p \in M \text{ and } v,w \in T_pM.
\]
That such a $g$ exists is precisely Lee's condition that $M$ be a pseudo-Riemannian submanifold: it says the pullback tensor $F^*\bar{g}$ is itself nondegenerate.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.IsPullbackAlong
lean.code:
def IsPullbackAlong (g : PseudoRiemannianMetric I M) (g' : PseudoRiemannianMetric I' M')
    (f : C^∞⟮I, M; I', M'⟯) : Prop :=
  ∀ (x : M) (v w : TangentSpace I x),
    g.form x v w = g'.form (f x) (mfderiv I I' f x v) (mfderiv I I' f x w)

omit [FiniteDimensional ℝ E] [FiniteDimensional ℝ E'] in

blocks.label: lem:pseudo-submanifold-immersion
blocks.title: A Pseudo-Riemannian Submanifold is Immersed
blocks.body: Let $F : M \to \bar{M}$ be smooth and suppose the pseudo-Riemannian metric $g$ on $M$ is induced by $\bar{g}$ along $F$. Then $dF_p$ is injective for every $p \in M$; that is, $F$ is an immersion.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.injective_mfderiv_of_isPullbackAlong
lean.code:
theorem injective_mfderiv_of_isPullbackAlong {g : PseudoRiemannianMetric I M}
    {g' : PseudoRiemannianMetric I' M'} {f : C^∞⟮I, M; I', M'⟯}
    (hg : IsPullbackAlong I I' g g' f) (x : M) : Function.Injective (mfderiv I I' f x) := by
  intro a b hab
  have hzero : mfderiv I I' f x (a - b) = 0 := by rw [map_sub, hab, sub_self]
  refine sub_eq_zero.mp (g.bilin_separatingLeft x (a - b) fun w => ?_)
  rw [ContMDiffPseudoMetric.bilin_apply, hg x (a - b) w, hzero]
  simp

omit [FiniteDimensional ℝ E'] in

deps.title: The Tensor Induced Along a Map
deps.label: def:induced-tensor-along-map

blocks.tex:
Let $v \in T_pM$ satisfy $dF_p(v) = 0$. For every $w \in T_pM$,
\[
  g_p(v,w) = \bar{g}_{F(p)}\bigl(dF_p(v), dF_p(w)\bigr) = \bar{g}_{F(p)}\bigl(0, dF_p(w)\bigr) = 0 .
\]
So $v$ is orthogonal to all of $T_pM$ with respect to $g_p$, and nondegeneracy of $g_p$ forces $v = 0$. Since $dF_p$ is linear with trivial kernel, it is injective.

This is the precise sense in which nondegeneracy of the induced tensor, and not immersivity, is the substantive hypothesis: it implies the latter. It does not come for free — the diagonal $\{\xi = \tau\}$ in $\mathbb{R}^{1,1}$ is an embedded line on which the induced tensor vanishes identically — whereas in the Riemannian case the two conditions coincide, by Lemma \ref{lem:pullback-immersion-metric}.

blocks.label: lem:riemannian-submanifold-induced-tensor
blocks.title: A Riemannian Submanifold is a Pseudo-Riemannian Submanifold
blocks.body: Let $F : M \to \bar{M}$ be a smooth immersion and let $\bar{g}$ be a Riemannian metric on $\bar{M}$. Then the induced metric $F^*\bar{g}$ of Definition \ref{def:induced-metric} is induced by $\bar{g}$ along $F$ in the sense of Definition \ref{def:induced-tensor-along-map}.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.isPullbackAlong_pullbackMetric
lean.code:
theorem isPullbackAlong_pullbackMetric (g' : RiemannianMetric I' M') (f : C^∞⟮I, M; I', M'⟯)
    (himm : ∀ p : M, Function.Injective (mfderiv I I' f p)) :
    IsPullbackAlong I I' (pullbackMetric g' f f.contMDiff himm).toPseudoRiemannianMetric
      g'.toPseudoRiemannianMetric f :=
  fun _ _ _ => rfl

deps.title: The Tensor Induced Along a Map
deps.label: def:induced-tensor-along-map

deps.title: The metric induced by an immersion
deps.label: def:induced-metric

blocks.tex: By Lemma \ref{lem:pullback-immersion-metric} the tensor $F^*\bar{g}$ is positive definite, hence nondegenerate, so it is a pseudo-Riemannian metric on $M$. Its defining formula is $(F^*\bar{g})_p(v,w) = \bar{g}_{F(p)}(dF_p v, dF_p w)$, which is the condition of Definition \ref{def:induced-tensor-along-map}.

For an indefinite ambient metric that route is closed, and the pullback must be built by hand. It is worth isolating first what pulling back actually uses, which is nothing beyond bilinearity --- and indeed nothing beyond \emph{linearity of the transporting maps}, which need not be differentials at all.

blocks.label: def:bilinear-comp-of-family
blocks.title: Transport of a Field of Bilinear Forms by a Family of Linear Maps
blocks.body:
Let $F : M \to \tilde{M}$ be a map, let $b$ assign to each $y \in \tilde{M}$ a bilinear form $b_y$ on $T_y\tilde{M}$, and let $A$ assign to each $p \in M$ a linear map $A_p : T_pM \to T_{F(p)}\tilde{M}$. The \emph{transport of $b$ by $A$} is the field of bilinear forms on $M$ defined by
\[
  (A^*b)_p(v,w) \;=\; b_{F(p)}\bigl(A_p v,\, A_p w\bigr), \qquad v,w \in T_pM .
\]
Each $(A^*b)_p$ is indeed bilinear, being the composite of the bilinear $b_{F(p)}$ with the linear map $A_p$ in each of its two arguments.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.bilinearCompOf
lean.code:
noncomputable def bilinearCompOf
    (b : ∀ y : M', TangentSpace I' y →L[ℝ] TangentSpace I' y →L[ℝ] ℝ) {F : M → M'}
    (A : ∀ x : M, TangentSpace I x →L[ℝ] TangentSpace I' (F x)) (p : M) :
    TangentSpace I p →L[ℝ] TangentSpace I p →L[ℝ] ℝ :=
  let A' : E →L[ℝ] E' := A p
  let B : E' →L[ℝ] E' →L[ℝ] ℝ := b (F p)
  (B.bilinearComp A' A' : E →L[ℝ] E →L[ℝ] ℝ)

omit [IsManifold I ∞ M] [IsManifold I' ∞ M'] in

blocks.label: lem:bilinear-comp-of-family-smooth
blocks.title: Transport by a Smooth Family of Linear Maps is Smooth
blocks.body: Let $b$ be a smooth field of bilinear forms on $T\tilde{M}$, let $p_0 \in M$, let $F : M \to \tilde{M}$ be smooth at $p_0$, and let $A_p : T_pM \to T_{F(p)}\tilde{M}$ be a family of linear maps whose representation in tangent coordinates is smooth at $p_0$. Then the section $p \mapsto (A^*b)_p$ is smooth at $p_0$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.contMDiffAt_bilinearCompOf
lean.code:
theorem contMDiffAt_bilinearCompOf
    (b : ∀ y : M', TangentSpace I' y →L[ℝ] TangentSpace I' y →L[ℝ] ℝ)
    (hb : ContMDiff I' (I'.prod 𝓘(ℝ, E' →L[ℝ] E' →L[ℝ] ℝ)) ∞
      (fun y ↦ (⟨y, b y⟩ :
        Bundle.TotalSpace (E' →L[ℝ] E' →L[ℝ] ℝ)
          (fun y ↦ TangentSpace I' y →L[ℝ] TangentSpace I' y →L[ℝ] ℝ))))
    {F : M → M'} {x₀ : M} (hF : ContMDiffAt I I' ∞ F x₀)
    (A : ∀ x : M, TangentSpace I x →L[ℝ] TangentSpace I' (F x))
    (hA : ContMDiffAt I 𝓘(ℝ, E →L[ℝ] E') ∞ (inTangentCoordinates I I' id F A x₀) x₀) :
    ContMDiffAt I (I.prod 𝓘(ℝ, E →L[ℝ] E →L[ℝ] ℝ)) ∞
      (fun x ↦ (⟨x, bilinearCompOf b A x⟩ :
        Bundle.TotalSpace (E →L[ℝ] E →L[ℝ] ℝ)
          (fun x ↦ TangentSpace I x →L[ℝ] TangentSpace I x →L[ℝ] ℝ))) x₀ := by
  rw [contMDiffAt_hom_bundle]
  refine ⟨contMDiffAt_id, ?_⟩
  set sT := trivializationAt E (TangentSpace I) x₀ with hsT
  set tT := trivializationAt E' (TangentSpace I') (F x₀) with htT
  have hx₀ : x₀ ∈ sT.baseSet := mem_baseSet_trivializationAt E (TangentSpace I) x₀
  have hfx₀ : F x₀ ∈ tT.baseSet := mem_baseSet_trivializationAt E' (TangentSpace I') (F x₀)
  set D : M → (E →L[ℝ] E') := inTangentCoordinates I I' id F A x₀ with hD
  set G : M' → (E' →L[ℝ] E' →L[ℝ] ℝ) := fun y =>
    ContinuousLinearMap.inCoordinates E' (TangentSpace I') (E' →L[ℝ] ℝ)
      (fun y => TangentSpace I' y →L[ℝ] ℝ) (F x₀) y (F x₀) y (b y) with hG
  have hGsmooth : ContMDiffAt I' 𝓘(ℝ, E' →L[ℝ] E' →L[ℝ] ℝ) ∞ G (F x₀) :=
    ((contMDiffAt_hom_bundle _).mp hb.contMDiffAt).2
  have hΨ : ContMDiffAt I 𝓘(ℝ, E →L[ℝ] E →L[ℝ] ℝ) ∞
      (fun x => ((D x).precomp ℝ).comp ((G (F x)).comp (D x))) x₀ := by
    have h1 : ContMDiffAt I 𝓘(ℝ, E →L[ℝ] E' →L[ℝ] ℝ) ∞
        (fun x => (G (F x)).comp (D x)) x₀ :=
      (hGsmooth.comp x₀ hF).clm_comp hA
    exact (ContMDiffAt.clm_precomp (F₃ := ℝ) hA).clm_comp h1
  refine hΨ.congr_of_eventuallyEq ?_
  have hUs : {x | x ∈ sT.baseSet} ∈ 𝓝 x₀ := sT.open_baseSet.mem_nhds hx₀
  have hUt : {x | F x ∈ tT.baseSet} ∈ 𝓝 x₀ :=
    hF.continuousAt (tT.open_baseSet.mem_nhds hfx₀)
  filter_upwards [hUs, hUt] with x hx hfx
  refine ContinuousLinearMap.ext fun a => ContinuousLinearMap.ext fun b' => ?_
  have hRHS : (((ContinuousLinearMap.precomp ℝ (D x)).comp ((G (F x)).comp (D x))) a) b'
      = G (F x) (D x a) (D x b') := rfl
  -- the target coordinate change undoes itself through `A`
  have hkey : ∀ u : E, tT.symm (F x) (D x u) = A x (sT.symm x u) := by
    intro u
    have hDu : D x u = tT.continuousLinearEquivAt ℝ (F x) hfx
        (A x ((sT.continuousLinearEquivAt ℝ x hx).symm u)) := by
      rw [hD]
      simp only [inTangentCoordinates, id_eq]
      rw [ContinuousLinearMap.inCoordinates_eq hx hfx]
      rfl
    have hcoeT : (tT.symm (F x) : E' → TangentSpace I' (F x))
        = ⇑(tT.continuousLinearEquivAt ℝ (F x) hfx).symm := by
      rw [Trivialization.symm_continuousLinearEquivAt_eq tT hfx]; rfl
    have hcoeS : (sT.symm x : E → TangentSpace I x)
        = ⇑(sT.continuousLinearEquivAt ℝ x hx).symm := by
      rw [Trivialization.symm_continuousLinearEquivAt_eq sT hx]; rfl
    rw [hDu, hcoeT, ContinuousLinearEquiv.symm_apply_apply, hcoeS]
  rw [hRHS, hG]
  have htrivM' :
      trivializationAt ℝ (Bundle.Trivial M' ℝ) (F x₀) = Bundle.Trivial.trivialization M' ℝ :=
    Bundle.Trivial.eq_trivialization M' ℝ _
  have htrivM : trivializationAt ℝ (Bundle.Trivial M ℝ) x₀ = Bundle.Trivial.trivialization M ℝ :=
  -- … (truncated)

deps.title: Transport of a Field of Bilinear Forms by a Family of Linear Maps
deps.label: def:bilinear-comp-of-family

blocks.tex:
Smoothness is local, so read the section $p \mapsto (A^*b)_p$ in tangent coordinates around $p_0$ and around $F(p_0)$. It becomes the map
\[
  p \;\longmapsto\; B(F(p))\bigl(D(p)\,\cdot,\, D(p)\,\cdot\bigr),
\]
where $D(p)$ is $A_p$ read in tangent coordinates and $B$ is $b$ read in coordinates, both with values in fixed model spaces. Now $p \mapsto D(p)$ is smooth at $p_0$ by hypothesis, and $p \mapsto B(F(p))$ is smooth at $p_0$ as a composite of the smooth section $b$ with $F$. Finally the map $(\beta, A) \mapsto \beta(A\,\cdot, A\,\cdot)$, from pairs of a form on the model space of $T\tilde{M}$ and an operator into it, to forms on the model space of $TM$, is continuous and polynomial in its arguments --- linear in $\beta$ and quadratic in $A$ --- hence smooth. The coordinate representation is the composite of these, so it is smooth at $p_0$.

The transporting family is left arbitrary because the two consumers need exactly that. Pulling back along $F$ is the case $A_p = dF_p$, immediately below; but the quotient metric of Theorem \ref{thm:group-action-riemannian-submersion} is the case where $A_p$ is the horizontal lift $L_{\sigma(p)}$ of a Riemannian submersion along a local section, and that family is \emph{not} the differential of any map.

blocks.label: def:pullback-form-of-family
blocks.title: The Pullback of a Field of Bilinear Forms
blocks.body:
Let $F : M \to \tilde{M}$ be a map and let $b$ assign to each $y \in \tilde{M}$ a bilinear form $b_y$ on $T_y\tilde{M}$. The \emph{pullback of $b$ along $F$} is the transport of $b$ by the family of differentials $A_p = dF_p$ in the sense of Definition \ref{def:bilinear-comp-of-family}:
\[
  (F^*b)_p(v,w) \;=\; b_{F(p)}\bigl(dF_p v,\, dF_p w\bigr), \qquad v,w \in T_pM .
\]
Each $(F^*b)_p$ is indeed bilinear, being the composite of the bilinear $b_{F(p)}$ with the linear map $dF_p$ in each of its two arguments.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.pullbackFormOf
lean.code:
noncomputable def pullbackFormOf
    (b : ∀ y : M', TangentSpace I' y →L[ℝ] TangentSpace I' y →L[ℝ] ℝ) (F : M → M') (p : M) :
    TangentSpace I p →L[ℝ] TangentSpace I p →L[ℝ] ℝ :=
  let A : E →L[ℝ] E' := mfderiv I I' F p
  let B : E' →L[ℝ] E' →L[ℝ] ℝ := b (F p)
  (B.bilinearComp A A : E →L[ℝ] E →L[ℝ] ℝ)

omit [IsManifold I ∞ M] [IsManifold I' ∞ M'] in

deps.title: Transport of a Field of Bilinear Forms by a Family of Linear Maps
deps.label: def:bilinear-comp-of-family

blocks.tex: Neither positivity, nor nondegeneracy, nor even symmetry of $b$ plays any part here, so both the Riemannian pullback of Definition \ref{def:induced-metric} and the indefinite one of Definition \ref{def:pseudo-pullback-form} below are instances of this definition, and both inherit its smoothness.

blocks.label: lem:pullback-form-of-family-smooth
blocks.title: The Pullback of a Smooth Field of Forms is Smooth
blocks.body: Let $F : M \to \tilde{M}$ be smooth and let $b$ be a smooth field of bilinear forms on $T\tilde{M}$, that is, a smooth section $y \mapsto b_y$ of the bundle of bilinear forms. Then $F^*b$ is a smooth field of bilinear forms on $TM$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.contMDiff_pullbackFormOf
lean.code:
theorem contMDiff_pullbackFormOf
    (b : ∀ y : M', TangentSpace I' y →L[ℝ] TangentSpace I' y →L[ℝ] ℝ)
    (hb : ContMDiff I' (I'.prod 𝓘(ℝ, E' →L[ℝ] E' →L[ℝ] ℝ)) ∞
      (fun y ↦ (⟨y, b y⟩ :
        Bundle.TotalSpace (E' →L[ℝ] E' →L[ℝ] ℝ)
          (fun y ↦ TangentSpace I' y →L[ℝ] TangentSpace I' y →L[ℝ] ℝ))))
    {F : M → M'} (hF : ContMDiff I I' ∞ F) :
    ContMDiff I (I.prod 𝓘(ℝ, E →L[ℝ] E →L[ℝ] ℝ)) ∞
      (fun x ↦ (⟨x, pullbackFormOf b F x⟩ :
        Bundle.TotalSpace (E →L[ℝ] E →L[ℝ] ℝ)
          (fun x ↦ TangentSpace I x →L[ℝ] TangentSpace I x →L[ℝ] ℝ))) := fun x₀ =>
  contMDiffAt_bilinearCompOf b hb hF.contMDiffAt (fun x => mfderiv I I' F x)
    (hF.contMDiffAt.mfderiv_const (by simp))

deps.title: Transport by a Smooth Family of Linear Maps is Smooth
deps.label: lem:bilinear-comp-of-family-smooth

deps.title: The Pullback of a Field of Bilinear Forms
deps.label: def:pullback-form-of-family

blocks.tex: This is the case $A_p = dF_p$ of Lemma \ref{lem:bilinear-comp-of-family-smooth}, applied at each $p_0 \in M$. Its hypothesis on $A$ holds: the differential of a smooth map, read in tangent coordinates, is smooth in the base point, which is the standard smoothness of $x \mapsto dF_x$ as a section of the bundle $\mathrm{Hom}(TM, F^*T\tilde{M})$.

blocks.label: def:pseudo-pullback-form
blocks.title: The Pullback of an Indefinite Metric
blocks.body:
Let $(\tilde{M},\tilde{g})$ be a pseudo-Riemannian manifold and let $F : M \to \tilde{M}$ be a map. The \emph{pullback tensor} $F^*\tilde{g}$ is the pullback of the field $y \mapsto \tilde{g}_y$ along $F$ in the sense of Definition \ref{def:pullback-form-of-family}:
\[
  (F^*\tilde{g})_p(v,w) \;=\; \tilde{g}_{F(p)}\bigl(dF_p v,\, dF_p w\bigr), \qquad v,w \in T_pM .
\]

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.pseudoPullbackForm
lean.code:
noncomputable def pseudoPullbackForm (g' : PseudoRiemannianMetric I' M') (F : M → M') (p : M) :
    TangentSpace I p →L[ℝ] TangentSpace I p →L[ℝ] ℝ :=
  pullbackFormOf (fun y => g'.form y) F p

omit [IsManifold I ∞ M] in

deps.title: The Pullback of a Field of Bilinear Forms
deps.label: def:pullback-form-of-family

blocks.tex: This is Lee's $\iota^*\tilde{g}$. It is a field of bilinear forms for every $F$ whatever; whether it is a \emph{metric} is a separate question, and the one Proposition \ref{prop:pseudo-riemannian-hypersurface-signature} answers.

blocks.label: lem:pseudo-pullback-form-symm
blocks.title: The Pullback Tensor is Symmetric
blocks.body: With $(\tilde{M},\tilde{g})$ and $F$ as in Definition \ref{def:pseudo-pullback-form}, $(F^*\tilde{g})_p(v,w) = (F^*\tilde{g})_p(w,v)$ for every $p \in M$ and $v,w \in T_pM$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.pseudoPullbackForm_symm
lean.code:
theorem pseudoPullbackForm_symm (g' : PseudoRiemannianMetric I' M') (F : M → M') (p : M)
    (v w : TangentSpace I p) :
    pseudoPullbackForm g' F p v w = pseudoPullbackForm g' F p w v :=
  g'.symm _ _ _

deps.title: The Pullback of an Indefinite Metric
deps.label: def:pseudo-pullback-form

blocks.tex:
Unwinding Definition \ref{def:pseudo-pullback-form} twice and using symmetry of $\tilde{g}_{F(p)}$ in the middle step,
\[
  (F^*\tilde{g})_p(v,w) = \tilde{g}_{F(p)}(dF_pv, dF_pw) = \tilde{g}_{F(p)}(dF_pw, dF_pv) = (F^*\tilde{g})_p(w,v) .
\]

blocks.label: lem:pseudo-pullback-form-smooth
blocks.title: The Pullback Tensor is Smooth
blocks.body: With $(\tilde{M},\tilde{g})$ as in Definition \ref{def:pseudo-pullback-form} and $F : M \to \tilde{M}$ smooth, the field $p \mapsto (F^*\tilde{g})_p$ is smooth.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.pseudoPullbackForm_contMDiff
lean.code:
theorem pseudoPullbackForm_contMDiff (g' : PseudoRiemannianMetric I' M') {F : M → M'}
    (hF : ContMDiff I I' ∞ F) :
    ContMDiff I (I.prod 𝓘(ℝ, E →L[ℝ] E →L[ℝ] ℝ)) ∞
      (fun x ↦ (⟨x, pseudoPullbackForm g' F x⟩ :
        Bundle.TotalSpace (E →L[ℝ] E →L[ℝ] ℝ)
          (fun x ↦ TangentSpace I x →L[ℝ] TangentSpace I x →L[ℝ] ℝ))) :=
  contMDiff_pullbackFormOf (fun y => g'.form y) g'.contMDiff hF

deps.title: The Pullback of an Indefinite Metric
deps.label: def:pseudo-pullback-form

deps.title: The Pullback of a Smooth Field of Forms is Smooth
deps.label: lem:pullback-form-of-family-smooth

blocks.tex: By condition (c) of Definition \ref{def:smooth-field-scalar-products}, $y \mapsto \tilde{g}_y$ is a smooth section of the bundle of bilinear forms on $T\tilde{M}$, so Lemma \ref{lem:pullback-form-of-family-smooth} applies to it verbatim. Nondegeneracy of $\tilde{g}$ plays no part.

blocks.label: def:pseudo-induced-metric
blocks.title: The Metric Induced by an Indefinite Ambient Metric
blocks.body: Let $(\tilde{M},\tilde{g})$ be a pseudo-Riemannian manifold and let $F : M \to \tilde{M}$ be smooth. Suppose the pullback tensor $F^*\tilde{g}$ is nondegenerate at every point, that is, for every $p \in M$ and every nonzero $v \in T_pM$ there is a $w \in T_pM$ with $(F^*\tilde{g})_p(v,w) \neq 0$. Then $F^*\tilde{g}$ is a pseudo-Riemannian metric on $M$, the \emph{metric induced by $F$}.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.pseudoPullbackMetric
lean.code:
noncomputable def pseudoPullbackMetric (g' : PseudoRiemannianMetric I' M')
    (F : C^∞⟮I, M; I', M'⟯)
    (hnd : ∀ (p : M) (v : TangentSpace I p), v ≠ 0 →
      ∃ w, pseudoPullbackForm g' F p v w ≠ 0) :
    PseudoRiemannianMetric I M where
  form p := pseudoPullbackForm g' F p
  symm p v w := pseudoPullbackForm_symm g' F p v w
  nondegenerate := hnd
  contMDiff := pseudoPullbackForm_contMDiff g' F.contMDiff

deps.title: The Pullback of an Indefinite Metric
deps.label: def:pseudo-pullback-form

blocks.tex: The three conditions of Definition \ref{def:smooth-field-scalar-products} for the tangent bundle of $M$ are met in turn: symmetry at each point is Lemma \ref{lem:pseudo-pullback-form-symm}, nondegeneracy at each point is the hypothesis, in exactly the witness form (b) requires, and smoothness of the section is Lemma \ref{lem:pseudo-pullback-form-smooth}. So $F^*\tilde{g}$ is a smooth field of scalar products on $TM$, which by Definition \ref{def:pseudo-riemannian-metric} is a pseudo-Riemannian metric.

Nondegeneracy is a genuine hypothesis here, and this is where the indefinite case parts company with the Riemannian one. By Lemma \ref{lem:pullback-immersion-metric}, positive definiteness of a pullback is \emph{equivalent} to $F$ being an immersion, so in the Riemannian case the immersion hypothesis alone builds the metric, as Definition \ref{def:induced-metric} does. Nondegeneracy of a pullback is not implied by immersion: a nondegenerate form can restrict to a degenerate one on a subspace, as the diagonal $\{\xi = \tau\}$ in $\mathbb{R}^{1,1}$ already shows. Deciding when it does not is the content of Proposition \ref{prop:pseudo-riemannian-hypersurface-signature}, and that is why the proposition exists at all.

blocks.label: lem:pseudo-riemannian-submanifold-induced-tensor
blocks.title: A Pseudo-Riemannian Pullback is an Induced Tensor
blocks.body: Let $(\tilde{M},\tilde{g})$, $F$ and the nondegeneracy hypothesis be as in Definition \ref{def:pseudo-induced-metric}. Then the induced metric $F^*\tilde{g}$ is induced by $\tilde{g}$ along $F$ in the sense of Definition \ref{def:induced-tensor-along-map}.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.isPullbackAlong_pseudoPullbackMetric
lean.code:
theorem isPullbackAlong_pseudoPullbackMetric (g' : PseudoRiemannianMetric I' M')
    (F : C^∞⟮I, M; I', M'⟯)
    (hnd : ∀ (p : M) (v : TangentSpace I p), v ≠ 0 → ∃ w, pseudoPullbackForm g' F p v w ≠ 0) :
    IsPullbackAlong I I' (pseudoPullbackMetric g' F hnd) g' F :=
  fun _ _ _ => rfl

deps.title: The Tensor Induced Along a Map
deps.label: def:induced-tensor-along-map

deps.title: The Pullback of an Indefinite Metric
deps.label: def:pseudo-pullback-form

deps.title: The Metric Induced by an Indefinite Ambient Metric
deps.label: def:pseudo-induced-metric

blocks.tex: By Definition \ref{def:pseudo-induced-metric} the tensor $g = F^*\tilde{g}$ is a pseudo-Riemannian metric on $M$, so it is eligible. The condition of Definition \ref{def:induced-tensor-along-map} is $g_p(v,w) = \tilde{g}_{F(p)}(dF_pv, dF_pw)$ for all $p$, $v$ and $w$, and for $g = F^*\tilde{g}$ that is the defining formula of Definition \ref{def:pseudo-pullback-form}.

This is the indefinite counterpart of Lemma \ref{lem:riemannian-submanifold-induced-tensor}, and it is what makes the hypothesis of Definition \ref{def:induced-tensor-along-map} --- assumed by Propositions \ref{prop:pseudo-riemannian-hypersurface-signature} and \ref{prop:pseudo-riemannian-adapted-frame} and by Corollary \ref{cor:normal-bundle-pseudo-riemannian-submanifold} alike --- available for a genuinely indefinite ambient metric. Lemma \ref{lem:riemannian-submanifold-induced-tensor} supplies that hypothesis only when $\tilde{g}$ is Riemannian, and in that case Proposition \ref{prop:pseudo-riemannian-hypersurface-signature} says no more than that a hypersurface of a Riemannian manifold is Riemannian.

blocks.label: lem:extend-nondegenerate-family
blocks.title: Extension of a Nondegenerate Family to a Nondegenerate Frame
blocks.body: Let $g$ be a smooth field of scalar products on a rank-$m$ vector bundle $V \to B$, let $u \subseteq B$ be open, let $x_0 \in u$, and let $X_1,\ldots,X_k$ be sections of $V$ that are nondegenerate on $u$. Then $k \leq m$, and there are an open set $v$ with $x_0 \in v \subseteq u$ and sections $Y_1,\ldots,Y_m$ of $V$, nondegenerate on $v$, with $Y_i = X_i$ on $v$ for every $i \leq k$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.exists_isLocalNondegenerateOn_extend
lean.code:
theorem exists_isLocalNondegenerateOn_extend (g : Bundle.ContMDiffPseudoMetric IB ∞ F V)
    {k : ℕ} {X : Fin k → (x : B) → V x} {u : Set B} {x₀ : B}
    (hX : IsLocalNondegenerateOn IB F ∞ g X u) (hu : IsOpen u) (hx₀ : x₀ ∈ u) :
    ∃ (v : Set B) (Y : Fin (finrank ℝ F) → (x : B) → V x) (hk : k ≤ finrank ℝ F),
      IsOpen v ∧ x₀ ∈ v ∧ v ⊆ u ∧ IsLocalNondegenerateOn IB F ∞ g Y v ∧
        ∀ (i : Fin k) (x : B), x ∈ v → Y (Fin.castLE hk i) x = X i x := by
  classical
  haveI : ∀ x : B, FiniteDimensional ℝ (V x) := fun x => finiteDimensional_fibre (F := F) x
  have hrank : finrank ℝ (V x₀) = finrank ℝ F := finrank_fibre (F := F) (V := V) x₀
  -- the family at `x₀` is a nondegenerate tuple, hence independent, hence not too long
  have h0 : IsNondegenerateTuple (g.bilin x₀) (fun i => X i x₀) := hX.nondegenerateTuple hx₀
  have hk : k ≤ finrank ℝ F := by
    simpa [hrank] using h0.linearIndependent.fintype_card_le_finrank
  -- complete it to a nondegenerate basis of the fibre (Lee's Lemma 2.62)
  obtain ⟨w, hw, hwX⟩ :=
    exists_isNondegenerateTuple_basis (g.bilin_isSymm x₀) (g.bilin_nondegenerate x₀)
      (fun i => X i x₀) h0 (by omega)
  -- transport the index type across `finrank ℝ (V x₀) = finrank ℝ F`
  set w' : Fin (finrank ℝ F) → V x₀ := fun j => w (Fin.cast hrank.symm j) with hw'def
  have hw' : IsNondegenerateTuple (g.bilin x₀) w' := by
    have hdet := (isNondegenerateTuple_iff_forall_det_gramMatrix_ne_zero (g.bilin x₀) w).mp hw
    refine (isNondegenerateTuple_iff_forall_det_gramMatrix_ne_zero (g.bilin x₀) w').mpr ?_
    intro j hj
    have hj' : j ≤ finrank ℝ (V x₀) := by omega
    have hcomp : (w' ∘ Fin.castLE hj) = w ∘ Fin.castLE hj' := by
      funext i; simp [hw'def]
    rw [hcomp]
    exact hdet j hj'
  -- spread the completion to smooth sections near `x₀`
  obtain ⟨v₁, W, hv₁open, hx₀v₁, hWsmooth, hWeq⟩ :=
    LeeLib.AppendixA.exists_contMDiffOn_section_eq_basis (F := F) (V := V) IB ∞ x₀
      (hw'.basis hrank.symm)
  have hWval : ∀ j, W j x₀ = w' j := fun j => by rw [hWeq j]; simp
  -- glue: keep `X` below the cut, take the spread completion above it
  set Y : Fin (finrank ℝ F) → (x : B) → V x :=
    fun j x => if h : (j : ℕ) < k then X ⟨(j : ℕ), h⟩ x else W j x with hYdef
  have hYX : ∀ (i : Fin k) (x : B), Y (Fin.castLE hk i) x = X i x := by
    intro i x
    simp only [hYdef, Fin.val_castLE, dif_pos i.isLt, Fin.eta]
  -- at `x₀` the glued family *is* the nondegenerate basis `w'`
  have hYx₀ : (fun j => Y j x₀) = w' := by
    funext j
    by_cases h : (j : ℕ) < k
    · have hval : w' j = X ⟨(j : ℕ), h⟩ x₀ := by
        rw [hw'def, ← hwX ⟨(j : ℕ), h⟩]
        congr 1
      simp only [hYdef, dif_pos h, hval]
    · simp only [hYdef, dif_neg h, hWval j]
  have hYnd : IsNondegenerateTuple (g.bilin x₀) (fun j => Y j x₀) := by rw [hYx₀]; exact hw'
  -- smooth on the intersection, where both halves are defined
  have hYsmooth : ∀ j, ContMDiffOn IB (IB.prod 𝓘(ℝ, F)) ∞ (T% (Y j)) (u ∩ v₁) := by
    intro j
    by_cases h : (j : ℕ) < k
    · exact ((hX.contMDiffOn ⟨(j : ℕ), h⟩).mono Set.inter_subset_left).congr
        fun x _ => by simp only [hYdef, dif_pos h]
    · exact ((hWsmooth j).mono Set.inter_subset_right).congr
        fun x _ => by simp only [hYdef, dif_neg h]
  -- nondegeneracy at `x₀` propagates to a neighbourhood
  obtain ⟨v, hvopen, hx₀v, hvsub, hnd⟩ :=
    exists_isLocalNondegenerateOn_nhds g (hu.inter hv₁open) hYsmooth ⟨hx₀, hx₀v₁⟩ hYnd
  -- … (truncated)

deps.title: A Nondegenerate Tuple is Linearly Independent
deps.label: lem:nondegenerate-tuple-independent

deps.title: Nondegeneracy of a Tuple of Smooth Sections is an Open Condition
deps.label: lem:open-nondegenerate-tuple

deps.title: Smooth Sections Through a Prescribed Basis
deps.label: lem:sections-through-prescribed-basis

deps.title: A Nondegenerate Tuple of Full Length is a Basis
deps.label: lem:nondegenerate-tuple-basis

blocks.tex:
The tuple $(X_1|_{x_0},\ldots,X_k|_{x_0})$ is nondegenerate in the $m$-dimensional scalar product space $(V_{x_0}, g_{x_0})$, hence linearly independent by Lemma \ref{lem:nondegenerate-tuple-independent}; so $k \leq m$. By Lemma \ref{lem:completion-nondegenerate-bases} it extends to a nondegenerate $m$-tuple $(w_1,\ldots,w_m)$ of $V_{x_0}$ with $w_i = X_i|_{x_0}$ for $i \leq k$, and by Lemma \ref{lem:nondegenerate-tuple-basis} that tuple is a basis of $V_{x_0}$.

Lemma \ref{lem:sections-through-prescribed-basis} applied to this basis yields an open $v_1 \ni x_0$ and sections $W_1,\ldots,W_m$, smooth on $v_1$, with $W_j|_{x_0} = w_j$. Define
\[
  Y_j \;=\; \begin{cases} X_j, & j \leq k,\\ W_j, & j > k,\end{cases}
\]
so that $Y_j$ is smooth on $u \cap v_1$ for every $j$, and $Y_j|_{x_0} = w_j$ for every $j$: for $j \leq k$ because $w_j = X_j|_{x_0}$, and for $j > k$ by the choice of $W_j$. Hence $(Y_1|_{x_0},\ldots,Y_m|_{x_0}) = (w_1,\ldots,w_m)$ is a nondegenerate $m$-tuple, and Lemma \ref{lem:open-nondegenerate-tuple} produces an open $v$ with $x_0 \in v \subseteq u \cap v_1$ on which $(Y_1,\ldots,Y_m)$ is nondegenerate. Finally $Y_i = X_i$ on $v$ for $i \leq k$ by construction.

blocks.label: prop:pseudo-riemannian-adapted-frame
blocks.title: Pseudo-Riemannian Adapted Orthonormal Frames
blocks.body: Suppose $(\bar{M}, \bar{g})$ is a pseudo-Riemannian manifold, and $F : M \to \bar{M}$ presents $M$ as a pseudo-Riemannian or Riemannian submanifold, the induced metric being $g$. For each $p \in M$, there exists a smooth orthonormal frame for $F^* T\bar{M}$ on a neighborhood of $p$ that is adapted to $M$, in the sense that its first $n = \dim M$ members span $dF_x(T_xM)$ at every point $x$ of that neighborhood.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.exists_adapted_pseudo_orthonormalFrame
lean.code:
theorem exists_adapted_pseudo_orthonormalFrame (g : PseudoRiemannianMetric I M)
    (g' : PseudoRiemannianMetric I' M') (f : C^∞⟮I, M; I', M'⟯)
    (hg : IsPullbackAlong I I' g g' f) (p : M) :
    ∃ (v : Set M) (Y : Fin (finrank ℝ E') → (x : M) →
        ((f : M → M') *ᵖ (TangentSpace I' : M' → Type _)) x) (hn : finrank ℝ E ≤ finrank ℝ E'),
      IsOpen v ∧ p ∈ v ∧ IsLocalFrameOn I E' ∞ Y v ∧
      (∀ x ∈ v, IsOrthonormal ((g'.pullback f).bilin x) fun j => Y j x) ∧
      (∀ x ∈ v, ∀ i : Fin (finrank ℝ E), Y (Fin.castLE hn i) x ∈ tangentRange f x) ∧
      (∀ x ∈ v, span ℝ (Set.range fun i : Fin (finrank ℝ E) => Y (Fin.castLE hn i) x)
        = tangentRange f x) := by
  classical
  set V := ((f : M → M') *ᵖ (TangentSpace I' : M' → Type _)) with hV
  set G : Bundle.ContMDiffPseudoMetric I ∞ E' V := g'.pullback f with hG
  haveI : ∀ x : M, FiniteDimensional ℝ (V x) := fun x => finiteDimensional_fibre (F := E') x
  have himm := injective_mfderiv_of_isPullbackAlong hg
  -- an orthonormal frame for `(M, g)` near `p` — Lee's Proposition 2.66.  This, rather than a
  -- chart frame, is the tangential input: it is what pushes forward to a *nondegenerate* tuple
  obtain ⟨u, X, huopen, hpu, hXframe, hXon⟩ := exists_pseudo_orthonormalFrame_nhds g p
  -- push it into the ambient fibres; the pairing is unchanged because `g = f^* g̃`
  set Z : Fin (finrank ℝ E) → (x : M) → V x := fun i x => pushforward f (X i) x with hZdef
  have hZon : ∀ x ∈ u, IsOrthonormal (G.bilin x) fun i => Z i x := by
    intro x hx
    have hpair : ∀ i j, G.bilin x (Z i x) (Z j x) = g.bilin x (X i x) (X j x) := by
      intro i j
      rw [ContMDiffPseudoMetric.bilin_apply, ContMDiffPseudoMetric.bilin_apply, hG,
        Bundle.ContMDiffPseudoMetric.pullback_form, hg x (X i x) (X j x)]
      rfl
    exact ⟨fun i j hij => by rw [hpair i j]; exact (hXon x hx).1 i j hij,
      fun i => by rw [hpair i i]; exact (hXon x hx).2 i⟩
  have hZ : IsLocalNondegenerateOn I E' ∞ G Z u :=
    { nondegenerateTuple := fun {x} hx => (hZon x hx).isNondegenerateTuple
      contMDiffOn := fun i => contMDiffOn_pushforward huopen (hXframe.contMDiffOn i) }
  -- extend to a nondegenerate frame of the ambient bundle near `p`
  obtain ⟨v, Y₀, hn, hvopen, hpv, hvsub, hY₀, hY₀Z⟩ :=
    exists_isLocalNondegenerateOn_extend (IB := I) (F := E') (V := V) G hZ huopen hpu
  -- members below the cut are pushforwards, hence tangent to `M`
  have hY₀tan : ∀ x ∈ v, ∀ i : Fin (finrank ℝ E), Y₀ (Fin.castLE hn i) x ∈ tangentRange f x :=
    fun x hx i => by rw [hY₀Z i x hx]; exact pushforward_mem_tangentRange f (X i) x
  -- Gram-Schmidt preserves the flag, so members below the cut stay inside the span of the
  -- tangential ones.  This is the whole reason the *ordered* extension mattered.
  have hGStan : ∀ x ∈ v, ∀ i : Fin (finrank ℝ E),
      pseudoGramSchmidtFrame G Y₀ (Fin.castLE hn i) x ∈ tangentRange f x := by
    intro x hx i
    have hi : ((Fin.castLE hn i : Fin (finrank ℝ E')) : ℕ) + 1 ≤ finrank ℝ E' :=
      (Fin.castLE hn i).isLt
    have hmem : pseudoGramSchmidtFrame G Y₀ (Fin.castLE hn i) x
        ∈ prefixSpan (fun j => pseudoGramSchmidtFrame G Y₀ j x) ((i : ℕ) + 1) := by
      refine subset_span ⟨Fin.castLE hn i, ?_, rfl⟩
      simp
    rw [prefixSpan_pseudoGramSchmidtFrame G hY₀ hx _ (by omega)] at hmem
    refine span_le.2 ?_ hmem
    rintro _ ⟨j, hj, rfl⟩
    have hjlt : (j : ℕ) < finrank ℝ E := by
      simp only [Set.mem_setOf_eq] at hj; omega
    have hjeq : j = Fin.castLE hn ⟨(j : ℕ), hjlt⟩ := by ext; simp
    rw [hjeq]
    exact hY₀tan x hx ⟨(j : ℕ), hjlt⟩
  have hGSon : ∀ x ∈ v, IsOrthonormal (G.bilin x) fun j => pseudoGramSchmidtFrame G Y₀ j x :=
    fun x hx => pseudoGramSchmidtFrame_isOrthonormal G hY₀ hx
  refine ⟨v, pseudoGramSchmidtFrame G Y₀, hn, hvopen, hpv, ?_, hGSon, hGStan, ?_⟩
  -- … (truncated)

deps.title: The Tensor Induced Along a Map
deps.label: def:induced-tensor-along-map

deps.title: Extension of a Nondegenerate Family to a Nondegenerate Frame
deps.label: lem:extend-nondegenerate-family

deps.title: Orthonormalization of a Nondegenerate Family of Vector Fields
deps.label: prop:orthonormalize-nondegenerate-family

deps.title: The Ambient Metric Along a Submanifold
deps.label: lem:pullback-pseudo-metric-bundle

blocks.tex:
Write $m = \dim\bar{M}$ and $n = \dim M$, and equip $F^*T\bar{M}$ with the ambient metric $F^*\bar{g}$ of Lemma \ref{lem:pullback-pseudo-metric-bundle}. By Lemma \ref{lem:pseudo-submanifold-immersion}, $F$ is an immersion, so $dF_x$ is injective and $\dim dF_x(T_xM) = n$ for every $x$.

\emph{The tangential half.} By Proposition \ref{prop:orthonormal-frames-pseudo-riemannian} applied to $(M,g)$ there are an open $u \ni p$ and a smooth orthonormal frame $(E_1,\ldots,E_n)$ for $TM$ on $u$. Put $Z_i = dF(E_i)$, a smooth section of $F^*T\bar{M}$ on $u$. Because $g$ is induced by $\bar{g}$ along $F$,
\[
  (F^*\bar{g})_x(Z_i|_x, Z_j|_x) = \bar{g}_{F(x)}\bigl(dF_x(E_i|_x), dF_x(E_j|_x)\bigr) = g_x(E_i|_x, E_j|_x),
\]
so $(Z_1,\ldots,Z_n)$ is orthonormal at every $x \in u$, and hence nondegenerate there by Lemma \ref{lem:orthonormal-tuple-nondegenerate}. This is the step the Riemannian argument does not need and cannot be replaced by a coordinate frame: an arbitrary frame of $\bar{M}$ pushed to the fibres need not be nondegenerate.

\emph{Extension and orthonormalization.} By Lemma \ref{lem:extend-nondegenerate-family} there are an open $v$ with $p \in v \subseteq u$ and sections $(Y_1,\ldots,Y_m)$ of $F^*T\bar{M}$, nondegenerate on $v$, with $Y_i = Z_i$ on $v$ for $i \leq n$. Proposition \ref{prop:orthonormalize-nondegenerate-family} applied to $(Y_j)$ yields sections $(b_1,\ldots,b_m)$, smooth and orthonormal at every point of $v$, with $\operatorname{span}(b_1|_x,\ldots,b_k|_x) = \operatorname{span}(Y_1|_x,\ldots,Y_k|_x)$ for every $k \leq m$ and $x \in v$. Being orthonormal, $(b_1|_x,\ldots,b_m|_x)$ is linearly independent, and $m$ independent vectors in the $m$-dimensional fibre form a basis; so $(b_j)$ is a frame on $v$.

\emph{Adaptedness.} Fix $x \in v$ and $k \leq n$. Then
\[
  \operatorname{span}(b_1|_x,\ldots,b_k|_x) = \operatorname{span}(Y_1|_x,\ldots,Y_k|_x) = \operatorname{span}(Z_1|_x,\ldots,Z_k|_x) \subseteq dF_x(T_xM),
\]
the last inclusion because each $Z_i|_x = dF_x(E_i|_x)$ lies in the image of $dF_x$, which is a subspace. Taking $k = n$, the left-hand side is spanned by $n$ linearly independent vectors, so has dimension $n$; as it is contained in $dF_x(T_xM)$, which also has dimension $n$, the two are equal. Thus $(b_1,\ldots,b_n)$ spans $dF_x(T_xM)$ at every $x \in v$, which is the assertion that $(b_1,\ldots,b_m)$ is adapted to $M$.
The next corollary is proved in the same way as Proposition 2.16.

blocks.label: def:pseudo-normal-space
blocks.title: The Normal Space of a Pseudo-Riemannian Submanifold
blocks.body:
Let $(\bar{M},\bar{g})$ be a pseudo-Riemannian $m$-manifold and let $F : M \to \bar{M}$ present an $n$-manifold $M$ as a smooth immersion. For $x \in M$, the \emph{normal space to $M$ at $x$} is the subspace of the ambient tangent space $T_{F(x)}\bar{M}$ given by
\[
  N_x M \;=\; \bigl\{\, v \in T_{F(x)}\bar{M} \;:\; \bar{g}_{F(x)}(v,w) = 0 \text{ for every } w \in dF_x(T_xM) \,\bigr\}.
\]

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.pseudoNormalSpace
lean.code:
def pseudoNormalSpace :
    Submodule ℝ (((f : M → M') *ᵖ (TangentSpace I' : M' → Type _)) x) where
  carrier := {v | ∀ w ∈ tangentRange f x, g'.form (f x) v w = 0}
  add_mem' := by
    intro a b ha hb w hw
    have key : (g'.form (f x)) (a + b) = (g'.form (f x)) a + (g'.form (f x)) b :=
      (g'.form (f x)).map_add a b
    rw [key, ContinuousLinearMap.add_apply, ha w hw, hb w hw, add_zero]
  zero_mem' := by
    intro w _
    have key : (g'.form (f x))
        (0 : ((f : M → M') *ᵖ (TangentSpace I' : M' → Type _)) x) = 0 := map_zero _
    rw [key, ContinuousLinearMap.zero_apply]
  smul_mem' := by
    intro c a ha w hw
    have key : (g'.form (f x)) (c • a) = c • (g'.form (f x)) a :=
      (g'.form (f x)).map_smul c a
    rw [key, ContinuousLinearMap.smul_apply, ha w hw, smul_zero]

omit [FiniteDimensional ℝ E] [IsManifold I ∞ M] [FiniteDimensional ℝ E'] in
@[simp]

deps.title: The Ambient Metric Along a Submanifold
deps.label: lem:pullback-pseudo-metric-bundle

blocks.label: lem:pseudo-normal-space-frame
blocks.title: Local Frames for the Normal Space
blocks.body: Let $(\bar{M},\bar{g})$ be a pseudo-Riemannian $m$-manifold and let $F : M \to \bar{M}$ present $M$ as an $n$-dimensional pseudo-Riemannian or Riemannian submanifold. Every $p \in M$ has a neighborhood $v$ carrying $m-n$ sections of $F^* T\bar{M}$, smooth on $v$, whose values at each $x \in v$ are orthonormal and span $N_x M$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.exists_pseudo_localFrame_normalSpace
lean.code:
theorem exists_pseudo_localFrame_normalSpace (g : PseudoRiemannianMetric I M)
    (g' : PseudoRiemannianMetric I' M') (f : C^∞⟮I, M; I', M'⟯)
    (hg : IsPullbackAlong I I' g g' f) (p : M) :
    ∃ (v : Set M) (hn : finrank ℝ E ≤ finrank ℝ E')
      (N : Fin (finrank ℝ E' - finrank ℝ E) → (x : M) →
        ((f : M → M') *ᵖ (TangentSpace I' : M' → Type _)) x),
      IsOpen v ∧ p ∈ v ∧
      (∀ j, ContMDiffOn I (I.prod 𝓘(ℝ, E')) ∞ (fun x => TotalSpace.mk' E' x (N j x)) v) ∧
      (∀ x ∈ v, IsOrthonormal ((g'.pullback f).bilin x) fun j => N j x) ∧
      (∀ x ∈ v, ∀ j, N j x ∈ pseudoNormalSpace g' f x) ∧
      (∀ x ∈ v, span ℝ (Set.range fun j => N j x) = pseudoNormalSpace g' f x) := by
  classical
  obtain ⟨v, Y, hn, hvopen, hpv, hY, hYon, hYtan, hYspan⟩ :=
    exists_adapted_pseudo_orthonormalFrame g g' f hg p
  -- the pairing of the ambient metric along `M` is `g̃` at the image point, definitionally
  have hbilin : ∀ (x : M) (a b : ((f : M → M') *ᵖ (TangentSpace I' : M' → Type _)) x),
      (g'.pullback f).bilin x a b = g'.form (f x) a b := fun _ _ _ => rfl
  set N : Fin (finrank ℝ E' - finrank ℝ E) → (x : M) →
      ((f : M → M') *ᵖ (TangentSpace I' : M' → Type _)) x :=
    fun j x => Y (finUpper hn j) x with hN
  -- each is normal: orthogonal to every member below the cut, hence to their span
  have hnormal : ∀ x ∈ v, ∀ j, N j x ∈ pseudoNormalSpace g' f x := by
    intro x hx j w hw
    rw [← hYspan x hx] at hw
    refine form_span_eq_zero (N j x) _ ?_ w hw
    rintro _ ⟨i, rfl⟩
    rw [hN, ← hbilin]
    exact (hYon x hx).1 (finUpper hn j) (Fin.castLE hn i) (castLE_ne_finUpper hn i j).symm
  refine ⟨v, hn, N, hvopen, hpv, fun j => hY.contMDiffOn _, ?_, hnormal, ?_⟩
  · -- orthonormality is inherited from the adapted frame along an injective reindexing
    exact fun x hx => (hYon x hx).comp _ (finUpper_injective hn)
  · intro x hx
    refine le_antisymm ?_ ?_
    · rw [span_le]
      rintro _ ⟨j, rfl⟩
      exact hnormal x hx j
    · -- expand a normal vector in the adapted frame, which is a basis of the fibre
      intro w hw
      have hbasis : ∀ a, (hY.toBasisAt hx) a = Y a x := fun a => hY.toBasisAt_coe hx a
      have hexp : ∑ a, (hY.toBasisAt hx).repr w a • Y a x = w := by
        conv_rhs => rw [← (hY.toBasisAt hx).sum_repr w]
        exact Finset.sum_congr rfl fun a _ => by rw [hbasis a]
      -- `⟪b_a, w⟫ = c_a ⟪b_a, b_a⟫`: the off-diagonal terms of the expansion drop out
      have hrepr : ∀ a, g'.form (f x) (Y a x) w
          = (hY.toBasisAt hx).repr w a * g'.form (f x) (Y a x) (Y a x) := by
        intro a
        have h1 : g'.form (f x) (Y a x) w
            = ∑ b, (hY.toBasisAt hx).repr w b * g'.form (f x) (Y a x) (Y b x) := by
          conv_lhs => rw [← hexp]
          exact form_sum_right _ _ _
        rw [h1, Finset.sum_eq_single a]
        · intro b _ hb
          rw [← hbilin, (hYon x hx).1 a b (Ne.symm hb), mul_zero]
        · intro h
          exact absurd (Finset.mem_univ a) h
      -- below the cut the frame is tangent, so a normal `w` pairs to zero and the
      -- coefficient must vanish — here `⟪b_a, b_a⟫ = ±1 ≠ 0` is what makes the division legal
      have hlow : ∀ i : Fin (finrank ℝ E), (hY.toBasisAt hx).repr w (Fin.castLE hn i) = 0 := by
        intro i
        have hself : g'.form (f x) (Y (Fin.castLE hn i) x) (Y (Fin.castLE hn i) x) ≠ 0 := by
  -- … (truncated)

deps.title: The Normal Space of a Pseudo-Riemannian Submanifold
deps.label: def:pseudo-normal-space

blocks.tex:
Let $(b_1,\ldots,b_m)$ be an adapted orthonormal frame on a neighborhood $v$ of $p$, as furnished by Proposition \ref{prop:pseudo-riemannian-adapted-frame}, and take $N_j = b_{n+j}$ for $j = 1,\ldots,m-n$. These are smooth on $v$, and orthonormal at each $x \in v$, both being inherited from $(b_i)$.

\emph{They are normal.} Fix $x \in v$ and $j$. For every $i \leq n$ we have $\langle N_j|_x, b_i|_x \rangle = 0$, the indices $n+j$ and $i$ being distinct; so $N_j|_x$ is orthogonal to each member of $(b_1|_x,\ldots,b_n|_x)$, and hence to their span, which is $dF_x(T_xM)$ by adaptedness. Thus $N_j|_x \in N_xM$.

\emph{They span.} The previous paragraph gives $\operatorname{span}(N_1|_x,\ldots,N_{m-n}|_x) \subseteq N_xM$. For the reverse, let $w \in N_xM$. Being orthonormal, $(b_1|_x,\ldots,b_m|_x)$ is linearly independent and so a basis of the $m$-dimensional fibre; write $w = \sum_a c_a b_a|_x$. Pairing with $b_a|_x$ and using orthogonality of the distinct members,
\[
  \langle b_a|_x, w\rangle = c_a \langle b_a|_x, b_a|_x\rangle .
\]
For $a \leq n$ the vector $b_a|_x$ lies in $dF_x(T_xM)$, so $\langle b_a|_x, w \rangle = 0$ because $w$ is normal; and $\langle b_a|_x,b_a|_x\rangle = \pm 1 \neq 0$ by orthonormality, whence $c_a = 0$. (It is here that the indefinite case needs orthonormality rather than mere independence: a null $b_a$ would make the displayed equation vacuous and leave $c_a$ undetermined.) So $w = \sum_{a > n} c_a b_a|_x$ lies in $\operatorname{span}(N_1|_x,\ldots,N_{m-n}|_x)$.

Lemma \ref{lem:pseudo-normal-space-frame} is the hypothesis of Lemma \ref{lem:local-frame-criterion-for-subbundles}, but that lemma additionally requires the ambient bundle to carry a Riemannian fibre metric, which it uses only to normalize the given frames. An indefinite $\bar{g}$ is not such a metric, and Proposition \ref{prop:normal-bundle}'s move of supplying the ambient metric itself is therefore unavailable. The remedy is that the fibre metric of Lemma \ref{lem:local-frame-criterion-for-subbundles} is required to bear no relation to the family of subspaces: an arbitrary Riemannian metric on $\bar{M}$ serves, and one exists by Proposition \ref{prop:existence-riemannian-metric}.

blocks.label: cor:normal-bundle-pseudo-riemannian-submanifold
blocks.title: Normal Bundle to a Pseudo-Riemannian Submanifold
blocks.body: Suppose $(\bar{M}, \bar{g})$ is a pseudo-Riemannian $m$-manifold, and $F : M \to \bar{M}$ presents $M$ as an $n$-dimensional embedded or immersed pseudo-Riemannian or Riemannian submanifold. The set of vectors normal to $M$ is a smooth rank-$(m-n)$ vector subbundle of $T\bar{M}|_M$, called the \emph{normal bundle} to $M$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.contMDiffVectorBundle_pseudoNormalSpace
lean.code:
theorem contMDiffVectorBundle_pseudoNormalSpace (g : PseudoRiemannianMetric I M)
    (g' : PseudoRiemannianMetric I' M') (f : C^∞⟮I, M; I', M'⟯)
    (hg : IsPullbackAlong I I' g g' f) (h : RiemannianMetric I' M') :
    letI : Bundle.RiemannianBundle ((f : M → M') *ᵖ (TangentSpace I' : M' → Type _)) :=
      ⟨(h.pullback f).toRiemannianMetric⟩
    letI hs := hasLocalSubframes_pseudoNormalSpace g g' f hg h
    letI := subTotalSpaceTopology hs
    letI := subFiberBundle hs
    letI := subVectorBundle hs
    ContMDiffVectorBundle ∞ (EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin (finrank ℝ E' - finrank ℝ E)))
      (fun x => ↥(pseudoNormalSpace g' f x)) I := by
  letI : Bundle.RiemannianBundle ((f : M → M') *ᵖ (TangentSpace I' : M' → Type _)) :=
    ⟨(h.pullback f).toRiemannianMetric⟩
  exact subContMDiffVectorBundle (F := E') (hasLocalSubframes_pseudoNormalSpace g g' f hg h)

deps.title: The Normal Space of a Pseudo-Riemannian Submanifold
deps.label: def:pseudo-normal-space

deps.title: Local Frames for the Normal Space
deps.label: lem:pseudo-normal-space-frame

deps.title: Every smooth manifold admits a Riemannian metric
deps.label: prop:existence-riemannian-metric

blocks.tex: By Proposition \ref{prop:existence-riemannian-metric} choose a Riemannian metric $h$ on $\bar{M}$; by Lemma \ref{lem:ambient-tangent-bundle-riemannian} it makes $F^*T\bar{M}$ a Riemannian vector bundle over $M$. Lemma \ref{lem:pseudo-normal-space-frame} provides, near every point of $M$, sections of $F^*T\bar{M}$ smooth there and forming a basis of $N_xM$ at each point, orthonormality for $\bar{g}$ giving in particular linear independence. This is exactly the hypothesis of Lemma \ref{lem:local-frame-criterion-for-subbundles} applied to the bundle $F^*T\bar{M}$ with fibre metric $h$ and the family of subspaces $D_x = N_xM$, which therefore exhibits $NM$ as a smooth rank-$(m-n)$ subbundle of $F^*T\bar{M}$. The metric $h$ enters only through Lemma \ref{lem:local-frame-criterion-for-subbundles}'s normalization of frames and is unrelated to $\bar{g}$; the subspaces $N_xM$ and the frames spanning them are defined by $\bar{g}$ alone.

Pseudo-Riemannian metrics are obtained by relaxing the positivity requirement for Riemannian metrics. In addition, there are other useful generalizations that result when we relax other requirements. In this section we touch briefly on two of those generalizations. We will not treat these anywhere else in the book, but it is useful to know the definitions.

The first generalization arises when we relax the requirement that the metric be defined on the whole tangent space.

A \emph{sub-Riemannian metric} (also sometimes known as a \emph{singular Riemannian metric} or \emph{Carnot--Carathéodory metric}) on a smooth manifold $M$ is a smooth fiber metric on a smooth tangent distribution $S \subseteq TM$ (i.e., a vector subbundle of $TM$). Since lengths make sense only for vectors in $S$, the only curves whose lengths can be measured are those whose velocity vectors lie everywhere in $S$. Therefore, one usually imposes some condition on $S$ that guarantees that any two nearby points can be connected by such a curve. This is, in a sense, the opposite of the Frobenius integrability condition, which would restrict every such curve to lie in a single leaf of a foliation.

Sub-Riemannian metrics arise naturally in the study of the abstract models of real submanifolds of complex space $\mathbb{C}^n$, called \emph{CR manifolds} (short for \emph{Cauchy--Riemann manifolds}). CR manifolds are real manifolds endowed with a tangent distribution $S \subseteq TM$ whose fibers carry the structure of complex vector spaces (with an additional integrability condition that need not concern us here). In the model case of a submanifold $M \subseteq \mathbb{C}^n$, $S$ is the set of vectors tangent to $M$ that remain tangent after multiplication by $i = \sqrt{-1}$ in the ambient complex coordinates. If $S$ is sufficiently far from being integrable, choosing a fiber metric on $S$ results in a sub-Riemannian metric whose geometric properties closely reflect the complex-analytic properties of $M$ as a subset of $\mathbb{C}^n$.

Another motivation for studying sub-Riemannian metrics arises from \emph{control theory}. In this subject, one is given a manifold with a vector field depending on parameters called \emph{controls}, with the goal being to vary the controls so as to obtain a solution curve with desired properties, often one that minimizes some function such as arc length. If the vector field is constrained to be everywhere tangent to a distribution $S$ on the manifold (for example, in the case of a robot arm whose motion is restricted by the orientations of its hinges), then the function can often be modeled as a sub-Riemannian metric and optimal solutions modeled as sub-Riemannian geodesics.

A useful introduction to the geometry of sub-Riemannian metrics is provided in the article [Str86].

blocks.label: def:open-submanifold-metric
blocks.title: The open submanifold metric
blocks.body: Let $W \subseteq \mathbb{R}^n$ be open and let $g$ be a Riemannian metric on $\mathbb{R}^n$. The metric \emph{induced on $W$} is $\iota^* g$, where $\iota : W \hookrightarrow \mathbb{R}^n$ is the inclusion. By Lemma \ref{lem:mfderiv-open-inclusion} the inclusion is an immersion, so Lemma \ref{lem:pullback-immersion-metric} applies and $\iota^* g$ is a Riemannian metric.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.openSubmanifoldMetric
lean.code:
def openSubmanifoldMetric (g : RiemannianMetric 𝓘(ℝ, F) F) (W : Opens F) :
    RiemannianMetric 𝓘(ℝ, F) W :=
  pullbackMetric g (fun y : W => (y : F)) contMDiff_subtype_val
    (injective_mfderiv_opens_subtypeVal W)

deps.title: The metric induced by an immersion
deps.label: def:induced-metric

deps.title: The differential of the inclusion of an open subset
deps.label: lem:mfderiv-open-inclusion

blocks.label: lem:mfderiv-open-inclusion
blocks.title: The differential of the inclusion of an open subset
blocks.body: An open set $W \subseteq \mathbb{R}^n$ is a smooth manifold whose single chart is the inclusion $\iota : W \hookrightarrow \mathbb{R}^n$, and every tangent space $T_x W$ is canonically $\mathbb{R}^n$. Under these identifications $d\iota_x = \operatorname{id}$ for every $x \in W$. In particular $\iota$ is an immersion.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.mfderiv_opens_subtypeVal
lean.code:
theorem mfderiv_opens_subtypeVal (U : Opens M) (x : U) :
    mfderiv I I (fun y : U => (y : M)) x = ContinuousLinearMap.id ℝ E :=
  (hasMFDerivAt_opens_subtypeVal U x).mfderiv

blocks.tex: The differential is computed from the coordinate representation of $\iota$, which is $\iota \circ \iota^{-1}$ read through the chart. Since the chart of $W$ is the inclusion itself, this composite is the identity map of $\mathbb{R}^n$ \emph{restricted to $W$} — it is undefined outside $W$, so the two maps are not equal globally. As $W$ is open it is a neighborhood of $x$, and a derivative depends only on the germ at the point, so $d\iota_x$ is the derivative of the identity, namely $\operatorname{id}$.

blocks.label: lem:sphere-tangent-orthogonal-radius
blocks.title: Tangent directions to the sphere are orthogonal to the radius
blocks.body:
Let $\iota : S^{n-1} \hookrightarrow \mathbb{R}^n$ be the inclusion. For every $\omega \in S^{n-1}$ and every $w \in T_\omega S^{n-1}$,
\[
\langle \omega, d\iota_\omega(w)\rangle = 0.
\]

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.inner_coe_sphere_mfderiv_eq_zero
lean.code:
theorem inner_coe_sphere_mfderiv_eq_zero {n : ℕ} [Fact (finrank ℝ E = n + 1)]
    (ω₀ : sphere (0 : E) 1) (w : TangentSpace (𝓡 n) ω₀) :
    ⟪(ω₀ : E), (mfderiv (𝓡 n) 𝓘(ℝ, E) ((↑) : sphere (0 : E) 1 → E) ω₀ w : E)⟫ = 0 := by
  have hmem : (mfderiv (𝓡 n) 𝓘(ℝ, E) ((↑) : sphere (0 : E) 1 → E) ω₀ w : E) ∈ (ℝ ∙ (ω₀ : E))ᗮ := by
    rw [← range_mfderiv_coe_sphere (n := n) ω₀]
    exact ⟨w, rfl⟩
  exact hmem (ω₀ : E) (Submodule.mem_span_singleton_self _)

blocks.tex: The image of $d\iota_\omega$ is the orthogonal complement $(\mathbb{R}\omega)^\perp$ of the line spanned by $\omega$. Since $\omega$ lies in $\mathbb{R}\omega$, every element of the image is orthogonal to $\omega$.

blocks.label: def:polar-map
blocks.title: The polar map
blocks.body: $\Phi : \mathbb{R}^+ \times S^{n-1} \to \mathbb{R}^n$ is defined by $\Phi(\rho, \omega) = \rho\,\omega$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.polarMap
lean.code: def polarMap : posReals × sphere (0 : E) 1 → E := fun p => ((p.1 : ℝ)) • ((p.2 : E))

blocks.label: lem:mfderiv-polar-map
blocks.title: The differential of the polar map
blocks.body:
For $(r, \omega) \in \mathbb{R}^+ \times S^{n-1}$ and $(a, w) \in T_r\mathbb{R}^+ \oplus T_\omega S^{n-1}$,
\[
d\Phi_{(r,\omega)}(a, w) = a\,\omega + r\,d\iota_\omega(w).
\]

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.mfderiv_polarMap_apply
lean.code:
theorem mfderiv_polarMap_apply {n : ℕ} [Fact (finrank ℝ E = n + 1)]
    (p : posReals × sphere (0 : E) 1) (v : TangentSpace (𝓘(ℝ, ℝ).prod (𝓡 n)) p) :
    (show E from (mfderiv (𝓘(ℝ, ℝ).prod (𝓡 n)) 𝓘(ℝ, E) (polarMap E) p) v) =
      (show ℝ from v.1) • ((p.2 : E))
        + (p.1 : ℝ) • (show E from mfderiv (𝓡 n) 𝓘(ℝ, E) ((↑) : sphere (0 : E) 1 → E) p.2 v.2) := by
  have hsplit := mfderiv_prod_eq_add_apply (v := v)
    ((contMDiff_polarMap (E := E) (n := n)).mdifferentiableAt (by simp))
  -- The two partial maps of `Φ` are literally the two maps computed above.
  have e1 : (fun z : posReals => polarMap E (z, p.2))
      = fun z : posReals => (z : ℝ) • ((p.2 : E)) := rfl
  have e2 : (fun z : sphere (0 : E) 1 => polarMap E (p.1, z))
      = fun z : sphere (0 : E) 1 => (p.1 : ℝ) • ((z : E)) := rfl
  rw [show (show E from (mfderiv (𝓘(ℝ, ℝ).prod (𝓡 n)) 𝓘(ℝ, E) (polarMap E) p) v) = _ from hsplit,
    e1, e2]
  congr 1
  · exact mfderiv_polarMap_fst (p.2 : E) p.1 v.1
  · exact mfderiv_polarMap_snd (p.1 : ℝ) p.2 v.2

/-! ### Orthogonality of the radial and spherical directions -/

deps.title: The differential of the polar map
deps.label: lem:mfderiv-polar-map

deps.title: The differential of the inclusion of an open subset
deps.label: lem:mfderiv-open-inclusion

deps.title: Tangent directions to the sphere are orthogonal to the radius
deps.label: lem:sphere-tangent-orthogonal-radius

blocks.tex: $\Phi$ is a map on a product manifold, so its differential is the sum of its two partial differentials. Holding $\omega$ fixed, $r \mapsto r\,\omega$ is linear in $r$ precomposed with the inclusion $\mathbb{R}^+ \hookrightarrow \mathbb{R}$, whose differential is the identity by Lemma \ref{lem:mfderiv-open-inclusion}; its differential therefore sends $a$ to $a\,\omega$. Holding $r$ fixed, $\omega \mapsto r\,\omega$ is the inclusion $\iota$ followed by the fixed scaling $u \mapsto r\,u$, which is linear, so its differential sends $w$ to $r\,d\iota_\omega(w)$.

We verify $\Phi^*\bar{g} = g_{\mathbb{R}^+} \oplus \rho^2 \mathring{g}$ at each point; that $\Phi$ is a bijection onto $\mathbb{R}^n \setminus \{0\}$ is checked separately below.

Fix $(r,\omega)$ and let $(a,w), (a',w')$ be tangent vectors there. By Lemma \ref{lem:mfderiv-polar-map},
\[
\langle d\Phi(a,w), d\Phi(a',w')\rangle
 = \langle a\omega + r\,d\iota(w),\ a'\omega + r\,d\iota(w')\rangle,
\]
which expands into four terms:
\[
a a' \langle\omega,\omega\rangle + a r \langle \omega, d\iota(w')\rangle + a' r \langle d\iota(w), \omega\rangle + r^2 \langle d\iota(w), d\iota(w')\rangle.
\]
Now $\langle\omega,\omega\rangle = |\omega|^2 = 1$ because $\omega \in S^{n-1}$, and the two middle terms vanish by Lemma \ref{lem:sphere-tangent-orthogonal-radius}. Since $\mathring{g}_\omega(w,w') = \langle d\iota(w), d\iota(w')\rangle$ by the definition of the round metric as $\iota^*\bar{g}$, what remains is
\[
a a' + r^2\,\mathring{g}_\omega(w,w'),
\]
which is exactly the warped product metric $g_{\mathbb{R}^+} \oplus \rho^2\mathring{g}$ evaluated at $(r,\omega)$, the radial factor $g_{\mathbb{R}^+}$ being $\iota^*\bar{g} = d\rho^2$ by Lemma \ref{lem:mfderiv-open-inclusion} and the warping function being $\rho(r) = r$.

For the bijection: $|\Phi(r,\omega)| = r|\omega| = r > 0$, so $\Phi$ never takes the value $0$; if $\Phi(r,\omega) = \Phi(r',\omega')$ then taking norms gives $r = r'$ and then cancelling the nonzero scalar $r$ gives $\omega = \omega'$, so $\Phi$ is injective; and any $x \neq 0$ equals $\Phi(|x|, x/|x|)$, so $\Phi$ is onto $\mathbb{R}^n \setminus \{0\}$.

Throughout, the tangent space to the level set of $f$ through a regular point $p$ is $\ker df_p$, so ``$X$ is orthogonal to the level sets at regular points'' means $\langle X_p, w\rangle_g = 0$ for every regular $p$ and every $w \in \ker df_p$; and $Xf = df(X)$, $|X|_g^2 = \langle X, X\rangle_g$.

Suppose first that $X = \operatorname{grad} f$. Then $Xf = df(X) = \langle \operatorname{grad} f, X\rangle_g = \langle X, X\rangle_g = |X|_g^2$ by Lemma \ref{lem:gradient-characterization}, and for $w \in \ker df_p$ the same lemma gives $\langle X_p, w\rangle_g = df_p(w) = 0$. Neither implication used the hypothesis that $X$ vanishes nowhere.

Conversely, suppose $Xf = |X|_g^2$ and $X$ is orthogonal to the level sets at regular points. Fix $p \in M$. Since $X_p \neq 0$ and $g$ is positive definite, $\langle X_p, X_p\rangle_g > 0$, so
\[
df_p(X_p) = \langle X_p, X_p \rangle_g > 0 .
\]
In particular $df_p \neq 0$, so \emph{every} point of $M$ is a regular point of $f$ and the orthogonality hypothesis is available at $p$. The claim $X_p = \operatorname{grad} f|_p$ is then Lemma \ref{lem:transversal-representative-eq-gradient}.

blocks.label: lem:transversal-representative-eq-gradient
blocks.title: A transversal orthogonal representative is the gradient
blocks.body: Let $(M,g)$ be a Riemannian manifold, $f \in C^\infty(M)$, $p \in M$, and $v \in T_pM$ with $v \neq 0$. Suppose $\langle v, v\rangle_g = df_p(v)$ and $\langle v, w\rangle_g = 0$ for every $w \in \ker df_p$. Then $v = \operatorname{grad} f|_p$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.eq_grad_of_ne_zero_of_innerAt_self_of_orthogonal_ker
lean.code:
theorem eq_grad_of_ne_zero_of_innerAt_self_of_orthogonal_ker (p : M) (v : TangentSpace I p)
    (hne : v ≠ 0) (hself : g.innerAt p v v = extDerivFun (I := I) f p v)
    (horth : ∀ w : TangentSpace I p, extDerivFun (I := I) f p w = 0 → g.innerAt p v w = 0) :
    v = grad g f p := by
  -- `v ≠ 0` makes the pairing with itself strictly positive, so `v ∉ ker df_p`.
  have hpos : 0 < g.innerAt p v v :=
    lt_of_le_of_ne (g.innerAt_self_nonneg p v)
      (Ne.symm fun h => hne ((g.innerAt_self_eq_zero_iff p v).mp h))
  have hdv : extDerivFun (I := I) f p v ≠ 0 := hself ▸ hpos.ne'
  refine eq_grad_of_innerAt g f p v fun w => ?_
  set t : ℝ := extDerivFun (I := I) f p w / extDerivFun (I := I) f p v with ht
  -- `w - t • v` lies in `ker df_p` by the choice of `t`.
  have hu : extDerivFun (I := I) f p (w - t • v) = 0 := by
    rw [map_sub, map_smul, ht, smul_eq_mul, div_mul_cancel₀ _ hdv, sub_self]
  have h0 := horth _ hu
  -- Expand the pairing against the split vector.
  have hexp : g.innerAt p v (w - t • v) = g.innerAt p v w - t * g.innerAt p v v := by
    show (g.inner p v) (w - t • v) = (g.inner p v) w - t * (g.inner p v) v
    rw [map_sub, map_smul]
    rfl
  rw [hexp] at h0
  have hvw : g.innerAt p v w = t * g.innerAt p v v := by linarith
  rw [hvw, hself, ht]
  field_simp

blocks.tex:
Since $v \neq 0$ and $g$ is positive definite, $df_p(v) = \langle v,v\rangle_g > 0$; in particular $df_p(v) \neq 0$, so $v \notin \ker df_p$.

Let $w \in T_pM$ be arbitrary and set $t = df_p(w)/df_p(v)$, which is defined because $df_p(v) \neq 0$. Then
\[
df_p(w - t\,v) = df_p(w) - t\,df_p(v) = df_p(w) - df_p(w) = 0,
\]
so $w - t\,v \in \ker df_p$ and the orthogonality hypothesis gives $\langle v, w - t\,v\rangle_g = 0$. Expanding by bilinearity,
\[
\langle v, w \rangle_g \;=\; t \langle v, v\rangle_g \;=\; t\, df_p(v) \;=\; df_p(w).
\]
As $w$ was arbitrary, $v$ represents $df_p$ under $g$, and Lemma \ref{lem:gradient-characterization} identifies the unique such vector as $\operatorname{grad} f|_p$. Hence $v = \operatorname{grad} f|_p$.

Note that no dimension count is used: the decomposition $w = (w - t\,v) + t\,v$ is explicit, so the argument does not need $\ker df_p$ to be a hyperplane.

blocks.label: def:inner-k-covectors
blocks.title: def:inner-k-covectors
blocks.body:
Let $(e_m)_{m \in \iota'}$ be an orthonormal basis of $V$. For $\omega, \eta \in \Lambda^k(V^*)$ set
\[
\langle \omega, \eta \rangle_e = \frac{1}{k!} \sum_{s \colon \{1,\ldots,k\} \to \iota'} \omega\big(e_{s(1)}, \ldots, e_{s(k)}\big)\, \eta\big(e_{s(1)}, \ldots, e_{s(k)}\big),
\]
the sum running over all maps $s$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.innerForms
lean.code:
@[simp] theorem innerFormsₗ_apply (e : OrthonormalBasis ι' ℝ V) (w θ : V [⋀^ι]→L[ℝ] ℝ) :
    innerFormsₗ e w θ = innerForms e w θ := rfl

/-! ### Uniqueness and frame independence (Lee, Problem 2-16) -/

omit [DecidableEq ι'] in

blocks.tex: The sum is over all index maps rather than over increasing multi-indices $i_1 < \cdots < i_k$. The two prescriptions agree: a map $s$ that repeats a value contributes $0$ because $\omega$ and $\eta$ are alternating, and each increasing multi-index is counted once for each of the $k!$ maps with the same image, all contributing equally since the two sign changes cancel. Written this way, symmetry, bilinearity and positive definiteness are visible directly from the formula.

blocks.label: lem:inner-k-covectors-posdef
blocks.title: lem:inner-k-covectors-posdef
blocks.body: $\langle \cdot, \cdot \rangle_e$ is a symmetric bilinear form on $\Lambda^k(V^*)$, and $\langle \omega, \omega \rangle_e > 0$ for every $\omega \ne 0$. Thus it is an inner product.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.innerForms_self_pos
lean.code:
theorem innerForms_self_pos (e : OrthonormalBasis ι' ℝ V) {w : V [⋀^ι]→L[ℝ] ℝ} (hw : w ≠ 0) :
    0 < innerForms e w w := by
  obtain ⟨v, hv⟩ : ∃ v : ι → ι', w (fun i => e (v i)) ≠ 0 := by
    by_contra h
    push Not at h
    exact hw (ext_of_orthonormalBasis e fun v => by simp [h v])
  refine div_pos ?_ (by exact_mod_cast Nat.factorial_pos _)
  exact Finset.sum_pos' (fun _ _ => mul_self_nonneg _)
    ⟨v, Finset.mem_univ v, mul_self_pos.2 hv⟩

blocks.tex: Symmetry and bilinearity are immediate from the formula, each summand being a product of two values that are linear in $\omega$ and in $\eta$ respectively. For positivity, $\langle \omega, \omega \rangle_e = \frac{1}{k!}\sum_s \omega(e_{s(1)},\ldots,e_{s(k)})^2 \ge 0$. If $\omega \ne 0$ then, since an alternating map is determined by its values on tuples of basis vectors, some $\omega(e_{s(1)},\ldots,e_{s(k)})$ is nonzero, so at least one summand is positive.

blocks.label: lem:inner-k-covectors-wedge
blocks.title: lem:inner-k-covectors-wedge
blocks.body:
For all covectors $\omega^1, \ldots, \omega^k, \eta^1, \ldots, \eta^k \in V^*$,
\[
\langle \omega^1 \wedge \cdots \wedge \omega^k, \eta^1 \wedge \cdots \wedge \eta^k \rangle_e = \det\big(\langle \omega^i, \eta^j \rangle\big).
\]

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.innerForms_wedgeCovectors
lean.code:
theorem innerForms_wedgeCovectors (e : OrthonormalBasis ι' ℝ V) (a b : ι → (V →L[ℝ] ℝ)) :
    innerForms e (wedgeCovectors a) (wedgeCovectors b)
      = (Matrix.of fun i j => innerDual (a i) (b j)).det := by
  have hmaster := AlternatingMap.sum_det_submatrix_smul_apply
    (wedgeCovectors b).toAlternatingMap (e : ι' → V) (Matrix.of fun (i : ι) (m : ι') => a i (e m))
  simp only [smul_eq_mul, ContinuousAlternatingMap.coe_toAlternatingMap] at hmaster
  have hsub : ∀ s : ι → ι',
      ((Matrix.of fun (i : ι) (m : ι') => a i (e m)).submatrix id s).det
        = wedgeCovectors a (fun i => e (s i)) := by
    intro s
    rw [wedgeCovectors_apply]
    rfl
  have hrhs :
      ((wedgeCovectors b) fun i => ∑ m, (Matrix.of fun (i : ι) (m : ι') => a i (e m)) i m • e m)
        = (Matrix.of fun i j => innerDual (a i) (b j)).det := by
    have hsharp : ∀ i : ι,
        (∑ m, (Matrix.of fun (i : ι) (m : ι') => a i (e m)) i m • e m) = sharpL (a i) :=
      fun i => (sharpL_eq_sum e (a i)).symm
    simp only [hsharp, wedgeCovectors_apply]
    rw [← Matrix.det_transpose]
    congr 1
    ext i j
    simp only [Matrix.transpose_apply, Matrix.of_apply]
    rw [apply_sharpL, innerDual_comm]
  rw [Finset.sum_congr rfl fun s _ => congrArg (· * _) (hsub s), hrhs] at hmaster
  rw [innerForms, hmaster, nsmul_eq_mul]
  have hfac : ((Fintype.card ι).factorial : ℝ) ≠ 0 :=
    Nat.cast_ne_zero.2 (Nat.factorial_ne_zero _)
  field_simp

omit [DecidableEq ι'] in

blocks.tex:
Let $A_{im} = \omega^i(e_m)$. Apply Lemma~\ref{lem:alternating-cauchy-binet} with $\theta = \eta^1 \wedge \cdots \wedge \eta^k$, the family $(e_m)$, and the matrix $A$. By the determinant convention for the wedge product, $\det(A[\,\cdot\,, s]) = (\omega^1 \wedge \cdots \wedge \omega^k)(e_{s(1)},\ldots,e_{s(k)})$, so the left-hand side of that lemma is $k!$ times the sum defining $\langle \omega^1 \wedge \cdots \wedge \omega^k, \eta^1 \wedge \cdots \wedge \eta^k \rangle_e$. On the right-hand side, $\sum_m \omega^i(e_m)e_m = (\omega^i)^\sharp$ by the expansion of a vector in an orthonormal basis, so it is
\[
k!\,(\eta^1 \wedge \cdots \wedge \eta^k)\big((\omega^1)^\sharp, \ldots, (\omega^k)^\sharp\big) = k!\,\det\big(\eta^i((\omega^j)^\sharp)\big) = k!\,\det\big(\langle \eta^i, \omega^j\rangle\big),
\]
using (2.26a). Cancelling $k!$ and transposing the matrix gives the claim.

blocks.label: lem:k-covector-wedge-expansion
blocks.title: lem:k-covector-wedge-expansion
blocks.body:
For every $\omega \in \Lambda^k(V^*)$,
\[
k!\,\omega = \sum_{u \colon \{1,\ldots,k\} \to \iota'} \omega\big(e_{u(1)}, \ldots, e_{u(k)}\big)\; e_{u(1)}^\flat \wedge \cdots \wedge e_{u(k)}^\flat,
\]
where $v^\flat = \langle v, \cdot \rangle$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.factorial_smul_eq_sum_wedgeCovectors
lean.code:
theorem factorial_smul_eq_sum_wedgeCovectors (e : OrthonormalBasis ι' ℝ V)
    (w : V [⋀^ι]→L[ℝ] ℝ) :
    ((Fintype.card ι).factorial : ℝ) • w
      = ∑ u : ι → ι', w (fun i => e (u i)) • wedgeCovectors (fun i => flatL (e (u i))) := by
  refine ext_of_orthonormalBasis e fun v => ?_
  have hmaster := AlternatingMap.sum_det_submatrix_smul_apply
    w.toAlternatingMap (e : ι' → V) (Matrix.of fun (i : ι) (m : ι') => ⟪e (v i), e m⟫_ℝ)
  simp only [smul_eq_mul, ContinuousAlternatingMap.coe_toAlternatingMap] at hmaster
  have hcollapse : ∀ i : ι,
      (∑ m, (Matrix.of fun (i : ι) (m : ι') => ⟪e (v i), e m⟫_ℝ) i m • e m) = e (v i) :=
