entries.label: lem:riemannian-submersion-inner-eq
entries.title: A Riemannian submersion's base metric is the induced form
entries.body: If $\pi : (\tilde{M},\tilde{g}) \to (M,g)$ is a Riemannian submersion then $g_{\pi(x)} = \beta_x$ for every $x \in \tilde{M}$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.IsRiemannianSubmersion.inner_eq
lean.code:
theorem IsRiemannianSubmersion.inner_eq {g : RiemannianMetric I M} {g' : RiemannianMetric I' M'}
    {π : C^∞⟮I, M; I', M'⟯} (h : IsRiemannianSubmersion g g' π) (x : M) :
    g'.inner (π x) = submersionInner g π x := by
  ext u v
  rw [submersionInner_apply,
    ← h.2 x _ _ (horizontalLiftAt_mem g π x u) (horizontalLiftAt_mem g π x v),
    mfderiv_horizontalLiftAt g π h.1 x u, mfderiv_horizontalLiftAt g π h.1 x v]

deps.title: The horizontal lift is the unique $B$-orthogonal right inverse
deps.label: lem:horizontal-lift-linear-spec

deps.title: The inner product induced on the base
deps.label: def:submersion-inner

entries.label: prop:riemannian-submersion-metric-unique
entries.title: Uniqueness of the submersion metric
entries.body: Let $\tilde{g}$ be a Riemannian metric on $\tilde{M}$ and let $\pi : \tilde{M} \to M$ be a surjective smooth submersion. There is \emph{at most one} Riemannian metric $g$ on $M$ making $\pi$ a Riemannian submersion. This is the uniqueness half of Theorem \ref{thm:group-action-riemannian-submersion}, and it needs no group action.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.isRiemannianSubmersion_unique
lean.code:
theorem isRiemannianSubmersion_unique {g : RiemannianMetric I M}
    {g₁ g₂ : RiemannianMetric I' M'} {π : C^∞⟮I, M; I', M'⟯}
    (h₁ : IsRiemannianSubmersion g g₁ π) (h₂ : IsRiemannianSubmersion g g₂ π)
    (hsurj : Function.Surjective π) : g₁ = g₂ := by
  refine RiemannianMetric.ext_inner fun y => ?_
  obtain ⟨x, rfl⟩ := hsurj y
  rw [h₁.inner_eq x, h₂.inner_eq x]

end Defs

/-! ## The group action

Lee's hypotheses on the `G`-action, stated separately as he states them.  The
action enters the proof only through `submersionInner_smul`: it is what makes the
inner product on the base independent of the chosen point of the fibre. -/

section Action

variable
  {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [NormedSpace ℝ E] [FiniteDimensional ℝ E]
  {H : Type*} [TopologicalSpace H] {I : ModelWithCorners ℝ E H}
  {M : Type*} [TopologicalSpace M] [ChartedSpace H M] [IsManifold I ∞ M]
  {E' : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E'] [NormedSpace ℝ E'] [FiniteDimensional ℝ E']
  {H' : Type*} [TopologicalSpace H'] {I' : ModelWithCorners ℝ E' H'}
  {M' : Type*} [TopologicalSpace M'] [ChartedSpace H' M'] [IsManifold I' ∞ M']
  {G : Type*} [Group G] [MulAction G M]

variable (g : RiemannianMetric I M) (π : C^∞⟮I, M; I', M'⟯)

/-- **The action is by isometries** (Lee, §"Riemannian Submersions"): each
`x ↦ a • x` is smooth and its differential preserves `g`.

Lee phrases this as "the map `x ↦ φ · x` is an isometry for each `φ ∈ G`".  Being
a group action by smooth maps, each `a • ·` is automatically a diffeomorphism
(with inverse `a⁻¹ • ·`), so no invertibility need be assumed — it is derived in
`IsIsometricAction.mfderiv_smul_inv_smul`. -/
structure IsIsometricAction : Prop where
  /-- Each group element acts smoothly. -/
  contMDiff : ∀ a : G, ContMDiff I I ∞ (fun x : M => a • x)
  /-- Each group element preserves the metric. -/
  inner_smul : ∀ (a : G) (x : M) (v w : TangentSpace I x),
    g.inner (a • x) (mfderiv I I (fun y : M => a • y) x v)
      (mfderiv I I (fun y : M => a • y) x w) = g.inner x v w

deps.title: A Riemannian submersion's base metric is the induced form
deps.label: lem:riemannian-submersion-inner-eq

entries.label: lem:mfderiv-smul-inv-smul
entries.title: The action's differential is invertible
entries.body: Let $G$ act on $\tilde{M}$ by smooth maps. For $a \in G$ and $x \in \tilde{M}$, the differentials of $a \cdot {-}$ at $x$ and of $a^{-1} \cdot {-}$ at $a \cdot x$ are mutually inverse.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.IsIsometricAction.mfderiv_smul_inv_smul
lean.code:
theorem IsIsometricAction.mfderiv_smul_inv_smul (h : IsIsometricAction (G := G) g) (a : G) (x : M)
    (w : TangentSpace I (a • x)) :
    mfderiv I I (fun y : M => a • y) x
        (mfderiv I I (fun y : M => a⁻¹ • y) (a • x) w) = w := by
  have hcomp : ((fun y : M => a • y) ∘ (fun y : M => a⁻¹ • y)) = id :=
    funext fun y => smul_inv_smul a y
  have h1 := mfderiv_comp_apply_of_eq (I := I) (I' := I) (I'' := I) (x := a • x)
    (g := fun y : M => a • y) (f := fun y : M => a⁻¹ • y)
    (h.mdifferentiableAt a x) (h.mdifferentiableAt a⁻¹ (a • x)) (inv_smul_smul a x) w
  rw [← h1, hcomp, mfderiv_id]
  rfl

omit [FiniteDimensional ℝ E] [FiniteDimensional ℝ E'] [IsManifold I' ∞ M'] in

entries.label: lem:horizontal-space-smul
entries.title: A vertical action preserves the splitting
entries.body: Let $\pi : \tilde{M} \to M$ be a smooth submersion and let $G$ act on $\tilde{M}$ by smooth maps preserving $\tilde{g}$, with $\pi(a \cdot x) = \pi(x)$ for all $a, x$. Then $d(a \cdot {-})_x$ carries $V_x$ onto $V_{a \cdot x}$, and carries $H_x$ into $H_{a \cdot x}$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.horizontalSpace_smul
lean.code:
theorem horizontalSpace_smul (h : IsIsometricAction (G := G) g)
    (hvert : IsVerticalAction π (G := G)) (a : G) (x : M)
    {v : TangentSpace I x} (hv : v ∈ horizontalSpace g π x) :
    mfderiv I I (fun y : M => a • y) x v ∈ horizontalSpace g π (a • x) := by
  intro w hw
  obtain ⟨z, hz, rfl⟩ := verticalSpace_smul_surj h hvert a x hw
  rw [h.inner_smul a x v z]
  exact hv z hz

omit [IsManifold I' ∞ M'] in

entries.label: lem:submersion-inner-smul
entries.title: The induced form is constant on fibers
entries.body: With hypotheses as in Lemma \ref{lem:horizontal-space-smul}, $\beta_{a \cdot x} = \beta_x$ for every $a \in G$ and $x \in \tilde{M}$ (an identity of bilinear forms on $T_{\pi(x)}M = T_{\pi(a \cdot x)}M$).

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.submersionInner_smul
lean.code:
theorem submersionInner_smul (hπ : IsSubmersion π) (h : IsIsometricAction (G := G) g)
    (hvert : IsVerticalAction π (G := G)) (a : G) (x : M) :
    ((submersionInner g π (a • x) : _) : E' →L[ℝ] E' →L[ℝ] ℝ)
      = ((submersionInner g π x : _) : E' →L[ℝ] E' →L[ℝ] ℝ) := by
  -- `L_{a • x} = d(a • ·)_x ∘ L_x`, by uniqueness of the horizontal lift at `a • x`.
  have key : ∀ u : E', ((horizontalLiftAt g π (a • x) u : _) : E)
      = mfderiv I I (fun y : M => a • y) x (horizontalLiftAt g π x u) := by
    have huniq := (horizontalLiftAt_unique g π hπ (a • x)
      (L := ((mfderiv I I (fun y : M => a • y) x : _) : E →L[ℝ] E).comp
        ((horizontalLiftAt g π x : _) : E' →L[ℝ] E))
      (fun u => by
        show mfderiv I I' π (a • x)
          (mfderiv I I (fun y : M => a • y) x (horizontalLiftAt g π x u)) = u
        rw [mfderiv_comp_smul h hvert a x]
        exact mfderiv_horizontalLiftAt g π hπ x u)
      (fun u => horizontalSpace_smul h hvert a x (horizontalLiftAt_mem g π x u))).symm
    exact fun u => congrArg (fun L => L u) huniq
  ext u v
  show g.inner (a • x) (horizontalLiftAt g π (a • x) u) (horizontalLiftAt g π (a • x) v)
    = g.inner x (horizontalLiftAt g π x u) (horizontalLiftAt g π x v)
  rw [show (horizontalLiftAt g π (a • x) u : TangentSpace I (a • x))
        = mfderiv I I (fun y : M => a • y) x (horizontalLiftAt g π x u) from key u,
    show (horizontalLiftAt g π (a • x) v : TangentSpace I (a • x))
        = mfderiv I I (fun y : M => a • y) x (horizontalLiftAt g π x v) from key v]
  exact h.inner_smul a x _ _

end Action

/-! ## Theorem 2.28: existence of the quotient metric

Everything above is in place: `submersionInner` is the only candidate
(`isRiemannianSubmersion_unique`), and `submersionInner_smul` says it does not
depend on which point of the fibre computes it.  What remains is to turn that
into a `RiemannianMetric` on `M'` — to choose a point in each fibre, and to check
that the resulting field is symmetric, positive definite and *smooth*.

The choice is `Function.surjInv`, which is where surjectivity of `π` is used; the
choice is harmless precisely by `submersionInner_congr`.  Smoothness is the only
analytic step, and it is the reason Appendix A.17 exists: `surjInv` is not
smooth — it is not even continuous — so it cannot be differentiated.  Instead one
replaces it, *near each point*, by a smooth local section `σ`, which represents
the same field because the fibre point does not matter, and then

  `g'_y = g_{σ y}(L_{σ y} ·, L_{σ y} ·)`

is a composite of smooth things: `σ` is smooth, `g` is a smooth 2-tensor field,
and `x ↦ L_x` is smooth in tangent coordinates
(`contMDiffAt_horizontalLiftAt_inTangentCoordinates`).  Assembling those three is
`contMDiffAt_bilinearCompOf`, whose generality — an *arbitrary* smooth family of
linear maps rather than a differential — exists exactly for this: `L` is not the
differential of any map.

`I.Boundaryless` is inherited from `LeeLib.AppendixA.exists_localSection`, and is
needed only upstairs; `M'` may have boundary. -/

section Existence

variable
  {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [NormedSpace ℝ E] [FiniteDimensional ℝ E]
  {H : Type*} [TopologicalSpace H] {I : ModelWithCorners ℝ E H} [I.Boundaryless]
  -- … (truncated)

entries.label: lem:submersion-inner-congr
entries.title: The induced form depends only on the fiber
entries.body: With hypotheses as in Lemma \ref{lem:horizontal-space-smul}, suppose in addition that $G$ acts transitively on the fibers of $\pi$. If $\pi(x) = \pi(y)$ then $\beta_x = \beta_y$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.submersionInner_congr
lean.code:
theorem submersionInner_congr (hπ : IsSubmersion π) (h : IsIsometricAction (G := G) g)
    (hvert : IsVerticalAction π (G := G)) (htrans : IsFibreTransitiveAction π (G := G))
    {x y : M} (hxy : π x = π y) :
    ((submersionInner g π y : _) : E' →L[ℝ] E' →L[ℝ] ℝ)
      = ((submersionInner g π x : _) : E' →L[ℝ] E' →L[ℝ] ℝ) := by
  obtain ⟨a, rfl⟩ := htrans x y hxy
  exact submersionInner_smul hπ h hvert a x

omit [I.Boundaryless] [IsManifold I' ∞ M'] in

entries.label: lem:horizontal-lift-in-coordinates-smooth
entries.title: The horizontal lift is smooth in tangent coordinates
entries.body: Let $\pi : \tilde{M} \to M$ be a smooth submersion of a Riemannian manifold $(\tilde{M},\tilde{g})$ and let $x_0 \in \tilde{M}$. The family of linear maps $x \mapsto L_x : T_{\pi(x)}M \to T_x\tilde{M}$, read in tangent coordinates around $x_0$ and around $\pi(x_0)$, is smooth at $x_0$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.contMDiffAt_horizontalLiftAt_inTangentCoordinates
lean.code:
theorem contMDiffAt_horizontalLiftAt_inTangentCoordinates (hπ : IsSubmersion π) (x₀ : M) :
    ContMDiffAt I 𝓘(ℝ, E' →L[ℝ] E) ∞
      (inTangentCoordinates I' I (fun x => π x) id (fun x => horizontalLiftAt g π x) x₀) x₀ := by
  have hx₀ : x₀ ∈ (trivializationAt E (TangentSpace I) x₀).baseSet := by simp
  have hy₀ : π x₀ ∈ (trivializationAt E' (TangentSpace I') (π x₀)).baseSet := by simp
  -- The trivialized metric is still positive definite: `θ` is an isomorphism.
  have hBpos : ∀ v : E, v ≠ 0 → 0 < metricInCoordinates g x₀ x₀ v v := by
    intro v hv
    rw [metricInCoordinates_apply g x₀ x₀ (mem_chart_source H x₀)]
    refine g.pos x₀ _ ?_
    exact fun h => hv (((trivializationAt E (TangentSpace I) x₀).continuousLinearEquivAt ℝ x₀
      hx₀).symm.map_eq_zero_iff.mp h)
  -- The trivialized differential is still surjective: it is `dπ` between two isomorphisms.
  have hAsurj : Function.Surjective (mfderivInCoordinates π x₀ x₀) := by
    intro w
    set ι₀ := (trivializationAt E' (TangentSpace I') (π x₀)).continuousLinearEquivAt ℝ (π x₀) hy₀
    set θ₀ := (trivializationAt E (TangentSpace I) x₀).continuousLinearEquivAt ℝ x₀ hx₀
    obtain ⟨s, hs⟩ := hπ x₀ (ι₀.symm w)
    refine ⟨θ₀ s, ?_⟩
    rw [mfderivInCoordinates_apply]
    have hθ : (trivializationAt E (TangentSpace I) x₀).symmL ℝ x₀ (θ₀ s) = s := θ₀.symm_apply_apply s
    rw [hθ, hs, ← Bundle.Trivialization.coe_continuousLinearEquivAt_eq (R := ℝ) _ hy₀]
    exact ι₀.apply_symm_apply w
  -- Near `x₀`, the lift in tangent coordinates is the lift of the trivialized data.
  have key : (inTangentCoordinates I' I (fun x => π x) id (fun x => horizontalLiftAt g π x) x₀)
      =ᶠ[nhds x₀] (fun x => horizontalLift (metricInCoordinates g x₀ x)
        (mfderivInCoordinates π x₀ x)) := by
    filter_upwards [((chartAt H x₀).open_source).mem_nhds (mem_chart_source H x₀),
      π.contMDiff.continuous.continuousAt.preimage_mem_nhds
        (((chartAt H' (π x₀)).open_source).mem_nhds (mem_chart_source H' (π x₀)))]
      with x hx hy
    have hxb : x ∈ (trivializationAt E (TangentSpace I) x₀).baseSet := by simpa using hx
    have hyb : π x ∈ (trivializationAt E' (TangentSpace I') (π x₀)).baseSet := by simpa using hy
    set θ := (trivializationAt E (TangentSpace I) x₀).continuousLinearEquivAt ℝ x hxb
    set ι := (trivializationAt E' (TangentSpace I') (π x₀)).continuousLinearEquivAt ℝ (π x) hyb
    have hB' : ∀ a b : E, metricInCoordinates g x₀ x a b = g.inner x (θ.symm a) (θ.symm b) :=
      metricInCoordinates_apply g x₀ x hx
    have hA' : ∀ a : E, mfderivInCoordinates π x₀ x a = ι (mfderiv I I' π x (θ.symm a)) := by
      intro a
      rw [mfderivInCoordinates_apply,
        ← Bundle.Trivialization.coe_continuousLinearEquivAt_eq (R := ℝ) _ hyb]
      rfl
    ext u
    rw [horizontalLift_congr (E := E) (E' := E') (F := E) (F' := E')
      (inner_pos g x) (hπ x) θ ι hB' hA' u]
    show inTangentCoordinates I' I (fun x => π x) id (fun x => horizontalLiftAt g π x) x₀ x u = _
    simp only [inTangentCoordinates, id_eq]
    rw [ContinuousLinearMap.inCoordinates_eq hyb hxb]
    rfl
  rw [key.contMDiffAt_iff]
  exact contMDiffAt_horizontalLift (contMDiffAt_metricInCoordinates g x₀)
    (contMDiffAt_mfderivInCoordinates π x₀) hBpos hAsurj

entries.label: thm:group-action-riemannian-submersion
entries.title: thm:group-action-riemannian-submersion
entries.body: Let $(\tilde{M}, \tilde{g})$ be a Riemannian manifold, let $\pi: \tilde{M} \to M$ be a surjective smooth submersion, and let $G$ be a group acting on $\tilde{M}$. If the action is isometric, vertical, and transitive on fibers, then there is a unique Riemannian metric on $M$ such that $\pi$ is a Riemannian submersion.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.existsUnique_isRiemannianSubmersion_metric
lean.code:
theorem existsUnique_isRiemannianSubmersion_metric (hπ : IsSubmersion π)
    (h : IsIsometricAction (G := G) g) (hvert : IsVerticalAction π (G := G))
    (htrans : IsFibreTransitiveAction π (G := G)) (hsurj : Function.Surjective π) :
    ∃! g' : RiemannianMetric I' M', IsRiemannianSubmersion g g' π :=
  ⟨quotientMetric g π hπ h hvert htrans hsurj,
    isRiemannianSubmersion_quotientMetric hπ h hvert htrans hsurj,
    fun _ hg' => isRiemannianSubmersion_unique hg'
      (isRiemannianSubmersion_quotientMetric hπ h hvert htrans hsurj) hsurj⟩

entries.label: cor:quotient-manifold-lie-group-riemannian-metric
entries.title: cor:quotient-manifold-lie-group-riemannian-metric
entries.body: Suppose $(\tilde{M}, \tilde{g})$ is a Riemannian manifold, and $G$ is a Lie group acting smoothly, freely, properly, and isometrically on $\tilde{M}$. Then the orbit space $M = \tilde{M}/G$ has a unique smooth manifold structure and Riemannian metric such that $\pi$ is a Riemannian submersion.

entries.label: ex:fubini-study-metric
entries.title: The Fubini--Study Metric
entries.body:
Let $n$ be a positive integer, and consider the complex projective space $\mathbb{CP}^n$ defined in Example C.19. That example shows that the map $\pi : \mathbb{C}^{n+1} \setminus \{0\} \to \mathbb{CP}^n$ sending each point in $\mathbb{C}^{n+1} \setminus \{0\}$ to its span is a surjective smooth submersion. Identifying $\mathbb{C}^{n+1}$ with $\mathbb{R}^{2n+2}$ endowed with its Euclidean metric, we can view the unit sphere $S^{2n+1}$ with its round metric $\bar{g}$ as an embedded Riemannian submanifold of $\mathbb{C}^{n+1} \setminus \{0\}$. Let $p: S^{2n+1} \to \mathbb{CP}^n$ denote the restriction of the map $\pi$. Then $p$ is smooth, and it is surjective because every 1-dimensional complex subspace contains elements of unit norm. We need to show that it is a submersion. Let $z_0 \in S^{2n+1}$ and set $\zeta_0 = p(z_0) \in \mathbb{CP}^n$. Since $\pi$ is a smooth submersion, it has a smooth local section $\sigma : U \to \mathbb{C}^{n+1}$ defined on a neighborhood $U$ of $\zeta_0$ and satisfying $\sigma(\zeta_0) = z_0$ (Thm. A.17). Let $\nu: \mathbb{C}^{n+1} \setminus \{0\} \to S^{2n+1}$ be the radial projection onto the sphere:
\[
\nu(z) = \frac{z}{|z|}.
\]

Since dividing an element of $\mathbb{C}^{n+1}$ by a nonzero scalar does not change its span, it follows that $p \circ \nu = \pi$. Therefore, if we set $\bar{\sigma} = \nu \circ \sigma$, we have $p \circ \bar{\sigma} = p \circ \nu \circ \sigma = \pi \circ \sigma = \text{Id}_U$, so $\bar{\sigma}$ is a local section of $p$. By Theorem A.17, this shows that $p$ is a submersion.

Define an action of $S^1$ on $S^{2n+1}$ by complex multiplication:
\[
\lambda \cdot (z^1,\ldots,z^{n+1}) = (\lambda z^1,\ldots,\lambda z^{n+1}),
\]
for $\lambda \in S^1$ (viewed as a complex number of norm 1) and $z = (z^1,\ldots,z^{n+1}) \in S^{2n+1}$. This is easily seen to be isometric, vertical, and transitive on fibers of $p$. By Theorem 2.28, therefore, there is a unique metric on $\mathbb{CP}^n$ such that the map $p: S^{2n+1} \to \mathbb{CP}^n$ is a Riemannian submersion. This metric is called the \textbf{Fubini--Study metric}; you will have a chance to study its geometric properties in Problems 3-19 and 8-13.

entries.label: def:smooth-covering-map
entries.title: def:smooth-covering-map
entries.body:
A smooth map $\pi: \tilde{M} \to M$ is a \emph{smooth covering map} if it is surjective, is a topological covering map, and is a local diffeomorphism.

By Proposition A.49(c) a topological covering map is a smooth covering map, in the sense of Appendix A, exactly when it is a local diffeomorphism, so this description agrees with the sheetwise one.

deps.title: Elementary Properties of Smooth Covering Maps
deps.label: prop:elementary-covering-maps

entries.label: def:covering-automorphism-group
entries.title: def:covering-automorphism-group
entries.body: Let $\pi: \tilde{M} \to M$ be a smooth covering map. A \emph{covering automorphism} of $\pi$ is a diffeomorphism $\varphi: \tilde{M} \to \tilde{M}$ satisfying $\pi \circ \varphi = \pi$. The covering automorphisms form a group $\operatorname{Aut}_\pi(\tilde{M})$ under composition, which acts on $\tilde{M}$ by evaluation.

entries.label: def:normal-covering-fibre-transitive
entries.title: def:normal-covering-fibre-transitive
entries.body:
A smooth covering map $\pi: \tilde{M} \to M$ is a \emph{normal covering} if $\operatorname{Aut}_\pi(\tilde{M})$ acts transitively on each fiber of $\pi$.

By Proposition C.21 this holds if and only if the image of $\pi_*: \pi_1(\tilde{M}, \tilde{x}) \to \pi_1(M, \pi(\tilde{x}))$ is a normal subgroup of $\pi_1(M, \pi(\tilde{x}))$, which is the condition the name refers to.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.IsNormalCovering
lean.code:
def IsNormalCovering (π : C^∞⟮I, M; I', M'⟯) : Prop :=
  IsFibreTransitiveAction (G := CoveringAut π) π

entries.label: def:covering-automorphism-invariant-metric
entries.title: def:covering-automorphism-invariant-metric
entries.body: Let $\pi: \tilde{M} \to M$ be a smooth covering map. A metric $\tilde{g}$ on $\tilde{M}$ is \emph{invariant under all covering automorphisms} if $\varphi^*\tilde{g} = \tilde{g}$ for every $\varphi \in \operatorname{Aut}_\pi(\tilde{M})$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.IsCoveringAutInvariant
lean.code:
def IsCoveringAutInvariant (g : RiemannianMetric I M) (π : C^∞⟮I, M; I', M'⟯) : Prop :=
  ∀ φ : CoveringAut π, IsMetricPreserving g g (φ : M → M)

entries.label: def:riemannian-covering
entries.title: def:riemannian-covering
entries.body: Let $(\tilde{M}, \tilde{g})$ and $(M, g)$ be Riemannian manifolds. A smooth covering map $\pi: \tilde{M} \to M$ is a \emph{Riemannian covering} if $\pi^*g = \tilde{g}$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.IsRiemannianCovering
lean.code:
def IsRiemannianCovering (g : RiemannianMetric I M) (g' : RiemannianMetric I' M')
    (π : C^∞⟮I, M; I', M'⟯) : Prop :=
  IsSmoothCoveringMap π ∧ IsMetricPreserving g g' (π : M → M')

omit [FiniteDimensional ℝ E] [FiniteDimensional ℝ E'] in

entries.label: lem:riemannian-covering-local-isometry
entries.title: A Riemannian Covering Is a Local Isometry
entries.body: Every Riemannian covering is a local isometry.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.IsRiemannianCovering.isLocalIsometry
lean.code:
theorem IsRiemannianCovering.isLocalIsometry
    {g : RiemannianMetric I M} {g' : RiemannianMetric I' M'} {π : C^∞⟮I, M; I', M'⟯}
    (h : IsRiemannianCovering g g' π) : IsLocalIsometry g g' (π : M → M') :=
  ⟨h.1.isLocalDiffeomorph, h.2⟩

/-! ## An equidimensional Riemannian submersion is a local isometry -/

variable {g : RiemannianMetric I M} {g' : RiemannianMetric I' M'} {π : C^∞⟮I, M; I', M'⟯}

omit [FiniteDimensional ℝ E] [FiniteDimensional ℝ E'] in

entries.label: prop:equidimensional-riemannian-submersion-local-isometry
entries.title: prop:equidimensional-riemannian-submersion-local-isometry
entries.body: Let $\pi: \tilde{M} \to M$ be a smooth submersion whose differential $d\pi_x$ is injective at every $x \in \tilde{M}$, and let $\tilde{g}$ and $g$ be metrics on $\tilde{M}$ and $M$. Then $\pi$ is a Riemannian submersion if and only if $\pi^*g = \tilde{g}$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.isRiemannianSubmersion_iff_isMetricPreserving
lean.code:
theorem isRiemannianSubmersion_iff_isMetricPreserving (hπ : IsSubmersion π)
    (hinj : ∀ x : M, Function.Injective (mfderiv I I' π x)) :
    IsRiemannianSubmersion g g' π ↔ IsMetricPreserving g g' (π : M → M') := by
  refine ⟨fun h p => ?_, fun h => ⟨hπ, fun x v w _ _ => ?_⟩⟩
  · ext v w
    rw [pullbackForm_apply]
    exact h.2 p v w (by rw [horizontalSpace_eq_top g π (hinj p)]; trivial)
      (by rw [horizontalSpace_eq_top g π (hinj p)]; trivial)
  · exact h.inner_mfderiv x v w

omit [FiniteDimensional ℝ E] [FiniteDimensional ℝ E'] in

entries.label: prop:riemannian-covering-metric
entries.title: prop:riemannian-covering-metric
entries.body: Suppose $\pi: \tilde{M} \to M$ is a smooth normal covering map, and $\tilde{g}$ is any metric on $\tilde{M}$ that is invariant under all covering automorphisms. Then there is a unique metric $g$ on $M$ such that $\pi$ is a Riemannian covering.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.existsUnique_isRiemannianCovering_metric
lean.code:
theorem existsUnique_isRiemannianCovering_metric (hπ : IsSmoothCoveringMap π)
    (hnormal : IsNormalCovering π) (hinv : IsCoveringAutInvariant g π) :
    ∃! g' : RiemannianMetric I' M', IsRiemannianCovering g g' π := by
  have hsub : IsSubmersion π := hπ.isSubmersion
  have hbridge : ∀ g' : RiemannianMetric I' M',
      IsRiemannianSubmersion g g' π ↔ IsRiemannianCovering g g' π := fun g' =>
    (isRiemannianSubmersion_iff_isMetricPreserving hsub hπ.injective_mfderiv).trans
      ⟨fun h => ⟨hπ, h⟩, fun h => h.2⟩
  obtain ⟨g', hg', huniq⟩ := existsUnique_isRiemannianSubmersion_metric (G := CoveringAut π)
    hsub (isIsometricAction_coveringAut hinv) isVerticalAction_coveringAut hnormal hπ.surjective
  exact ⟨g', (hbridge g').mp hg', fun g'' h => huniq g'' ((hbridge g'').mpr h)⟩

deps.title: Elementary Properties of Smooth Covering Maps
deps.label: prop:elementary-covering-maps

entries.label: prop:riemannian-quotient-metric
entries.title: prop:riemannian-quotient-metric
entries.body: Suppose $(\widetilde{M}, \tilde{g})$ is a Riemannian manifold, and $\Gamma$ is a discrete Lie group acting smoothly, freely, properly, and isometrically on $\widetilde{M}$. Then $\widetilde{M}/\Gamma$ has a unique Riemannian metric such that the quotient map $\pi: \widetilde{M} \to \widetilde{M}/\Gamma$ is a normal Riemannian covering.

entries.label: cor:isometric-to-quotient
entries.title: cor:isometric-to-quotient
entries.body: Suppose $(M, g)$ and $(\widetilde{M}, \tilde{g})$ are connected Riemannian manifolds, $\pi: \widetilde{M} \to M$ is a normal Riemannian covering map, and $\Gamma = \text{Aut}_\pi(\widetilde{M})$. Then $M$ is isometric to $\widetilde{M}/\Gamma$.

entries.label: ex:real-projective-space-metric
entries.title: ex:real-projective-space-metric
entries.body: The two-element group $\Gamma = \{\pm1\}$ acts smoothly, freely, properly, and isometrically on $S^n$ by multiplication. Example C.24 shows that the quotient space is diffeomorphic to the real projective space $\mathbb{RP}^n$ and the quotient map $q: S^n \to \mathbb{RP}^n$ is a smooth normal covering map. Because the action is isometric, Proposition 2.32 shows that there is a unique metric on $\mathbb{RP}^n$ such that $q$ is a Riemannian covering.

entries.label: ex:open-mobius-band
entries.title: The Open Möbius Band
entries.body: The open Möbius band is the quotient space $M = \mathbb{R}^2/\mathbb{Z}$, where $\mathbb{Z}$ acts on $\mathbb{R}^2$ by $n \cdot (x,y) = (x+n, (-1)^n y)$. This action is smooth, free, proper, and isometric, and therefore $M$ inherits a flat Riemannian metric such that the quotient map is a Riemannian covering. (See Problem 2-8.)

entries.label: def:flat-map
entries.title: Lowering an index
entries.body: Let $(M,g)$ be a Riemannian manifold and $p \in M$. The \emph{flat map} at $p$ is the linear map $\flat : T_p M \to T_p^* M$ given by $v^\flat = g_p(v, \cdot)$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.flat
lean.code:
noncomputable def flat : TangentSpace I p →L[ℝ] (TangentSpace I p →L[ℝ] ℝ) := g.inner p

omit [FiniteDimensional ℝ E] in

entries.label: lem:flat-bijective
entries.title: The flat map is a bijection
entries.body: Let $(M,g)$ be a Riemannian manifold with $\dim M < \infty$ and $p \in M$. Then $\flat : T_p M \to T_p^* M$ is bijective.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.flat_bijective
lean.code:
theorem flat_bijective : Function.Bijective (flat g p) :=
  ⟨Function.LeftInverse.injective (sharp_flat g p),
    Function.RightInverse.surjective (flat_sharp g p)⟩

entries.label: def:sharp-map
entries.title: Raising an index
entries.body:
Let $(M,g)$ be a Riemannian manifold with $\dim M < \infty$ and $p \in M$. The \emph{sharp map} at $p$ is the inverse $\sharp = \flat^{-1} : T_p^* M \to T_p M$, which exists by Lemma \ref{lem:flat-bijective}. Thus $\alpha^\sharp$ is the unique $v \in T_p M$ with $g_p(v,\cdot) = \alpha$, characterized by
\[
g_p(\alpha^\sharp, w) = \alpha(w) \qquad \text{for all } w \in T_p M .
\]

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.sharp
lean.code:
noncomputable def sharp (a : TangentSpace I p →L[ℝ] ℝ) : TangentSpace I p :=
  letI : Bundle.RiemannianBundle (TangentSpace I : M → Type _) := ⟨g.toRiemannianMetric⟩
  haveI : FiniteDimensional ℝ (TangentSpace I p) := inferInstanceAs (FiniteDimensional ℝ E)
  (InnerProductSpace.toDual ℝ (TangentSpace I p)).symm a

deps.title: The flat map is a bijection
deps.label: lem:flat-bijective

entries.label: def:gradient
entries.title: The gradient
entries.body: Let $(M,g)$ be a Riemannian manifold with $\dim M < \infty$ and $f : M \to \mathbb{R}$ smooth. The \emph{gradient} of $f$ is $\operatorname{grad} f = (df)^\sharp$, so that $\operatorname{grad} f|_p = (df_p)^\sharp \in T_p M$ for each $p \in M$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.grad
lean.code: noncomputable def grad : TangentSpace I p := sharp g p (extDerivFun (I := I) f p)

entries.label: lem:gradient-characterization
entries.title: Characterization of the gradient
entries.body: Let $(M,g)$ be a Riemannian manifold with $\dim M < \infty$ and $f : M \to \mathbb{R}$ smooth. Then (2.14) holds: $df_p(w) = \langle \operatorname{grad} f|_p, w\rangle$ for all $p \in M$ and $w \in T_p M$. Moreover $\operatorname{grad} f|_p$ is the unique vector with this property.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.innerAt_grad
lean.code:
@[simp] theorem innerAt_grad (w : TangentSpace I p) :
    g.innerAt p (grad g f p) w = extDerivFun (I := I) f p w :=
  innerAt_sharp g p _ w

deps.title: The flat map is a bijection
deps.label: lem:flat-bijective

entries.label: lem:gradient-orthogonal-kernel
entries.title: The gradient is orthogonal to the kernel of the differential
entries.body: Let $(M,g)$ be a Riemannian manifold with $\dim M < \infty$, let $f : M \to \mathbb{R}$ be smooth, and let $p \in M$. If $w \in T_p M$ satisfies $df_p(w) = 0$, then $\langle \operatorname{grad} f|_p, w \rangle = 0$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.innerAt_grad_eq_zero_of_mem_ker
lean.code:
theorem innerAt_grad_eq_zero_of_mem_ker (w : TangentSpace I p)
    (hw : mfderiv I 𝓘(ℝ, ℝ) f p w = 0) : g.innerAt p (grad g f p) w = 0 := by
  rw [innerAt_grad]
  show (NormedSpace.fromTangentSpace (f p)).toContinuousLinearMap (mfderiv I 𝓘(ℝ, ℝ) f p w) = 0
  rw [hw, map_zero]

entries.label: lem:gradient-eq-zero-iff
entries.title: Critical points are the zeros of the gradient
entries.body: Let $(M,g)$ be a Riemannian manifold with $\dim M < \infty$, let $f : M \to \mathbb{R}$ be smooth, and let $p \in M$. Then $\operatorname{grad} f|_p = 0$ if and only if $df_p = 0$. Hence the regular points of $f$ are exactly the points where $\operatorname{grad} f$ does not vanish.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.grad_eq_zero_iff
lean.code:
theorem grad_eq_zero_iff : grad g f p = 0 ↔ extDerivFun (I := I) f p = 0 := by
  constructor
  · intro h
    refine ContinuousLinearMap.ext fun w => ?_
    rw [← innerAt_grad g f p w, h]
    simp [RiemannianMetric.innerAt]
  · intro h
    refine flat_injective g p (ContinuousLinearMap.ext fun w => ?_)
    rw [flat_apply, innerAt_grad, h]
    simp

deps.title: The flat map is a bijection
deps.label: lem:flat-bijective

entries.label: lem:gradient-orthogonal-iff
entries.title: Orthogonality to the gradient characterizes the kernel of the differential
entries.body: Let $(M,g)$ be a Riemannian manifold with $\dim M < \infty$, let $f : M \to \mathbb{R}$ be smooth, let $p \in M$, and let $w \in T_p M$. Then $\langle \operatorname{grad} f|_p, w\rangle = 0$ if and only if $df_p(w) = 0$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.innerAt_grad_eq_zero_iff
lean.code:
theorem innerAt_grad_eq_zero_iff (w : TangentSpace I p) :
    g.innerAt p (grad g f p) w = 0 ↔ mfderiv I 𝓘(ℝ, ℝ) f p w = 0 := by
  refine ⟨fun h => ?_, innerAt_grad_eq_zero_of_mem_ker g f p w⟩
  rw [innerAt_grad] at h
  -- `extDerivFun f p w` is `fromTangentSpace (f p) (df_p w)`, and
  -- `fromTangentSpace` is a continuous linear equivalence, hence injective.
  have h' : (NormedSpace.fromTangentSpace (f p)).toContinuousLinearMap
      (mfderiv I 𝓘(ℝ, ℝ) f p w) = 0 := h
  have := (NormedSpace.fromTangentSpace (f p)).injective
    (a₁ := mfderiv I 𝓘(ℝ, ℝ) f p w) (a₂ := 0) (by simpa using h')
  exact this

end GradKer

/-! ### The regular-point set -/

section RegularSet

variable (I) in

entries.label: def:regular-set
entries.title: The regular set
entries.body: Let $M$ be a smooth manifold and $f : M \to \mathbb{R}$ smooth. The \emph{regular set} of $f$ is $\mathcal{R} = \{x \in M : df_x \neq 0\}$, the set of regular points of $f$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.regularSet
lean.code: def regularSet (f : M → ℝ) : Set M := {x : M | mfderiv I 𝓘(ℝ, ℝ) f x ≠ 0}

entries.label: lem:regular-set-eq-gradient-nonvanishing
entries.title: The regular set is where the gradient does not vanish
entries.body: Let $(M,g)$ be a Riemannian manifold with $\dim M < \infty$ and $f : M \to \mathbb{R}$ smooth. Then $x \in \mathcal{R}$ if and only if $\operatorname{grad} f|_x \neq 0$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.mem_regularSet_iff_grad_ne_zero
lean.code:
theorem mem_regularSet_iff_grad_ne_zero (g : RiemannianMetric I M) (f : M → ℝ)
    (x : M) : x ∈ regularSet I f ↔ grad g f x ≠ 0 := by
  rw [mem_regularSet_iff, ne_eq, ne_eq, grad_eq_zero_iff]
  refine not_congr ⟨fun h => ?_, fun h => ?_⟩
  · ext w
    simp only [ContinuousLinearMap.zero_apply]
    exact congrArg (NormedSpace.fromTangentSpace (f x)).toContinuousLinearMap
      (congrFun (congrArg DFunLike.coe h) w) |>.trans (by simp)
  · ext w
    have := congrFun (congrArg DFunLike.coe h) w
    simp only [ContinuousLinearMap.zero_apply] at this ⊢
    exact (NormedSpace.fromTangentSpace (f x)).injective (by simpa using this)

omit [FiniteDimensional ℝ E] [I.Boundaryless] in

deps.title: Critical points are the zeros of the gradient
deps.label: lem:gradient-eq-zero-iff

entries.label: lem:mfderiv-nonvanishing-open
entries.title: The non-vanishing locus of a differential is open
entries.body: Let $F : M \to N$ be a smooth map of smooth manifolds. Then $\{x \in M : dF_x \neq 0\}$ is open in $M$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.isOpen_setOf_mfderiv_ne_zero
lean.code:
theorem isOpen_setOf_mfderiv_ne_zero
    {E' : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E'] [NormedSpace ℝ E']
    {H' : Type*} [TopologicalSpace H'] {I' : ModelWithCorners ℝ E' H'}
    {M' : Type*} [TopologicalSpace M'] [ChartedSpace H' M'] [IsManifold I' ∞ M']
    {f : M → M'} (hf : ContMDiff I I' ∞ f) :
    IsOpen {x : M | mfderiv I I' f x ≠ 0} := by
  rw [isOpen_iff_mem_nhds]
  intro x₀ hx₀
  set sT := trivializationAt E (TangentSpace I) x₀ with hsT
  set tT := trivializationAt E' (TangentSpace I') (f x₀) with htT
  have hx₀s : x₀ ∈ sT.baseSet := mem_baseSet_trivializationAt E (TangentSpace I) x₀
  have hfx₀ : f x₀ ∈ tT.baseSet := mem_baseSet_trivializationAt E' (TangentSpace I') (f x₀)
  set D := inTangentCoordinates I I' id f (fun x => mfderiv I I' f x) x₀ with hD
  have hDsmooth : ContMDiffAt I 𝓘(ℝ, E →L[ℝ] E') ∞ D x₀ :=
    ContMDiffAt.mfderiv_const (I := I) (I' := I') hf.contMDiffAt (by simp)
  -- In tangent coordinates the differential is conjugated by the two trivializations.
  have hDu : ∀ x, ∀ hx : x ∈ sT.baseSet, ∀ hfx : f x ∈ tT.baseSet, ∀ u : E,
      D x u = tT.continuousLinearEquivAt ℝ (f x) hfx
        (mfderiv I I' f x ((sT.continuousLinearEquivAt ℝ x hx).symm u)) := by
    intro x hx hfx u
    rw [hD]
    simp only [inTangentCoordinates, id_eq]
    rw [ContinuousLinearMap.inCoordinates_eq hx hfx]
    rfl
  -- Hence it vanishes exactly where the differential does.
  have key : ∀ x, ∀ hx : x ∈ sT.baseSet, ∀ hfx : f x ∈ tT.baseSet,
      (D x = 0 ↔ mfderiv I I' f x = 0) := by
    intro x hx hfx
    constructor
    · intro h
      ext v
      have hu := hDu x hx hfx (sT.continuousLinearEquivAt ℝ x hx v)
      rw [h] at hu
      simp only [ContinuousLinearMap.zero_apply, ContinuousLinearEquiv.symm_apply_apply] at hu
      have := (tT.continuousLinearEquivAt ℝ (f x) hfx).map_eq_zero_iff.mp hu.symm
      simp [this]
    · intro h
      ext u
      rw [hDu x hx hfx u, h]
      simpa using (tT.continuousLinearEquivAt ℝ (f x) hfx).map_zero
  have hDx₀ : D x₀ ≠ 0 := fun h => hx₀ ((key x₀ hx₀s hfx₀).mp h)
  have hbase : {x : M | f x ∈ tT.baseSet} ∈ 𝓝 x₀ :=
    hf.continuous.continuousAt (tT.open_baseSet.mem_nhds hfx₀)
  filter_upwards [hDsmooth.continuousAt (isOpen_ne.mem_nhds hDx₀),
    sT.open_baseSet.mem_nhds hx₀s, hbase] with x hx hxs hxf
  exact fun h0 => hx ((key x hxs hxf).mpr h0)

omit [FiniteDimensional ℝ E] [I.Boundaryless] in

entries.label: lem:regular-set-open
entries.title: The regular set is open
entries.body: Let $M$ be a smooth manifold and $f : M \to \mathbb{R}$ smooth. Then the regular set $\mathcal{R}$ of $f$ is open in $M$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.isOpen_regularSet
lean.code:
theorem isOpen_regularSet {f : M → ℝ} (hf : ContMDiff I 𝓘(ℝ, ℝ) ∞ f) :
    IsOpen (regularSet I f) :=
  isOpen_setOf_mfderiv_ne_zero hf

end RegularSet

/-! ### Proposition 2.37: `grad f` is normal to a regular level set -/

section Normal

variable {f : M → ℝ} (g : RiemannianMetric I M) (hf : ContMDiff I 𝓘(ℝ, ℝ) ∞ f)
  (n : ℕ) [Fact (Module.finrank ℝ E = n + 1)] (c : ℝ)

deps.title: The non-vanishing locus of a differential is open
deps.label: lem:mfderiv-nonvanishing-open

entries.label: prop:gradient-normal-regular-level-set
entries.title: The gradient is normal to a regular level set
entries.body:
Let $(M,g)$ be a Riemannian manifold of dimension $n+1$ without boundary, let $f \in C^\infty(M)$, and let $c$ be a regular value of $f$. Then for every $x_0 \in f^{-1}(c)$ and every $w \in T_{x_0} f^{-1}(c)$,
\[
  \bigl\langle \operatorname{grad} f|_{x_0},\, d(\iota_c)_{x_0}(w) \bigr\rangle = 0 ,
\]
where $\iota_c : f^{-1}(c) \hookrightarrow M$ is the inclusion. That is, $\operatorname{grad} f$ is normal to $f^{-1}(c)$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.innerAt_grad_mfderiv_levelSet_val_eq_zero
lean.code:
theorem innerAt_grad_mfderiv_levelSet_val_eq_zero
    (hreg : ∀ x : M, f x = c → mfderiv I 𝓘(ℝ, ℝ) f x ≠ 0)
    (x₀ : (f ⁻¹' {c} : Set M)) (w : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin n)) :
    letI := levelSetChartedSpace hf n c hreg
    g.innerAt ↑x₀ (grad g f ↑x₀)
        (mfderiv (𝓡 n) I ((↑) : (f ⁻¹' {c} : Set M) → M) x₀ w) = 0 := by
  letI := levelSetChartedSpace hf n c hreg
  exact innerAt_grad_eq_zero_of_mem_ker g f ↑x₀ _
    (mfderivReal_mfderiv_levelSet_val hf n c hreg x₀ w)

deps.title: The gradient is orthogonal to the kernel of the differential
deps.label: lem:gradient-orthogonal-kernel

deps.title: The tangent space of a regular level set is the kernel of the differential
deps.label: prop:regular-level-set-tangent-kernel

deps.title: A regular level set of a real-valued function is a smooth hypersurface
deps.label: prop:regular-level-set-hypersurface-codim-one

entries.label: prop:tangent-space-regular-level-set-gradient-orthogonal
entries.title: The tangent space of a regular level set is the orthogonal complement of the gradient
entries.body:
In the situation of Proposition \ref{prop:gradient-normal-regular-level-set}, conversely, every $u \in T_{x_0} M$ with $\langle \operatorname{grad} f|_{x_0}, u\rangle = 0$ lies in the image of $d(\iota_c)_{x_0}$. Hence
\[
  d(\iota_c)_{x_0}\bigl(T_{x_0} f^{-1}(c)\bigr) = \bigl(\operatorname{grad} f|_{x_0}\bigr)^{\perp} ,
\]
so the normal space of $f^{-1}(c)$ at $x_0$ is the line spanned by $\operatorname{grad} f|_{x_0}$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.exists_mfderiv_levelSet_val_eq_of_innerAt_grad_eq_zero
lean.code:
theorem exists_mfderiv_levelSet_val_eq_of_innerAt_grad_eq_zero
    (hreg : ∀ x : M, f x = c → mfderiv I 𝓘(ℝ, ℝ) f x ≠ 0)
    (x₀ : (f ⁻¹' {c} : Set M)) (u : E)
    (hu : g.innerAt ↑x₀ (grad g f ↑x₀) u = 0) :
    letI := levelSetChartedSpace hf n c hreg
    ∃ w : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin n),
      (mfderiv (𝓡 n) I ((↑) : (f ⁻¹' {c} : Set M) → M) x₀ w : E) = u := by
  letI := levelSetChartedSpace hf n c hreg
  have hker : u ∈ levelHyperplane (I := I) f ↑x₀ :=
    (mem_levelHyperplane_iff (I := I) f ↑x₀ u).2
      ((innerAt_grad_eq_zero_iff g f ↑x₀ u).1 hu)
  rw [← range_mfderiv_levelSet_val hf n c hreg x₀] at hker
  -- `LinearMap.range` is `Set.range` on the nose, so membership destructures
  -- directly; `LinearMap.mem_range` cannot fire here because its
  -- `RingHomSurjective` instance argument is still a metavariable.
  obtain ⟨w, hw⟩ := hker
  exact ⟨w, hw⟩

end Normal

/-!
### Proposition 2.37 for a possibly-critical level set

Lee's `M_c = f⁻¹(c) ∩ ℛ` allows the level set to contain critical points, and
keeps only its regular part.  Everything above is stated for a *regular value*
(`hreg`: `df_x ≠ 0` at every point of `f⁻¹(c)`), which is the case Lee's own
proof reduces to and the case his applications use.

The reduction of the general statement to this one is carried out in
`RegularSetHypersurface.lean`, on top of the openness of `ℛ` proved here
(`isOpen_regularSet`): `ℛ` is an open submanifold of `M`, `M_c` is the level set
of `f|_ℛ` at `c`, and `c` is a regular value of that restriction *by
construction*, because restricting to an open set does not change the
differential.

Openness did not need the chart comparison an earlier note here called for:
rather than identify `df_x` with the derivative of a chart representative,
`isOpen_setOf_mfderiv_ne_zero` reads `x ↦ df_x` in *tangent* coordinates, where
mathlib's `ContMDiffAt.mfderiv_const` already supplies smoothness and the
trivialization equivalences make vanishing of the coordinate representation
equivalent to vanishing of `df_x`.

The remaining transfer needed `d(ℛ ↪ M) = id` for `ℛ` open in an *arbitrary*
manifold.  An earlier note here cited `OpenSubmanifold.lean` for that, but at the
time that file proved it only for an open subset of a normed space (the flat case
`W : Opens F` used by `PolarCoordinates.lean`); the general form has since been
added there (`mfderiv_opens_subtypeVal`, `mfderiv_opens_restrict`).
-/

deps.title: Orthogonality to the gradient characterizes the kernel of the differential
deps.label: lem:gradient-orthogonal-iff

deps.title: The tangent space of a regular level set is the kernel of the differential
deps.label: prop:regular-level-set-tangent-kernel

entries.label: lem:opens-inclusion-differential
entries.title: The differential of an open-submanifold inclusion
entries.body:
Let $M$ be a smooth manifold and $U \subseteq M$ open, regarded as an open submanifold. Then for each $x \in U$ the differential of the inclusion $\iota : U \hookrightarrow M$ is the identity,
\[
  d\iota_x = \operatorname{id} : T_x U \to T_x M ,
\]
under the identification of $T_x U$ with $T_x M$ induced by the charts. In particular $d\iota_x$ is an isomorphism.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.mfderiv_opens_subtypeVal
lean.code:
theorem mfderiv_opens_subtypeVal (U : Opens M) (x : U) :
    mfderiv I I (fun y : U => (y : M)) x = ContinuousLinearMap.id ℝ E :=
  (hasMFDerivAt_opens_subtypeVal U x).mfderiv

entries.label: lem:opens-restrict-differential
entries.title: Restriction to an open set preserves the differential
entries.body:
Let $F : M \to M'$ be a smooth map, $U \subseteq M$ open, and $x \in U$. Then
\[
  d(F|_U)_x = dF_x .
\]

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.mfderiv_opens_restrict
lean.code:
theorem mfderiv_opens_restrict (U : Opens M) (F : M → M') {x : U}
    (hF : MDifferentiableAt I I' F ↑x) :
    mfderiv I I' (fun y : U => F ↑y) x = mfderiv I I' F ↑x := by
  have hcomp : mfderiv I I' ((fun z : M => F z) ∘ (fun y : U => (y : M))) x
      = (mfderiv I I' F ↑x).comp (mfderiv I I (fun y : U => (y : M)) x) :=
    mfderiv_comp x hF (hasMFDerivAt_opens_subtypeVal U x).mdifferentiableAt
  rw [Function.comp_def] at hcomp
  rw [hcomp, mfderiv_opens_subtypeVal]
  ext v
  rfl

end General

/-! ## The flat case: an open subset of a normed space

Here the ambient manifold is the model space itself, `I = 𝓘(ℝ, F)`, so the
general results above apply verbatim. -/

section Flat

variable {F : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup F] [NormedSpace ℝ F] [FiniteDimensional ℝ F]

deps.title: The differential of an open-submanifold inclusion
deps.label: lem:opens-inclusion-differential

entries.label: lem:regular-restrict-regular-value
entries.title: Every value is a regular value of $f|_{\mathcal{R}}$
entries.body: Let $M$ be a smooth manifold, $f \in C^\infty(M)$, and $\mathcal{R}$ the regular set of $f$, regarded as an open submanifold of $M$ (Lemma \ref{lem:regular-set-open}). Then $d(f|_{\mathcal{R}})_x \neq 0$ for every $x \in \mathcal{R}$; that is, every $c \in \mathbb{R}$ is a regular value of $f|_{\mathcal{R}}$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.regularRestrict_regular
lean.code:
theorem regularRestrict_regular (y : regularOpens hf) :
    mfderiv I 𝓘(ℝ, ℝ) (regularRestrict hf) y ≠ 0 := by
  rw [mfderiv_regularRestrict]
  exact y.2

deps.title: Restriction to an open set preserves the differential
deps.label: lem:opens-restrict-differential

entries.label: prop:regular-level-set-hypersurface
entries.title: prop:regular-level-set-hypersurface
entries.body:
Suppose $M$ is a smooth $(n+1)$-manifold without boundary, $f \in C^\infty(M)$, and
$\mathcal{R} \subseteq M$ is the set of regular points of $f$. For each $c \in \mathbb{R}$, the set $M_c = f^{-1}(c) \cap \mathcal{R}$ is a smooth $n$-manifold.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.isManifold_regularLevelSet
lean.code:
theorem isManifold_regularLevelSet :
    letI := regularLevelSetChartedSpace hf c n
    IsManifold (𝓡 n) ∞ (regularLevelSet hf c) :=
  isManifold_levelSet (contMDiff_regularRestrict hf) n c (regularRestrict_hreg hf c)

/-! ### The inclusion into `M` -/

deps.title: Every value is a regular value of $f|_{\mathcal{R}}$
deps.label: lem:regular-restrict-regular-value

deps.title: A regular level set of a real-valued function is a smooth hypersurface
deps.label: prop:regular-level-set-hypersurface-codim-one

entries.label: lem:regular-part-image
entries.title: The regular part, as a subset of $M$
entries.body: In the situation of Proposition \ref{prop:regular-level-set-hypersurface}, the image of $M_c = (f|_{\mathcal{R}})^{-1}(c)$ under the inclusion $M_c \hookrightarrow \mathcal{R} \hookrightarrow M$ is exactly $f^{-1}(c) \cap \mathcal{R}$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.range_regularLevelSetIncl
lean.code:
theorem range_regularLevelSetIncl :
    range (regularLevelSetIncl hf c) = f ⁻¹' {c} ∩ regularSet I f := by
  ext x
  constructor
  · rintro ⟨z, rfl⟩
    exact ⟨z.2, (z : regularOpens hf).2⟩
  · rintro ⟨hxc, hxr⟩
    exact ⟨⟨⟨x, hxr⟩, hxc⟩, rfl⟩

entries.label: lem:regular-part-embedded
entries.title: The regular part is embedded
entries.body: In the situation of Proposition \ref{prop:regular-level-set-hypersurface}, the inclusion $M_c \hookrightarrow M$ is a topological embedding.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.isEmbedding_regularLevelSetIncl
lean.code:
theorem isEmbedding_regularLevelSetIncl :
    letI := regularLevelSetChartedSpace hf c n
    Topology.IsEmbedding (regularLevelSetIncl hf c) :=
  Topology.IsEmbedding.subtypeVal.comp Topology.IsEmbedding.subtypeVal

entries.label: lem:regular-part-inclusion-smooth
entries.title: The inclusion of the regular part is smooth
entries.body: In the situation of Proposition \ref{prop:regular-level-set-hypersurface}, the inclusion $M_c \hookrightarrow M$ is smooth.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.contMDiff_regularLevelSetIncl
lean.code:
theorem contMDiff_regularLevelSetIncl :
    letI := regularLevelSetChartedSpace hf c n
    ContMDiff (𝓡 n) I ∞ (regularLevelSetIncl hf c) := by
  letI := regularLevelSetChartedSpace hf c n
  exact contMDiff_subtype_val.comp
    (contMDiff_levelSet_val (contMDiff_regularRestrict hf) n c (regularRestrict_hreg hf c))

deps.title: The inclusion of a regular level set is a smooth immersion

entries.label: lem:regular-part-inclusion-immersion
entries.title: The inclusion of the regular part is an immersion
entries.body: In the situation of Proposition \ref{prop:regular-level-set-hypersurface}, the differential of the inclusion $M_c \hookrightarrow M$ is injective at every point. With Lemma \ref{lem:regular-part-embedded}, $M_c$ is an embedded smooth hypersurface in $M$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.injective_mfderiv_regularLevelSetIncl
lean.code:
theorem injective_mfderiv_regularLevelSetIncl (z : regularLevelSet hf c) :
    letI := regularLevelSetChartedSpace hf c n
    Injective (mfderiv (𝓡 n) I (regularLevelSetIncl hf c) z) := by
  letI := regularLevelSetChartedSpace hf c n
  rw [mfderiv_regularLevelSetIncl hf c n z]
  exact mfderiv_levelSet_val_injective (contMDiff_regularRestrict hf) n c
    (regularRestrict_hreg hf c) z

deps.title: The differential of an open-submanifold inclusion
deps.label: lem:opens-inclusion-differential

deps.title: The inclusion of a regular level set is a smooth immersion
deps.label: prop:regular-level-set-inclusion-smooth

entries.label: lem:regular-part-tangent-kernel
entries.title: The tangent space of the regular part
entries.body: In the situation of Proposition \ref{prop:regular-level-set-hypersurface}, for every $x \in M_c$ the image of the differential of the inclusion $M_c \hookrightarrow M$ is exactly $\ker df_x$; that is, $T_x M_c = \ker df_x$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.range_mfderiv_regularLevelSetIncl
lean.code:
theorem range_mfderiv_regularLevelSetIncl (z : regularLevelSet hf c) :
    letI := regularLevelSetChartedSpace hf c n
    (mfderiv (𝓡 n) I (regularLevelSetIncl hf c) z :
        EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin n) →L[ℝ] E).range
      = levelHyperplane (I := I) f (regularLevelSetIncl hf c z) := by
  letI := regularLevelSetChartedSpace hf c n
  have h := range_mfderiv_levelSet_val (contMDiff_regularRestrict hf) n c
    (regularRestrict_hreg hf c) z
  have hhyp : levelHyperplane (I := I) (regularRestrict hf) ↑z
      = levelHyperplane (I := I) f (regularLevelSetIncl hf c z) := by
    show LinearMap.ker (levelDifferential (I := I) (regularRestrict hf) ↑z : E →ₗ[ℝ] ℝ)
      = LinearMap.ker (levelDifferential (I := I) f (regularLevelSetIncl hf c z) : E →ₗ[ℝ] ℝ)
    rw [levelDifferential, levelDifferential, mfderiv_regularRestrict]
    rfl
  rw [mfderiv_regularLevelSetIncl hf c n z, ← hhyp]
  exact h

entries.label: prop:gradient-normal-regular-part
entries.title: $\operatorname{grad} f$ is normal to the regular part
entries.body:
Let $(M,g)$ be a Riemannian manifold of dimension $n+1$ without boundary, $f \in C^\infty(M)$, and $c \in \mathbb{R}$. Then for every $x \in M_c$ and every $w \in T_x M_c$,
\[
  \bigl\langle \operatorname{grad} f|_{x},\, d\iota_{x}(w) \bigr\rangle = 0 ,
\]
where $\iota : M_c \hookrightarrow M$ is the inclusion. That is, $\operatorname{grad} f$ is everywhere normal to $M_c$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.innerAt_grad_mfderiv_regularLevelSetIncl_eq_zero
lean.code:
theorem innerAt_grad_mfderiv_regularLevelSetIncl_eq_zero (g : RiemannianMetric I M)
    (z : regularLevelSet hf c) (w : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin n)) :
    letI := regularLevelSetChartedSpace hf c n
    g.innerAt (regularLevelSetIncl hf c z) (grad g f (regularLevelSetIncl hf c z))
        (mfderiv (𝓡 n) I (regularLevelSetIncl hf c) z w) = 0 := by
  letI := regularLevelSetChartedSpace hf c n
  exact innerAt_grad_eq_zero_of_mem_ker g f _ _
    (mfderivReal_mfderiv_regularLevelSetIncl hf c n z w)

deps.title: The gradient is orthogonal to the kernel of the differential
deps.label: lem:gradient-orthogonal-kernel

deps.title: The tangent space of the regular part
deps.label: lem:regular-part-tangent-kernel

entries.label: lem:gradient-nonvanishing-regular-part
entries.title: $\operatorname{grad} f$ does not vanish on the regular part
entries.body: In the situation of Proposition \ref{prop:gradient-normal-regular-part}, $\operatorname{grad} f|_x \neq 0$ for every $x \in M_c$. Together with Proposition \ref{prop:gradient-normal-regular-part} and Lemma \ref{lem:regular-part-tangent-kernel}, $\operatorname{grad} f|_x$ spans the normal line of the hypersurface $M_c$ at $x$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.grad_ne_zero_regularLevelSet
lean.code:
theorem grad_ne_zero_regularLevelSet (g : RiemannianMetric I M) (z : regularLevelSet hf c) :
    grad g f (regularLevelSetIncl hf c z) ≠ 0 :=
  (mem_regularSet_iff_grad_ne_zero g f _).1 (z : regularOpens hf).2

deps.title: The regular set is where the gradient does not vanish
deps.label: lem:regular-set-eq-gradient-nonvanishing

entries.label: prop:inner-products-of-tensors
entries.title: Inner Products of Tensors

entries.label: def:smooth-orientation
entries.title: Smooth Orientation
entries.body:
A \emph{pointwise orientation} of a smooth $n$-manifold $M$ is a choice of orientation $o_x$ of
the vector space $T_x M$ for each $x \in M$. It is \emph{smooth} if every $p \in M$ has an open
neighbourhood $u$ carrying a smooth local frame $(Y_1, \ldots, Y_n)$ for $TM$ that is
positively oriented throughout $u$: for every $x \in u$ the basis $(Y_1|_x, \ldots, Y_n|_x)$ of
$T_x M$ induces $o_x$. An \emph{oriented} Riemannian manifold is a Riemannian manifold together
with a smooth orientation.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.IsSmoothOrientation
lean.code:
def IsSmoothOrientation (o : PointwiseOrientation I M) : Prop :=
  ∀ p : M, ∃ (u : Set M) (Y : Fin (finrank ℝ E) → (x : M) → TangentSpace I x),
    IsOpen u ∧ p ∈ u ∧ ∃ hY : IsLocalFrameOn I E ∞ Y u,
      ∀ x, ∀ hx : x ∈ u, (hY.toBasisAt hx).orientation = o x

omit [FiniteDimensional ℝ E] in

entries.label: lem:gram-schmidt-preserves-orientation
entries.title: Gram--Schmidt Preserves Orientation
entries.body:
Let $V$ be an $n$-dimensional real inner product space and let $b = (b_1, \ldots, b_n)$ be a
basis of $V$. Then the Gram--Schmidt orthonormalization of $b$ induces the same orientation of
$V$ as $b$ does.

entries.label: prop:oriented-orthonormal-frames
entries.title: Existence of Oriented Orthonormal Frames
entries.body:
Let $(M,g)$ be a Riemannian $n$-manifold with a smooth orientation $o$. Then every $p \in M$ has
an open neighbourhood $u$ carrying a smooth local frame $(Z_1, \ldots, Z_n)$ for $TM$ that is
both orthonormal and positively oriented at every $x \in u$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.exists_orientedOrthonormalFrame_nhds
lean.code:
theorem exists_orientedOrthonormalFrame_nhds (g : RiemannianMetric I M)
    {o : PointwiseOrientation I M} (ho : IsSmoothOrientation o) (p : M) :
    ∃ (u : Set M) (Z : Fin (finrank ℝ E) → (x : M) → TangentSpace I x),
      IsOpen u ∧ p ∈ u ∧ ∃ hZ : IsLocalFrameOn I E ∞ Z u,
        (∀ x ∈ u, ∀ i j, g.inner x (Z i x) (Z j x) = if i = j then 1 else 0) ∧
        ∀ x, ∀ hx : x ∈ u, (hZ.toBasisAt hx).orientation = o x := by
  classical
  obtain ⟨u, Y, hu, hpu, hY, hor⟩ := ho p
  letI : Bundle.RiemannianBundle (TangentSpace I : M → Type _) := ⟨g.toRiemannianMetric⟩
  have hZframe : IsLocalFrameOn I E ∞
      (gramSchmidtFrame (V := (TangentSpace I : M → Type _)) Y) u :=
    isLocalFrameOn_gramSchmidtFrame (IB := I) (F := E) (V := (TangentSpace I : M → Type _)) hY
  refine ⟨u, gramSchmidtFrame (V := (TangentSpace I : M → Type _)) Y, hu, hpu, hZframe, ?_, ?_⟩
  · -- orthonormality, transported to `g.inner` exactly as `exists_orthonormalFrame` does
    intro x hx i j
    have hon := gramSchmidtFrame_orthonormal (IB := I) (F := E)
      (V := (TangentSpace I : M → Type _)) hY.isLocalIndepOn hx
    show ⟪gramSchmidtFrame (V := (TangentSpace I : M → Type _)) Y i x,
        gramSchmidtFrame (V := (TangentSpace I : M → Type _)) Y j x⟫_ℝ = _
    rcases eq_or_ne i j with rfl | hij
    · rw [if_pos rfl, real_inner_self_eq_norm_sq, hon.1 i, one_pow]
    · rw [if_neg hij]
      exact hon.2 hij
  · -- orientation: Gram-Schmidt did not flip it
    intro x hx
    haveI : FiniteDimensional ℝ (TangentSpace I x) := inferInstanceAs (FiniteDimensional ℝ E)
    have hcard : finrank ℝ (TangentSpace I x) = Fintype.card (Fin (finrank ℝ E)) := by
      rw [Fintype.card_fin]; rfl
    have hcoe : ⇑(hY.toBasisAt hx) = fun i => Y i x :=
      funext fun i => IsLocalFrameOn.toBasisAt_coe hY hx i
    -- the Gram-Schmidt'd frame's basis IS mathlib's `gramSchmidtOrthonormalBasis`
    have hbases : hZframe.toBasisAt hx
        = (gramSchmidtOrthonormalBasis hcard ⇑(hY.toBasisAt hx)).toBasis := by
      refine Basis.eq_of_apply_eq fun i => ?_
      rw [IsLocalFrameOn.toBasisAt_coe, OrthonormalBasis.coe_toBasis]
      rw [gramSchmidtOrthonormalBasis_apply hcard (f := ⇑(hY.toBasisAt hx)) (i := i) ?_]
      · rw [hcoe]; rfl
      · rw [hcoe]
        simpa [gramSchmidtNormed, norm_eq_zero] using
          gramSchmidt_ne_zero i (hY.linearIndependent hx)
    rw [hbases, InnerProductSpace.gramSchmidtOrthonormalBasis_orientation]
    exact hor x hx

end SmoothOrientation

/-! ## The Riemannian volume form -/

section VolumeForm

variable
  {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [NormedSpace ℝ E] [FiniteDimensional ℝ E]
  {H : Type*} [TopologicalSpace H] {I : ModelWithCorners ℝ E H}
  {M : Type*} [TopologicalSpace M] [ChartedSpace H M] [IsManifold I ∞ M]

entries.label: def:volume-form-pointwise
entries.title: The Riemannian Volume Form
entries.body:
Let $(M,g)$ be a Riemannian $n$-manifold and let $o$ be a pointwise orientation of $M$. For each
$x \in M$ let $dV_g|_x$ be the volume form of the oriented inner product space
$(T_x M, g_x, o_x)$, that is, the unique alternating $n$-form on $T_x M$ taking the value $1$ on
every orthonormal basis of $T_x M$ inducing $o_x$. The family $dV_g : x \mapsto dV_g|_x$ is the
\emph{Riemannian volume form} of $(M, g, o)$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.RiemannianMetric.volumeForm

entries.label: prop:volume-form-determinant
entries.title: The Determinant Formula
entries.body:
Let $(M,g)$ be a Riemannian $n$-manifold with pointwise orientation $o$, let $(E_1,\ldots,E_n)$
be a local orthonormal frame on an open set $u$ that is positively oriented at $x \in u$, and let
$v_1, \ldots, v_n \in T_x M$. Then
\[
dV_g|_x(v_1, \ldots, v_n) = \det \bigl( \langle E_i|_x, v_j \rangle_g \bigr)_{i,j=1}^{n}.
\]

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.RiemannianMetric.volumeForm_apply_eq_det
lean.code:
theorem volumeForm_apply_eq_det (g : RiemannianMetric I M) (o : PointwiseOrientation I M)
    (hY : IsLocalFrameOn I E ∞ Y u)
    (hon : ∀ x ∈ u, ∀ i j, g.inner x (Y i x) (Y j x) = if i = j then 1 else 0)
    (hx : x ∈ u) (hor : (hY.toBasisAt hx).orientation = o x)
    (v : Fin (finrank ℝ E) → TangentSpace I x) :
    g.volumeForm o x v = (Matrix.of fun i j => g.inner x (Y i x) (v j)).det := by
  letI : Bundle.RiemannianBundle (TangentSpace I : M → Type _) := ⟨g.toRiemannianMetric⟩
  haveI : Fact (finrank ℝ (TangentSpace I x) = finrank ℝ E) := ⟨rfl⟩
  have hon' := orthonormal_toBasisAt g hY hon hx
  have := volumeFormL_apply_eq_det (o x) ((hY.toBasisAt hx).toOrthonormalBasis hon') (by
    rwa [Basis.toBasis_toOrthonormalBasis]) v
  simpa only [Basis.coe_toOrthonormalBasis, IsLocalFrameOn.toBasisAt_coe] using this

omit [FiniteDimensional ℝ E] in

entries.label: prop:volume-form-value-one
entries.title: The Characterizing Property of $dV_g$
entries.body:
Let $(M,g)$ be a Riemannian $n$-manifold with pointwise orientation $o$ and let
$(E_1, \ldots, E_n)$ be a local orthonormal frame on an open set $u$ that is positively oriented
at $x \in u$. Then $dV_g|_x(E_1|_x, \ldots, E_n|_x) = 1$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.RiemannianMetric.volumeForm_apply_eq_one
lean.code:
theorem volumeForm_apply_eq_one (g : RiemannianMetric I M) (o : PointwiseOrientation I M)
    (hY : IsLocalFrameOn I E ∞ Y u)
    (hon : ∀ x ∈ u, ∀ i j, g.inner x (Y i x) (Y j x) = if i = j then 1 else 0)
    (hx : x ∈ u) (hor : (hY.toBasisAt hx).orientation = o x) :
    g.volumeForm o x (fun i => Y i x) = 1 := by
  letI : Bundle.RiemannianBundle (TangentSpace I : M → Type _) := ⟨g.toRiemannianMetric⟩
  haveI : Fact (finrank ℝ (TangentSpace I x) = finrank ℝ E) := ⟨rfl⟩
  have hon' := orthonormal_toBasisAt g hY hon hx
  have := volumeFormL_apply_eq_one (o x) ((hY.toBasisAt hx).toOrthonormalBasis hon') (by
    rwa [Basis.toBasis_toOrthonormalBasis])
  simpa only [Basis.coe_toOrthonormalBasis, IsLocalFrameOn.toBasisAt_coe] using this

omit [FiniteDimensional ℝ E] in

entries.label: prop:volume-form-uniqueness
entries.title: Uniqueness of $dV_g$
entries.body:
Let $(M,g)$ be a Riemannian $n$-manifold with pointwise orientation $o$, let $(E_1,\ldots,E_n)$
be a local orthonormal frame on an open set $u$ that is positively oriented at $x \in u$, and let
$\theta$ be an alternating $n$-form on $T_x M$ with $\theta(E_1|_x, \ldots, E_n|_x) = 1$. Then
$\theta = dV_g|_x$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.RiemannianMetric.volumeForm_unique
lean.code:
theorem volumeForm_unique (g : RiemannianMetric I M) (o : PointwiseOrientation I M)
    (hY : IsLocalFrameOn I E ∞ Y u)
    (hon : ∀ x ∈ u, ∀ i j, g.inner x (Y i x) (Y j x) = if i = j then 1 else 0)
    (hx : x ∈ u) (hor : (hY.toBasisAt hx).orientation = o x)
    (θ : (TangentSpace I x) [⋀^Fin (finrank ℝ E)]→L[ℝ] ℝ)
    (hθ : θ (fun i => Y i x) = 1) :
    θ = g.volumeForm o x := by
  have hcoe : ⇑(hY.toBasisAt hx) = fun i => Y i x :=
    funext fun i => IsLocalFrameOn.toBasisAt_coe hY hx i
  have hbasis : ∀ v : Fin (finrank ℝ E) → TangentSpace I x,
      θ v = (hY.toBasisAt hx).det v * θ (fun i => Y i x) := by
    intro v
    have := (θ.toAlternatingMap).eq_smul_basis_det (hY.toBasisAt hx)
    have hv := congrArg (fun f => f v) this
    simpa [Basis.det_apply, mul_comm, hcoe] using hv
  ext v
  rw [hbasis v, hθ, mul_one]
  letI : Bundle.RiemannianBundle (TangentSpace I : M → Type _) := ⟨g.toRiemannianMetric⟩
  haveI : Fact (finrank ℝ (TangentSpace I x) = finrank ℝ E) := ⟨rfl⟩
  have hon' := orthonormal_toBasisAt g hY hon hx
  have hrob := (o x).volumeForm_robust ((hY.toBasisAt hx).toOrthonormalBasis hon') (by
    rwa [Basis.toBasis_toOrthonormalBasis])
  show _ = (o x).volumeForm v
  rw [hrob, Basis.toBasis_toOrthonormalBasis]

/-! ### Lee 2.41(c): the coordinate formula `dV_g = √det(g_ij) ε¹ ∧ ⋯ ∧ εⁿ`

Lee states (c) for a coordinate frame `(∂/∂x^i)` with its dual coframe `(dx^i)`.  Neither exists
in the pinned mathlib — there is no coordinate frame of `TM` as a named object — so (c) is stated
here for an **arbitrary oriented smooth local frame** `(Y_i)` and a dual coframe `(ε^i)`.  That is
strictly more general and specializes to Lee's statement verbatim: a chart's coordinate frame is a
local frame (`Trivialization.localFrame`), `g_ij = g(∂_i, ∂_j)` is its `frameMatrix`, and `(dx^i)`
is its dual coframe. -/

entries.label: lem:top-forms-proportional
entries.title: Top-Degree Forms are Proportional
entries.body:
Let $V$ be an $n$-dimensional real vector space, let $b = (b_1, \ldots, b_n)$ be a basis of $V$,
and let $\omega$ and $\theta$ be alternating $n$-forms on $V$ with
$\theta(b_1, \ldots, b_n) \ne 0$. Then
\[
\omega = \frac{\omega(b_1, \ldots, b_n)}{\theta(b_1, \ldots, b_n)} \, \theta .
\]

entries.label: def:wedge-covectors
entries.title: The Wedge Product of Covectors
entries.body:
Let $V$ be a real topological vector space and let $f_1, \ldots, f_n$ be continuous linear
functionals on $V$. Their \emph{wedge product} is the alternating $n$-form
\[
(f_1 \wedge \cdots \wedge f_n)(v_1, \ldots, v_n) = \det \bigl( f_i(v_j) \bigr)_{i,j=1}^{n} .
\]
This is alternating and multilinear in $(v_1, \ldots, v_n)$ because the determinant is
alternating and multilinear in the columns of its matrix, and it is continuous because a
determinant is a polynomial in the entries $f_i(v_j)$, each of which depends continuously on
$v_j$. This is the determinant convention for the wedge product, as used throughout.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.wedgeCovectors
lean.code:
def wedgeCovectors (f : ι → (V →L[ℝ] ℝ)) : V [⋀^ι]→L[ℝ] ℝ where
  toMultilinearMap := (wedgeCovectorsₗ f).toMultilinearMap
  map_eq_zero_of_eq' := (wedgeCovectorsₗ f).map_eq_zero_of_eq'
  cont :=
    Continuous.congr
      (Continuous.matrix_det
        (continuous_pi fun i => continuous_pi fun j => (f i).continuous.comp (continuous_apply j)))
      fun v => (wedgeCovectorsₗ_apply f v).symm

@[simp]

entries.label: prop:volume-form-coframe
entries.title: The Coframe Characterization of $dV_g$
entries.body:
Let $(M,g)$ be a Riemannian $n$-manifold with pointwise orientation $o$, and let
$(E_1, \ldots, E_n)$ be a local orthonormal frame on an open set $u$ that is positively oriented
at $x \in u$. Let $(e^1, \ldots, e^n)$ be the dual coframe, $e^i = \langle E_i, \cdot \rangle_g$.
Then
\[
dV_g|_x = e^1 \wedge \cdots \wedge e^n .
\]
This is characterization (a) of Lee's Proposition 2.41.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.RiemannianMetric.volumeForm_eq_wedgeCovectors
lean.code:
theorem volumeForm_eq_wedgeCovectors (g : RiemannianMetric I M) (o : PointwiseOrientation I M)
    (hY : IsLocalFrameOn I E ∞ Y u)
    (hon : ∀ x ∈ u, ∀ i j, g.inner x (Y i x) (Y j x) = if i = j then 1 else 0)
    (hx : x ∈ u) (hor : (hY.toBasisAt hx).orientation = o x) :
    g.volumeForm o x = wedgeCovectors (fun i => g.inner x (Y i x)) := by
  ext v
  rw [wedgeCovectors_apply]
  exact volumeForm_apply_eq_det g o hY hon hx hor v

omit [FiniteDimensional ℝ E] in

entries.label: prop:volume-form-smooth
entries.title: The Riemannian Volume Form is Smooth
entries.body:
Let $(M,g)$ be a Riemannian $n$-manifold with a smooth orientation $o$. Then $dV_g$ is a smooth
$n$-form on $M$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.RiemannianMetric.contMDiff_volumeForm
lean.code:
theorem contMDiff_volumeForm (g : RiemannianMetric I M) {o : PointwiseOrientation I M}
    (ho : IsSmoothOrientation o) :
    ContMDiff I (I.prod 𝓘(ℝ, E [⋀^Fin (finrank ℝ E)]→L[ℝ] ℝ)) ∞
      (fun x => TotalSpace.mk' (E [⋀^Fin (finrank ℝ E)]→L[ℝ] ℝ) x (g.volumeForm o x)) :=
  fun x₀ => g.contMDiffAt_volumeForm ho x₀

/-! ### Lee's Proposition 2.41 itself

Everything above is either fibrewise or takes a local oriented orthonormal frame as an
argument.  Lee's proposition is a statement about the `n`-form *field* `dV_g` on all of `M`:
it exists, it is smooth, it is characterized by (b), and it is *unique* with that property.

Uniqueness globally does not follow from `volumeForm_unique` by itself — that lemma pins one
fibre, and only once a frame at that point is handed to it.  What upgrades it is
`exists_orientedOrthonormalFrame_nhds`: a smooth orientation supplies an oriented orthonormal
frame near *every* point, so property (b) can be invoked at each `x` in turn. -/

deps.title: Smoothness of the pairing of vector fields
deps.label: lem:inner-product-vector-fields-smooth

entries.label: def:frame-matrix
entries.title: The Matrix of $g$ in a Frame
entries.body:
Let $(M,g)$ be a Riemannian manifold and $(Y_1,\ldots,Y_n)$ a local frame on an open set $u$. The
\emph{matrix of $g$ in the frame $(Y_i)$} at $x \in u$ is
\[
g_{ij}(x) = \langle Y_i|_x, Y_j|_x \rangle_g .
\]
For a coordinate frame $(\partial/\partial x^i)$ this is Lee's $(g_{ij})$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.RiemannianMetric.frameMatrix
lean.code:
def frameMatrix (g : RiemannianMetric I M)
    (Y : Fin (finrank ℝ E) → (x : M) → TangentSpace I x) (x : M) :
    Matrix (Fin (finrank ℝ E)) (Fin (finrank ℝ E)) ℝ :=
  Matrix.of fun i j => g.inner x (Y i x) (Y j x)

omit [FiniteDimensional ℝ E] in

entries.label: lem:frame-matrix-nonsingular
entries.title: The Gram Determinant of a Frame is Nonzero
entries.body:
With the notation of Definition \ref{def:frame-matrix}, $\det\bigl(g_{ij}(x)\bigr) \ne 0$ for
every $x \in u$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.RiemannianMetric.frameMatrix_det_ne_zero
lean.code:
theorem frameMatrix_det_ne_zero (g : RiemannianMetric I M)
    (hY : IsLocalFrameOn I E ∞ Y u) (hx : x ∈ u) : (g.frameMatrix Y x).det ≠ 0 := by
  letI : Bundle.RiemannianBundle (TangentSpace I : M → Type _) := ⟨g.toRiemannianMetric⟩
  have hcoe : ⇑(hY.toBasisAt hx) = fun i => Y i x :=
    funext fun i => IsLocalFrameOn.toBasisAt_coe hY hx i
  have hli : LinearIndependent ℝ (fun i => Y i x) := hcoe ▸ hY.linearIndependent hx
  rw [frameMatrix_eq_gram]
  exact Matrix.det_gram_ne_zero_iff_linearIndependent.mpr hli

deps.title: The Matrix of $g$ in a Frame
deps.label: def:frame-matrix

entries.label: def:dual-coframe
entries.title: The Dual Coframe of a Frame
entries.body:
With the notation of Definition \ref{def:frame-matrix}, write $\bigl(g^{ij}(x)\bigr)$ for the
inverse of the matrix $\bigl(g_{ij}(x)\bigr)$, which exists by Lemma
\ref{lem:frame-matrix-nonsingular}. The \emph{dual coframe} of $(Y_i)$ is the family of covectors
\[
\varepsilon^i|_x = \sum_{j} g^{ij}(x) \, \langle Y_j|_x, \cdot \rangle_g .
\]
For a coordinate frame this is the coordinate coframe $(dx^i)$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.RiemannianMetric.dualCoframe
lean.code:
def dualCoframe (g : RiemannianMetric I M)
    (Y : Fin (finrank ℝ E) → (x : M) → TangentSpace I x) (x : M) (i : Fin (finrank ℝ E)) :
    TangentSpace I x →L[ℝ] ℝ :=
  ∑ j, (g.frameMatrix Y x)⁻¹ i j • g.inner x (Y j x)

omit [FiniteDimensional ℝ E] in

deps.title: The Matrix of $g$ in a Frame
deps.label: def:frame-matrix

deps.title: The Gram Determinant of a Frame is Nonzero
deps.label: lem:frame-matrix-nonsingular

entries.label: lem:dual-coframe-duality
entries.title: The Dual Coframe is Dual
entries.body:
With the notation of Definition \ref{def:dual-coframe}, $\varepsilon^i|_x(Y_k|_x) = \delta^i_k$
for all $i,k$ and all $x \in u$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.RiemannianMetric.dualCoframe_apply_frame
lean.code:
theorem dualCoframe_apply_frame (g : RiemannianMetric I M)
    (hY : IsLocalFrameOn I E ∞ Y u) (hx : x ∈ u) (i k : Fin (finrank ℝ E)) :
    g.dualCoframe Y x i (Y k x) = if i = k then 1 else 0 := by
  classical
  have hstep : g.dualCoframe Y x i (Y k x)
      = ((g.frameMatrix Y x)⁻¹ * g.frameMatrix Y x) i k := by
    simp [dualCoframe, frameMatrix, ContinuousLinearMap.sum_apply, Matrix.mul_apply]
  rw [hstep, Matrix.nonsing_inv_mul _ (Ne.isUnit (g.frameMatrix_det_ne_zero hY hx))]
  simp [Matrix.one_apply]

omit [FiniteDimensional ℝ E] in

deps.title: The Dual Coframe of a Frame
deps.label: def:dual-coframe

deps.label: def:frame-matrix

entries.label: lem:volume-form-gram-determinant
entries.title: The Gram Determinant Formula, Pointwise
entries.body:
Let $V$ be an oriented $n$-dimensional inner product space with volume form $dV$, and let
$(b_1,\ldots,b_n)$ be a positively oriented basis of $V$, not assumed orthonormal. Then
\[
dV(b_1,\ldots,b_n) = \sqrt{\det\bigl(\langle b_i, b_j \rangle\bigr)} .
\]

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.volumeFormL_apply_eq_sqrt_det_gram
lean.code:
theorem volumeFormL_apply_eq_sqrt_det_gram (o : Orientation ℝ V (Fin n)) (b : Basis (Fin n) ℝ V)
    (hb : b.orientation = o) :
    o.volumeFormL ⇑b = Real.sqrt ((Matrix.gram ℝ ⇑b).det) := by
  classical
  haveI : FiniteDimensional ℝ V := Module.Basis.finiteDimensional_of_finite b
  have hcard : finrank ℝ V = Fintype.card (Fin n) := by
    rw [Fintype.card_fin]; exact Fact.out
  set e := gramSchmidtOrthonormalBasis hcard ⇑b with he
  have hor : e.toBasis.orientation = o := by
    rw [he, InnerProductSpace.gramSchmidtOrthonormalBasis_orientation, hb]
  set m : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ℝ := Matrix.of fun i j => ⟪e i, b j⟫_ℝ with hm
  have hdet : o.volumeFormL ⇑b = m.det := volumeFormL_apply_eq_det o e hor ⇑b
  have hgram : Matrix.gram ℝ ⇑b = mᴴ * m := by
    have h := Matrix.gram_eq_conjTranspose_mul (𝕜 := ℝ) e ⇑b
    rw [h]
    congr 1 <;> · ext i j; simp [hm, e.repr_apply_apply]
  have hpos : 0 < m.det := by
    have hd : e.toBasis.det ⇑b = m.det := by
      rw [Basis.det_apply]
      congr 1
      ext i j
      simp [hm, Basis.toMatrix_apply, e.coe_toBasis_repr_apply, e.repr_apply_apply]
    rw [← hd]
    exact (e.toBasis.orientation_eq_iff_det_pos b).mp (by rw [hor, hb])
  rw [hdet, hgram, Matrix.det_mul, Matrix.det_conjTranspose, RCLike.star_def,
    starRingEnd_apply, star_trivial, ← sq, Real.sqrt_sq hpos.le]

end Pointwise

/-! ## Smooth orientations of a manifold -/

section SmoothOrientation

variable
  {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [NormedSpace ℝ E] [FiniteDimensional ℝ E]
  {H : Type*} [TopologicalSpace H] {I : ModelWithCorners ℝ E H}
  {M : Type*} [TopologicalSpace M] [ChartedSpace H M] [IsManifold I ∞ M]

variable (I M) in

entries.label: prop:volume-form-frame-value
entries.title: The Value of $dV_g$ on an Oriented Frame
entries.body:
Let $(M,g)$ be a Riemannian $n$-manifold with a pointwise orientation, and let $(Y_1,\ldots,Y_n)$
be a local frame on $u$ that is positively oriented at $x \in u$. Then
\[
dV_g|_x(Y_1|_x, \ldots, Y_n|_x) = \sqrt{\det\bigl(g_{ij}(x)\bigr)} .
\]

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.RiemannianMetric.volumeForm_apply_eq_sqrt_det_frameMatrix
lean.code:
theorem volumeForm_apply_eq_sqrt_det_frameMatrix (g : RiemannianMetric I M)
    (o : PointwiseOrientation I M) (hY : IsLocalFrameOn I E ∞ Y u) (hx : x ∈ u)
    (hor : (hY.toBasisAt hx).orientation = o x) :
    g.volumeForm o x (fun i => Y i x) = Real.sqrt (g.frameMatrix Y x).det := by
  letI : Bundle.RiemannianBundle (TangentSpace I : M → Type _) := ⟨g.toRiemannianMetric⟩
  haveI : Fact (finrank ℝ (TangentSpace I x) = finrank ℝ E) := ⟨rfl⟩
  have hcoe : ⇑(hY.toBasisAt hx) = fun i => Y i x :=
    funext fun i => IsLocalFrameOn.toBasisAt_coe hY hx i
  have h := volumeFormL_apply_eq_sqrt_det_gram (o x) (hY.toBasisAt hx) hor
  rw [hcoe] at h
  rw [show g.volumeForm o x (fun i => Y i x) = (o x).volumeFormL (fun i => Y i x) from rfl, h,
    frameMatrix_eq_gram]

omit [FiniteDimensional ℝ E] in

deps.title: The Matrix of $g$ in a Frame
deps.label: def:frame-matrix

entries.label: prop:volume-form-coordinate-formula
entries.title: Lee 2.41(c): the Coordinate Formula for $dV_g$
entries.body:
Let $(M,g)$ be a Riemannian $n$-manifold with a pointwise orientation, let $(Y_1,\ldots,Y_n)$ be a
local frame on $u$ that is positively oriented at $x \in u$, and let $(\varepsilon^1,\ldots,
\varepsilon^n)$ be any coframe dual to it at $x$, that is $\varepsilon^i(Y_k|_x) = \delta^i_k$.
Then
\[
dV_g|_x = \sqrt{\det\bigl(g_{ij}(x)\bigr)} \; \varepsilon^1 \wedge \cdots \wedge \varepsilon^n .
\]
In particular, for oriented local coordinates $(x^1,\ldots,x^n)$, taking $(Y_i)$ to be the
coordinate frame $(\partial/\partial x^i)$ and $\varepsilon^i = dx^i$ gives
$dV_g = \sqrt{\det(g_{ij})}\, dx^1 \wedge \cdots \wedge dx^n$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.RiemannianMetric.volumeForm_eq_sqrt_det_smul_wedgeCovectors
lean.code:
theorem volumeForm_eq_sqrt_det_smul_wedgeCovectors (g : RiemannianMetric I M)
    (o : PointwiseOrientation I M) (hY : IsLocalFrameOn I E ∞ Y u) (hx : x ∈ u)
    (hor : (hY.toBasisAt hx).orientation = o x)
    (ε : Fin (finrank ℝ E) → (TangentSpace I x →L[ℝ] ℝ))
    (hε : ∀ i k, ε i (Y k x) = if i = k then 1 else 0) :
    g.volumeForm o x = Real.sqrt (g.frameMatrix Y x).det • wedgeCovectors ε := by
  classical
  have hcoe : ⇑(hY.toBasisAt hx) = fun i => Y i x :=
    funext fun i => IsLocalFrameOn.toBasisAt_coe hY hx i
  have hwedge : wedgeCovectors ε (fun i => Y i x) = 1 := by
    rw [wedgeCovectors_apply,
      show (Matrix.of fun i j => ε i (Y j x)) = (1 : Matrix _ _ ℝ) by
        ext i j; rw [Matrix.of_apply, hε i j, Matrix.one_apply]]
    exact Matrix.det_one
  have hne : wedgeCovectors ε ⇑(hY.toBasisAt hx) ≠ 0 := by
    rw [hcoe, hwedge]; norm_num
  rw [ContinuousAlternatingMap.eq_smul_of_apply_basis_ne_zero (hY.toBasisAt hx)
    (g.volumeForm o x) (wedgeCovectors ε) hne, hcoe, hwedge,
    volumeForm_apply_eq_sqrt_det_frameMatrix g o hY hx hor, div_one]

/-! ### Smoothness: `dV_g` is an `n`-form, not merely a pointwise family

This is the existence half of Lee's Proposition 2.41, and the only part of the volume form that
is not fibrewise.  Everything above holds for an arbitrary pointwise orientation; smoothness is
exactly where `IsSmoothOrientation` is used, and it is what makes `dV_g` a *differential form*. -/

deps.title: The Matrix of $g$ in a Frame
deps.label: def:frame-matrix

deps.title: The Value of $dV_g$ on an Oriented Frame
deps.label: prop:volume-form-frame-value

entries.label: prop:riemannian-volume-form
entries.title: The Riemannian Volume Form
entries.body:
Let $(M, g)$ be an oriented Riemannian $n$-manifold with or without boundary. There is a unique $n$-form $dV_g$ on
$M$, called the \emph{Riemannian volume form}, characterized by any one of the following
three equivalent properties:

\begin{enumerate}[label=(\alph*)]
\item If $(e^1, \ldots, e^n)$ is any local oriented orthonormal coframe for $T^* M$, then
$$dV_g = e^1 \wedge \cdots \wedge e^n.$$

\item If $(E_1, \ldots, E_n)$ is any local oriented orthonormal frame for $TM$, then
$$dV_g(E_1, \ldots, E_n) = 1.$$

\item If $(x^1, \ldots, x^n)$ are any oriented local coordinates, then
$$dV_g = \sqrt{\det(g_{ij})} \, dx^1 \wedge \cdots \wedge dx^n.$$
\end{enumerate}

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.RiemannianMetric.riemannian_volumeForm
lean.code:
theorem riemannian_volumeForm (g : RiemannianMetric I M) {o : PointwiseOrientation I M}
    (ho : IsSmoothOrientation o) :
    ∃! θ : ∀ x : M, (TangentSpace I x) [⋀^Fin (finrank ℝ E)]→L[ℝ] ℝ,
      ContMDiff I (I.prod 𝓘(ℝ, E [⋀^Fin (finrank ℝ E)]→L[ℝ] ℝ)) ∞
        (fun x => TotalSpace.mk' (E [⋀^Fin (finrank ℝ E)]→L[ℝ] ℝ) x (θ x)) ∧
      ∀ (u : Set M) (Y : Fin (finrank ℝ E) → (x : M) → TangentSpace I x)
        (hY : IsLocalFrameOn I E ∞ Y u),
        (∀ x ∈ u, ∀ i j, g.inner x (Y i x) (Y j x) = if i = j then 1 else 0) →
        ∀ (x : M) (hx : x ∈ u), (hY.toBasisAt hx).orientation = o x →
          θ x (fun i => Y i x) = 1 := by
  refine ⟨g.volumeForm o, ⟨contMDiff_volumeForm g ho, ?_⟩, ?_⟩
  · intro u Y hY hon x hx hor
    exact volumeForm_apply_eq_one g o hY hon hx hor
  · intro θ hθ
    exact volumeForm_eq_of_apply_orientedOrthonormalFrame_eq_one g ho θ
      (fun u Y hY hon x hx hor => hθ.2 u Y hY hon x hx hor)

deps.title: The Riemannian Volume Form is Smooth
deps.label: prop:volume-form-smooth

entries.label: def:pullback-alternating
entries.title: The Pullback of a Differential Form
entries.body:
Let $\varphi : M \to \tilde{M}$ be a smooth map and $w$ a $k$-form on $\tilde{M}$. The
\emph{pullback} $\varphi^* w$ is the $k$-form on $M$ given at $x$ by
\[
(\varphi^* w)|_x(v_1,\ldots,v_k) = w|_{\varphi(x)}\bigl(d\varphi_x(v_1), \ldots, d\varphi_x(v_k)\bigr).
\]

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.pullbackAlternating
lean.code:
def pullbackAlternating {k : ℕ} (φ : M → M')
    (w : ∀ y : M', (TangentSpace I' y) [⋀^Fin k]→L[ℝ] ℝ) (x : M) :
    (TangentSpace I x) [⋀^Fin k]→L[ℝ] ℝ :=
  (w (φ x)).compContinuousLinearMap (mfderiv I I' φ x)

omit [FiniteDimensional ℝ E] [IsManifold I ∞ M] [IsManifold I' ∞ M'] in
@[simp]

entries.label: lem:orthonormal-pushforward
entries.title: A Metric-Preserving Map Pushes Orthonormal Frames Forward
entries.body:
Let $\varphi : (M,g) \to (\tilde{M},\tilde{g})$ be metric-preserving and let $(E_i)$ be a
$g$-orthonormal frame on an open set $u$. Then for every $x \in u$ the family
$\bigl(d\varphi_x(E_i|_x)\bigr)$ is $\tilde{g}$-orthonormal.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.IsMetricPreserving.orthonormal_pushforward
lean.code:
theorem IsMetricPreserving.orthonormal_pushforward (h : IsMetricPreserving g g' φ)
    (hon : ∀ x ∈ u, ∀ i j, g.inner x (Y i x) (Y j x) = if i = j then 1 else 0) (hx : x ∈ u) :
    letI : Bundle.RiemannianBundle (TangentSpace I' : M' → Type _) := ⟨g'.toRiemannianMetric⟩
    Orthonormal ℝ (fun i => mfderiv I I' φ x (Y i x)) := by
  letI : Bundle.RiemannianBundle (TangentSpace I' : M' → Type _) := ⟨g'.toRiemannianMetric⟩
  rw [orthonormal_iff_ite]
  intro i j
  show ⟪mfderiv I I' φ x (Y i x), mfderiv I I' φ x (Y j x)⟫_ℝ = _
  rw [show ⟪mfderiv I I' φ x (Y i x), mfderiv I I' φ x (Y j x)⟫_ℝ
      = g'.inner (φ x) (mfderiv I I' φ x (Y i x)) (mfderiv I I' φ x (Y j x)) from rfl,
    h.inner_mfderiv x (Y i x) (Y j x)]
  exact hon x hx i j

omit [FiniteDimensional ℝ E] in

entries.label: lem:push-basis
entries.title: The Pushforward Frame is a Basis
entries.body:
In the situation of Lemma \ref{lem:orthonormal-pushforward}, the family
$\bigl(d\varphi_x(E_i|_x)\bigr)$ is a basis of $T_{\varphi(x)}\tilde{M}$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.IsMetricPreserving.pushBasis
lean.code:
def IsMetricPreserving.pushBasis (h : IsMetricPreserving g g' φ)
    (hon : ∀ x ∈ u, ∀ i j, g.inner x (Y i x) (Y j x) = if i = j then 1 else 0) (hx : x ∈ u) :
    Basis (Fin (finrank ℝ E)) ℝ (TangentSpace I' (φ x)) :=
  haveI : FiniteDimensional ℝ (TangentSpace I' (φ x)) := inferInstanceAs (FiniteDimensional ℝ E)
  Basis.mk (h.linearIndependent_pushforward hon hx)
    ((h.linearIndependent_pushforward hon hx).span_eq_top_of_card_eq_finrank'
      (by simp only [Fintype.card_fin]; rfl)).ge

@[simp]

deps.label: lem:push-basis

deps.label: def:pullback-alternating
