entries.label: thm:rank-theorem
entries.title: Rank Theorem

entries.label: thm:local-embedding-theorem
entries.title: Local Embedding Theorem
entries.body:
}
Every smooth immersion is locally an embedding: if $F: M \to N$ is a smooth immersion, then for every $p \in M$, there is a neighborhood $U$ of $p$ in $M$ such that $F|_U$ is an embedding.

entries.label: thm:local-section-theorem
entries.title: Local Section Theorem
entries.body:
}
Suppose $M$ and $N$ are smooth manifolds and $\pi: M \to N$ is a smooth map. Then $\pi$ is a smooth submersion if and only if every point of $M$ is in the image of a smooth \textbf{local section} of $\pi$ (a map $\sigma: U \to M$ defined on some open subset $U \subseteq N$, with $\pi \circ \sigma = \text{Id}_U$).

entries.label: lem:surjection-splits
entries.title: A surjection splits its domain
entries.body: Let $A : E \to E'$ be a surjective linear map of finite-dimensional real vector spaces and let $P : E \to \ker A$ be a linear projection onto $\ker A$. Then $v \mapsto (Av, Pv)$ is a linear isomorphism $E \cong E' \times \ker A$.

lean.name: LeeLib.AppendixA.prodKerProjEquiv
lean.code:
noncomputable def prodKerProjEquiv (hA : Function.Surjective A) :
    E ≃L[ℝ] E' × (LinearMap.ker (A : E →ₗ[ℝ] E')) :=
  ContinuousLinearMap.equivProdOfSurjectiveOfIsCompl A (kerProj A)
    (LinearMap.range_eq_top.mpr hA) (kerProj_range A)
    (by rw [kerProj_ker]; exact isCompl_kerCompl A)

@[simp]

entries.label: prop:local-section-euclidean
entries.title: Local sections, Euclidean form
entries.body: Let $E, E'$ be finite-dimensional real vector spaces, let $f : E \to E'$ be $C^\infty$ at $a$, and suppose $df_a$ is surjective. Then there is a map $\sigma : E' \to E$, $C^\infty$ at $f(a)$, with $\sigma(f(a)) = a$ and $f(\sigma(y)) = y$ for every $y$ near $f(a)$.

lean.name: LeeLib.AppendixA.exists_localSection_of_surjective_fderiv
lean.code:
theorem exists_localSection_of_surjective_fderiv {f : E → E'} {a : E}
    (hf : ContDiffAt ℝ ∞ f a) (hsurj : Function.Surjective (fderiv ℝ f a)) :
    ∃ σ : E' → E, ContDiffAt ℝ ∞ σ (f a) ∧ σ (f a) = a ∧ ∀ᶠ y in 𝓝 (f a), f (σ y) = y := by
  set A := fderiv ℝ f a with hAdef
  set P := kerProj A with hPdef
  have hfd : HasFDerivAt f A a := (hf.differentiableAt (by simp)).hasFDerivAt
  -- `G` pairs `f` with the projection onto `ker (df a)`; its derivative is an isomorphism.
  set G : E → E' × (LinearMap.ker (A : E →ₗ[ℝ] E')) := fun x => (f x, P x) with hGdef
  have hGd : ContDiffAt ℝ ∞ G a := hf.prodMk (P.contDiff.contDiffAt)
  set Fe := prodKerProjEquiv A hsurj with hFedef
  have hGf : HasFDerivAt G (Fe : E →L[ℝ] E' × (LinearMap.ker (A : E →ₗ[ℝ] E'))) a := by
    rw [hFedef, prodKerProjEquiv_coe]
    exact hfd.prodMk P.hasFDerivAt
  have hn : (∞ : WithTop ℕ∞) ≠ 0 := by decide
  have hs : HasStrictFDerivAt G (Fe : E →L[ℝ] E' × (LinearMap.ker (A : E →ₗ[ℝ] E'))) a :=
    hGd.hasStrictFDerivAt' hGf hn
  -- The inverse function theorem inverts `G`; freezing the second slot at `P a` sections `f`.
  refine ⟨fun y => hGd.localInverse hGf hn (y, P a), ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
  · have ht : ContDiffAt ℝ ∞ (fun y : E' => (y, P a)) (f a) :=
      contDiffAt_id.prodMk contDiffAt_const
    exact (hGd.to_localInverse hGf hn).comp (f a) ht
  · exact hGd.localInverse_apply_image hGf hn
  · have ht : Tendsto (fun y : E' => (y, P a)) (𝓝 (f a)) (𝓝 (G a)) :=
      (continuous_id.prodMk continuous_const).continuousAt
    filter_upwards [ht.eventually hs.eventually_right_inverse] with y hy
    exact congrArg Prod.fst hy

end Euclidean

/-! ## The manifold local section theorem -/

section Manifold

variable
  {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [NormedSpace ℝ E] [FiniteDimensional ℝ E]
  {H : Type*} [TopologicalSpace H] {I : ModelWithCorners ℝ E H} [I.Boundaryless]
  {M : Type*} [TopologicalSpace M] [ChartedSpace H M] [IsManifold I ∞ M]
  {E' : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E'] [NormedSpace ℝ E'] [FiniteDimensional ℝ E']
  {H' : Type*} [TopologicalSpace H'] {I' : ModelWithCorners ℝ E' H'}
  {M' : Type*} [TopologicalSpace M'] [ChartedSpace H' M'] [IsManifold I' ∞ M']

omit [IsManifold I' ∞ M'] in
/-- **Lee, Theorem A.17**: *a smooth map whose differential at `x` is surjective
admits a smooth local section through `x`.*

There is a map `σ : M' → M`, smooth at `f x`, with `σ (f x) = x` and
`f (σ y) = y` for every `y` near `f x`.  Applied at every point of a submersion,
this is the statement that a submersion is locally a projection — which is how
Lee uses it, and it is what makes the quotient metric of Theorem 2.28 smooth.

Mathlib has no rank theorem, so this is proved directly from the inverse function

entries.label: prop:slice-chart-euclidean
entries.title: Slice chart of a submersion, Euclidean form
entries.body:
Let $E, E'$ be finite-dimensional real vector spaces, let $f : E \to E'$ be $C^\infty$ at $a$, and suppose $df_a$ is surjective. Set $G(x) = (f(x), P(x))$, where $P : E \to \ker df_a$ is a continuous linear projection. Then $G$ restricts to a homeomorphism of an open neighborhood $U$ of $a$ onto an open subset $G(U) \subseteq E' \times \ker df_a$; moreover $G$ is $C^\infty$ at $a$ and $G^{-1}$ is $C^\infty$ at $G(a)$, and the first component of $G$ is $f$. Consequently, for every $c \in E'$,
\[
G(U \cap f^{-1}(c)) = G(U) \cap \left(\{c\} \times \ker df_a\right);
\]
that is, in the coordinates $G$ the level set $f^{-1}(c)$ is the affine slice $\{c\} \times \ker df_a$, whose second factor is a linear subspace of dimension $\dim E - \dim E'$.

lean.name: LeeLib.AppendixA.sliceChart

entries.label: prop:local-section-manifold
entries.title: Local sections of a submersion
entries.body: Let $\pi : M \to N$ be a smooth map of smooth manifolds, with $M$ modelled on a boundaryless model. If $d\pi_p$ is surjective, then there is $\sigma : N \to M$, smooth at $\pi(p)$, with $\sigma(\pi(p)) = p$ and $\pi(\sigma(y)) = y$ for every $y$ near $\pi(p)$.

lean.name: LeeLib.AppendixA.exists_localSection
lean.code:
theorem exists_localSection {f : M → M'} (hf : ContMDiff I I' ∞ f) {x : M}
    (hsurj : Function.Surjective (mfderiv I I' f x)) :
    ∃ σ : M' → M, ContMDiffAt I' I ∞ σ (f x) ∧ σ (f x) = x ∧
      ∀ᶠ y in 𝓝 (f x), f (σ y) = y := by
  set φ := extChartAt I x with hφdef
  set ψ := extChartAt I' (f x) with hψdef
  set a := φ x with hadef
  set F := writtenInExtChartAt I I' x f with hFdef
  have hxs : x ∈ φ.source := mem_extChartAt_source (I := I) x
  have hys : f x ∈ ψ.source := mem_extChartAt_source (I := I') (f x)
  have hinv : φ.symm a = x := extChartAt_to_inv (I := I) x
  -- `F a = ψ (f x)`: the chart round-trip at the centre is the identity.
  have hFa : F a = ψ (f x) := by rw [hFdef, writtenInExtChartAt, Function.comp_apply,
    Function.comp_apply, hinv]
  -- The chart representation is `C^∞` at `a` (`range I = univ` kills the `Within`).
  have hFd : ContDiffAt ℝ ∞ F a := by
    have h := (contMDiffAt_iff (I := I) (I' := I') (n := ∞) (f := f) (x := x)).mp hf.contMDiffAt
    have h2 := h.2
    rw [I.range_eq_univ, contDiffWithinAt_univ] at h2
    exact h2
  -- The submersion hypothesis, read in the chart: `mfderiv` *is* this `fderivWithin`.
  have hFsurj : Function.Surjective (fderiv ℝ F a) := by
    have hmd : MDifferentiableAt I I' f x := hf.mdifferentiableAt (by simp)
    rw [mfderiv, if_pos hmd, I.range_eq_univ, fderivWithin_univ] at hsurj
    exact hsurj
  obtain ⟨σE, hσEd, hσEa, hσEr⟩ := exists_localSection_of_surjective_fderiv hFd hFsurj
  refine ⟨fun y => φ.symm (σE (ψ y)), ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
  · -- `σ` is a composite of `ψ` (smooth), `σE` (smooth), and `φ.symm` (smooth on the
    -- chart target, which is open and contains `σE (ψ (f x)) = a`).
    have h1 : ContMDiffAt I' 𝓘(ℝ, E') ∞ ψ (f x) := contMDiffAt_extChartAt (I := I') (x := f x)
    have h2 : ContMDiffAt 𝓘(ℝ, E') 𝓘(ℝ, E) ∞ σE (ψ (f x)) := by
      rw [← hFa]; exact hσEd.contMDiffAt
    have hσa : σE (ψ (f x)) = a := by rw [← hFa]; exact hσEa
    have h3 : ContMDiffAt 𝓘(ℝ, E) I ∞ φ.symm (σE (ψ (f x))) := by
      rw [hσa]
      exact (contMDiffOn_extChartAt_symm (I := I) (n := ∞) x).contMDiffAt
        ((isOpen_extChartAt_target (I := I) x).mem_nhds
          (by rw [hadef]; exact mem_extChartAt_target (I := I) x))
    exact (h3.comp (f x) (h2.comp (f x) h1))
  · show φ.symm (σE (ψ (f x))) = x
    rw [← hFa, hσEa, hinv]
  · -- `ψ (f (σ y)) = ψ y` near `f x`; cancel `ψ`, which is injective on its source.
    have hcont : ContinuousAt ψ (f x) := continuousAt_extChartAt (I := I') (f x)
    have hpull : ∀ᶠ y in 𝓝 (f x), F (σE (ψ y)) = ψ y := by
      have ht : Tendsto ψ (𝓝 (f x)) (𝓝 (F a)) := by rw [hFa]; exact hcont
      exact ht.eventually hσEr
    -- `y` and `f (σ y)` must both sit in `ψ.source` for cancellation.
    have hy : ∀ᶠ y in 𝓝 (f x), y ∈ ψ.source := extChartAt_source_mem_nhds (I := I') (f x)
    have hfσ : ∀ᶠ y in 𝓝 (f x), f (φ.symm (σE (ψ y))) ∈ ψ.source := by
      have hc : ContinuousAt (fun y => f (φ.symm (σE (ψ y)))) (f x) := by
        have h1 : ContMDiffAt I' 𝓘(ℝ, E') ∞ ψ (f x) := contMDiffAt_extChartAt (I := I') (x := f x)
        have h2 : ContMDiffAt 𝓘(ℝ, E') 𝓘(ℝ, E) ∞ σE (ψ (f x)) := by
          rw [← hFa]; exact hσEd.contMDiffAt
        have hσa : σE (ψ (f x)) = a := by rw [← hFa]; exact hσEa
        have h3 : ContMDiffAt 𝓘(ℝ, E) I ∞ φ.symm (σE (ψ (f x))) := by
          rw [hσa]
          exact (contMDiffOn_extChartAt_symm (I := I) (n := ∞) x).contMDiffAt
            ((isOpen_extChartAt_target (I := I) x).mem_nhds
              (by rw [hadef]; exact mem_extChartAt_target (I := I) x))
        exact (hf.contMDiffAt.comp (f x) (h3.comp (f x) (h2.comp (f x) h1))).continuousAt
  -- … (truncated)

entries.label: prop:properties-of-submersions
entries.title: Properties of Submersions

entries.label: prop:uniqueness-smooth-quotients
entries.title: Uniqueness of Smooth Quotients
entries.body: Suppose $M$, $N_1$, and $N_2$ are smooth manifolds, and $\pi_1 : M \to N_1$ and $\pi_2 : M \to N_2$ are surjective smooth submersions that are constant on each other's fibers. Then there exists a unique diffeomorphism $F : N_1 \to N_2$ such that $F \circ \pi_1 = \pi_2$.

deps.title: Existence, Uniqueness, and Smoothness for Linear ODEs
deps.label: thm:linear-ode-existence-uniqueness

entries.label: thm:global-rank-theorem
entries.title: Global Rank Theorem
entries.body:
Suppose $M$ and $N$ are smooth manifolds, and $F : M \to N$ is a smooth map of constant rank.

(a) If $F$ is surjective, then it is a smooth submersion.
(b) If $F$ is injective, then it is a smooth immersion.
(c) If $F$ is bijective, then it is a diffeomorphism.

entries.label: prop:properly-embedded-closed
entries.title: prop:properly-embedded-closed
entries.body: If $\tilde{M}$ is a smooth manifold with or without boundary, then an embedded submanifold $M \subseteq \tilde{M}$ is properly embedded if and only if it is a closed subset of $\tilde{M}$.

entries.label: prop:slice-coordinates
entries.title: Slice Coordinates
entries.body:
Let $\tilde{M}$ be a smooth $m$-manifold without boundary and let $M \subseteq \tilde{M}$ be an embedded $n$-dimensional submanifold with or without boundary. Then for each $p \in M$ there exist a neighborhood $\tilde{U}$ of $p$ in $\tilde{M}$ and smooth coordinates $(x^1, \ldots, x^m)$ for $\tilde{M}$ on $\tilde{U}$ such that $M \cap \tilde{U}$ is a set of one of the following forms:
\[
M \cap \tilde{U} = \begin{cases}
\{x \in \tilde{U} : x^{n+1} = \cdots = x^m = 0\} & \text{if } p \notin \partial M, \\
\{x \in \tilde{U} : x^{n+1} = \cdots = x^m = 0 \text{ and } x^n \geq 0\} & \text{if } p \in \partial M.
\end{cases}
\]

In such a chart, $(x^1, \ldots, x^n)$ form smooth local coordinates for $M$.

entries.label: thm:constant-rank-level-set
entries.title: Constant-Rank Level Set Theorem
entries.body: Suppose $\bar{M}$ and $N$ are smooth manifolds, and $\Phi: \bar{M} \to N$ is a smooth map with constant rank $r$. Every level set of $\Phi$ is a properly embedded submanifold of codimension $r$ in $M$.

entries.label: cor:submersion-level-set
entries.title: Submersion Level Set Theorem
entries.body: Suppose $\bar{M}$ and $N$ are smooth manifolds, and $\Phi: \bar{M} \to N$ is a smooth submersion. Every level set of $\Phi$ is a properly embedded submanifold of $\bar{M}$, whose codimension is equal to $\dim N$.

entries.label: cor:regular-level-set
entries.title: Regular Level Set Theorem
entries.body: Let $\bar{M}$ and $N$ be smooth manifolds, and let $\Phi: \bar{M} \to N$ be a smooth map. Every regular level set of $\Phi$ is a properly embedded submanifold of $M$ whose codimension is equal to $\dim N$.

deps.title: Slice chart of a submersion, Euclidean form
deps.label: prop:slice-chart-euclidean

entries.label: lem:straighten-scalar-affine
entries.title: Straightening a scalar function into an affine coordinate
entries.body:
Let $E$ be a Banach space, let $s \subseteq E$ be open, let $f : E \to \mathbb{R}$ be smooth on $s$, and let $u_0 \in s$ satisfy $df_{u_0} = \ell \neq 0$. Then there is a diffeomorphism $G$ between open subsets of $E$ with $u_0 \in \operatorname{dom} G \subseteq s$ and $G(u_0) = u_0$, such that
\[
  f(G^{-1}(v)) = f(u_0) + \ell(v - u_0)
\]
for every $v$ in the image of $G$. In particular $G$ carries $f^{-1}(f(u_0))$ onto the affine hyperplane slice $\{v : \ell(v - u_0) = 0\}$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.exists_openPartialHomeomorph_comp_symm_eq_affine
lean.code:
theorem exists_openPartialHomeomorph_comp_symm_eq_affine [CompleteSpace E]
    {f : E → ℝ} {s : Set E} (hs : IsOpen s) (hf : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ f s)
    {u₀ : E} (hu₀ : u₀ ∈ s) {ℓ : E →L[ℝ] ℝ} (hf' : HasFDerivAt f ℓ u₀)
    (hl : ℓ ≠ 0) :
    ∃ G : OpenPartialHomeomorph E E, G.source ⊆ s ∧ u₀ ∈ G.source ∧
      G u₀ = u₀ ∧ ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ G G.source ∧
      ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ G.symm G.target ∧
      ∀ v ∈ G.target, f (G.symm v) = f u₀ + ℓ (v - u₀) := by
  classical
  -- a direction `e` on which `ℓ` is `1`
  obtain ⟨w, hw⟩ : ∃ w, ℓ w ≠ 0 := by
    by_contra h
    push Not at h
    exact hl (ContinuousLinearMap.ext fun v => by simpa using h v)
  set e : E := (ℓ w)⁻¹ • w with he_def
  have he : ℓ e = 1 := by
    rw [he_def, map_smul, smul_eq_mul, inv_mul_cancel₀ hw]
  -- the straightening map and its regularity
  have hg_smooth : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (straighteningMap f u₀ ℓ e) s :=
    contDiffOn_straighteningMap hf u₀ ℓ e
  have hgAt : ContDiffAt ℝ ∞ (straighteningMap f u₀ ℓ e) u₀ :=
    hg_smooth.contDiffAt (hs.mem_nhds hu₀)
  have hg' := hasFDerivAt_straighteningMap hf' e
  have hn : (∞ : WithTop ℕ∞) ≠ 0 := by simp
  -- the inverse function theorem produces the local diffeomorphism
  set G₀ : OpenPartialHomeomorph E E :=
    hgAt.toOpenPartialHomeomorph (straighteningMap f u₀ ℓ e) hg' hn with hG₀_def
  have hG₀_coe : (G₀ : E → E) = straighteningMap f u₀ ℓ e := rfl
  have hu₀_G₀ : u₀ ∈ G₀.source :=
    hgAt.mem_toOpenPartialHomeomorph_source hg' hn
  -- the open locus where the derivative stays invertible
  set t : Set E := (s ∩ G₀.source) ∩
    (fderiv ℝ (straighteningMap f u₀ ℓ e)) ⁻¹' {L : E →L[ℝ] E | IsUnit L}
    with ht_def
  have ht_open : IsOpen t := by
    apply ContinuousOn.isOpen_inter_preimage
    · exact (hg_smooth.continuousOn_fderiv_of_isOpen hs
        (by exact_mod_cast le_top)).mono inter_subset_left
    · exact hs.inter G₀.open_source
    · exact Units.isOpen
  have ht_sub_s : t ⊆ s := fun u hu => hu.1.1
  have ht_sub_G₀ : t ⊆ G₀.source := fun u hu => hu.1.2
  have hu₀_t : u₀ ∈ t := by
    refine ⟨⟨hu₀, hu₀_G₀⟩, ?_⟩
    show IsUnit (fderiv ℝ (straighteningMap f u₀ ℓ e) u₀)
    rw [hg'.fderiv, show ((ContinuousLinearEquiv.refl ℝ E : E ≃L[ℝ] E) :
      E →L[ℝ] E) = 1 from ContinuousLinearMap.ext fun x => by
        simp [ContinuousLinearMap.one_def]]
    exact isUnit_one
  -- restrict to that locus
  refine ⟨G₀.restrOpen t ht_open, ?_, ?_, ?_, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
  · rw [OpenPartialHomeomorph.restrOpen_source]
    exact fun u hu => ht_sub_s hu.2
  · rw [OpenPartialHomeomorph.restrOpen_source]
    exact ⟨hu₀_G₀, hu₀_t⟩
  · exact straighteningMap_apply_self f u₀ ℓ e
  · rw [OpenPartialHomeomorph.restrOpen_source]
    exact hg_smooth.mono fun u hu => ht_sub_s hu.2
  · -- the inverse is `C^∞` on the whole target
    intro v hv
  -- … (truncated)

entries.label: lem:adapted-chart-scalar-affine
entries.title: The adapted chart at a regular point
entries.body:
Let $M$ be a smooth manifold without boundary, $f \in C^\infty(M)$, and $y \in M$ with $df_y \neq 0$. Let $\kappa$ be a chart of $M$ at $y$. Then there is a diffeomorphism $G$ between open subsets of the model space, with $\kappa(y) \in \operatorname{dom} G \subseteq \operatorname{im} \kappa$ and $G(\kappa(y)) = \kappa(y)$, such that
\[
  f\bigl(\kappa^{-1}(G^{-1}(v))\bigr) = f(y) + df_y(v - \kappa(y))
\]
for every $v$ in the image of $G$. Thus in the corrected chart $G \circ \kappa$ the function $f$ is affine, and the level set $f^{-1}(f(y))$ is the affine hyperplane slice $\{v : df_y(v - \kappa(y)) = 0\}$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.exists_extChartAt_openPartialHomeomorph_comp_symm_eq_affine
lean.code:
theorem exists_extChartAt_openPartialHomeomorph_comp_symm_eq_affine
    {f : M → ℝ} (hf : ContMDiff I 𝓘(ℝ, ℝ) ∞ f) (y : M)
    (hdf : mfderiv I 𝓘(ℝ, ℝ) f y ≠ 0) :
    ∃ G : OpenPartialHomeomorph E E,
      G.source ⊆ (extChartAt I y).target ∧
      extChartAt I y y ∈ G.source ∧
      G (extChartAt I y y) = extChartAt I y y ∧
      ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ G G.source ∧ ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ G.symm G.target ∧
      ∀ v ∈ G.target, f ((extChartAt I y).symm (G.symm v))
        = f y + mfderivReal (I := I) f y (v - extChartAt I y y) := by
  obtain ⟨G, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6⟩ :=
    exists_openPartialHomeomorph_comp_symm_eq_affine
      (isOpen_extChartAt_target (I := I) y)
      (contDiffOn_comp_extChartAt_symm hf y)
      (mem_extChartAt_target (I := I) y)
      (hasFDerivAt_comp_extChartAt_symm hf y) hdf
  refine ⟨G, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, fun v hv => ?_⟩
  have h := h6 v hv
  rw [Function.comp_apply, Function.comp_apply, extChartAt_to_inv] at h
  rw [mfderivReal_def]
  exact h

deps.title: Straightening a scalar function into an affine coordinate
deps.label: lem:straighten-scalar-affine

entries.label: def:level-slice-chart
entries.title: The slice chart of a level set
entries.body:
With $M$, $f$, $c$ as above and $y \in f^{-1}(c)$, let $G$ be an adapted chart at $y$ as in Lemma \ref{lem:adapted-chart-scalar-affine}. Since $df_y \neq 0$, the kernel $\ker df_y$ is a hyperplane in the $(n+1)$-dimensional model space, so it has dimension $n$; fix a linear isomorphism $\iota : \mathbb{R}^n \to \ker df_y$ and let $\pi$ be the projection onto $\ker df_y$ along any vector $e$ with $df_y(e) = 1$, read in the coordinates $\iota$. The \emph{slice chart of $f^{-1}(c)$ at $y$} is the map
\[
  x \longmapsto \pi\bigl(G(\kappa(x)) - \kappa(y)\bigr),
\]
defined for $x \in f^{-1}(c)$ in the domain of $G \circ \kappa$, with inverse $z \mapsto \kappa^{-1}(G^{-1}(\kappa(y) + \iota(z)))$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.levelSliceChart
lean.code:
def levelSliceChart (hy : f y = c) :
    OpenPartialHomeomorph (f ⁻¹' {c} : Set M) (EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin n)) where
  toFun x := sliceProj (I := I) n hdf
    (adaptedStraightening hf y hdf (extChartAt I y ↑x) - extChartAt I y y)
  invFun z :=
    if hz : z ∈ levelSliceTarget hf y hdf n then
      ⟨(extChartAt I y).symm ((adaptedStraightening hf y hdf).symm
        (extChartAt I y y + sliceEmb (I := I) n hdf z)),
        levelSliceInv_mem hf y hdf n c hz hy⟩
    else ⟨y, by simp [hy]⟩
  source := Subtype.val ⁻¹' levelChartDomain hf y hdf
  target := levelSliceTarget hf y hdf n
  map_source' x hx := by
    rw [mem_preimage] at hx
    have hxc : f ↑x = f y := by rw [levelSet_prop c x, hy]
    show extChartAt I y y + sliceEmb (I := I) n hdf _
      ∈ (adaptedStraightening hf y hdf).target
    rw [levelSlice_emb_proj hf y hdf n hxc hx]
    exact (adaptedStraightening hf y hdf).map_source hx.2
  map_target' z hz := by
    rw [mem_preimage, dif_pos hz]
    exact levelSliceInv_mem_domain hf y hdf n hz
  left_inv' x hx := by
    rw [mem_preimage] at hx
    have hxc : f ↑x = f y := by rw [levelSet_prop c x, hy]
    have hmem : sliceProj (I := I) n hdf
        (adaptedStraightening hf y hdf (extChartAt I y ↑x) - extChartAt I y y)
        ∈ levelSliceTarget hf y hdf n := by
      show extChartAt I y y + sliceEmb (I := I) n hdf _
        ∈ (adaptedStraightening hf y hdf).target
      rw [levelSlice_emb_proj hf y hdf n hxc hx]
      exact (adaptedStraightening hf y hdf).map_source hx.2
    rw [dif_pos hmem]
    refine Subtype.ext ?_
    show (extChartAt I y).symm ((adaptedStraightening hf y hdf).symm _) = ↑x
    rw [levelSlice_emb_proj hf y hdf n hxc hx,
      (adaptedStraightening hf y hdf).left_inv hx.2,
      (extChartAt I y).left_inv hx.1]
  right_inv' z hz := by
    rw [dif_pos hz]
    have h1 : (adaptedStraightening hf y hdf).symm
        (extChartAt I y y + sliceEmb (I := I) n hdf z)
        ∈ (adaptedStraightening hf y hdf).source :=
      (adaptedStraightening hf y hdf).map_target hz
    have h2 := adaptedStraightening_source_subset hf y hdf h1
    show sliceProj (I := I) n hdf
      (adaptedStraightening hf y hdf (extChartAt I y ((extChartAt I y).symm
        ((adaptedStraightening hf y hdf).symm
          (extChartAt I y y + sliceEmb (I := I) n hdf z)))) - extChartAt I y y) = z
    rw [(extChartAt I y).right_inv h2,
      (adaptedStraightening hf y hdf).right_inv hz, add_sub_cancel_left,
      sliceProj_sliceEmb]
  open_source :=
    (isOpen_levelChartDomain hf y hdf).preimage continuous_subtype_val
  open_target := isOpen_levelSliceTarget hf y hdf n
  continuousOn_toFun := by
    have h1 : ContinuousOn
        (fun x : M => adaptedStraightening hf y hdf (extChartAt I y x))
        (levelChartDomain hf y hdf) :=
      (adaptedStraightening hf y hdf).continuousOn.comp
  -- … (truncated)

deps.title: The adapted chart at a regular point
deps.label: lem:adapted-chart-scalar-affine

entries.label: prop:regular-level-set-hypersurface-codim-one
entries.title: A regular level set of a real-valued function is a smooth hypersurface
entries.body: Let $M$ be a smooth $(n+1)$-manifold without boundary, $f \in C^\infty(M)$, and let $c$ be a regular value of $f$. Then the slice charts of Definition \ref{def:level-slice-chart} form a smooth atlas on $f^{-1}(c)$, making it a smooth $n$-manifold.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.isManifold_levelSet
lean.code:
theorem isManifold_levelSet
    (hreg : ∀ x : M, f x = c → mfderiv I 𝓘(ℝ, ℝ) f x ≠ 0) :
    letI := levelSetChartedSpace hf n c hreg
    IsManifold (𝓡 n) ∞ (f ⁻¹' {c} : Set M) := by
  letI := levelSetChartedSpace hf n c hreg
  refine isManifold_of_contDiffOn (𝓡 n) ∞ (f ⁻¹' {c} : Set M) ?_
  intro e e' he he'
  obtain ⟨x, hx⟩ := mem_iUnion.1 he
  obtain ⟨x', hx'⟩ := mem_iUnion.1 he'
  rw [mem_singleton_iff] at hx hx'
  subst hx hx'
  simp only [modelWithCornersSelf_coe, modelWithCornersSelf_coe_symm,
    Set.range_id, Set.inter_univ, Set.preimage_id, Function.comp_def, id_eq]
  exact contDiffOn_levelSliceChart_trans hf n c ↑x ↑x' _ _ _ _

deps.title: The adapted chart at a regular point
deps.label: lem:adapted-chart-scalar-affine

deps.title: The slice chart of a level set
deps.label: def:level-slice-chart

entries.label: prop:regular-level-set-inclusion-smooth
entries.title: The inclusion of a regular level set is a smooth immersion
entries.body: In the situation of Proposition \ref{prop:regular-level-set-hypersurface-codim-one}, the inclusion $\iota_c : f^{-1}(c) \hookrightarrow M$ is smooth, and its differential is injective at every point. Since $\iota_c$ is a topological embedding, $f^{-1}(c)$ is an embedded smooth hypersurface in $M$.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.contMDiff_levelSet_val
lean.code:
theorem contMDiff_levelSet_val
    (hreg : ∀ x : M, f x = c → mfderiv I 𝓘(ℝ, ℝ) f x ≠ 0) :
    letI := levelSetChartedSpace hf n c hreg
    ContMDiff (𝓡 n) I ∞ ((↑) : (f ⁻¹' {c} : Set M) → M) := by
  letI := levelSetChartedSpace hf n c hreg
  intro x₀
  have hy : f ↑x₀ = c := levelSet_prop c x₀
  have hdf : mfderiv I 𝓘(ℝ, ℝ) f ↑x₀ ≠ 0 := hreg ↑x₀ hy
  rw [contMDiffAt_iff]
  refine ⟨continuous_subtype_val.continuousAt, ?_⟩
  have hz₀ : extChartAt (𝓡 n) x₀ x₀ ∈ levelSliceTarget hf ↑x₀ hdf n :=
    (levelSliceChart hf ↑x₀ hdf n c hy).map_source
      (mem_levelChartDomain_self hf ↑x₀ hdf)
  have ha : ContDiffAt ℝ ∞
      (fun w : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin n) =>
        extChartAt I (↑x₀ : M) ↑x₀ + sliceEmb (I := I) n hdf w)
      (extChartAt (𝓡 n) x₀ x₀) :=
    (contDiff_const.add (sliceEmb (I := I) n hdf).contDiff).contDiffAt
  have hb : ContDiffAt ℝ ∞ (adaptedStraightening hf ↑x₀ hdf).symm
      (extChartAt I (↑x₀ : M) ↑x₀
        + sliceEmb (I := I) n hdf (extChartAt (𝓡 n) x₀ x₀)) :=
    (contDiffOn_adaptedStraightening_symm hf ↑x₀ hdf).contDiffAt
      ((adaptedStraightening hf ↑x₀ hdf).open_target.mem_nhds hz₀)
  have hcomp := hb.comp (extChartAt (𝓡 n) x₀ x₀) ha
  exact ((hcomp.congr_of_eventuallyEq
    (writtenInExtChartAt_levelSet_val hf n c hreg x₀)).contDiffWithinAt)

deps.title: The slice chart of a level set
deps.label: def:level-slice-chart

deps.title: A regular level set of a real-valued function is a smooth hypersurface
deps.label: prop:regular-level-set-hypersurface-codim-one

entries.label: prop:regular-level-set-tangent-kernel
entries.title: The tangent space of a regular level set is the kernel of the differential
entries.body: In the situation of Proposition \ref{prop:regular-level-set-hypersurface-codim-one}, for every $x_0 \in f^{-1}(c)$ the image of $d(\iota_c)_{x_0}$ is exactly $\ker df_{x_0}$; that is, $T_{x_0} f^{-1}(c) = \ker df_{x_0}$ under the usual identification.

lean.name: LeeLib.Ch02.range_mfderiv_levelSet_val
lean.code:
theorem range_mfderiv_levelSet_val
    (hreg : ∀ x : M, f x = c → mfderiv I 𝓘(ℝ, ℝ) f x ≠ 0)
    (x₀ : (f ⁻¹' {c} : Set M)) :
    letI := levelSetChartedSpace hf n c hreg
    (mfderiv (𝓡 n) I ((↑) : (f ⁻¹' {c} : Set M) → M) x₀ :
        EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin n) →L[ℝ] E).range
      = levelHyperplane (I := I) f ↑x₀ := by
  letI := levelSetChartedSpace hf n c hreg
  have hclean : LinearMap.range
      ((((fderiv ℝ (adaptedStraightening hf ↑x₀ (hreg ↑x₀ (levelSet_prop c x₀))).symm
          (extChartAt I (↑x₀ : M) ↑x₀))
        ∘L sliceEmb (I := I) n (hreg ↑x₀ (levelSet_prop c x₀))) :
          EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin n) →L[ℝ] E) : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin n) →ₗ[ℝ] E)
      = levelHyperplane (I := I) f ↑x₀ := by
    refine Submodule.eq_of_le_of_finrank_eq ?_ ?_
    · rintro u hu
      obtain ⟨v, rfl⟩ := LinearMap.mem_range.1 hu
      have h := mfderivReal_mfderiv_levelSet_val hf n c hreg x₀ v
      rw [mfderiv_levelSet_val hf n c hreg x₀] at h
      exact (mem_levelHyperplane_iff (I := I) f ↑x₀ _).2 h
    · have hinj : Function.Injective
          ⇑((((fderiv ℝ (adaptedStraightening hf ↑x₀
                (hreg ↑x₀ (levelSet_prop c x₀))).symm
              (extChartAt I (↑x₀ : M) ↑x₀))
            ∘L sliceEmb (I := I) n (hreg ↑x₀ (levelSet_prop c x₀))) :
              EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin n) →L[ℝ] E)) :=
        (fderiv_adaptedStraightening_symm_injective hf ↑x₀
          (hreg ↑x₀ (levelSet_prop c x₀))).comp
          (sliceEmb_injective n (hreg ↑x₀ (levelSet_prop c x₀)))
      rw [LinearMap.finrank_range_of_inj hinj, finrank_euclideanSpace_fin,
        finrank_levelHyperplane n (hreg ↑x₀ (levelSet_prop c x₀))]
  rw [mfderiv_levelSet_val hf n c hreg x₀]
  exact hclean

deps.title: The inclusion of a regular level set is a smooth immersion
deps.label: prop:regular-level-set-inclusion-smooth

deps.title: A regular level set of a real-valued function is a smooth hypersurface
deps.label: prop:regular-level-set-hypersurface-codim-one

entries.label: prop:existence-local-defining-functions
entries.title: Existence of Local Defining Functions
entries.body: Suppose $\tilde{M}$ is a smooth manifold and $M \subseteq \tilde{M}$ is an embedded submanifold of codimension $k$. For each point $p \in M$, there exist a neighborhood $U$ of $p$ in $\tilde{M}$ and a smooth submersion $\Phi: U \to \mathbb{R}^k$ such that $M \cap U$ is a level set of $\Phi$.

entries.label: prop:tangent-space-submanifold
entries.title: Tangent Space to a Submanifold
entries.body:
Suppose $\tilde{M}$ is a smooth manifold, $M \subseteq \tilde{M}$ is an embedded submanifold, and $p \in M$. As a subspace of $T_p \tilde{M}$, the tangent space $T_p M$ is characterized by
\[
T_p M = \{v \in T_p \tilde{M} : vf = 0 \text{ whenever } f \in C^\infty(\tilde{M}) \text{ and } f|_M = 0\}.
\]

If $\Phi$ is a local defining function for $M$ on a neighborhood of $p$, then
\[
T_p M = \ker d\Phi_p.
\]

entries.label: prop:lagrange-multiplier-rule
entries.title: Lagrange Multiplier Rule
entries.body: Let $\tilde{M}$ be a smooth $m$-manifold and $M \subseteq \tilde{M}$ be an embedded hypersurface. Suppose $f \in C^\infty(\tilde{M})$, and $p \in M$ is a point at which $f$ attains a local maximum or minimum value among points in $M$. If $\Phi: U \to \mathbb{R}$ is a local defining function for $M$ on a neighborhood $U$ of $p$, then there is a real number $\lambda$ (called a \textbf{Lagrange multiplier}) such that $df_p = \lambda d\Phi_p$.

entries.label: prop:double-manifold
entries.title: prop:double-manifold
entries.body: Suppose $M$ is a smooth manifold with nonempty boundary. Then $D(M)$ is a topological manifold without boundary, and it has a smooth structure such that the natural maps $M \to M_1 \to D(M)$ and $M \to M_2 \to D(M)$ are smooth embeddings. Moreover, $D(M)$ is compact if and only if $M$ is compact, and connected if and only if $M$ is connected.

entries.label: lem:vector-bundle-chart-lemma
entries.title: Vector Bundle Chart Lemma

entries.label: lem:local-frame-criterion-for-smoothness
entries.title: Local Frame Criterion for Smoothness
entries.body: Let $\pi: E \to M$ be a smooth vector bundle, and let $\tau: M \to E$ be a rough section. If $(\sigma_i)$ is a smooth local frame for $E$ over an open subset $U \subseteq M$, then $\tau$ is smooth on $U$ if and only if its component functions with respect to $(\sigma_i)$ are smooth.

entries.label: lem:local-frame-criterion-for-subbundles
entries.title: Local Frame Criterion for Subbundles
entries.body: Let $\pi: E \to M$ be a smooth vector bundle endowed with a smooth fibre metric $\langle\cdot,\cdot\rangle$, and suppose that for each $p \in M$ we are given a $k$-dimensional linear subspace $D_p \subseteq E_p$. Suppose further that each $p \in M$ has a neighborhood $U$ on which there are smooth local sections $\sigma_1, \ldots, \sigma_k: U \to E$ such that $\sigma_1(q), \ldots, \sigma_k(q)$ form a basis for $D_q$ at each $q \in U$. Then $D = \bigcup_{p \in M} D_p \subseteq E$ is a smooth subbundle of $E$: it carries a smooth rank-$k$ vector bundle structure over $M$ whose fibre at $p$ is $D_p$ with the linear structure inherited from $E_p$.

lean.name: LeeLib.AppendixA.subContMDiffVectorBundle
lean.code:
theorem subContMDiffVectorBundle (h : HasLocalSubframes IB F n k D) :
    letI := subTotalSpaceTopology h
    letI := subFiberBundle h
    letI := subVectorBundle h
    ContMDiffVectorBundle n (EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin k)) (fun x => ↥(D x)) IB :=
  letI := subVectorPrebundle_isContMDiff h
  (subVectorPrebundle h).contMDiffVectorBundle IB

entries.label: rem:subbundle-criterion-metric
entries.title: rem:subbundle-criterion-metric
entries.body: Lee states the preceding lemma for a bare smooth vector bundle, with no fibre metric. The metric enters our proof only to normalize the given local $D$-frames, which is what makes the transition functions visibly smooth (they become Gram matrices); it is not needed for the truth of the statement. The hypothesis is harmless where the lemma is applied: Proposition \ref{prop:normal-bundle} applies it inside the ambient tangent bundle $F^*T\tilde M$ of Lemma \ref{lem:ambient-tangent-bundle-riemannian}, which carries the ambient metric $\tilde g$ by construction. Removing the hypothesis in general would require the statement that every smooth vector bundle over a smooth manifold admits a fibre metric --- the bundle analogue of Proposition \ref{prop:existence-riemannian-metric}, which we have only for tangent bundles.

entries.label: lem:pushforward-vector-field
entries.title: LeeSM, Prop. 8.19 \& Cor. 8.21

entries.label: lem:derivation-characterization
entries.title: LeeSM, Prop. 8.15
entries.body: Let $M$ be a smooth manifold with or without boundary. A map $D : C^\infty(M) \to C^\infty(M)$ is a derivation if and only if it is of the form $Df = Xf$ for some $X \in \mathfrak{X}(M)$.

entries.label: prop:lie-bracket-properties
entries.title: Properties of Lie Brackets
entries.body:
[LeeSM, Prop. 8.28] Let $M$ be a smooth manifold with or without boundary and $X, Y, Z \in \mathfrak{X}(M)$.
\begin{enumerate}[label=(\alph*)]
\item $[X, Y]$ is bilinear over $\mathbb{R}$ as a function of $X$ and $Y$.
\item $[X, Y] = -[Y, X]$ (antisymmetry).
\item $[X, [Y, Z]] + [Y, [Z, X]] + [Z, [X, Y]] = 0$ (Jacobi identity).
\item For $f, g \in C^\infty(M)$, $[fX, gY] = fg[X, Y] + (fXg)Y - (gYf)X$.
\end{enumerate}

entries.label: prop:naturality-of-lie-brackets
entries.title: Naturality of Lie Brackets
entries.body: Let $F : M \to N$ be a smooth map between manifolds with or without boundary, and let $X_1, X_2 \in \mathfrak{X}(M)$ and $Y_1, Y_2 \in \mathfrak{X}(N)$ be vector fields such that $X_i$ is $F$-related to $Y_i$ for $i = 1, 2$. Then $[X_1, X_2]$ is $F$-related to $[Y_1, Y_2]$. In particular, if $F$ is a diffeomorphism, then $F_*[X_1, X_2] = [F_* X_1, F_* X_2]$.

entries.label: cor:brackets-vector-fields-tangent-to-submanifolds
entries.title: Brackets of Vector Fields Tangent to Submanifolds
entries.body: Let $\tilde{M}$ be a smooth manifold and let $M$ be an immersed submanifold with or without boundary in $\tilde{M}$. If $Y_1$ and $Y_2$ are smooth vector fields on $\tilde{M}$ that are tangent to $M$, then $[Y_1, Y_2]$ is also tangent to $M$.

entries.label: thm:fundamental-theorem-on-flows
entries.title: Fundamental Theorem on Flows
entries.body:
[LeeSM, Thm. 9.12] Let $X$ be a smooth vector field on a smooth manifold $M$ (without boundary). There is a
unique smooth maximal flow $\theta: \mathcal{D} \to M$ whose infinitesimal generator is $X$. This
flow has the following properties:
\begin{enumerate}[label=(\alph*)]
\item For each $p \in M$, the curve $\theta^{(p)}: \mathcal{D}^{(p)} \to M$ is the unique maximal integral
curve of $X$ starting at $p$.
\item If $s \in \mathcal{D}^{(p)}$, then $\mathcal{D}^{(\theta(s,p))}$ is the interval $\mathcal{D}^{(p)} - s = \{t - s : t \in \mathcal{D}^{(p)}\}$.
\item For each $t \in \mathbb{R}$, the set $M_t$ is open in $M$, and $\theta_t: M_t \to M_{-t}$ is a diffeomorphism with inverse $\theta_{-t}$.
\end{enumerate}

entries.label: lem:escape-lemma
entries.title: Escape Lemma
entries.body:
Suppose $M$ is a smooth manifold and $X \in \mathfrak{X}(M)$.
If $\gamma: I \to M$ is a maximal integral curve of $X$ whose domain $I$ has a finite least
upper bound $b$, then for every $t_0 \in I$, $\gamma([t_0, b))$ is not contained in any compact
subset of $M$.

entries.label: prop:canonical-form-vector-field
entries.title: Canonical Form for a Vector Field
entries.body:
[LeeSM, Thm. 9.22] Let $X$ be a smooth vector field on a smooth manifold $M$, and suppose $p \in M$ is a point
where $X_p \ne 0$. There exist smooth coordinates $(x^i)$ on some neighborhood of $p$ in
which $X$ has the coordinate representation $\partial/\partial x^1$.

entries.label: prop:lie-derivative-equals-bracket
entries.title: LeeSM, Thm.~9.38
entries.body: Suppose $M$ is a smooth manifold and $X, Y \in \mathfrak{X}(M)$. The Lie derivative of $Y$ with respect to $X$ is equal to the Lie bracket $[X,Y]$.

entries.label: prop:invariant-vector-field
entries.title: LeeSM, Thm.~9.42
entries.body: Let $M$ be a smooth manifold and $X \in \mathfrak{X}(M)$. A smooth vector field is invariant under the flow of $X$ if and only if its Lie derivative with respect to $X$ is identically zero.

entries.label: prop:canonical-commuting-fields
entries.title: Canonical Form for Commuting Vector Fields
entries.body: [LeeSM, Thm.~9.46] Let $M$ be a smooth $n$-manifold, and let $(X_1,\ldots,X_k)$ be a linearly independent $k$-tuple of smooth commuting vector fields on an open subset $W \subseteq M$. For each $p \in W$, there exists a smooth coordinate chart $(U, (x^i))$ centered at $p$ such that $X_i = \partial/\partial x^i$ for $i = 1,\ldots,k$.

entries.label: prop:elementary-covering-maps
entries.title: Elementary Properties of Smooth Covering Maps
entries.body:
[LeeSM, Prop.~4.33]
\begin{enumerate}[label=(\alph*)]
\item Every smooth covering map is a local diffeomorphism, a smooth submersion, an open map, and a quotient map.
\item An injective smooth covering map is a diffeomorphism.
\item A topological covering map is a smooth covering map if and only if it is a local diffeomorphism.
\end{enumerate}

entries.label: prop:covering-proper-finite-sheeted
entries.title: prop:covering-proper-finite-sheeted
entries.body: A covering map is a proper map if and only if it is finite-sheeted.

entries.label: ex:covering-n-torus
entries.title: A Covering of the $n$-Torus
entries.body: The $n$-torus is the manifold $\mathbb{T}^n = S^1 \times \cdots \times S^1$, regarded as the subset of $\mathbb{R}^{2n}$ defined by $(x^1)^2 + (x^2)^2 = 1,\ \ldots,\ (x^{2n-1})^2 + (x^{2n})^2 = 1$. The smooth map $X: \mathbb{R}^n \to \mathbb{T}^n$ given by $X(u^1, \ldots, u^n) = (\cos u^1, \sin u^1, \ldots, \cos u^n, \sin u^n)$ is a smooth covering map. $\mathbin{//}$

entries.label: prop:lifts-smooth-maps-smooth
entries.title: Lifts of Smooth Maps are Smooth
entries.body:
If $\pi: \tilde{M} \to M$ is a smooth covering map, $B$ is a smooth manifold with or without boundary, and $F: B \to M$ is a smooth map, then every lift of $F$ is smooth.

Proof. Since $\pi$ is a smooth submersion, every lift $\tilde{F}: B \to \tilde{M}$ can be written locally as $\tilde{F} = \sigma \circ F$, where $\sigma$ is a smooth local section of $\pi$ (see Thm. A.17). $\square$

entries.label: prop:lifting-properties-covering
entries.title: Lifting Properties of Covering Maps
entries.body:
Suppose $\pi: \tilde{M} \to M$ is a covering map.

(a) \textsc{Unique Lifting Property} [LeeTM, Thm. 11.12] If $B$ is a connected topological space and $F: B \to M$ is a continuous map, then any two lifts of $F$ that agree at one point are identical.

(b) \textsc{Path Lifting Property} [LeeTM, Cor. 11.14] Suppose $f: [0,1] \to M$ is a continuous path. For every $\tilde{p} \in \pi^{-1}(f(0))$, there exists a unique lift $\tilde{f}: [0,1] \to \tilde{M}$ of $f$ such that $\tilde{f}(0) = \tilde{p}$.

(c) \textsc{Monodromy Theorem} [LeeTM, Thm. 11.15] Suppose $f, g: [0,1] \to M$ are path-homotopic paths and $\tilde{f}, \tilde{g}: [0,1] \to \tilde{M}$ are their lifts starting at the same point. Then $f$ and $g$ are path-homotopic and $\tilde{f}(1) = \tilde{g}(1)$.

entries.label: thm:injectivity-theorem
entries.title: Injectivity Theorem
entries.body: [LeeTM, Thm. 11.16] If $\pi: \tilde{M} \to M$ is a covering map, then for each point $\tilde{x} \in \tilde{M}$, the induced fundamental group homomorphism $\pi_*: \pi_1(\tilde{M}, \tilde{x}) \to \pi_1(M, \pi(\tilde{x}))$ is injective.

entries.label: thm:lifting-criterion
entries.title: Lifting Criterion
entries.body: [LeeTM, Thm. 11.18] Suppose $\pi: \tilde{M} \to M$ is a covering map, $B$ is a connected and locally path-connected topological space, and $F: B \to M$ is a continuous map. Given $b \in B$ and $\tilde{x} \in \tilde{M}$ such that $\pi(\tilde{x}) = F(b)$, the map $F$ has a lift to $\tilde{M}$ if and only if $F_*(\pi_1(B,b)) \subseteq \pi_*(\pi_1(\tilde{M}, \tilde{x}))$.

entries.label: cor:lifting-maps-simply-connected
entries.title: Lifting Maps from Simply Connected Spaces
entries.body: [LeeTM, Cor. 11.19] Suppose $\pi: \tilde{M} \to M$ and $F: B \to M$ satisfy the hypotheses of Theorem A.56, and in addition $B$ is simply connected. Then every continuous map $F: B \to M$ has a lift to $\tilde{M}$. Given any $b \in B$, the lift can be chosen to take $b$ to any point in the fiber over $F(b)$.

entries.label: cor:covering-map-homeomorphism
entries.title: Covering Map Homeomorphism Criterion
entries.body:
A covering map $\pi: \tilde{M} \to M$ is a homeomorphism if and only if the induced homomorphism $\pi_*: \pi_1(\tilde{M}, \tilde{x}) \to \pi_1(M, \pi(\tilde{x}))$ is surjective for some (hence every) $\tilde{x} \in \tilde{M}$. A smooth covering map is a diffeomorphism if and only if the induced homomorphism is surjective.

Proof. By Theorem A.56, the hypothesis implies that the identity map $\operatorname{Id}: M \to M$ has a lift $\widetilde{\operatorname{Id}}: \tilde{M} \to \tilde{M}$, which in this case is a continuous inverse for $\pi$. If $\pi$ is a smooth covering map, then the lift is also smooth. $\square$

entries.label: cor:coverings-simply-connected
entries.title: Coverings of Simply Connected Spaces
entries.body: [LeeTM, Cor. 11.33] If $M$ is a simply connected manifold with or without boundary, then every covering of $M$ is a homeomorphism, and if $M$ is smooth, every smooth covering is a diffeomorphism.

entries.label: prop:universal-covering-manifold
entries.title: Existence of a Universal Covering Manifold
entries.body: [LeeSM, Cor. 4.31] If $M$ is a connected smooth manifold, then there exists a simply connected smooth manifold $\tilde{M}$, called the universal covering manifold of $M$, and a smooth covering map $\pi: \tilde{M} \to M$. It is unique in the sense that if $\tilde{M}'$ is any other simply connected smooth manifold that admits a smooth covering map $\pi': \tilde{M}' \to M$, then there exists a diffeomorphism $\Phi: \tilde{M} \to \tilde{M}'$ such that $\pi' \circ \Phi = \pi$.

entries.label: prop:fiber-cardinality-fundamental-group
entries.title: LeeTM, Cor. 11.31
entries.body: With $\pi: \tilde{M} \to M$ as in the previous proposition, each fiber of $\pi$ has the same cardinality as the fundamental group of $M$.

deps.title: Existence of a Universal Covering Manifold
deps.label: prop:universal-covering-manifold

entries.label: prop:multilinear-maps-tensor-isomorphism
entries.title: prop:multilinear-maps-tensor-isomorphism
entries.body:
Let $V$ be a finite-dimensional vector space. There is a natural (basis-independent) isomorphism between $T^{(k+1,l)}(V)$ and the space of multilinear maps
\[
\underbrace{V^* \times \cdots \times V^*}_{k \text{ copies}} \times \underbrace{V \times \cdots \times V}_{l \text{ copies}} \to V.
\]

entries.label: lem:sum-sign-prod-det
entries.title: lem:sum-sign-prod-det
entries.body:
Let $C$ be a square matrix over $R$ indexed by $\iota$. Then
\[
\sum_{\sigma \in S_\iota} \sum_{\tau \in S_\iota} \operatorname{sgn}(\sigma)\operatorname{sgn}(\tau) \prod_{i \in \iota} C_{\sigma(i)\,\tau(i)} = k!\,\det C .
\]

lean.name: Matrix.sum_sum_sign_mul_sign_mul_prod
lean.code:
theorem sum_sum_sign_mul_sign_mul_prod (C : Matrix m m R) :
    ∑ σ : Equiv.Perm m, ∑ τ : Equiv.Perm m,
        ((Equiv.Perm.sign σ : ℤ) : R) * ((Equiv.Perm.sign τ : ℤ) : R) * ∏ i, C (σ i) (τ i)
      = (Fintype.card m).factorial * C.det := by
  have key : ∀ σ : Equiv.Perm m,
      (∑ τ : Equiv.Perm m,
        ((Equiv.Perm.sign σ : ℤ) : R) * ((Equiv.Perm.sign τ : ℤ) : R) * ∏ i, C (σ i) (τ i))
        = C.det := by
    intro σ
    rw [← Equiv.sum_comp (Equiv.mulRight σ)
      (fun τ => ((Equiv.Perm.sign σ : ℤ) : R) * ((Equiv.Perm.sign τ : ℤ) : R)
        * ∏ i, C (σ i) (τ i))]
    rw [← Matrix.det_transpose, Matrix.det_apply']
    refine Finset.sum_congr rfl fun ρ _ => ?_
    have hprod : (∏ i, C (σ i) ((Equiv.mulRight σ ρ) i)) = ∏ i, Cᵀ (ρ i) i := by
      rw [← Equiv.prod_comp σ (fun j => Cᵀ (ρ j) j)]
      rfl
    rw [hprod]
    have hsign : ((Equiv.Perm.sign σ : ℤ) : R) * ((Equiv.Perm.sign (Equiv.mulRight σ ρ) : ℤ) : R)
        = ((Equiv.Perm.sign ρ : ℤ) : R) := by
      rcases Int.units_eq_one_or (Equiv.Perm.sign σ) with h | h <;>
        simp [Equiv.Perm.sign_mul, h]
    rw [hsign]
  rw [Finset.sum_congr rfl fun σ _ => key σ]
  simp [Finset.sum_const, Fintype.card_perm]

omit [Fintype n] [DecidableEq n] in

entries.label: lem:cauchy-binet
entries.title: lem:cauchy-binet
entries.body:
\textbf{(Cauchy--Binet formula.)} Let $M$ and $N$ be matrices over $R$ with rows indexed by $\iota$ and columns by $\iota'$. Then
\[
\sum_{s \colon \iota \to \iota'} \det\big(M[\,\cdot\,, s]\big)\,\det\big(N[\,\cdot\,, s]\big) = k!\,\det\big(MN^{T}\big),
\]
the sum running over all maps $s$, not only the injective or increasing ones.

lean.name: Matrix.sum_det_submatrix_mul_det_submatrix
lean.code:
theorem sum_det_submatrix_mul_det_submatrix (M N : Matrix m n R) :
    ∑ s : m → n, (M.submatrix id s).det * (N.submatrix id s).det
      = (Fintype.card m).factorial * (M * Nᵀ).det := by
  rw [Finset.sum_congr rfl fun s _ => det_submatrix_mul_det_submatrix M N s]
  rw [Finset.sum_comm]
  rw [← sum_sum_sign_mul_sign_mul_prod (M * Nᵀ)]
  refine Finset.sum_congr rfl fun σ _ => ?_
  rw [Finset.sum_comm]
  refine Finset.sum_congr rfl fun τ _ => ?_
  rw [← Finset.mul_sum]
  congr 1
  rw [← Fintype.prod_sum (fun (i : m) (j : n) => M (σ i) j * N (τ i) j)]
  refine Finset.prod_congr rfl fun i _ => ?_
  rw [Matrix.mul_apply]
  rfl

end Matrix

namespace AlternatingMap

variable {R : Type*} [CommRing R] {V N : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module R V]
  [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N]
  {ι ι' : Type*} [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι] [Fintype ι'] [DecidableEq ι']

omit [DecidableEq ι'] in

entries.label: lem:alternating-cauchy-binet
entries.title: lem:alternating-cauchy-binet
entries.body:
Let $V$ and $N$ be $R$-modules, let $\theta \colon V^{\iota} \to N$ be alternating and $R$-multilinear, let $e \colon \iota' \to V$ be a family of vectors, and let $A$ be a matrix over $R$ with rows indexed by $\iota$ and columns by $\iota'$. Then
\[
\sum_{s \colon \iota \to \iota'} \det\big(A[\,\cdot\,, s]\big) \cdot \theta\big(e_{s(1)}, \ldots, e_{s(k)}\big) = k! \cdot \theta\Big(\sum_{m \in \iota'} A_{1m} e_m, \ldots, \sum_{m \in \iota'} A_{km} e_m\Big).
\]

lean.name: AlternatingMap.sum_det_submatrix_smul_apply
lean.code:
theorem sum_det_submatrix_smul_apply (θ : V [⋀^ι]→ₗ[R] N) (e : ι' → V) (A : Matrix ι ι' R) :
    ∑ s : ι → ι', (A.submatrix id s).det • θ (fun i => e (s i))
      = (Fintype.card ι).factorial • θ (fun i => ∑ m, A i m • e m) := by
  have hRHS : θ (fun i => ∑ m, A i m • e m)
      = ∑ u : ι → ι', (∏ i, A i (u i)) • θ (fun i => e (u i)) := by
    have h := θ.toMultilinearMap.map_sum (fun (i : ι) (m : ι') => A i m • e m)
    refine h.trans (Finset.sum_congr rfl fun u _ => ?_)
    exact θ.toMultilinearMap.map_smul_univ (fun i => A i (u i)) (fun i => e (u i))
  have key : ∀ σ : Equiv.Perm ι,
      (∑ s : ι → ι',
        (((Equiv.Perm.sign σ : ℤ) : R) * ∏ i, A (σ i) (s i)) • θ (fun i => e (s i)))
        = ∑ u : ι → ι', (∏ i, A i (u i)) • θ (fun i => e (u i)) := by
    intro σ
    rw [← Equiv.sum_comp (Equiv.arrowCongr σ.symm (Equiv.refl ι'))
      (fun s : ι → ι' => (((Equiv.Perm.sign σ : ℤ) : R) * ∏ i, A (σ i) (s i))
        • θ (fun i => e (s i)))]
    refine Finset.sum_congr rfl fun u _ => ?_
    have hu : (Equiv.arrowCongr σ.symm (Equiv.refl ι')) u = fun i => u (σ i) := rfl
    rw [hu]
    have hprod : (∏ i, A (σ i) (u (σ i))) = ∏ j, A j (u j) :=
      Equiv.prod_comp σ (fun j => A j (u j))
    have hperm : (θ fun i => e (u (σ i))) = Equiv.Perm.sign σ • θ (fun i => e (u i)) :=
      θ.map_perm (fun i => e (u i)) σ
    rw [hprod, hperm]
    rcases Int.units_eq_one_or (Equiv.Perm.sign σ) with h | h <;> simp [h]
  rw [hRHS, Finset.smul_sum]
  have hLHS : ∀ s : ι → ι', (A.submatrix id s).det • θ (fun i => e (s i))
      = ∑ σ : Equiv.Perm ι,
          (((Equiv.Perm.sign σ : ℤ) : R) * ∏ i, A (σ i) (s i)) • θ (fun i => e (s i)) := by
    intro s
    rw [Matrix.det_apply', Finset.sum_smul]
    rfl
  rw [Finset.sum_congr rfl fun s _ => hLHS s, Finset.sum_comm,
    Finset.sum_congr rfl fun σ _ => key σ]
  rw [Finset.sum_const, Finset.card_univ, Fintype.card_perm, ← Finset.smul_sum]

entries.label: lem:tensor-characterization-lemma
entries.title: Tensor Characterization Lemma
entries.body:
[LeeSM, Lemma 12.24] A map
\[
\mathcal{F}: \underbrace{\mathcal{T}^1(M) \times \cdots \times \mathcal{T}^1(M)}_{k \text{ factors}} \times \underbrace{\mathfrak{X}(M) \times \cdots \times \mathfrak{X}(M)}_{l \text{ factors}} \to C^\infty(M)
\]
is induced by a smooth $(k,l)$-tensor field as above if and only if it is multilinear over $C^\infty(M)$. Similarly, a map
\[
\mathcal{F}: \underbrace{\mathcal{T}^1(M) \times \cdots \times \mathcal{T}^1(M)}_{k \text{ factors}} \times \underbrace{\mathfrak{X}(M) \times \cdots \times \mathfrak{X}(M)}_{l \text{ factors}} \to \mathfrak{X}(M)
\]
is induced by a smooth $(k+1,l)$-tensor field as in Proposition B.1 if and only if it is multilinear over $C^\infty(M)$.

entries.label: prop:tensor-pullback-properties
entries.title: Properties of Tensor Pullbacks
entries.body:
[LeeSM, Prop. 12.25] Suppose $F: M \to N$ and $G: P \to M$ are smooth maps, $A$ and $B$ are covariant tensor fields on $N$, and $f$ is a real-valued function on $N$.
\begin{enumerate}
\item[(a)] $F^*(fB) = (f \circ F) F^* B$.
\item[(b)] $F^*(A \otimes B) = F^* A \otimes F^* B$.
\item[(c)] $F^*(A + B) = F^* A + F^* B$.
\item[(d)] $F^* B$ is a (continuous) tensor field, and it is smooth if $B$ is smooth.
\item[(e)] $(F \circ G)^* B = G^*(F^* B)$.
\item[(f)] $(\text{Id}_N)^* B = B$.
\end{enumerate}

entries.label: prop:pullback-computation-coordinates
entries.title: prop:pullback-computation-coordinates
entries.body:
Let $F: M \to N$ be smooth, and let $B$ be a covariant $k$-tensor field on $N$. If $p \in M$ and $(y^i)$ are smooth coordinates for $N$ on a neighborhood of $F(p)$, then $F^* B$ has the following expression in a neighborhood of $p$:
\[
F^* \left( B_{i_1 \ldots i_k} \, dy^{i_1} \otimes \cdots \otimes dy^{i_k} \right) = \left( B_{i_1 \ldots i_k} \circ F \right) d(y^{i_1} \circ F) \otimes \cdots \otimes d(y^{i_k} \circ F).
\]

entries.label: prop:lie-derivative-tensor-product-rule
entries.title: prop:lie-derivative-tensor-product-rule
entries.body:
Suppose $M$ is a smooth manifold, $X \in \mathfrak{X}(M)$, and $A$ is a smooth covariant $k$-tensor field on $M$. For all smooth vector fields $Z_1, \ldots, Z_k$:
\[
(\mathscr{L}_X A)(Z_1, \ldots, Z_k) = X(A(Z_1, \ldots, Z_k)) - A(\mathscr{L}_X Z_1, Z_2, \ldots, Z_k) - A(Z_1, \mathscr{L}_X Z_2, \ldots, Z_k) - \cdots - A(Z_1, \ldots, \mathscr{L}_X Z_k).
\]

entries.label: prop:invariant-tensor-characterization
entries.title: prop:invariant-tensor-characterization
entries.body: Let $M$ be a smooth manifold and $X \in \mathfrak{X}(M)$. A smooth covariant tensor field is invariant under the flow of $X$ if and only if its Lie derivative with respect to $X$ is identically zero.

entries.label: prop:exterior-differentiation-properties
entries.title: Properties of Exterior Differentiation
entries.body:
[LeeSM, Thm. 14.24] Suppose $M$ is a smooth $n$-manifold with or without boundary.
\begin{enumerate}
\item[(a)] For each $k = 0, \ldots, n$, the operator $d: \Omega^k(M) \to \Omega^{k+1}(M)$ is well defined, independently of coordinates.
\item[(b)] $d$ is linear over $\mathbb{R}$.
\item[(c)] $d \circ d \equiv 0$.
\item[(d)] If $\omega \in \Omega^k(M)$ and $\eta \in \Omega^l(M)$, then
\[
d(\omega \wedge \eta) = d\omega \wedge \eta + (-1)^k \omega \wedge d\eta.
\]
\end{enumerate}

entries.label: prop:exterior-derivative-one-form
entries.title: Exterior Derivative of a 1-form
entries.body:
[LeeSM, Prop. 14.29] If $\omega$ is a smooth 1-form and $X, Y$ are vector fields, then
\[
d\omega(X,Y) = X(\omega(Y)) - Y(\omega(X)) - \omega([X,Y]).
\]

entries.label: prop:exterior-derivative-naturality
entries.title: Naturality of the Exterior Derivative

entries.label: lem:poincare-lemma
entries.title: The Poincaré Lemma
entries.body: [LeeSM, Thm. 17.14] If $U$ is a star-shaped open subset of $\mathbb{R}^n$, then for $k \geq 1$, every closed $k$-form on $U$ is exact.

entries.label: prop:orientation-determined-by-n-form
entries.title: Orientation Determined by an $n$-Form
entries.body:
[LeeSM, Prop. 15.5]
Let $M$ be an $n$-manifold with or without boundary. Every nonvanishing $n$-form $\mu \in \Omega^n(M)$ determines a unique orientation of $M$ by declaring an ordered basis $(b_1, \ldots, b_n)$ for $T_p M$ to be positively oriented if and only if $\mu_p(b_1, \ldots, b_n) > 0$. Conversely, if $M$ is oriented, there is a smooth nonvanishing $n$-form that determines the orientation in this way.

entries.label: prop:orientation-of-hypersurface
entries.title: Orientation of a Hypersurface
entries.body:
[LeeSM, Prop. 15.21]
Suppose $M$ is an oriented smooth $n$-manifold with or without boundary, $S \subseteq M$ is a smooth immersed hypersurface, and $N$ is a continuous vector field along $S$ (i.e., a continuous map $N : S \to TM$ such that $N_p \in T_p M$ for each $p \in S$). If $N$ is nowhere tangent to $S$, then $S$ has a unique orientation determined by the $(n-1)$-form $\iota^*(N \lrcorner \mu)$, where $\iota : S \hookrightarrow M$ is inclusion and $\mu$ is any positively oriented orientation form for $M$.

entries.label: prop:induced-orientation-boundary
entries.title: Induced Orientation on a Boundary
entries.body: If $M$ is an oriented smooth manifold with boundary, then $\partial M$ is orientable, and it has a canonical orientation (called the \emph{induced orientation} or \emph{Stokes orientation}) determined by $\iota^*(N \lrcorner \mu)$, where $\iota: \partial M \hookrightarrow M$ is inclusion, $N$ is any outward-pointing vector field along $\partial M$, and $\mu$ is any positively oriented orientation form for $M$.

entries.label: prop:orientation-covering-theorem
entries.title: Orientation Covering Theorem
entries.body: If $M$ is a connected, nonorientable smooth manifold, then there exist an oriented smooth manifold $\tilde{M}$ and a two-sheeted smooth covering map $\tilde{\pi}: \tilde{M} \to M$, called the \emph{orientation covering of $M$}.

entries.label: cor:simply-connected-orientable
entries.title: cor:simply-connected-orientable
entries.body: Every simply connected smooth manifold is orientable.

entries.label: prop:properties-integrals-forms
entries.title: Properties of Integrals of Forms
entries.body:
Suppose $M$ and $N$ are nonempty oriented smooth $n$-manifolds with or without boundary, and $\omega, \eta$ are compactly supported $n$-forms on $M$.
\begin{enumerate}
\item[(a)] \textbf{LINEARITY:} If $a, b \in \mathbb{R}$, then
\[
\int_M (a\omega + b\eta) = a \int_M \omega + b \int_M \eta.
\]
\item[(b)] \textbf{ORIENTATION REVERSAL:} If $-M$ denotes $M$ with the opposite orientation, then
\[
\int_{-M} \omega = -\int_M \omega.
\]
\item[(c)] \textbf{POSITIVITY:} If $\omega$ is a positively oriented orientation form, then $\int_M \omega > 0$.
\item[(d)] \textbf{DIFFEOMORPHISM INVARIANCE:} If $F: N \to M$ is an orientation-preserving or orientation-reversing diffeomorphism, then
\[
\int_M \omega = \begin{cases}
\int_N F^*\omega & \text{if } F \text{ is orientation-preserving,} \\
-\int_N F^*\omega & \text{if } F \text{ is orientation-reversing.}
\end{cases}
\]
\end{enumerate}

entries.label: thm:stokes-theorem
entries.title: Stokes's Theorem

entries.label: cor:exact-integral-zero
entries.title: cor:exact-integral-zero
entries.body: Suppose $M$ is a compact oriented smooth $n$-manifold (without boundary). Then the integral of every exact $n$-form on $M$ is zero.

entries.label: prop:lie-group-homomorphism-properties
entries.title: LeeSM, Thms. 7.5 \& 21.27

entries.label: ex:lie-group-examples
entries.title: Lie Groups
entries.body:
(a) Every countable group with the discrete topology is a zero-dimensional Lie group, called a \emph{discrete Lie group}.

(b) $\mathbb{R}^n$ and $\mathbb{C}^n$ are Lie groups under addition.

(c) The sets $\mathbb{R}^*$, $\mathbb{R}^+$, and $\mathbb{C}^*$ of nonzero real numbers, positive real numbers, and nonzero complex numbers, respectively, are Lie groups under multiplication.

(d) The unit circle $S^1 \subseteq \mathbb{C}$ is a 1-dimensional Lie group under complex multiplication, called the \emph{circle group}.

(e) Given Lie groups $G_1, \ldots, G_k$, their \emph{direct product group} is the Lie group whose underlying manifold is $G_1 \times \cdots \times G_k$, and whose multiplication is given by $(g_1, \ldots, g_k)(g'_1, \ldots, g'_k) = (g_1 g'_1, \ldots, g_k g'_k)$. For example, the $n$-torus is the $n$-fold product group $T^n = S^1 \times \cdots \times S^1$. The 2-torus is often simply called \emph{the torus}.

(f) The \emph{general linear groups} $GL(n, \mathbb{R})$ and $GL(n, \mathbb{C})$, consisting of all invertible $n \times n$ real or complex matrices, respectively, are Lie groups under matrix multiplication. The identity element in both cases is the $n \times n$ identity matrix, denoted by $I_n$. More generally, if $V$ is an $n$-dimensional real or complex vector space, the group $GL(V)$ of invertible linear maps from $V$ to itself is a Lie group isomorphic to $GL(n, \mathbb{R})$ or $GL(n, \mathbb{C})$.

(g) The real and complex \emph{special linear groups} are the subgroups $SL(n, \mathbb{R}) \subseteq GL(n, \mathbb{R})$ and $SL(n, \mathbb{C}) \subseteq GL(n, \mathbb{C})$ consisting of matrices of determinant 1.

(h) The \emph{orthogonal group} $O(n) \subseteq GL(n, \mathbb{R})$ is the subgroup consisting of orthogonal matrices, those that satisfy $A^T A = I_n$, where $A^T$ is the transpose of $A$. The \emph{special orthogonal group} is the subgroup $SO(n) = O(n) \cap SL(n, \mathbb{R})$.

(i) Similarly, the \emph{unitary group} $U(n) \subseteq GL(n, \mathbb{C})$ is the subgroup consisting of complex unitary matrices, those that satisfy $A^* A = I_n$, where $A^* = \overline{A}^T$ is the conjugate transpose of $A$, called its \emph{adjoint}. The \emph{special unitary group} is $SU(n) = U(n) \cap SL(n, \mathbb{C})$.

entries.label: prop:evaluation-map-lie-algebra-isomorphism
entries.title: LeeSM, Thm. 8.37
entries.body: Let $G$ be a Lie group with identity $e$. The evaluation map $X \mapsto X_e$ is a vector space isomorphism from $\operatorname{Lie}(G)$ to $T_e G$, so $\operatorname{Lie}(G)$ has the same dimension as $G$ itself.

entries.label: ex:lie-algebras
entries.title: Lie Algebras
entries.body:
\begin{enumerate}
\item[(a)] If $M$ is a smooth positive-dimensional manifold, the space $\mathfrak{X}(M)$ of all smooth vector fields on $M$ is an infinite-dimensional Lie algebra under the bracket.
\item[(b)] The vector space $\mathfrak{gl}(n, \mathbb{R})$ of all $n \times n$ real matrices is an $n^2$-dimensional Lie algebra under the \textbf{commutator bracket} $[A, B] = AB - BA$. The canonical identification of both $\mathfrak{gl}(n, \mathbb{R})$ and $\operatorname{Lie}(\operatorname{GL}(n, \mathbb{R}))$ with the tangent space to $\operatorname{GL}(n, \mathbb{R})$ at the identity yields a Lie algebra isomorphism $\mathfrak{gl}(n, \mathbb{R}) \cong \operatorname{Lie}(\operatorname{GL}(n, \mathbb{R}))$ [LeeSM, Prop.\ 8.41].
\item[(c)] Similarly, the space $\mathfrak{gl}(n, \mathbb{C})$ of $n \times n$ complex matrices is a $2n^2$-dimensional (real) Lie algebra under the commutator bracket, isomorphic to the Lie algebra of $\operatorname{GL}(n, \mathbb{C})$ [LeeSM, Prop.\ 8.48].
\item[(d)] The space $\mathfrak{o}(n)$ of skew-symmetric $n \times n$ real matrices is a Lie subalgebra of $\mathfrak{gl}(n, \mathbb{R})$, isomorphic to the Lie algebra of $O(n)$ [LeeSM, Example 8.47].
\item[(e)] If $V$ is a vector space, the space $\mathfrak{gl}(V)$ of all linear maps from $V$ to itself is a Lie algebra under the commutator bracket $[A, B] = A \circ B - B \circ A$. In case $V$ is finite-dimensional, $\mathfrak{gl}(V)$ is isomorphic to the Lie algebra of the Lie group $\operatorname{GL}(V)$.
\item[(f)] A Lie algebra in which all brackets are zero is called an \textbf{abelian Lie algebra}. Every vector space can be made into an abelian Lie algebra by defining all brackets to be zero. The Lie algebra of a connected Lie group is abelian if and only if the group is abelian (see [LeeSM, Problems 8-25 and 20-7]).
\end{enumerate}

entries.label: prop:induced-lie-algebra-homomorphism
entries.title: The Induced Lie Algebra Homomorphism
entries.body: [LeeSM, Thm. 8.44] Let $G$ and $H$ be Lie groups, and let $\mathfrak{g}$ and $\mathfrak{h}$ be their Lie algebras. Given a Lie group homomorphism $F: G \to H$, there is a unique Lie algebra homomorphism $F_*: \mathfrak{g} \to \mathfrak{h}$, called the \textit{induced Lie algebra homomorphism of $F$}, with the property that for each $X \in \mathfrak{g}$, the vector field $F_* X \in \mathfrak{h}$ is $F$-related to $X$.

entries.label: prop:properties-of-induced-homomorphisms
entries.title: Properties of Induced Homomorphisms
entries.body:
Let $G, H, K$ be Lie groups.

\begin{enumerate}
\item[$(a)$] $(Id_G)_* = Id_{\operatorname{Lie}(G)} : \operatorname{Lie}(G) \to \operatorname{Lie}(G)$.
\item[$(b)$] If $F_1 : G \to H$ and $F_2 : H \to K$ are Lie group homomorphisms, then
\[
(F_2 \circ F_1)_* = (F_2)_* \circ (F_1)_* : \operatorname{Lie}(G) \to \operatorname{Lie}(K).
\]
\item[$(c)$] Isomorphic Lie groups have isomorphic Lie algebras.
\end{enumerate}

entries.label: prop:properties-of-lie-group-exponential-map
entries.title: Properties of the Lie Group Exponential Map

entries.label: thm:closed-subgroup-theorem
entries.title: Closed Subgroup Theorem
entries.body: Suppose $G$ is a Lie group and $H \subseteq G$ is a subgroup in the algebraic sense. Then $H$ is an embedded Lie subgroup of $G$ if and only if it is closed in the topological sense.

deps.title: Properties of the Lie Group Exponential Map
deps.label: prop:properties-of-lie-group-exponential-map

entries.label: prop:discrete-subgroups-dimension-zero
entries.title: prop:discrete-subgroups-dimension-zero
entries.body: Every discrete subgroup of a Lie group is a closed Lie subgroup of dimension zero.

entries.label: ex:adjoint-representations-lie-groups
entries.title: Adjoint Representations of Lie Groups

entries.label: prop:adjoint-induced-from-group
entries.title: prop:adjoint-induced-from-group
entries.body: Let $G$ be a Lie group and $\mathfrak{g}$ its Lie algebra, and let $\text{Ad}: G \to \text{GL}(\mathfrak{g})$ and $\text{ad}: \mathfrak{g} \to \mathfrak{gl}(\mathfrak{g})$ be their respective adjoint representations. The induced Lie algebra representation $\text{Ad}_*: \mathfrak{g} \to \mathfrak{gl}(\mathfrak{g})$ is given by $\text{Ad}_* = \text{ad}$.

entries.label: ex:semidirect-products
entries.title: Semidirect Products
entries.body: Group actions provide an important way to construct Lie groups out of other Lie groups. Suppose $H$ and $N$ are Lie groups and $\theta : H \times N \to N$ is a smooth left action of $H$ on $N$ by automorphisms of $N$, meaning that for each $h \in H$, the map $\theta_h : N \to N$ given by $\theta_h(n) = h \cdot n$ is a Lie group automorphism. The \textit{semidirect product of $H$ and $N$} determined by $\theta$, denoted by $N \rtimes_\theta H$, is the Lie group whose underlying manifold is the Cartesian product $N \times H$, and whose group multiplication is $(n,h)(n',h') = (n\theta_h(n'),hh')$.

entries.label: thm:equivariant-rank-theorem
entries.title: Equivariant Rank Theorem
entries.body: Suppose $M, N$ are smooth manifolds, and $G$ is a Lie group acting smoothly and transitively on $M$ and smoothly on $N$. If $F : M \to N$ is a smooth $G$-equivariant map, then $F$ has constant rank. Thus, if $F$ is surjective, it is a smooth submersion; if it is injective, it is a smooth immersion; and if it is bijective, it is a diffeomorphism.

entries.label: prop:proper-action-condition
entries.title: prop:proper-action-condition
entries.body: Suppose $G$ is a Lie group acting smoothly on a smooth manifold $M$. The action is proper if and only if the following condition is satisfied: if $(p_i)$ is a sequence in $M$ and $(\varphi_i)$ is a sequence in $G$ such that both $(p_i)$ and $(\varphi_i \cdot p_i)$ converge, then a subsequence of $(\varphi_i)$ converges.

entries.label: cor:compact-lie-group-actions-proper
entries.title: cor:compact-lie-group-actions-proper
entries.body: Every smooth action by a compact Lie group on a smooth manifold is proper.

entries.label: thm:quotient-manifold-theorem
entries.title: Quotient Manifold Theorem
entries.body: Suppose $G$ is a Lie group acting smoothly, freely, and properly on a smooth manifold $M$. Then the orbit space $M/G$ is a topological manifold whose dimension is equal to the difference $\dim M - \dim G$, and it has a unique smooth structure with the property that the quotient map $\pi: M \to M/G$ is a smooth submersion.

entries.label: ex:real-projective-spaces
entries.title: Real Projective Spaces
entries.body: For each nonnegative integer $n$, the $n$-dimensional \textit{real projective space}, denoted by $\mathbb{RP}^n$, is defined as the set of one-dimensional linear subspaces of $\mathbb{R}^{n+1}$. It can be identified with the orbit space of $\mathbb{R}^{n+1} \setminus \{0\}$ under the action of the group $\mathbb{R}^*$ of nonzero real numbers given by scalar multiplication: $\lambda \cdot (x^1, \ldots, x^{n+1}) = (\lambda x^1, \ldots, \lambda x^{n+1})$. It is easy to check that this action is smooth and free. To see that it is proper, we use Proposition~C.15. Suppose $(x_i)$ is a sequence in $\mathbb{R}^{n+1} \setminus \{0\}$ and $(\lambda_i)$ is a sequence in $\mathbb{R}^*$ such that $x_i \to x \in \mathbb{R}^{n+1} \setminus \{0\}$ and $\lambda_i x_i \to y \in \mathbb{R}^{n+1} \setminus \{0\}$. Then $|\lambda_i| = |\lambda_i x_i|/|x_i|$. Thus the numbers $\lambda_i$ all lie in a compact set of the form $\{\lambda : 1/C \le |\lambda| \le C\}$ for some positive number $C$, so a subsequence converges to a nonzero real number. Therefore, by the quotient manifold theorem, $\mathbb{RP}^n$ has a unique structure as a smooth $n$-dimensional manifold such that the quotient map $\mathbb{R}^{n+1} \setminus \{0\} \to \mathbb{RP}^n$ is a smooth submersion.

entries.label: ex:complex-projective-spaces
entries.title: Complex Projective Spaces
entries.body: Similarly, the $n$-dimensional \textit{complex projective space}, denoted by $\mathbb{CP}^n$, is the set of 1-dimensional complex subspaces of $\mathbb{C}^{n+1}$, identified with the orbit space of $\mathbb{C}^{n+1} \setminus \{0\}$ under the $\mathbb{C}^*$-action given by $\lambda \cdot z = \lambda z$. The same argument as in the preceding example shows that this action is smooth, free, and proper, so $\mathbb{CP}^n$ is a smooth $2n$-dimensional manifold and the quotient map $\pi: \mathbb{C}^{n+1} \setminus \{0\} \to \mathbb{CP}^n$ is a smooth submersion.

entries.label: prop:covering-automorphisms-discrete-lie-group
entries.title: prop:covering-automorphisms-discrete-lie-group
entries.body: Let $\pi: \widetilde{M} \to M$ be a smooth covering map. With the discrete topology, $\mathrm{Aut}_\pi(\widetilde{M})$ is a discrete Lie group acting smoothly, freely, and properly on $M$.

entries.label: prop:normal-covering-transitive-action
entries.title: prop:normal-covering-transitive-action
entries.body: If $\pi: \widetilde{M} \to M$ is a smooth covering map, then the automorphism group of $\pi$ acts transitively on each fiber of $\pi$ if and only if $\pi$ is a normal covering.

entries.label: prop:automorphisms-universal-covering
entries.title: Automorphisms of the Universal Covering
entries.body: Suppose $M$ is a connected smooth manifold and $\pi: \widetilde{M} \to M$ is its universal covering. Then $\mathrm{Aut}_\pi(\widetilde{M})$ is isomorphic to the fundamental group of $M$, and it acts transitively on each fiber of $\pi$.

entries.label: prop:quotient-orbit-space-covering-map
entries.title: prop:quotient-orbit-space-covering-map
entries.body: Suppose $\widetilde{M}$ is a connected smooth manifold and $\Gamma$ is a discrete Lie group acting smoothly, freely, and properly on $\widetilde{M}$. Then the orbit space $\widetilde{M}/\Gamma$ has a unique smooth manifold structure such that the quotient map $\widetilde{M} \to \widetilde{M}/\Gamma$ is a smooth normal covering map.

entries.label: ex:universal-covering-projective-space
entries.title: The Universal Covering of $\mathbb{RP}^n$
entries.body: The two-element group $\Gamma = \{\pm 1\}$ acts smoothly, freely, and properly on $S^n$ by multiplication, and thus $S^n/\Gamma$ is a smooth manifold and the quotient map $\pi: S^n \to S^n/\Gamma$ is a two-sheeted smooth normal covering map. Note also that the quotient map $\mathbb{R}^{n+1} \to \mathbb{R}^{n+1}$ defined in Example C.18 restricts to a surjective smooth map $q: S^n \to \mathbb{RP}^n$, which is a submersion because each point of $S^n$ is in the image of a smooth local section. Since $q$ and $\pi$ are constant on each other's fibers, Proposition A.19 shows that there is a diffeomorphism $S^n/\Gamma \to \mathbb{RP}^n$, and $q$ is equal to the composition $S^n \to S^n/\Gamma \to \mathbb{RP}^n$. Since this is a smooth normal covering map followed by a diffeomorphism, $q$ is also a smooth normal covering map. Thus $S^n$ is the universal covering space of $\mathbb{RP}^n$, and the fundamental group of $\mathbb{RP}^n$ is isomorphic to $\{\pm 1\}$.
